title: Multi-step input composing range incorrectly styled on iOS
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open *Flutter Gallery* and select the *Text field* demo.
1. Tap the *Name* field.
1. Switch to Japanese/Chinese input and begin entering text.

The composing (a.k.a. mark) region is underlined, rather than appearing lightly selected.

See the following screenshots:

## Current Flutter composing style
![img_1413](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/351029/24939269/fcfc5392-1ef0-11e7-9862-48be9d95e015.PNG)

## Default iOS composing style (from Google Inbox)

![img_1414](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/351029/24939276/0bdb17f4-1ef1-11e7-8a29-97103bd4fda6.PNG)

## Default iOS selection style (from Google Inbox)

![img_1415](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/351029/24939281/10af74e6-1ef1-11e7-8a9e-fa8e9bae3969.PNG)



## Flutter Doctor

Running with a local engine with iOS text input patches. Base install is:

```
[✓] Flutter (on Mac OS X 10.12.4 16E195, channel master)
    • Flutter at /Users/cbracken/src/flu
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirmed the bug still reproduces on stable 3.3 and master 3.5 with Japanese IME on iOS. A comment noted that in macOS 14 (Sonoma) the native composing style changed and is now closer to Flutter's existing rendering, narrowing the visual gap. Flutter still shows a thin black underline whereas native iOS 17 shows a thick blue underline. No fix or PR linked; stalled.

title: Support keyboard events in flutter_driver
body:
Add an API for sending keyboard events to devices in Flutter Driver tests.

Useful for writing comprehensive tests of text input, input from physical keyboards, IME compose region etc.
comment_summary: Comments suggest using `integration_test` package (which supports `tester.testTextInput.receiveAction(TextInputAction.done)`) as a replacement for `flutter_driver` for keyboard event simulation. However, this does not cover physical key presses in integration tests. A commenter notes `tester.sendKeyEvent` in integration tests also does not reliably inject text on device. Status: stalled; the original `flutter_driver` API was never implemented.

title: Input validator async
body: The input validator does not support future<String> so if i want to validate making a request i can't because flutter does not support sync http request. Any suggestion?
comment_summary: Long-running feature request with workarounds described: (1) use local state and call validate() when async result returns; (2) use flutter_form_bloc package. Proposal to add AsyncForm/AsyncTextFormField/AsyncFormState without breaking existing API. A DartPad example is provided. No PR landed.

title: Refactor FormField into separate abstract and concrete classes
body:
Right now `TextFormField` extends `FormField` and that's the only place in Flutter where a class extends a concrete subclass of `StatefulWidget`. We should take the parts of `FormField` that `TextFormField` uses and refactor them into an abstract subclass of `StatefulWidget` that they both extend.

/cc @xster @abarth
comment_summary: Comments discuss API compatibility concerns and the general awkwardness of the `builder` pattern in `FormField`. The conclusion was that an API change is not happening but the issue remains open for study.

title: Text fields should automatically support previous and next buttons in iOS keyboard to tab between fields.
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run the Flutter Gallery application on an iPhone.
2. Select the "Text fields" demo.
3. Click on any text field.
4. Notice the keyboard does not show previous and next arrows to tab between field.

## Logs

N/A

## Flutter Doctor

[✓] iOS toolchain - develop for iOS devices (Xcode 8.

comment_summary: Confirmed still reproducing on stable and master. A commenter noted that native iOS Contacts app also does not show the previous/next buttons, suggesting it may not be universally expected. Related to #72625. Status: stalled, long-standing open issue.

title: [iOS/Android] Hot restart while keyboard is up leaves the keyboard up
body:
Fix:

* [x] iOS
* [ ] Android
comment_summary: Comments clarified the issue affects both iOS and Android (title updated accordingly). The root cause is the engine lacks a general hot-restart notification hook to reset plugin state; a pattern of sending an 'init' method via MethodChannel on hot restart was proposed as a workaround. A commenter suggested calling `SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.hide')` in `runApp()` or in `main()` as a Dart-side workaround. A team member (@loic-sharma) fixed iOS but never completed the Android fix; the issue remained open with Android unresolved and the assignee eventually removed after inactivity. The engine does expose a hot-restart notification API that embedders could use instead of a Dart-side fix.

title: TextSelectionControls.handlePaste is racy
body: handlePaste doesn't lock the text field while it's waiting for a response from the clipboard. The user could tap "paste" multiple times in a row, or paste then edit text, or paste then leave the page entirely. Results could vary from the appearance of inoperative UI (only one paste when you tap twice), to data corruption or data loss (paste overwriting what the user has since written), to crashes (if we try to interact with the delegate after it's been disposed).
comment_summary: A production crash was reported with a RangeError in handlePaste when the text editing value has a -1 start offset, confirming the race condition exists. The code path was verified to still exist in later Flutter versions. No reproducible sample exists. Status is stalled with no fix; the issue remains open.

title: getOffsetForCaret should return two values at an LTR/RTL boundary
body:
In `editable.dart`, `getEndpointsForSelection` says:
```
      // TODO(mpcomplete): This doesn't work well at an RTL/LTR boundary.
```
comment_summary: Comments describe the technical solution: `getOffsetForCaret` needs to accept affinity to return two positions at RTL/LTR boundaries. Long-term, `getOffsetForCaret` may be deprecated in favor of easier cursor-drawing APIs.

title: Autocorrect tooltips don't appear on iOS
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

When using a native iOS app without text prediction enabled, there is a pop-up that indicates when autocorrections are going to occur, and when backspacing on a correction a pop-up appears that gives alternative choices for the word.

In flutter, the autocorrections happen silently and require backspacing into the word to make the correction.  See the gif below for a comparison of the behavior.

Flutter should either integrate with the iOS autocorrection system to provide the popups or emulate them.  This is a pretty big usability concern which makes it more difficult to enter text because the corrections are harder to notice and harder to fix.

![text_correction](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/103489/32534122-0182048a-c409-11e7-8879-606857aff89e.gif)

## Logs

N/A, this is a design issue.

## Flutter Doctor

```
$ flutter doctor
Building flutter tool...
[✓] Flutter (on Mac OS X 10.12.6 16G29, locale en-US, channel master)
    • Flu
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments establish that implementing iOS autocorrect tooltips requires returning an accurate `CGRect` from `func firstRect(for range: UITextRange)` synchronously on the platform thread, which conflicts with Flutter's async communication model. @LongCatIsLooong investigated two approaches (blocking with CFRunLoop as done in `FlutterKeyboardManager.mm`, or async prefetching) but both have race conditions or visual glitches that remain unsolved. As of iOS 17 beta, a new regression was reported where the autocorrect highlight rectangle incorrectly highlights the most recently typed character at all times. Spellcheck (underline + context menu) has been implemented separately via engine PRs and framework PRs; the tooltip itself remains unimplemented with the assignee unassigned.

title: Text selection handles overlap UI elements (e.g. AppBars) when selection anchor is scrolled away
body:
Cursor is not respecting the app z-index (it should be under the AppBar)

## Steps to Reproduce

As you can see in the image bellow, the cursor is not Hidden by the AppBar.

![z-index](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1667961/33181696-573f334c-d058-11e7-8195-6f9628c4706f.png)

## Flutter Doctor
```
[✓] Flutter (on Linux, locale pt_BR.UTF-8, channel master)
    • Flutter at /home/bispo/workspace/flutter
    • Framework revision 167382480a (7 horas atrás), 2017-11-23 10:24:16 +0100
    • Engine revision 93b2179597
    • Tools Dart version 1.25.0-dev.11.0

[✓] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK 26.0.3)
    • Android SDK at /home/bispo/Android/Sdk
    • Android NDK at /home/bispo/Android/Sdk/ndk-bundle
    • Compiler in Android NDK at /home/bispo/Android/Sdk/ndk-bundle/toolchains/arm-linux-androideabi-4.9/prebuilt/linux-x86_64/bin/arm-linux-androideabi-gcc
    • Platform android-26, build-tools 26.0.3
    • Java binary at: /usr/local/
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The root cause is that selection handles are rendered in the root `Overlay`, which paints above all other widgets including AppBars, status bars, and bottom nav bars. Comments documented additional affected surfaces (status bar on iOS, bottom app bar, modal bottom sheet top edge). Proposed solutions include creating an Overlay in the `WidgetsApp` body scope or hiding handles via `ScrollNotificationObserver` when they would overlap UI chrome. A community contributor shared a working prototype using scroll notification detection. No fix landed; stalled.

title: TextSelection.isDirectional is not respected, make it do something useful eg: for Mac
body:
It seems like it's not wired up anywhere. We should make it do something useful and test it or remove it.

It's current presence is confusing.
comment_summary: Comments discuss that the behavior `isDirectional` is meant to control is currently handled by `DefaultTextEditingShortcuts`; the value could drive that logic instead of the platform.

title: a11y on Android: cut/paste not announced correctly
body:
When text is selected in a text field and "cut" is chosen from the Local Context Menu, an announcement acknowledging the copy is expected (e.g. "cut \<text\>"). In Flutter that announcement is missing.

Similarly, when "paste" is chosen from the Local Context Menu, an announcement acknowledging the paste (e.g. "pasted  \<text\>") is expected. This one is also missing in Flutter.
comment_summary: Comments provide a detailed root cause: Flutter's async/sync gap means `TYPE_VIEW_TEXT_CHANGED` is sent after TalkBack's `afterCut()` timestamp window, so TalkBack does not associate the event with the cut operation. Sending a fake anticipatory event or manually setting the event timestamp does not work. Behavior has partially improved over time (paste now announces "pasted texts" then reads all content), but the correct announcement for cut/paste actions is still missing on Android. Status: stalled, no PR, engine-level issue.

title: iOS a11y text entry is incomplete
body:
This is a follow-up for issue https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/12786.

As of https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/4575 we have partial support for a11y text entry on iOS.

What works:
 - When "tabbed" or "dragged" into
   - Text fields are announced as "Text field"
   - The hint "double tap to edit"
   - Content of text field is announced 
   - It is announced weather a text field is in edit mode
   - The insert mode (e.g. character mode) is not announced.
 - Entered characters are echoed back
 - After inserting space the last word is echoed back
 - Swiping up or down moves the courser
 - Selecting text with reverse-pinch

What remains to be done:
 - [ ] **No announcement** when a **currently edited** text field gains focus via the "tap" gesture
 - [ ] The position of the cursor is **not** announced 
 - [ ] Copy&paste does **not** work when rotor is in edit mode
 - [ ] TextFields for passwords (`secureTextEntry = YES`) are **not** announced as "secure tex
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed still reproducible on stable 3.19.6 and master 3.22. Comments provide re-verification of each checklist item: cursor-position announcement now partially works, password fields still not announced as 'secure text fields,' copy/paste in rotor edit mode still broken, and password field character count still wrong. Status: open, stalled.

title: Updated AppBar action button during validate callback in TextFormField
body:
Internal: b/292548353

Requirement: 
When `TextFormField` validate callback detects an error, update the AppBar in the Scaffold to disable an action button. 
I was trying to call setState in the validate callback to trigger another widget.build() call. Unfortunately I get "setState() or `markNeedsBuild()` called during build." error. 

What is the correct way to do this behavior. 

I am doing a work around by attaching an onChange callback inside a Form widget that wraps the `TextFormField`. Call validate() on the `TextFormField`'s FormState and setState that way. 

Since Form is not required for `TextFormField`, this doesn't seem like the correct way of doing this.
comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause: FormField's validator() callbacks are invoked during the build phase (inside FormFieldState.build), so calling setState() inside a validator triggers the 'setState() called during build' assertion. A team discussion noted a workaround using scheduleMicrotask() and another using Form's onChanged. Long-term, the team agreed Form should not call validators during build. A commenter identified the specific line in form.dart responsible. Issue remains open with no fix.

title: Support TextSpan in TextFields
body:
Android's `EditText` and `TextView` both support rich text spans.
But in Flutter, only `RichText` widget support rich text spans.
`TextField` should support rich text spans natively using `TextSpan`.

Android example of using text span in edit text component:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3154529/40371551-51f279c2-5e00-11e8-9e0e-f923af4b4ecb.png)
comment_summary: Comments note that `TextEditingController.buildTextSpan` can be overridden to return custom TextSpans (per #30688) as a workaround. A Stack-based workaround (transparent TextField over RichText) was also proposed.

title: Extend Textformfield validation - programatical interaction is missing
body:
I noticed there seems to be no way to interact with Textformfield validation programatically.
This is quite limiting. At least there should be mehtods to set or clear the errors of a Formfield.

But I suggest to rework the validation as a whole and add for example a structure like:

1. To TextFormField or the TextEditingController some methods:
- addValidator(Validator validator)
- removeValidator(Validator validator)
- clearValidators()
- setError(ValidationError error)
- getErrors()
- clearErrors()
- onValidationStateChanges(ValidationState state=>{})
- ...?

2. classes for ValidationState, Validator and ValidationError 

3. Some common Validators for email, length, phone number, regex and custom option

Arguable it would be favorable using an array  for validation errors. So every Validator class has assigned an error, and the TextFormField can have assigned various Validators. Maybe with an option to show only the first unmet Validationerror, or all of them.

T
...[truncated]
comment_summary: One comment highlights the most impactful missing feature: no way to programmatically clear a validation error, leaving empty fields with stale error messages after the user clears text.

title: Handle possible keyboard open/close failures in TextInputPlugin on Android
body:
The TextInputPlugin on Android communicates with Android's InputMethodManager to show and hide the keyboard.  The corresponding methods in TextInputPlugin are `showTextInput()` and `hideTextInput()`.  According to the InputMethodManager contract, calls to show and hide the keyboard can fail, but TextInputPlugin's method contracts return void instead of a boolean.

showTextInput() and hideTextInput() should be updated to return a boolean (or throw an exception, etc) and that success/failure should be made available to the Flutter side in case followup action needs to be taken.

title: No word-breaks for CJK locales
body:
Internal: b/112618495

Flutter cannot properly detect word breaks in Chinese and Japanese text because the ICU configuration that we ship with Flutter doesn't include the dictionary to provide that functionality.

Chinese example:
Type 你好吗 into a text field. Long-pressing on either of the first two characters should select both characters as they form one word.

Japanese example:
Type 日本語学校 into a text field (means Japanese language school). Long-pressing on either of the last two characters should select them both to select the word school (学校).

Compare the result of the two experiments above between a native textfield and a Flutter text field to see the difference.

Other languages (e.g. English or German words) work fine and long-pressing a word selects the full word.

/cc @cbracken @xster @Hixie
comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause: Flutter's ICU data (`icudtl.dat`, ~800KB) uses Chromium's Android ICU configuration which omits the CJK dictionary needed for word-break detection; including it would add ~1.9MB uncompressed. Proposed solutions include: using platform APIs (iOS `CFStringTokenizer`, Android `BreakIterator`), shipping different ICU builds per platform (as Chromium does), or allowing developers to select ICU features at build time. Reproduced on stable 3.19.5 and master 3.22.0-pre. Stalled waiting on an engine decision.

title: [text input ]Detect input via the on-screen keyboard
body:
Per the advice, I [asked this on StackOverflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/50341089/detecting-software-keyboard-input-using-flutter) some time ago now. I'm yet to receive any reply, so I'm starting to think this is a genuine issue.

In flutter, is it possible to detect keyboard input received via the on-screen keyboard?
comment_summary: Workaround provided: use TextInputFormatter to detect key differences between oldValue and newValue. No formal fix or PR.

title: TextInputType.emailAddress should imply autoCorrect: false
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

Create a textfield like this:

```dart
TextField(
  keyboardType: TextInputType.emailAddress
)
```

Run this on iOS. (I didn't check on Android yet.)

You get something like this: ` _______ `

Start typing an email address beginning with a well-known name.

` jim_______ `

Type ".", and notice that the name gets capitalized.

` Jim.______ `

For me, this was unexpected.

To get the behaviour I expected, that is an email address wouldn't autocorrect words, I had to additionally and explicitly do:

```dart
TextField(
  keyboardType: TextInputType.emailAddress,
  autocorrect: false
)
```

I don't think it ever makes sense to autoCorrect on an email field. So, I'm proposing that setting `keyboardType: TextInputType.emailAddress` should imply setting `autocorrect: false` unless `autocorrect` is explicitly set to `true`.


```
[✓] Flutter (Channel dev, v0.5.7, on Mac OS X 10.13.5 17F77, locale en-GB)
    • Flutter version 0.5.7 at 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments note this is not a breaking change since the sync API would remain. Changing autocorrect to depend on keyboardType breaks many tests. Suggestion to default autocorrect to false for email fields.

title: Dynamic Forms
body: How does flutter implement dynamic form ？？？
comment_summary: Comments suggest the `reactive_forms` package as an existing solution for dynamic forms.

title: Animation lag when navigating to screen with `TextField` with `autoFocus` set to `true` on iOS

This effects only `iOS`. tested on `iPhone 6` and `iPad (3rd gen)`

##Demo

[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1LPMdNMf6z6yYBCYM3JMegMx-SQw0vGUs/view?usp=sharing](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1LPMdNMf6z6yYBCYM3JMegMx-SQw0vGUs/view?usp=sharing)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. set `autoFocus = true` on first `TextField` in `text_form_field_demo.dart` of Flutter Gallery.
2. Run the gallery in release mode and navigate to Material -> Text Fields
3. observe the lag. 

if `autoFocus = true` is not set, then navigation animation is smooth. this seems to happen when the keyboard pops up.

```
[✓] Flutter (Channel dev, v0.5.8, on Mac OS X 10.13.2 17C88, locale en-IN)
    • Flutter version 0.5.8 at /Users/hari/development/flutter
    • Framework revision e4b989bf3d (2 weeks ago), 2018-08-09 09:45:44 -0700

[✓] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK 27.0.3)

comment_summary: Comments confirm the animation lag reproduces on iOS in release mode (not in debug) and also on macOS (first navigation only). The lag is caused by the keyboard appearing during the navigation transition. Workarounds involve delaying the focus request with `Future.delayed` or using a custom keyboard. The bug also reproduces on Android. Assigned to team-framework; no PR linked; stalled.

title: TextField does not lose input focus when window loses focus.
body:
To reproduce (Android):
  - place a flutter app in split screen mode with another app.
  - Begin editing a text field. 
  - Tap into the other app, this will dismiss the keyboard
  - Observe that the text field still appears to have focus, the cursor is blinking et cetera.

What should happen:

Upon focusing the other android app, the Flutter input focus should be reset.
comment_summary: Confirmed reproduced on stable 1.20.3 and master 3.5. Additional scenario: after backgrounding and foregrounding the app, the TextField still appears focused with cursor but keyboard is gone, requiring a tap to reopen. A regression where `EditableText` cannot recapture keyboard focus was also noted.

title: Text selection vertical extent should be defined per line (not per glyph)
body:
As [reported by @_mono on Twitter]( https://twitter.com/_mono/status/1023733699614060545), the Flutter text selection highlight rect should be computed at the line level as opposed to per glyph. To see this effect, paste the sequence `(　´･‿･｀)` into a Flutter text field and select it.

Example images below:

Flutter selection:
![flutter](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/351029/45705368-424c8180-bb2e-11e8-9502-3fa404afc066.png)

iOS OEM selection:
![ios](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/351029/45705373-45e00880-bb2e-11e8-930a-156f02e88a15.png)

Android OEM selection:
![android](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/351029/45706301-bdaf3280-bb30-11e8-9ee6-07384c82395c.png)
comment_summary: The root cause is that the engine intentionally computes selection rects per glyph/span rather than per line, tracing to an early engine commit; mixed character sets (e.g., the kaomoji string) are broken into alternating spans, making the mismatched heights visible. Engine PR #6335 and #6644 added a new `getBoxesForRange` API with `BoxHeightStyle`/`BoxWidthStyle` parameters enabling line-level rects, but the framework side was never updated to use them, so the bug was incorrectly closed. As of late 2022 the issue is mostly fixed on Android, iOS, and macOS but a slight vertical offset remains on Linux; status is stalled without a framework-side fix.

title: Option to mimic cmd+k event in IOS to open/hide virtual keyboard
body:
Hello,

I have a physical keyboard attached to my ios device, by default the virtual keyboard is hidden. This can be brought back by pressing cmd+k keys in the physical keyboard. Is there a way to programmatically trigger this key events so that we can bring the virtual keyboard by pressing a RaisedButton widget?

thank you in advance.
comment_summary: Comments clarify cmd+k works on physical devices (not just the simulator) to show/hide the virtual keyboard when a Bluetooth keyboard is connected. No implementation plan.

title: TextEditingController doesn't keep selection on text changes
body:
**Steps to reproduce:**

- Create TextField with "start value" text with `final tf = TextField(controller: TextEditingController(text: 'start value'));`.
- Tap to a middle of the textfield to set the cursor to the middle of the text (like "start| value").
- Programmatically update text in the textfield with `tf.controller.text = 'new value';`

**Expected result:**

- The cursor stays in the middle of the textfield (like "new v|alue").

**Actual result:**

- The cursor moves to the end on the iOS (like "new value|") and to the start on the Android (like "|new value").

**Extra notes:**

- As I can see per [TextEditingController sources](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/989cf18b0daed99ea7c83226bd76fa39693bf94c/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/editable_text.dart#L80) it explicitly drops selection with text update. 

**Flutter doctor:**
```
flutter -v doctor
[✓] Flutter (Channel beta, v0.8.2, on Mac OS X 10.13.6 17G65, locale en-US)
    • Flutter version 0.8.2 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the behavior is intentional by design (the framework explicitly drops selection on `.text =` assignment) but considered a fidelity gap. The workaround — read the selection before and restore it after — causes a visible cursor flicker on Android. Confirmed reproducible on iOS and Android through stable 3.13 and master 3.17; no fix or PR has been proposed.

title: Integrate with Smart Text Selection APIs
body:
Android has an open source [libtextclassifier API](https://android.googlesource.com/platform/external/libtextclassifier/+/master) which is used for smart text selection:

![image2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6687929/46423828-59719e80-c6ec-11e8-8c1d-733635afcaad.gif)

The text selection API is C++ and can potentially be used with iOS but there are a couple of problems:
- The library is part of AOSP so I am not sure if it can be included in Flutter as a first class dependency.
- They generate Intents on Android to open up relevant actions (such as Map). So the current recommended way of integration on Android is via the [Java API](https://android.googlesource.com/platform/frameworks/base/+/refs/heads/master/core/java/android/view/textclassifier/TextClassifier.java).
comment_summary: Comments clarify the feature is Android-only Java API, all inference runs on-device (5-15ms on Pixel 2), and users cannot opt out.

title: Flutter should be able to interact with host clipboard for rich content
body:
When a Flutter application is running on a Chromebook, it should be able to interact with the host clipboard to copy/paste text and other things (images, etc.).

This should support plain text, rich text, images, and application specific types.
comment_summary: Comments reference the `super_clipboard` package as a working solution for cross-platform rich clipboard on all supported platforms. No framework-level fix.

title: Provide API in TextField to open the keyboard
body:
From https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/15882#issuecomment-433563683:

I've just discovered calling `FocusScope.of(context).requestFocus(...)` is not good enough. It does not [request for the keyboard](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/9bf8502fef3f35d8d3ba692ce0383e9f55a34624/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/text_field.dart#L491). This can be an issue in the following case:

1. Tap on text field. We provide it focus. This opens the keyboard.
2. Tap Android back button. Keyboard is dismissed. Field still has focus.
3. Tap on text field again. We try to provide it focus again but this doesn't do anything. **There is no way to get the keyboard back up.** 

In order to display the keyboard, we need to call `requestKeyboard` on the `EditableTextState` but this is [not exposed to us](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/9bf8502fef3f35d8d3ba692ce0383e9f55a34624/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/text_field.dart#L441) as the global key is private.
comment_summary: The original scenario (focus not re-requestable after Android back button dismisses keyboard) was addressed by the merged onTap callback PR #24536. Comments propose a workaround of calling FocusScope.of(context).requestFocus(FocusNode()) before re-requesting focus to force defocus first, and a hack via TextSelectionGestureDetectorBuilderDelegate to call requestKeyboard() directly. Issue scope was narrowed to expose a public API to open the keyboard; still open with no dedicated API.

title: Keyboard disappears when TextField is dynamically wrapped
comment_summary: The root cause is that using a `UniqueKey` instead of a `GlobalKey` on a `TextField` causes Flutter to lose focus state when the widget is reparented during a rebuild. Comments confirm the bug still reproduces on Flutter 1.21 dev, 3.10, and 3.12 stable on Android, with one video showing the keyboard repeatedly dismissing and re-showing on each character typed. A commenter confirms that switching to `GlobalKey` eliminates the bug, and that keeping a custom `FocusNode` alive across builds does not fix it. Currently stalled with no PR or assigned fix.

title: Keyboard submit is off the screen on android Wear 2 device.
body:
Problem is on an Android Wear app, mobile is fine. 

There is no way to enter any input, as when the keyboard appears, submit is off the screen. Example...

```dart
return MaterialApp(
    home: Scaffold(
      body: new TextField(
        keyboardType: TextInputType.multiline,
        maxLines: null,
      ),
    )
  );
```
Screenshot at https://stackoverflow.com/questions/53161631/flutter-reposition-keyboard-down

## Logs
```
LEO DLXX      • QEV7N17B22001003 • android-arm • Android 8.0.0 (API 26)
sdk gwear x86 • emulator-5554    • android-x86 • Android 8.0.0 (API 26) (emulator)

[  +27 ms] executing: [/home/moo/android/flutter/] git rev-parse --abbrev-ref --symbolic @{u}
[  +27 ms] Exit code 0 from: git rev-parse --abbrev-ref --symbolic @{u}
[        ] origin/beta
[        ] executing: [/home/moo/android/flutter/] git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD
[   +4 ms] Exit code 0 from: git rev-parse --ab

comment_summary: Comments confirm the root cause is that WearOS uses a different keyboard mechanism from standard Android, so any non-WearOS-aware app has the same issue. Reproduced on latest stable (3.22) with the submit/done button not visible. No fix is planned; labeled as device-specific.

title: Emojis on keyboard
body:
Hello, I have a problem with the Emojis on Android Keyboard.

The thing is:
In the TextField I must insert a UserName, emojis are not allowed. Besides this while using the Android keyboard the emojis button appear and is available, but in iOS keyboard it does not appear. 

Maybe is not a bug, but I think is a good idea add some TextInputType to choose if add Emojis button or not and it should  be consistent between both systems whether you can insert Emojis or not.

Thanx
comment_summary: Comments add that emoji input availability on iOS is inconsistent. Multiple follow-up questions with no confirmed resolution.

title: Second slow tap on the same spot brings up selection controls
body: As described in the title. Right now the controls only appear on long press.
comment_summary: A commenter noted this issue appears related to #9288.

title: Ability to Integration Test Marked Text
body: In Flutter's integration/driver tests, it is not possible to programmatically type marked text into an iOS text field. Because of this, I couldn't write a regression test for https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/6989. The crash happened when marked text was cleared from a Cupertino text field. I was not able to reproduce the crash using our driver tests because of this despite trying various hacks in the engine code. I stopped short of trying something ugly like installing a Japanese keyboard and tapping on the screen at the right location to hit the right onscreen key. My failed attempts are located in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/24861. The [docs on UITextInput](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uitextinput?language=objc) contain more info on marked text for anyone that's unfamiliar with phonetic input methods like the Japanese Kana keyboard.

title: Programmatically show keyboard when scanner is connected
body:
Hi.
I want to use a bluetooth barcode scanner to automatically fill the fields in my flutter app. The problem is that it prevents a system keyboard from appearing when scanner is paired with a device. I have two fields: one to scan barcode and second to input some text, and I can not type anything in the second field because keyboard isn't showing. Is there a way to force system keyboard to appear even with hid device connected?

title: Add a way to detect keyboard language
body:
Can you please add a way to detect the keyboard language and a way to detect when the uses switches to another language?
This is needed for setting TextInput direction to RTL / LTR based on the currently selected keyboard language.
comment_summary: No platform-specific fix or PR has been proposed. Comments document that on iOS, UITextInputMode / UITextInputCurrentInputModeDidChangeNotification can expose the keyboard language, and on Android getCurrentInputMethodSubtype can get it but there is no documented callback for changes. Multiple community workarounds are shared: detecting RTL via regex on typed characters, using intl.Bidi.detectRtlDirectionality, and a third-party package flutter_bidi_text. Issue is stalled with no active work after 6+ years.

title: [google_maps_flutter] Maps Inside ListView with TextField is unable detect pinch/movie gestures, only double tap works
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug affects Android. Root cause is that after a TextField loses focus and the keyboard is dismissed, subsequent map touch gestures (pinch/move) are not properly handled. A workaround provided by a commenter: add gestureRecognizers: Set()..add(Factory<EagerGestureRecognizer>(() => EagerGestureRecognizer())) to the GoogleMap widget. Issue otherwise stalled.

title: TextField shouldn't merge its semantics node into parent
comment_summary: Comments confirm the semantics-merge bug persists through stable 3.7.x. A workaround of wrapping `TextField` in `Semantics(explicitChildNodes: true)` prevents the merge but creates two accessibility announcements instead of one. A more refined workaround using `ExcludeSemantics` on the sibling text and `Semantics(label: ...)` on the field achieves a single announcement with working double-tap-to-edit. No PR mentioned; status is open.

title: Cursor scroll in text fields too insensitive in Flutter
body:
On Android and on iOS, in both collapsed and expanded selection modes, the single line horizontal scroll is more 'pre-emptive' than our current implementation.

Whereas we try to select the text position based on the drag gesture and bring it into view if it were not, the native texts start scrolling before the drag position is outside the text field. 

This is more aggressive on iOS.
comment_summary: A commenter confirmed the issue is still present and CC'd @Renzo-Olivares for triage follow-up.

title: ScrollViews need to be direct children of Scaffold for TextFields to scroll into focus
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug reproduces through 3.6: `TextField`s in a pushed route without a `Scaffold` do not auto-scroll when the keyboard appears. The underlying mechanism (`Scaffold.resizeToAvoidBottomInset`) is what drives scroll-into-focus behavior, so screens without their own `Scaffold` don't benefit from it. No fix or PR; stalled.

title: Cursor should snap back into another inside the viewport when scrolling a text field
body:
Take a TextField and put the cursor somewhere. 

Scroll the whole textfield putting the cursor outside the viewport. The cursor should change its position to another place in the text that's currently in the viewport to match native Android behavior.
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug is reproducible on stable 3.3.8 and master 3.6. A commenter notes the behavior varies: standard web browser multiline text fields do not snap the cursor, while Android's messaging app does, suggesting it may not be the universal default. The issue is on Android and possibly other platforms. Status: open, stalled, no fix or PR.

When a `TextField` is unfocused, the `labelText` property in an `InputDecoration` is not equivalent to an Android edit text. It appears that it contains too much padding around the label. I attached images to showcase the issue.

The focused case looks fine.

## Steps to Reproduce

<!--
     Please tell us exactly how to reproduce the problem you are running into.

     Please attach a small application (ideally just one main.dart file) that
     r
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments provide user workarounds: `alignLabelWithHint: true` in `InputDecoration` and/or `isDense: true` + custom `contentPadding`. Confirmed still reproducible on stable 3.3 and master 3.7. No fix proposed or landed.

title: TextFormField doesn't support input shortcuts

title: LastPass doesn't support text fields in Flutter apps
body: Might be just another symptom of https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/14047
comment_summary: Comments confirm Bitwarden and 1Password work with some issues. LastPass appears to have its own compatibility problems with virtual views independent of Flutter. A LastPass support case was opened. The issue may be on LastPass's side.

title: TextFormField doesn't render its border as said in the docs.
body:
I think there's a problem in the way `TextFormField` deals with its border.

As far as I understand from the docs, the `border` parameter will take an `InputDecoration` shape and will substitute the color and weight from the others parameters like `enabledBorder` and `focusedBorder`.

So if this is true, the following code should work:

```
TextFormField(
  decoration: InputDecoration(
    enabledBorder: const OutlineInputBorder(
      borderSide: const BorderSide(color: Colors.orange),
     ) ,
     focusedBorder: const OutlineInputBorder(
       borderSide: const BorderSide(color: Colors.white),
     ) ,
     border: OutlineInputBorder(
       borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(100)
     )
  ),
)
```

In this code I define a border shape where corners are rounded and **ONLY** the color of the border changes if it's enabled or focused.

But in fact what I am seeing is that the shape in `border` is being totally ignored and the `TextFormField` is rendered as a 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments identify two distinct sub-problems: `OutlineInputBorder.borderRadius` defaults to `BorderRadius.circular(4)` and must be set on every border state independently (no inheritance from `border`), and large border radius values break the trig code that computes the focused border label gap. PR #93933 improved the label-gap rendering but did not add a `StadiumInputBorder`. A community member provided a standalone `StadiumInputBorder` implementation as a workaround; adding it to the framework is proposed but not yet done.

title: Add ability for text field TextInputAction to be dynamic
body: The textInputAction for a text field is hardcoded at creation of the field. I would like the ability for the text input action to change dynamically based on the content of the field. E.g. if the text field  has valid text in it, make the action show send, but if the field is blank, switch to done.

title: Add desktop shell support for text navigation key combinations
body:
On macOS, typing cmd+right invokes moveToRightEndOfLine: These methods are already received by doCommandBySelector: so we we just need to implement the logic.

On glfw we could use modifier flags https://www.glfw.org/docs/latest/group__mods.html#ga14994d3196c290aaa347248e51740274
comment_summary: Discussion extensively covers three architectural approaches for macOS: (1) let `NSKeybindingManager` map keys to intents and send those to the framework, (2) track key sequences that trigger `doCommandBySelector:`, or (3) preprocess key events before sending to `NSTextInputContext`. Approach 1 was favored as a starting point. Framework-side shortcuts via `Shortcuts` already work for common combos but do not respect user-customized macOS key bindings. The issue blocks IME accent-menu support (#78061) and is also relevant to physical keyboards on iPad. Still open; `_macShortcuts` removal was planned but not confirmed done.

title: Gaining/losing focus and opening/closing the keyboard must not be strongly coupled
body:
As of now, gaining/losing focus and opening/closing the keyboard are strongly coupled, and they shouldn't be. Please see: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/16863. 

One good solution is a change in `FocusNode`. It could have a few more methods besides `unfocus` (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/7247). to maintain backwards compatibility I suggest:

-  `closeKeyboard()` which would close the keyboard without removing focus from the TextField. 
-  `openKeyboard()` which would open the keyboard and connect it to the textField even if the TextField previously had focus already (please note `focus` doesn't open the keyboard if the keyboard already has focus). 
- `shouldOpenKeyboard` is a mutable field which is `true` by default. When the `TextField` gains focus by the user tapping it, or by the calling of `FocusScope.of(context).requestFocus(focusNode);` it would check this field to decide if it should open the keyboard or not.
comment_summary: Workarounds provided: SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.hide') after build (causes visible keyboard animation). The didChangeViewFocus API is now available (noted in a recent comment), potentially enabling a proper fix.

title: Flutter on iOS should support semantic autofilling of text fields
body:
See https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/32265 for some context

We currently support password autofill, but not as nicely as we could.  In a native iOS app, autofill works if there's a password box anywhere on the screen.  After a bug fix for the linked issue, we only support it if the box has focus.  We also only support passwords right now, but could be supporting other types of auto filled input (like user names, emails, etc.).

On issue with fixing this is that the framework currently increments the client id for a text input on each attach - and the engine has no knowledge of whether that client ID is for a new or existing text editing state.  The framework also does not pass semantic information for the type of text entry wanted (beyond keyboard types anyway).

Another issue is that the engine reuses the backing fields that the OS can interact with for autofilling for multiple fields. It would need to have a way to track (and free) those backing objects per text field
...[truncated]
comment_summary: One comment questions the claim about client IDs and notes that autofillable fields do have a stable autofill ID tied to their Element/State.

title: Is that possible to replace the keyboard position with another widget and keep TextField position unchanged during keyboard animation?
body:
Flutter version

```
[✓] Flutter (Channel master, v1.5.9-pre.205, on Mac OS X 10.13.6 17G4015, locale en-CN)
```

Hi, I will try my best to describe the problem. Basically, I want to achieve what many messenger apps do when clicking Emoji icon when keyboard is present. All messenger apps including Facebook messenger, WeChat etc.. are doing the same thing. But I have spent two days to try to do the same in Flutter, but failed. 

First, hiding and showing keyboard are easy by the code `SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.hide');` and `SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.show');`

When clicking the Emoji button, I want the textfield row position remains unchanged, then hide the keyboard and the keyboard original position should be replaced by the emoji panel. But the current behavior is the keyboard showing and hiding actions will change the UI layout during the animation. This does not happen to all the native apps that I use. Something like the follow
...[truncated]
comment_summary: One commenter provides a working code-level workaround using `Scaffold(resizeToAvoidBottomInset: !showEmojiKeyboard)` toggled when switching between the emoji panel and the system keyboard, preserving TextField position without layout jitter. The pub package `chat_bottom_container` is also suggested. No official PR; status is stalled.

title: Unicode input should be indicated.
body:
Ctrl+Shift+U (on certain operating systems) normally would bring up an underlined "u" in a text field to indicate it's awaiting a Unicode entry. This is not indicated on Flutter, despite it taking the input.

![Flutter](https://i.imgur.com/5LGa38g.png)
![Play Store, expected behavior.](https://i.imgur.com/JDQ2sZt.png)
comment_summary: A commenter confirmed the Ctrl+Shift+U Unicode input indicator is observed on Linux but not on macOS; Windows behavior was assumed to be similar to Linux and the platform label was added accordingly.

title: Mixing RTL and LTR text bugs
body:
We are seeing strange behavior when mixing RTL and LTR text.

Notably:

- Type arabic, Type chinese, Type arabic, switch to chinese keyboard, delete. The arabic will delete all at once.
- Emojis work strangely when mixing with RTL and LTR.
- Caret positioning is not consistent.

There are a few other minor issues that I am not clear on how to describe.
comment_summary: Early comment identified root cause: ParagraphStyle directionality is LTR for mixed text, causing spaces to be inserted at the wrong end of Arabic spans. The SkParagraph switch (#39420) was expected to fix many behaviors. Subsequent comments report partial improvements but deletion with opposite-direction keyboards remains broken.

title: Inline Widgets should have modified/ignore safe area changes for keyboard.
body:
When nesting a whole app inline, bringing up the keyboard causes the app to display a big empty area corresponding to the safe area.

This safe area may or may not be relevant for the inlined app.

![Screenshot_2019-06-21-17-52-56-196_io flutter demo gallery](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1887398/59957471-82d68400-944d-11e9-9f8c-a6cf4e8ae7bc.png)
comment_summary: A commenter attempted to reproduce with nested MaterialApp but could not. The issue may require a specific combination of Container/Card widgets. No root cause or fix.

title: Separate callbacks for TextEditingController's text and selection
body:
## Use case

I need to re-populate a list with results of search based on user typing
When the edit gets (de-)focused the TextEditingController's event is triggered as if the actual content changed.

## Proposal

Change TextEditingController so that it has two callbacks instead of one.
Assume it should also fix https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/34847
comment_summary: A commenter noted an additional related bug: assigning the same string value to controller.text still resets the selection instead of early-returning, which should be fixed alongside the callback separation.

title: TextField maxLines should be a double, not an int.
body:
We need to be able to define a fractional maximum number of lines for a TextField. When using an integer number of lines, and there is a lot of text, the user will only see a few of the lines and may think that there are no more.

On the contrary, if you define, say 5.5 lines, and there are in fact, say, 10 lines, the user immediately notices that there is more text to the bottom. In other words, half-lines are visual cues to the users that they are able to scroll to see the rest. 

It's a **small change** that **improves usability**. To be consistent, I would make minLines also double, although in terms of usability that one is probably not important.

This is NOT a breaking change, and preserves existing maxLines behavior. 

Note: I'm the author of https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/19105 which was fixed by https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/27205
comment_summary: Discussion clarifies this may be a breaking change since subclasses override maxLines as int; suggestion to use num type instead. Assignee (@gaganyadav80) was auto-removed after inactivity. Team member expressed openness to a PR.

title: `TextField` should receive the callbacks from `TextSelectionGestureDetector`
body:
Currently, the `TextField` only receives the callback `onTap`, which is called after the `_TextFieldState` handles `onSingleTapUp` from its `TextSelectionGestureDetector`. The lack of other callbacks bothers, though.

Besides supporting all the callbacks from `TextSelectionGestureDetector`, the details (e.g. `TapDownDetails`) should also be forwarded, which is not the case with the currently implemented `onTap` callback.

title: [Web] RawKeyboardListener does not return correct data
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

<!--
     Please tell us exactly how to reproduce the problem you are running into.

     Please attach a small application (ideally just one main.dart file) that
     reproduces the problem. You could use https://gist.github.com/ for this.

     If the problem is with your application's rendering, then please attach
     a screenshot and explain what the problem is.
-->

Gist of code to reproduce the problem:
https://gist.github.com/mjeffw/8f3f57d23b596fde3c1a8dac93a64549

1. Create a flutter web app with a TextField wrapped by a RawKeyboardListener
2. In the onKey callback, check for a specific key using the following code: `if (value.logicalKey == LogicalKeyboardKey.keyA) print('A');`
3. Run the web app and type the expected key. It never matches. 

If I print the logicalKey value of the RawKeyEvent, and type the letter 'A', here is the output:

```
dart_sdk.js:18740 LogicalKeyboardKey#00039(keyId: "0x00000039", keyLabel: "9", debugName: 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm that while the issue was not reproducible on web debug builds in some testing, it fails in release web builds. The skipped tests referencing this issue number remain skipped in the test suite. The issue is tracked via skipped tests in `raw_keyboard_test.dart` and `focus_traversal_test.dart`; those tests have not been un-skipped, so the issue remains open.

title: TextField should warn on vertical overflow
comment_summary: The issue was relabeled to track the need for a debug-mode overflow warning in `InputDecorator`. A prior fix (#63639) was reverted (#64573), and a follow-up attempt (#110966) was closed without merging. The latest analysis by @LongCatIsLooong proposes either changing the layout algorithm to prioritize text over content padding in single-line fields, or adding an opt-in `debugInputDecoratorOverflow` flag. Assignee was removed due to inactivity; no fix landed.

title: Text Editor like in web platform
comment_summary: A commenter clarified the request: the user wants a rich text FormField with formatting controls (bold, italic, lists, links) similar to GitHub's text editor, not just basic text input.

title: Feature request: Setting paragraph distance in Text and TextField
body: Currently, we have no control over paragraph spacing in both Text and TextField. This feature is important in text stying.

title: iOS accessibility focus not returned to textfields after keyboard closed when other widgets are present
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Enable VoiceOver on iOS
2. Open flutter gallery app and navigate to material > text fields
3. Tap on any textfield, double tap to start editing
4. Dismiss the keyboard with done
5. The accessibility focus does not return to the edited textfield, but move to other widget and start to read (if there's something to read).

e.g 
Completes editing name textfield -> starts read Email textfield
Completes editing password textfield -> accessibility focus goes to submit button

This really causes a mess and makes it very difficult to complete multiple text inputs for VoiceOver users. But everything works fine on Android. 

## Logs

```
[✓] Flutter (Channel stable, v1.7.8+hotfix.3, on Mac OS X 10.14.5 18F132, locale en-TW)
    • Flutter version 1.7.8+hotfix.3 
    • Framework revision b712a172f9 (2 weeks ago), 2019-07-09 13:14:38 -0700
    • Engine revision 54ad777fd2
    • Dart version 2.4.0

[✓] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (A
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the iOS VoiceOver accessibility focus issue (focus not returned to TextField after dismissing keyboard) reproduces on stable 3.3.9 and master 3.6.0-pre, not just the original 1.7 report. The same bug is noted in `SearchDelegate`, where dismissing the keyboard moves focus to the bottom or middle of search results. Stalled.

title: Quickly entering text in a textfield with a slow TextInputFormatter may ignore keypresses
comment_summary: Comments suspect the formatter's result overwrites not-yet-processed inputs buffered by the engine. A commenter reports the same issue with Zebra enterprise keyboards. Triage was unable to reproduce on latest master because the slow formatter caused the app to crash.

title: TextField doesn't appear within a direction:Axis.vertical Wrap
body: code: ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8746914/62993013-4bea8080-be88-11e9-84ea-93f3908b57bd.png) screenshot:
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug is specific to `direction: Axis.vertical` and does not occur with `Axis.horizontal`. Reproduced on stable and master; the exception is an assertion that `InputDecorator` cannot have an unbounded width, indicating `Wrap` does not provide a bounded width constraint in vertical axis mode. No fix or PR was proposed; issue is stalled.

title: Textfield in Drawer is hidden behind keyboard when in focus
body:
In this video, I click on the password text field. 
https://youtu.be/-q6xS5Q3CKo
comment_summary: Confirmed on iOS, Android, and web (mobile Chrome) including endDrawer, across stable 3.3 and master 3.7. Drawer is not a child of Scaffold so resizeToAvoidBottomInset does not apply. Two workarounds: (1) wrap Drawer content in a Column with SizedBox(height: MediaQuery.of(context).viewInsets.bottom), (2) use Scaffold as Drawer child with resizeToAvoidBottomInset: true. No fix PR linked.

title: [bug] TextInput cursor positioned over hintText
comment_summary: A detailed analysis in comments confirms the bug persists: the cursor (cursorWidth: 2.0 by default) is drawn on top of hint text rather than under it. The Material spec does not define cursor width; the Figma kit and Compose both show the cursor under the hint text. The fix proposed is to paint hint text over the cursor (z-order change), matching Compose behavior.

title: Selection of any justified-text is inaccurate in non-latin languages
body:
I hope I can clarify this issue because it is a little weird. It took me a while to figure out when exactly does it occur.

Here is a very primitive application:

```dart
// main.dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
void main() => runApp(MaterialApp(
  home: Scaffold(
    appBar: AppBar(),
    body: Padding(
      padding: const EdgeInsets.all(18.0),
      child: TextField(
        textAlign: TextAlign.justify,
        maxLines: null,
      ),
    ),
  ),
));
```

Now, type any latin charachter text. Try to select any single or multiple words, you will find that the selection blue highlight matches accurately the boundaries of the word(s). See this screen shot:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3346502/64191394-5a3e2200-ceb3-11e9-82a9-6f48d6333256.png)

_____________________

Then, put some Korean text. You will find that some words are poorly highlighted:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3346502/64191512-9bcecd0
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments narrow the scope: the inaccurate selection highlight affects Korean, Arabic, Hebrew, and Persian scripts but not Cyrillic (Russian). Using a language-appropriate font (e.g., Amiri for Arabic) fixes the issue for pure RTL text, but mixed Latin+RTL text remains broken even with a proper font. The `selectionHeightStyle`/`selectionWidthStyle` options partially helped but did not fully resolve the issue. The problem is present in all text-alignment modes for RTL scripts, not only justified text. Status: open since 2019, no PR, long-standing stall.

title: [iOS] Physical keyboard input fails after virtual keyboard is hidden
body:
I am currently developing a Flutter app which uses a bluetooth barcode scanner to input text in a text field. The bluetooth scanner is detected as a HID.

One of the constraints of this application is, that a user cannot enter a barcode manually using the virtual keyboard. To prevent this, I automatically hide the keyboard via `SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.hide')` on focus gain.

On Android, this works without issues (Android 9, Samsung tablet).

On iOS, when a HID is connected, it shows what seems to be called `inputAccessory` when a text field is focused. I noticed that after hiding the keyboard/inputAccessory programmatically, there is no input detected from the barcode scanner anymore, even if the text field is still in focus.

Invoking `TextInput.show` restores functionality, but also (obviously) shows the inputAccessory again, which is undesirable.

I would accept this as an iOS specific quirk, however, when I hide the inputAccessory manually by tapp
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.3 and master 3.7 on iOS; Android works without issues. Root cause is an iOS-specific quirk: programmatic `TextInput.hide` disconnects the text input connection so physical/HID keyboard input is no longer received, while manually hiding the inputAccessory via its own arrow button does not disconnect it. One community workaround shared involves calling `TextInput.hide` inside a controller listener on every change rather than once on focus. The issue is labeled `workaround available` but no Flutter fix has been landed.

title: [feature request] Text field of variable size
body:
As you can see below I want implement text field of variable size. Currently I intend to use stack and look for way to get hold of renderbox of text and find out it that is feasible.


![Payroll 2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1231593/64394275-feba9280-d072-11e9-86d6-5247d72c3d4d.png)


Thx
comment_summary: Comments clarify the request is for a TextField that grows horizontally to fit its content (intrinsic width behavior).

title: Scaffold should follow keyboard during keyboard height change
comment_summary: Comments confirm that Android and iOS do not expose frame-by-frame keyboard animation data. The suggestion was to fake the animation similar to how dialogs are animated around keyboard changes.

title: Ability to hide suffix in a text field when not focused
body: Right now, I'm using an IconButton widget as my suffix for my text field. Users of my app are able to click on the suffix from another text field. That is causing numerous focus issues with my app that could be solved if the suffix could be hidden with a boolean value while configuring the form field. If so, people who want to be able to see the suffix of a text field can do so at all times while others can choose to hide it until the field is focused on.

PR #123907 was linked as fixing `prefixIcon` vertical alignment but a commenter noted `prefix` (inline widget) aligns differently and needs further design consideration before being fully addressed. No fix specifically for `prefix` with `isDense`; stalled pending design decision.

title: FormField validators are invoked with incorrect/old values
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

Created a test file that explains what I get and what I expect. Can be found here: https://gist.github.com/daniel-v/f1133f3e39a5714a3774760dab3841b2

**In short**

I'd expect that when I call `FormState.validate()` in response to a `TextEditingController` notification, the validators would receive the value  of the `TextEditingController`. This isn't the case, it seems that `FormState` caches a value, which seems to be updated AFTER validators would run. FormState's value is passed to the validators, therefore validators are always receive a value that's old.

**Question**

Is that expected and is this how this should work?

**Repro**

```
flutter create repro_proj
cd repro_proj
curl https://gist.githubusercontent.com/daniel-v/f1133f3e39a5714a3774760dab3841b2/raw/f41c63228c5bfaadbd56210582613706d095856c/input_validation_test.dart -o test/form_validator_test.dart
flutter test test/form_validator_test.dart
```

## Logs

```
00:02 +0: Trigger
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirmed the bug still reproduces on master (3.6.0+). The root cause is that `FormFieldState.value` is updated after `TextEditingController` listeners fire, so calling `FormState.validate()` inside a controller listener passes the old cached value to validators. A workaround using `scheduleMicrotask` in the listener was proposed. No PR filed; issue remains open.

title: Keyboard closes if something rebuilds underneath it
body:
## Steps to Reproduce


1. ... Run the gist here https://gist.github.com/matejthetree/198c6c7463bd2a1a138b70f4b1175f48
Don't forget to add the plugin to the yaml.
`keyboard_visibility: 0.5.6 `
2. ... Press the textfield 
3. ... The keyboard will close automatically.

If you comment out line 63+ 
```
            _keyboardOn
                ? Container()
                : Text('keyboard is off'), //comment this line to make it work
```
Then, everything works.

How to go around this?




```
flutter doctor -v(base) stablos-MacBook-Pro:set_state_tf korijen$ flutter doctor -v
[✓] Flutter (Channel master, v1.10.3-pre.47, on Mac OS X 10.14.6 18G95, locale en-HR)
    • Flutter version 1.10.3-pre.47 at /Users/korijen/Work/SDKs/flutter
    • Framework revision 928245d07c (3 hours ago), 2019-09-16 06:14:41 -0400
    • Engine revision 224ab48f1b
    • Dart version 2.6.0 (build 2.6.0-dev.0.0 f8f0b845c2)

 
[✓] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android S
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug still reproduces through Flutter 3.6 on stable and master. Root cause is that a setState() call that changes the widget tree while the keyboard is open causes a rebuild that closes the keyboard, specifically when widget types change (e.g., Spacer replaced by Container). No fix proposed; issue remains stalled.

title: Trailing space doesn't work with TextField with TextAlign.right
body:
TextField  Widget Option textAlign Setting for [TextAlign.right] ,input  space key cursor fault


 `TextField(
            textAlign: TextAlign.right,
          )
`
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug persists through 2025 on all tested stable versions. The trailing space is visually trimmed/not rendered when TextAlign.right is used. A Skia bug was filed at bugs.chromium.org/p/skia/issues/detail?id=11933 and accepted. One commenter notes SkParagraph treats \r as a soft line break with CodeUnitFlag 0x0006, which may be related. A layout workaround using IntrinsicWidth inside a Row was suggested. No fix has landed in Flutter; status is stalled.

title: Add `saveValidated()` method to FormState
body:
## Use case

When you have a Form e.g. in a  `TabView` where some of the Fields are valid but others are not you want to save only the validated fields when the user switches to another page, so that when he comes back you can fill the already valid fields with data.
 
Currently `save()` saves all Fields including the not validated.

## Proposal

Add a method 

`FormState.saveValidFields()`  

That will call the `save` method of all valid fields.
comment_summary: Comments suggest this could be an argument to `save()` instead of a separate method.

title: Add `bool validateOnLostFocus` to the `FormField` and `Form` class
body:
## Use case

`Forms` and `FormField`have an `autoValidate` property. But if set to true it will validate on every keystroke/change which is annoying if you start typing e.g. an email address and with the first press the message `Not a valid email address is displayed` 
It would be much more user friendly if an autoValidate option existed that waits with validation till the user(focus) leaves the field when he has finished editing.

## Proposal

add a new property 

`bool validateOnLostFocus`

to the `Form` and `FormField` classes that allows this functionality
comment_summary: Comments discuss a proposal for AutovalidateMode enum (onChange, onBlur/unfocus) as a cleaner API. A commenter provides a workaround using a Focus wrapper around the Form with onFocusChange to trigger validation. The discussion notes that the current approach of using onUnfocus validation has a UX flaw: if the user edits an invalid field, the error message persists even as they type corrections.

title: Spellcheck on Flutter Web
body: Supporting spellcheck for text editing in Flutter Web Engine
comment_summary: Comments from the engine team confirm spellcheck requires: (1) scroll position sync between the hidden `<input>` element and Flutter's text field, (2) keeping the `<input>` alive and positioned after blur, and (3) keeping styling in sync. A JS-based workaround using `setAttribute('spellcheck', 'true')` on focus is provided and documented, but the squiggly underlines appear offset from the actual text position. The workaround does not work with semantics enabled. A prior engine PR #12192 enabled spellcheck for most browsers but not Safari Mobile. Status: stalled, no framework/engine fix, workaround available with known offset defect.

title: TextEditingController documentation should mention that it shouldn't be created in build
body:
If you use Pinyin to input Chinese characters, the intermediate letters will also be treated as "changed" texts when using "onChanged" callback if you set texts for the text controller in the build function. 
This also affects Japanese Input method too. 
 
If you want to reproduce this issue, please clone this repo:
https://github.com/rockingdice/test_multistep_input.git

Tap the Text Field at the bottom of the screen, use any multi-step input method will reproduce this issue.

I could put the controller outside the build as a workaround, but I don't think this behavior is correct. 

@cbracken maybe knew this issue?

This image demos the issue:
I inputted "nihongo" but it gives out all the intermediate letters after each inputted letter.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3458134/65229656-3871ab80-daff-11e9-8fe2-beabd3b81413.png)
comment_summary: Root cause clarified: creating a TextEditingController inside build() resets selection/composing region info on every rebuild, triggering spurious onChanged callbacks during IME composition. The fix is to create the controller in initState. @LongCatIsLooong noted the docs should warn against this footgun; no significant documentation update has landed. Issue reframed as a doc improvement task.

title: TextField/TextFormField labelText and hintText should be right-aligned with TextDirection.rtl
body:

I tried with `textAlign`, `textDirection`. Although, it works with `hintText` and the user input, it doesn't work with the `labelText`.

            hintText: 'hint Email',
            labelText: 'label Email',
            fillColor: Colors.white70),
        tex
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible with a code sample; video demonstration added. A commenter noted they implemented a solution fixing RTL alignment inconsistencies in InputDecoration.

title: [web] Support scrolling inside the text field
body:
There are 2 situations where the text field can scroll:
1. Very long text in a single-line text field (horizontal scrolling).
2. Many lines of text in a multi-line text field (vertical scrolling).

Currently, the scrolling works fine but the issue is that Flutter's field and the browser's field get out of sync, and they are scrolled to different positions.
comment_summary: The issue was initially marked as web-specific but confirmed on macOS desktop too (iOS and Android work fine). A commenter identified font-sync as a root cause on web (Safari uses a different default font than Roboto, causing layout offset between the Flutter canvas field and the hidden textarea), and showed that manually adding Roboto CSS to index.html aligns them. A basic scroll-sync via framework-to-engine messaging was also prototyped. The issue remains open with no merged fix.

title: [web] Support line height + word spacing in text fields
body:
TextInput.setStyle method call informs the engine about the styling info of an editable text.

This method does not support line height and word spacing.

title: Textspan does not support MultiTapGestureRecognizer
body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill our the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please tell us exactly how to reproduce the problem you are running into. -->
```dart
import 'package:flutter/gestures.dart';
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(MyApp());

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  // This widget is the root of your application.
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      them
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause is that `RenderParagraph.assembleSemanticsNode` only supports a fixed set of gesture recognizer types and throws an assertion when encountering `MultiTapGestureRecognizer`. The fundamental fix requires gesture recognizers to set up their own semantics configuration instead of `RenderParagraph` switching over known types (see #40480). Confirmed reproducible through stable 3.5; no fix landed.

title: The platform in editable.dart should probably be obtained from the BuildContext
body:
The platform in [editable.dart](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/b9817b4f595c9ee83bc0625efa60a19599565d63/packages/flutter/lib/src/rendering/editable.dart) should probably be obtained from the `BuildContext`.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/b9817b4f595c9ee83bc0625efa60a19599565d63/packages/flutter/lib/src/rendering/editable.dart#L1607

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/b9817b4f595c9ee83bc0625efa60a19599565d63/packages/flutter/lib/src/rendering/editable.dart#L1665
comment_summary: Comments note `RenderEditable` is in the rendering layer without `BuildContext`. The workaround is accepting the platform as a parameter from the caller who has `BuildContext` access.

title: Cupertino Text Field Placeholder should expand to multiple lines if max lines is not set
comment_summary: A maintainer clarified the expected behavior: with `maxLines: null` and a long placeholder in a constrained container, the placeholder should expand to multiple lines. The issue affects both `CupertinoTextField` and `TextField`. The root cause may be in the engine's `ParagraphStyle` handling when `maxLines` is null. Status: stalled, long-standing proposal with no fix.

title: Allow TextField label fixed size
body:
The UI design I need to implement has fixed sizes for all text field states, focused, empty, etc. AFAIK there is no way to implement this in Flutter - the label becomes larger when the text field is empty and not focused.
See 'Notes' and 'Hourly Rate' on the screenshots below.
I can work around this with a Column/Text/TextField for now but I think it makes sense to fix this on the InputDecorator level.

```
TextField(
        controller: _controller,
        decoration: InputDecoration(
          labelText: labelText,
        ),
      );
```

![Screenshot_20191104-124255](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1481807/68115463-1f7f5700-ff01-11e9-804d-79e3c1fed254.png)
![Screenshot_20191104-124250](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1481807/68115464-2017ed80-ff01-11e9-8539-33b826340a49.png)
comment_summary: Comments note the workaround is using a Column/Text/TextField combination. No API change proposed.

title: Allow control over TextField labelText spacing
body:
I need to implement a design where text field labels are farther up from the text field top.
I don't see any way Flutter allows this.
The closest thing is labelStyle height but it breaks label completely when it needs to wrap horizontally.
comment_summary: Comments provide a code sample showing the label spacing issue. No API proposal or workaround beyond the Column/Text/TextField pattern.

title: [web] Virtual Keyboard on Raspberry Pi
body:
I am still struggling with this issue.  I saw this issue that seemed somewhat related:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/32763

So if flutter web is used on an iphone, the virtual keyboard sounds like it will come up?  I'm wondering then how does flutter know when you're in an environment with or without a physical keyboard?  If I'm running in a web browser for example on a raspberry pi, and there is no keyboard plugged in (I haven't gotten hardware to try this yet...) then does the virtual keyboard come up?  

Is there a way to force flutter to think I do NOT have a physical keyboard?  If I test this on my laptop, the flutter web app works great with my physical keyboard, but I'd like to test with virtual and ultimately I want to us it on a pi with a touch screen so that may already work?
comment_summary: Comments clarified that on Raspberry Pi without a physical keyboard, no virtual keyboard appears in Chrome in kiosk mode because the OS (Raspbian) does not provide an automatic virtual keyboard for browser apps. The team confirmed Flutter has no plans to implement its own in-framework keyboard. The reporter found success using the `virtual_keyboard` pub package combined with `TextEditingController` updates and focus nodes to show/hide the widget keyboard. A workaround using the `readonly` property to prevent the OS keyboard from appearing on non-Pi platforms was also shared.

title: Make TextField grow horizontally first and then vertically
body:
## Use case

I am trying to make a TextField that has the following behaviors:
1. Wraps the text content (both vertically and horizontally).
2. Prioritize growing horizontally until it occupies the maximum width possible.
3. Start growing vertically when there's no room to grow horizontally.

I have an existing implementation that has a behavior that is close to that, but not quite the same:
1. Wraps the text content (both vertically and horizontally).
2. Starts growing vertically when the current width is reached, but when `space` is added, it shrinks vertically and grows back horizontally if it has enough space horizontally.

(This is probably hard to comprehend, will update this bug with screen recordings when I have the time)

The following is my implementation:
```
IntrinsicWidth(
  child: ConstrainedBox(
    constraints: BoxConstraints(
      minWidth: someWidth,
    ),
    child: TextField(
      minLines: 1,
      maxLines: 3,
    ),
  ),
),
```

See
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm horizontal growth is not supported for `TextField` (unlike `Text`). `TextWidthBasis` was mentioned as a reference for how `Text` handles this.
