title: Prevent soft keyboard to show when physical keyboard is used
body:
Hi,

I have a custom keyboard in my app and I have successfully prevented the default soft input panel (SIP) to show when I focus my text field. This is accomplished using two methods:

1. Using a custom focus node for the text field that has `@override bool consumeKeyboardToken() { return false; }`
2. Overriding the `void requestKeyboard()` in the text field state and hiding the keyboard.
See more here: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/16863

This works as expected but the soft input panel is displayed as soon as I press a key on my physical keyboard (either my PC keyboard in the emulator or a bluetooth connected keyboard to my physical device).

Overriding a RawKeyBoardListener and hiding the keyboard using `SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.hide');` makes it popup and then dissapear, causing a very irritating "flicker" and the entire layout of the application shifts around for a split second.

Hiding the keyboard per default when a physical keyboa
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments are predominantly user requests and workarounds with no official fix. The only actionable content: on Android, a platform channel workaround using `InputMethodManager.hideSoftInputFromWindow` is described in detail. The `triaged-desktop` label cleanup bot commented. No Flutter team member has provided a concrete fix plan or timeline. Status: stalled, workaround available (Android platform channel), no PR.

title: CupertinoTabScaffold prevents Textfield from gaining focus
comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause: `CupertinoTabScaffold` calls `_focusActiveTab()` in `didChangeDependencies()` unconditionally, which calls `setFirstFocus` on the tab's FocusScopeNode and steals focus from the TextField in the AppBar whenever dependencies change (e.g., keyboard showing). A user-provided workaround modifies `_focusActiveTab()` to only call `setFirstFocus` when the `tabFocusNodes` list is newly initialized. The bug was reproduced on stable 3.3 and master 3.6 by a triager. A final comment asks if it still reproduces on latest master, with no resolution.

title: Floating cursor on iOS doesn't move on initial drag
body:
In iOS, the users can move cursor fast by long-press and drag space key (floating cursor).

I have found strange behaviors of floating cursor in TextField: 

1.  As soon as floating cursor starts, the cursor freezes up for a while.
2. When stop dragging (=press-up) during the freeze, the cursor gets stuck in the position.

Below is the sample of case-2. 
(I used Japanese keyboard in this movie but it occurred when I used English keyboard.)

![sample](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/18113551/69475980-19e9a280-0e17-11ea-982d-2679bd4d1cbf.gif)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Use TextField and build ios
2. Long press and drag space key
3. Stop dragging immediately (case-2)

**Target Platform: iOS**
**Target OS version/browser: iOS13.2.3, iPhone 11**
**Devices: Both physical devices and emulators**


## Logs
Flutter doctor
```
❯ flutter doctor -v
[✓] Flutter (Channel stable, v1.9.1+hotfix.6, on Mac OS X 10.14.6 18G1012, locale ja-JP)
    • Flutter version 1.
...[truncated]
comment_summary: iOS-only bug confirmed across Chinese and Japanese keyboards on master as of late 2022: the floating cursor does not move leftward on initial drag. A commenter traced it to setMarkedText in FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm and found that commenting out the markedTextRange assignment fixes cursor movement but breaks language switching. Status is open with no fix or PR.

title: Android Cursor Pointer is off center
comment_summary: Comments confirm the off-center cursor pointer is reproducible on multiple Android devices and emulator versions (stable through 3.7/3.3). No root cause or proposed fix is provided in the discussion; comments are largely version confirmations and a final triage ping to @Renzo-Olivares.

title: [TextEditingController] listeners are triggered inconsistently
comment_summary: Confirmed still reproducible on iOS in Flutter 3.27.1; Android works correctly. Root cause in comments: on iOS, the selection baseOffset is reduced before the character is actually deleted, firing the listener twice. Workaround: set `enableInteractiveSelection: false`.

title: Add better handling to TextField for improved UX in Flutter Web in multi-line scenarios.
body:
## Use case
This is specifically related to Flutter Web I think.

When using a TextField widget in Flutter Web, and when that TextField widget has .maxLines > 1, pressing the Enter key does not fire the onSubmitted callback.  This is expected because in a multi-line scenario, the enter key has a different purpose.

Pressing the tab key to blur the TextField also does not trigger the onSubmitted callback.  Users expect to be able to tab out of field when using physical keyboard in a web scenario.  Developers expect to be notified of this, typically via an onBlur callback.

The current workaround that I have found here: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/21318 will work, but it requires getting deeper into the Flutter machinery that I'd like and manually programming an onBlur handler.  Here's an example where I'm building a multi-line TextField Widget for a dynamically generated view and I'm having to manually work with a FocusNode in order to achieve blur-like behavior.

...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments broaden scope: the lack of `onBlur` affects mobile too when users navigate between fields without using Done. The FocusNode listener workaround is acknowledged as functional. No PR.

title: Feature Request: Textfield allow override of key events before Textfield is changed.
body:
Hello. It would be nice to be able to get some callbacks with the chance to change behavior before it is applied to the Textfield. 
Here is an example.
A search box where the user presses the up/down arrow keys to select suggestions. The default behavior of the Textfield is to move the cursor to the beginning or end of the text field. Getting a callback with the key event before the state of the Textfield is updated and a chance to change what happens would be great. 


The other option would be allow rawkeyboardlistener to swallow or forward events to its children based on a handler. Right now if I wrap a TextField inside a Rawkeyboardlistener they both receive the key events without discrimination. 

I’m not sure what approach would be better. But either way it seems a sdk change is needed to make this happen. 

![Screen Shot 2019-12-06 at 2 49 22 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2245494/70355471-d855f000-1837-11ea-8964-9737d43d17f0.png)
comment_summary: One comment from another developer confirms the same use case (search box with up/down arrow navigation conflicting with TextField cursor movement) and asks about workarounds.

title: TextEditingController.text docs miss a reason
body:
`TextEditingController.text` docs say it shouldn't be updated in a `build` method, but it doesn't explain why, it just says the listeners will be called. Is this for performance reasons, so the listeners won't lock the `build` method? Or are there any other issues as well?

I think the docs should include a more explained reason on the side-effects, so devs could evaluate more the impacts of their code and their decisions, or facilitate fixing bugs.
comment_summary: Comments add a clear explanation of the root cause: setting text during build fires listeners synchronously, which can call setState and invalidate the current build frame. @Hixie confirmed this is the reason and suggested the general "no side effects in build" guidance should be added to State.build and StatelessWidget.build API docs; the issue will be addressed as part of this ticket.

title: TextField should have a selectAllOnFocus
body: `TextField` or `TextEditingController` should have a `selectAllOnFocus` property, just like Android has, to easily make the whole text selected once the `TextField` gets in focus.
comment_summary: A workaround using TextEditingController and FocusNode listener is provided in comments. The team lean toward not implementing the property natively given the workaround is straightforward, though original reporter says they wouldn't mind if it stayed low-priority.

title: API should including callback functions to get soft keyboard status including the keyboard visibility and height.
body: Text input is core function for every application. So it is very important for a framework with rich API's on inputting user experience. But now there is no api in flutter to get keyboard status. It should be a call back listener, that we could get keyboard visibility status, keyboard height, then we could adjust our app's layout, adding some overlays besides the keyboard to dismissing keyboard. Even more, It's better the overlay helper could sync animation with the keyboard show up. Following pic shows typical use of keyboard overlay helpers.
comment_summary: One comment notes this is blocked on Android's lack of a frame-by-frame keyboard animation API (see #45076).

title: FocusNode change notifier not called when focused TextField is removed
comment_summary: Comments explain that focus notifications are deferred between frames to avoid spurious notifications when a node moves in the tree, which is why the ChangeNotifier is not called synchronously on unmount. A proposal was made to add a reason to focus change notifications (e.g., unmounted) so clients can distinguish the cause. The proposal received positive reception. No fix or PR linked; stalled.

title: Inconsistent behavior of RawKeyboardListener on different devices/Android versions
body:
I am trying to get my flutter app to react to events generated when media keys are pressed on a little bluetooth keyboard paired with my android device.

Here's the code snippet that builds the widget I use to evaluate the scenario.

![raw-keyboard-listener-code](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1816194/71897913-dc836f00-314f-11ea-8bc9-30f23e1ec891.png)

If I test it on my Google Pixel C tablet running under Android 8.1.0, kernel version 3.18.0-g8ce8444 it works as expected, When the widget is displayed I can press the media buttons on the keyboard and the events are properly generated and passed to the app, handlers are fired etc.

On the other hand, if I test exactly the same code on my Samsung S9 running under Android 9, kernel version 4.9.59-17174122 my attempts to press the media buttons on the keyboard will take no effect until I press one of the numeric keys. After that, the following lines appear in the log 

![Screenshot from 2020-01-07 13:28:00](https://use
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause hypothesis: Samsung Android devices may not produce key events for media keys until an `InputConnection` is opened (which happens when a `TextField` is focused). Pixel devices always produce the events. Suggested to switch from deprecated `RawKeyboardListener` to `KeyboardListener`, though this likely doesn't fix the underlying issue. Status: needs a physical Samsung device to debug further, stalled.

title: When popping a route, controls should reacquire focus without opening the keyboard or scrolling to make visible.
comment_summary: Comments identify the regression cause: a change that enabled focus restoration upon popping a route, which did not exist in older SDK versions. The issue is confirmed on Android and also reproduced on Linux stable (3.19.x). Multiple workarounds shared: calling `FocusManager.instance.primaryFocus?.unfocus()` before pushing, using a `NavigatorObserver.didPush` to unfocus, hiding the keyboard via `SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.hide')` in `didPop`, or using `onTapOutside` to unfocus. No fix has landed; the issue remains open and stalled, with many users requesting resolution.

title: Include a keyboard dismissal callback in TextField
body:
## Use case

When using `TextField`s in Flutter to prompt users for text input, I want to easily detect and react to the user dismissing the keyboard for the current field, via the dismissal button.

Though it is possible to write a wrapper around `TextField` that provides a new callback, based on listening directly to the keyboard dismissal, it would be more-convenient if Flutter provided this functionality out-of-the-box.

## Proposals

* Augment `TextField.onEditingComplete` to also be called whenever the user selects the dismissal button on the keyboard.
* Provide a new method, e.g. `TextField.onEditingStopped` to be called whenever the user selects the dismissal button on the keyboard.  This option would not be a breaking change.

title: TextField: Allow showSelectionHandles=true even if readOnly=true
body:
## Use case

I'm using my own custom keyboard to input text into a text field. To prevent the default "soft input panel" (keyboard) to show on focus and other events we can use readOnly=true + showCursor=true. This is according to issue #16863 (see this commenthttps://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/16863#issuecomment-567201637). 

This is working great for me but in text_field.dart we can find this code snippet: `_showSelectionHandles = !widget.readOnly;`. I would like to show the selection handle under the cursor at all times, even if I'm in readOnly mode.

## Proposal

Allow showSelectionHandle to work even if I'm in readOnly mode. I have tested to copy text_field.dart to a separate file in my project, rename it to e.g. TextField2 and then explicitly setting showSelectionHandles=true even if I have readOnly=true and it seems to work great.

My proposal is to allow showSelectionHandle to be set explicitly as a constructor parameter to TextField and that value should be ho
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Only one comment suggesting the user submit a patch themselves; the team is unlikely to prioritize this esoteric feature.

title: Focus gets lost on Android TV
body:
Run the code below. It displays two raised buttons and a text field and another raised button.

You can use the DPad (or the cursor keys if trying this on the simulator) to change the focus between both buttons. I changed their focus color to make this very obvious.  You can active buttons with RETURN. You cannot activate them using the SELECT button on the DPad but that's something I can fix myself and but part of the bug report.

However, once you focus the text field, you cannot get your focus back and the app ist stuck until you stop and start it again. A hot restart is not enough to free the focus.

Once the text field has the focus, the keyboard opens. On the simulator, it has a DONE button. Selecting that button (don't press ENTER, that's cheating) closes the keyboard – but only if the text field has no `onEditingComplete` callback! As a workaround, overriding `onSubmitted` is triggered on DONE and then the keyboard still closes.

I would expect that `onEditingComplete` 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The root cause identified in comments is that on Android TV (and Google TV), the system keyboard appears as a popup overlay without changing the app's viewInsets.bottom, making it impossible to programmatically detect keyboard show/hide events to restore focus — a flutter-level limitation on that platform. The issue is confirmed on Android TV and Android emulator; multiple users reported it persisting through Flutter 3.7.6 with no official fix. Community workarounds shared include: wrapping TextField in a Focus widget with canRequestFocus: false and a manual onKey handler to redirect D-pad navigation (#39), building a fully custom on-screen keyboard with TextField set to readOnly: true and enabled: false (#32, #34, #35), and creating a stateful widget that swaps a button for a TextField on select (#38). Downgrading to Flutter stable 1.20.2 was confirmed to resolve the issue for some users, with Flutter beta 1.25.0-8.3.pre also cited as working (#22). The issue is stalled with no linked PR and no official fix as of the last comments.

title: TextField “onChanged” was called multi times when i use Chinese inputting mode!
comment_summary: Confirmed iOS-only (not Android) by early commenters; also occurs in Korean/other CJK input modes. Root cause is the engine calling `userUpdateTextEditingValue` twice for IME input. A `Future.delayed(10ms)` workaround was shared that defers `onChanged` until the controller text stabilizes. Still reproducible on stable 3.7 and 3.10; a commenter reports the underlying bug reappeared in flutter version 3.35.7 via issue #134507.

title: Can't bring custom selection into view from Scrollable when showing keyboard
body:
I have a forgot password screen that contains a button at the bottom of the ListView.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/21172855/74021805-90b92500-499c-11ea-8a01-9643dcf71fb8.png)

The default behaviour is the image below. But we want to make sure that the button is visible as well.  
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/21172855/74021811-96166f80-499c-11ea-9474-dd69afe37059.png)

This is the desired behaviour: 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/21172855/74021816-9a428d00-499c-11ea-82d5-f3718d71fe4a.png)

This code sample below is what I have at this point. The animate to does not work because the keyboard animates up. and at startup the the listview can not scroll so there is no need to scroll to the bottom. How can I fix this?
A small screen that could be used for testing:

```

class ForgotPasswordScreen extends StatefulWidget {

  const ForgotPasswordScreen();

  @override
  State<StatefulWidget> createState() => Fo
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed on Android. Scrollable.ensureVisible resolves immediately and does not account for keyboard animation delay, so it cannot replicate EditableText's scroll-into-view behavior. A workaround with a FocusNode listener plus Timer delay was proposed but deemed unsuitable for production. No fix PR linked; stalled.

title: [web][safari] unskip safari text editing tests
body: Some methods in text_editing_test.dart are skipped in Safari. We should either skip them or change them to work in in Safari. (note: there is a chance that the browser has a bug for the tests methods use case)

title: [web] The engine should send correct affinity with text selection
body:
Currently, when the user is using the keyboard to move the cursor in a text field, we report these selection changes to the framework but with no affinity information. This is causing the framework to always [default to `TextAffinity.downstream`](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/bf32974189d0b18c1606800e5a0d2cb05d7bee7f/packages/flutter/lib/src/services/text_input.dart#L639).

To see this issue:
* Use a multi-line text field that has multiple lines of text.
* Move the cursor to the beginning of a line.
* Click the left arrow key to move the cursor back.
* Notice the cursor doesn't move to the previous line yet.
comment_summary: Single comment attempting to reproduce the affinity issue could not replicate it on latest master, questioning if it is still valid.

title: AnimatedIcon more_vert to arrow_back
body: The text selection overflow menu in native Android animates the icon transition between the 3 dot more_vert icon and arrow_back, so Flutter should do this too! However, it seems that [AnimatedIcons](https://master-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/AnimatedIcons-class.html) doesn't support this transition. We should add it. This is what native Android looks like: I noticed this when implementing the overflow menu in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/49391.
comment_summary: Discussion in the linked PR #49391 contains further details on handling the full icon animation. No additional root cause analysis or fix proposed beyond the PR reference.

title: EditableText does not call onEditingComplete on focus loss (macOS)
body:
On desktop operating systems, it's typical for certain types of focus loss (via mouse input or keyboard navigation) to commit a text field's value, but Flutter's `EditableText` widget does not invoke the equivalent lifecycle method, `onEditingComplete`, in this situation. It appears that the only way to invoke the method on desktop is through an enter press.

I believe this to be in error, as the mobile equivalent to a tab/shift+tab press (`TextInputAction.next`, `TextInputAction.previous`) invokes `onEditingComplete`.

cc @gspencergoog
comment_summary: Reproduced on master. Also confirmed on iOS simulator (clicking another field doesn't fire `onEditingComplete`), and on Linux and web. A workaround is to wrap with a `Focus` widget to intercept focus loss. A clarification was added that `onEditingComplete` is intended for overriding default focus-change behavior, while `onSubmitted` signals user completion. Status: stalled, open with stale labels.

title: Textfield content disapears when text is too long
body:
http://pub.waves.pw/ms/textfield.mp4

[✓] Flutter (Channel stable, v1.12.13+hotfix.8, on Linux, locale en_US.UTF-8)
    • Flutter version 1.12.13+hotfix.8 at /home/renan/code/flutter
    • Framework revision 0b8abb4724 (11 days ago), 2020-02-11 11:44:36 -0800
    • Engine revision e1e6ced81d
    • Dart version 2.7.0

 
[✓] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK version 29.0.3)
    • Android SDK at /home/renan/Android/Sdk
    • Android NDK location not configured (optional; useful for native profiling support)
    • Platform android-29, build-tools 29.0.3
    • Java binary at: /home/renan/code/android-studio/jre/bin/java
    • Java version OpenJDK Runtime Environment (build 1.8.0_202-release-1483-b49-5587405)
    • All Android licenses accepted.

[✓] Android Studio (version 3.5)
    • Android Studio at /home/renan/code/android-studio
    • Flutter plugin version 43.0.1
    • Dart plugin version 191.8593
    • Java version OpenJDK Runtime Enviro
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The root cause is that the TextField's `InputDecorator` claims more vertical padding than the given height allows, causing `RenderEditable` to draw outside its bounds silently until text overflows and clipping kicks in. Workarounds confirmed: `isDense: true`, reducing `contentPadding` (including `EdgeInsets.zero` or `isCollapsed: true`). An initial overflow-warning fix landed via #55408 but did not fully resolve the issue; PR #63639 implementing a debug overflow indicator was subsequently reverted as #64573 due to a blocking issue (#48679). A later comment notes the same clipping also occurs with `OutlineInputBorder` without constraining height. Currently unassigned and waiting for a contributor to submit a fix.

title: Need to find how much of a long word could fit in one line before an unnatural line break
body:
As described in issue #35994, there are a number of use cases for doing non rectangular LTR or RTL text layout. Although not convenient, it is possible to do custom text layout by breaking a string into short runs and measuring each run as a `Paragraph` itself before painting it at some desired location. A word/line breaker would make this easier (see issue #50171), but for general cases breaking at a space works OK.

A problem arises, though, when there is text run that is longer than the available length for the line. Take the following example:

```
aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
```

There are no spaces to break at. That means that you have to measure every character or use some algorithm to test chunks of various lengths to see how much of it will fit. An unnatural break will be inserted at that location. The rest of the run will continue onto t
...[truncated]

title: Add keyboard integration test on Android
body: Once the engine has framework bundling pre-submit tests (@collinjackson can you link to the tracking bug?), add keyboard integration tests that involves focusing, unfocusing, selection/composing region tracking, typing, dismissing, etc.
comment_summary: Comments note the `getExtractedText()` API should be tested for responsible usage. Assignment was removed due to no progress.

title: SliverAppBar: Copy/Paste controls for TextField are enlarged when AppBar is expanded.
body:
This example is using a NestedScrollView + SliverAppBar as header. Inside the AppBar, is a TextField.
http://screens.gskinner.com/shawn/2020-03-13_00-36-22.mp4

You can see that the Copy/Paste/Select all controls grow with the scale of the Widget which is not desired.

```
List<String> items = List.generate(30, (index) => "List Item $index");
    return NestedScrollView(
      headerSliverBuilder: (context, bool isScrolled) => <Widget>[
        SliverAppBar(
          pinned: true,
          expandedHeight: 200,
          flexibleSpace: FlexibleSpaceBar(
            centerTitle: false,
            titlePadding: EdgeInsets.only(left: Insets.med, bottom: Insets.med),
            background: Opacity(opacity: .2, child: Image.network("https://picsum.photos/600/300", fit: BoxFit.cover)),
            title: TextField(
                decoration: InputDecoration(hintText: "Untitled List"),
                style: TextStyle(color: Colors.white, fontWeight: FontWeight.bold),
 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The root cause was identified: `FlexibleSpaceBar` applies a `Transform` widget to scale the app bar content, and `CompositedTransformFollower` (used for the text selection menu) picks up and applies that transform to the overlay, scaling the menu as well. The proposed fix is to use a new `FollowerLayer` that only matches the target's position without inheriting the scale. A commenter suggested counter-scaling the handles as the right approach. Issue confirmed on Android and iOS on latest stable and master. Status: open, no PR.

title: TextField: Keyboard not showing on `autofocus: true` after app is paused then resumed in Android
body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill our the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- You must include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Create a `TextField` with `autofocus: true`
2. Run application. The input should be focused and keyboard is showing.
3. Put the application on background, then open it again to send it to foreground.

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
TextField should be focused and the keyboard should be opened.
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue affects Android only; iOS keeps the keyboard visible on resume. The root cause is that the framework does not send a `show` message to the Android text input plugin when the app returns to the foreground, because `EditableText` does not respond to app lifecycle changes and Android app lifecycle changes do not trigger in-app focus changes. A related observation is that simply dragging down and releasing the notifications panel also causes keyboard loss on some Samsung devices (Android 14) but not on Pixel devices running Android 10. The issue is currently stalled with no assignee and no open PR.

When a text field is in the body of a sliver list, and you scroll the sliver list under a sliver app bar, the text field is no longer editable through iOS VoiceOver.

Seems similar to https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/45394.

## Steps to Reproduce

Using the following example app: 

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(Demo());
}

class Demo extends StatelessWid
...[truncated]
comment_summary: A commenter identifies the root cause: when the TextField is not centered in its accessibility semantics node, iOS double-tap from VoiceOver does not properly bring up the keyboard. A workaround is to wrap the TextField in `Semantics(container: true)` to tighten the semantics node. Issue remains open; fix requires changes in the iOS accessibility bridge.

title: After editing TextField, app auto focuses on back button; instead of next available a11y element on iOS
body:
See internal b/151685565.

Steps to Reproduce:

- Construct a page with a TextField and a Button
```
    const Text('Testing TextField A11y'),
    TextField(controller: TextEditingController()),
    FilledButton(onPressed: () {  },child: const Text('button'),),
```

- Double-Tap TextField to edit it
- Then focus on done key on the keyboard, and double tap
The keyboard will dismiss, then it will focus on the back button by default, not the next button.
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on iOS stable and master. After dismissing the keyboard via Done from a TextField, VoiceOver focuses the back button rather than the next logical element. Investigation noted this might be intentional (matching Gmail behavior), but remains unresolved. A related engine PR was mentioned but did not fix the issue on master 3.7. CC'd to @hangyujin. Status: stalled.

title: Widget focus highlighting for iOS/iPadOS
body:
Sister bug to https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/43365 for iPad keyboard support since it's different from macOS. 

This also raises the question of whether we should attempt to detect platform in Cupertino and have different highlighting behavior depending on the platform. 

However, figuring out what the focus highlight spec should be is non-trivial. After testing a bit, iPadOS (13.4) doesn't really have a (consistent) focus highlight handling strategy. It's also definitely different from macOS. 

![QONX7829](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/156888/77811118-8ee31800-7055-11ea-872b-fd51125c6e12.gif)

The task switcher looks the most polished. Springboard quick search looks similar, so does Siri search results. Based on our experience with other glass-frosty things like the action sheet, it's likely a combination of overlay and color dodge.

![EMVU4660](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/156888/77811156-dec1df00-7055-11ea-9fd9-10bc955aadbe.gif)

UITab
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments suggest revisiting in iOS 14 to see if behaviors consolidated. Issue #55809 was mentioned as related. No resolution.

title: SelectableText inside InkWell absorbs Tap and nothing happens
body:
Placing a `SelectableText` inside `InkWell` makes the `onTap` callback stop working. The ink splash is very minimal and the `SelectableText` selects all the text if I long press for a while.

The expected behavior would be that a tap would execute the `onTap` callback, the ink splash would be wide and visible as normal and a long press should be forwared to the `SelectableText` if the `InkWell` doesn't define a `onLongPress` callback, which would in turn select all the text at once.
comment_summary: Root cause explained in comments: SelectableText registers a tap gesture internally to update the invisible cursor, which wins the GestureArena over the InkWell's tap behind it. A workaround using TapGestureRecognizer on TextSpan inside SelectableText.rich() is shared. The ink splash behavior is also affected. No fix or PR has been proposed.

title: TextFields (and TextFormFields) should allow displaying error state without error text
body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation

-->

## Use case

There are occasions where multiple `TextField` (or more likely `TextFormField`) are used on a page and the UX could call for a custom error solution instead of each textfield displaying an error message. We want to maintain the validate logic that exists on the Form but allow the developer to decide whether each textfield should show its error text.

## Proposal

title: iOS text selection toolbar animation
body:
On the native iOS text selection menu/toolbar, tapping Select All causes the menu to fade out and then back in with new children.  In Flutter it's an unanimated sudden change.

| Native iOS | Flutter |
| ---------- | ------- |
| <video src="https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/948037/30b05cb4-6649-4b9c-9aad-1087568d1e3c" /> | ![select_all_flutter](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/389558/79013174-f3ee4180-7b1c-11ea-88d8-b269d15dd96e.gif) |
comment_summary: Comments clarify an existing animation controller handles button fading but the whole menu needs a size animation. Native iOS 17 uses a size animation for both show/hide and select-all transitions. Show/hide is tracked in #107732; this issue focuses on the select-all size animation.

title: Add consistent across all platforms onDonePressed callback to TextField
body:
This issue is a follow up of https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/49785
Please check it out.

TextField's **onSubmitted** and **onEditingComplete** behaive differently on different platforms. 

Currently, there are following differnces:
On mobile - **onSubmitted**  **is not** firing if you tap another TextField or blank space.
On web  - **onSubmitted** **is** firing if you tap another TextField or blank space (undesired).
On mobile web - **onSubmitted** **is** firing when the virtual keyboard is closed and when app sent to background (undesired).

I suggest adding **onDonePressed** callback that would be invoked **only** when user presses **Enter**.

It'd allow to have the same behavior on mobile and web without changing code and creating workarounds.

Thanks!

I'm adding row selection and keyboard navigation to a custom widget TreeTable ([built here](https://github.com/flutter/devtools/blob/ae483b098ec65941f457bf545bdc9059f6537231/pa
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified as the default directional focus key bindings in `app.dart` being enabled on macOS (and potentially Linux). The fix was to disable them at the top level and re-enable per-widget (e.g., ListView). Linux was noted to also use directional nav in some apps, Windows less so. Workarounds provided: wrap in `Shortcuts` with `Intent.doNothing`, or bind `DirectionalFocusIntent` to `DoNothingAction`. Status: partially addressed; the default bindings were intended to be removed but the issue remained open with stale labels.

title: FormField widgets inside ListView bypass form validation and loss of text when the widgets are out of visibility.
body:
## Problem Description
I was making a form for inputting user information. As there were multiple fields, some fields went out of screen visibility and when I am submitting the form these fields are not validated.

When I am using FormField widgets inside ListView :
1. If all fields are visible then they all were validated.
2. If some fields are not visible and are out of focus then they were not validated.
3. If some fields are not visible but anyone of the field is in focus then that widget is only validated while the other which are not visible are not validated.
4. If filling any formfield and scrolling it out of view and focusing on any another field then the text is lost of previous field.

When I am using FormField widgets inside SingleChildScrollView
1. All FormFields are validated, it doesn't matter if they are visible or not.
2. No text is lost on scrolling and focussing on another field, which was earlier.

But the common issue is that the field is not focussed 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug reproduces on stable 1.22.5, stable 3.3, and master 3.7 across Android and iOS: FormField widgets are disposed when scrolled out of view in a ListView, causing validation to be skipped and text to be lost. Multiple workarounds are discussed: replacing ListView with Column + SingleChildScrollView, using `AutomaticKeepAliveClientMixin` with `wantKeepAlive: true`, and wrapping fields with `KeepAlive`. Contributors note that PageView has the same issue and that the Form widget itself cannot detect that its children have been disposed. The issue remains open with no landed fix; the team noted a possible opt-in keep-alive configuration for form widgets and surfacing an error from `validate()` when children are missing.

title: TextField edit menu position problem
body:
When there is a lot of text in the TextField, the actual height of the text exceeds the TextField a lot,   so, if I select all the text, an edit menu will pop up, similar to 'copy / cut / select all'.

But the position of the edit menu will be far away from the TextField, and displayed on the first line of the actual height of the text. 

anybody can help please?
comment_summary: Issue confirmed reproducible on iOS across multiple Flutter versions up to stable 3.7 and master. Comments clarify the toolbar now stays near the visible portion of the TextField rather than jumping far away, but still differs from native iOS behavior: Flutter always shows the toolbar just above the visible text while native iOS shows it in the middle of the text when the text fills the whole viewport. No PR or fix mentioned; the remaining gap in behavior is documented but unresolved.

[RecipeRadar](https://www.reciperadar.com/) currently provides the user with the ability to enter multiple ingredients and/or kitchen equipment items that they would like to include or exclude when searching for recipes.

This functionality is built using a server-side auto-suggestion API that responds to the user's query text with suggested valid ingredient and equipment identifiers (and their corresponding human-readable names).

While the user-facing application does not use `flutter`, we are evaluating it as a possible next-generation application framework, particularly as [support for Progressive Web Apps](https://flutter.dev/web) as a target platform continues to develop.

One detractor that we have encountered is that `flutter` does not yet support a multi-selection auto-suggest search input.

We are currently using [select2's multi-select functionality](https://select2.org/getting-started/basic-usage#multi-select-boxes-pillbox) and have discovered existing 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments add a concrete use case: chip-style multi-select mimicking Gmail's To/CC/BCC field with separator-triggered tokenization. No pub.dev packages of sufficient quality exist. No PR or active implementation work is mentioned. Status is open and stalled.

comment_summary: Comments clarify `showCursor: false` hides the blinking cursor but not the text pointer (drag handle). `readOnly: true` was suggested as an alternative workaround.

title: Let iOS keyboard dismissal synchronize with scroll
body:
Since https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/21814, keyboards will dismiss during scroll. For higher fidelity, rather than one single brisk dismissal event, the keyboard dismissal should ideally be done gradually along with the scroll gesture. 

Android counterpart: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/62876
comment_summary: Comments confirm the feature has never landed despite being discussed for years. Partial engine work landed (flutter/engine#43972, #44457, #44586) for dismiss-on-scroll and flick gestures, but the fully synchronized interactive dismissal matching native iOS behavior is still missing due to the keyboard running as a separate process since iOS 17/18, making screenshot-based approaches no longer viable. A third-party plugin `cupertino_interactive_keyboard` is recommended as the best available workaround. The issue is assigned to @Matt2D with no recent updates; currently stalled.

title: FocusNode does not auto scroll to TextFormField when item already selected
body:
Scenario: this is a big form, the user selects the TextFormField and then manually scrolls down to the save button. if the _formKey.currentState.validate() detects validation issues we can to get the focus back the the TextFormField with issues.

first, we call _myFocusNode.unfocus(); // this works because the keyword is automatically close.
next, we call FocusScope.of(context).requestFocus(_myFocusNode) //this also works because we can start typing right after. but, **the FocusScope.of(context).requestFocus is not automatically scrolling back to the TextFormField.**

If we try to another TextFormField (Tfield2) other than the last selected, the Tfield2 gets focus and the scroll also makes it visible.

NOTE: if we start typing again, the scroll is executed and the TextFormField becomes visible. 

![Jun-06-2020 12-04-57](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5774873/83952488-066bbb00-a7ee-11ea-857e-83bbf4ff9ef7.gif)
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue is reproducible through Flutter 3.7 on iOS simulator and Android inside a `showModalBottomSheet` + `SingleChildScrollView`; calling `requestFocus` on a node that already has focus does not trigger auto-scroll. The root cause identified is that when the focus node already reports `hasFocus`, Flutter skips the scroll-into-view path. A workaround using a `ScrollController.animateTo(0)` before `requestFocus` is documented in the thread. The issue remains open on stable 3.3 and master 3.7 per a 2023 comment with a fresh video.

-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- You must include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Run `flutter create bug`.
2. Update the files as follows: 

Insert an autoFocus TextField widget into the existing column, as in:

```dart
mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
children: <Widget>[
  TextField(
    autofocus: true,
  ),
  Text(
    'You have pushed the button this many times:',
  ),
```

...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause is a race condition on Android where `InputMethodManager.isActive(view)` returns false when `showTextInput` is called slightly too early in profile/release mode; a workaround using `view.post(Runnable)` was proposed in comment #4. The issue also reproduces on web (Chrome and Edge in release mode) with random occurrence; a workaround of delaying focus request by ~150ms via a Timer also works. The race condition is expected to resolve if the platform thread is merged with the UI thread. Status is open with no merged fix.

title: TextField should support typing accented words with external keyboard
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug affects iOS and Android with external Bluetooth keyboards across many keyboard layouts (Spanish, Portuguese BR, Vietnamese, Czech, French-CA) and remains unfixed as of Flutter 3.32.7, spanning six years. A Windows fix via character combining exists at flutter/engine PR #27921 (`CharacterCombiner` class in `KeyboardManager.java`) but was never ported to iOS/Android mobile. Two community workarounds exist: a `dead_keys` pub.dev package (alpha, imperfect) and a custom `TextEditingController` with a hardcoded accented character lookup table. No official fix or PR is planned; issue is stalled and users report significant business impact.

title: [proposal] ability to change text overflow on the TextField
body:
If the user inputs more text than can fit into the available space of a single line `TextFormField`, then end of the text is simply cropped. 
In debug mode, an overflow warning is also displayed on screen.

Unlike the `Text` widget however, the `TextFormField` does not have an `overflow` parameter so that we can for example show an ellipsis in this situation.

Implementing `overflow` handling for the `TextFormField` will allow us to give valuable visual feedback to users.
comment_summary: Comments are largely me-too and bumps spanning multiple years with no root-cause analysis. One substantive comment links a related DevTools bug and notes @LongCatIsLooong is working on overflow ellipsis. The auto_size_text_field package is offered as an imperfect workaround; a TextPainter-based manual ellipsis workaround is also shared. No PR opened.

body:

## Use case

If the hint text of a textfield is larger than the space available, it cuts off the text with ellipses. In normal use-cases this is reasonable enough, but there are some where this isn't - particularly when dealing with accessibility & large text sizes.

If the hint text were to be scrollable in the same way that the non-hint-text is, that would eliminate this issue.

### With accessibility features off:
![image](https://
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments note the Material spec gives no guidance on scrollable hint text. No reference implementation exists for horizontally scrollable hints; feasibility is in question. No PR.

title: Allow to set the width of a TextFormField
body:
**The problem:**
There is no way to set the width of a TextFormField without setting the width of the error text underneath it.

**Alternative Solutions:**
- I've tried wrapping a TextFormField in a SizedBox to set its width. Problem is, the error text in red underneath the field gets the same width, so the error text doesn't look good (see attached photo).
- I've tried creating a custom widget (subclass of StatelessWidget) which will be a wrapper to TextFormField and that accepts a width property. In the build method, I put the TextFormField in a column and add my own Text widget to that column. The custom text should have shown depending on the state of the FormField. Problem is, there is no way to get the FormFieldState.
- At last, I managed to solve it by simply copying and pasting TextFormField code to a new file and making the changes from the previous bullet in order to create my own custom generic TextFormField with the required feature. Problem is, if you ever update Tex
...[truncated]
comment_summary: A workaround is proposed (displaying error in a separate Text widget) but reporter notes it is hacky. No formal fix or PR landed.

title: Autofill a page without interacting with its text fields
body:
Some (web) autofill services let the user autofill the current page in the extension's dropdown menu, so those extensions need to know the text fields ahead of time, even before any of the text fields gets the focus.

Extracted from https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/61301
comment_summary: A commenter linked three related issues (#162066, #137760, #152618) describing a related failure mode where autofill breaks when focus is taken away from the field mid-autofill (e.g., to enter a system password).

title: Improve Form autovalidation API
body:
This is a follow up on https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/59766.

The API for auto validation introduced in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/59766 has some preformance considerations in the `Form` widget that need to be fixed. This issue exist to keep track of that.

The actual issue is that the `Form` widget is auto validating all descendent form fields everytime one of them changes and it is also auto validating a field after it state was reset.
comment_summary: Comments link to the specific thread in PR #59766 that identified the performance issue. No fix implemented.

The bug is occurs in the following code:

import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {

}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwat

comment_summary: The root cause is that `TextField` contains an internal `Scrollable` widget that interferes with `PageStorage`, causing the outer `ListView`'s scroll position to reset every other open/close cycle. A commenter identified that assigning a `PageStorageKey` to the `TextField` itself resolves the issue. The fix was confirmed working by multiple users, but the issue persists without the workaround through at least Flutter 3.3 and 3.7; the issue is open and waiting on a framework-side improvement referenced by @xu-baolin.

title: TextArea widget in Flutter
body:
I want to suggest implementation of a new Input widget that is TextArea widget ,
rather than using TextField and adding the properties maxLines and maxLength to create a feel of TextArea
comment_summary: Comments discuss what new behavior a TextArea widget would add vs. TextField with different defaults. No concrete additions proposed; suggestion to implement as a pub.dev package.

-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- You must include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

![20200728_141801](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3411015/88660233-0649b680-d0df-11ea-8525-1671743e8fcf.gif)


1. Call showDatePicker method and choose edit mode in dialog. (Tested with 'tr' locale)
2. Try to change the TextField manually. It changes from dot(.) format to slashes(/). Then when you enter, you get 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments narrowed the root cause to specific Android device keyboards (Samsung, LG) not showing date separator characters when `InputType.TYPE_CLASS_DATETIME` is requested — the same underlying issue exists in native Android APIs. Multiple workarounds discussed: use `initialEntryMode: DatePickerEntryMode.calendarOnly`, expose `keyboardType` parameter (a PR was opened for this), use locale override (e.g., `Locale('en', 'GB')`), or extend `GregorianCalendarDelegate.parseCompactDate`. Status: open, awaiting a proper fix.

title: [mobile] spacebar cannot delete text  in RTL mode and cursor is misplaced
comment_summary: Comments narrow the issue to RTL text input where pressing spacebar or backspace fails to delete characters, specifically reproducible on physical Android devices with Arabic locale. The triage team could not consistently reproduce on `master` but the reporter confirmed it persists. One commenter suggested a workaround: in the `onChanged` callback, strip invisible RTL/LTR mark characters (`‎` and `‏`) using `replaceAll`. Issue remains open and stalled; no PR.

title: Another section is needed for autofill information
body:
Browser can use a section information on the autofill forms. 

Although this is not [mandatory](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/autocomplete), Safari Desktop, uses labels such as `shipping` in front of the address related fields, otherwise even though the address is visible in the saved forms information, it is not getting re-filled.

An example html form should looks like this:
```
<form method="post" action="#" id="usrForm">
  <input type="text" name="given-name" id="given-name" autocomplete="given-name">
  <input type="text" name="family-name" id="family-name" autocomplete="family-name">
  <input type="text" name="organization-title" id="organization-title" autocomplete="organization-title">
  <input type="text" name="organization" id="organization" autocomplete="organization">
  <input type="text" name="address-line1" id="address-line1" autocomplete="shipping address-line1">
  <input type="text" name="address-line2" id="address-line2" autoco
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Discussion confirms autofillHints already accepts multiple strings, so 'shipping' could be passed as part of the list. Challenge is applying the section correctly as a prefix to the autocomplete attribute. Mobile use case for sections is unclear.

title: Windows: Unable to paste in a focused TextField.
comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause as an invalid (negative) selection state after `focusNode.requestFocus()` sets focus without a valid cursor position, causing a `RangeError` on paste. The issue was confirmed on Windows stable 2.0.1 and master 1.27.0. A commenter suspects the same bug exists on macOS and Linux since the shortcut handler logic is shared. The web build skips the handler and is unaffected. Status: stalled, no PR.

title: Horizontal edge scrolling is broken
body:
Horizontal edge scrolling appears to be broken.  I've tried it on iOS and Android emulators and on a physical Pixel 2.  I've tried TextField and SelectableText.  The bug is present in all of these cases.

CupertinoTextField seems to have slightly better luck. It sometimes feels ok on the physical Pixel 2. On the Android emulator, I see some movement, but it's not really possible to scroll to the far right in one movement.

![edge_scroll](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/389558/90545483-922e8b80-e13d-11ea-91c5-43e0e561d777.gif)



## Steps to Reproduce

  1. Run an app with a one-line TextField (code below).
  1. Enter enough text to go beyond the edge of the screen.
  1. Scroll back to the beginning.
  1. Start a selection, and drag the right selection handle to the right edge of the input.
  1. The input doesn't scroll, and it's not possible to select the rightmost text without stopping the gesture.

<details>
<summary>Code</summary>

```dart
import 'packa
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm horizontal edge scrolling still does not work on Android (and SelectableText), as of master on API 34. A team comment noted the bug may be Android-specific (not appearing on iOS) and further observed that the issue disappears when the `TextField` does not span the full screen width; the root cause is likely the pointer leaving the hit-test area of the full-width field, ending the gesture. A test was skipped pending a fix. No fix or PR linked; stalled with team-text-input.

title: TextEditingController strange behavior on the listener
comment_summary: Comments confirm the listener is still being called extra times through at least 3.27.4, with no fix in sight. One commenter notes the double-fire on focus gain is expected behavior; the multi-call on `controller.clear()` inside the listener is the true regression, reproducible on both stable and master. No PR or workaround identified in comments; issue remains stalled.

title: Show suffix/prefix text in TextField even when it is not in focus and empty.
body:
Closed related issue: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/19488

@willlarche has closed the issue on 21 Aug 2018 because it was impossible to support both the floating animation behaviour and keeping the suffix/prefix at all times. Since flutter v.1.13.2 in 2020 the floating behaviour got smarter and I believe it should now be possible to always show the suffix when `floatingLabelBehavior: FloatingLabelBehavior.always` is set, since the disabled animation would not interfere with the suffix widget.
comment_summary: Comments document no satisfactory workaround: `prefixIcon`/`suffixIcon` misaligns text, and suggested tricks with `FloatingLabelBehavior.never` or `suffixIconConstraints: BoxConstraints()` are acknowledged as hacks. The feature request is to allow `suffix`/`prefix` text to be always visible, analogous to Android's `setExpandedHintEnabled(false)`. Issue cc'd to @bleroux. Status: open feature request, P2, no PR.

import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class Test extends StatefulWidget {

  @override
  _TestState createState() => _TestState();
}

class _TestState extends State<Test> {
  FocusNode _textFocus;

comment_summary: The issue is web-only; on desktop the Enter key event propagates through ancestor `FocusNode`s as expected. A commenter identified the root cause in the Flutter web engine: the Enter key's DOM event is `preventDefault`-ed in `text_editing.dart` to avoid inserting a newline, which prevents the key event from reaching the `FocusNode` hierarchy. The issue persists on both stable and master through at least Flutter 3.7 and 3.9; no fix or PR was proposed.

title: OK/Select on IR Remotes causing Exception
body:
I am receiving this exception when pressing the OK/DPAD_CENTER button.
This is happening on two separate systems, on running Android 4.4 and the other Android 9.0.

```
Attempted to send a key down event when no keys are in keysPressed. This state can occur if the key event being sent doesn't properly set its modifier flags. This was the event: RawKeyDownEvent#065c6(logicalKey: LogicalKeyboardKey#70077(keyId: "0x100070077", keyLabel: "", debugName: "Select"), physicalKey: PhysicalKeyboardKey#700e4(usbHidUsage: "0x000700e4", debugName: "Control Right")) and its data: RawKeyEventDataAndroid(keyLabel:  flags: 8, codePoint: 0, keyCode: 23, scanCode: 97, metaState: 0, modifiers down: {})
'package:flutter/src/services/raw_keyboard.dart':
Failed assertion: line 601 pos 12: '<optimized out>'
```

This happens on stable through to master

Doctor summary (to see all details, run flutter doctor -v):
[✓] Flutter (Channel master, 1.22.0-10.0.pre.82, on Linux, locale en_AU.UTF-8)
 
[✓]
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified by @gspencergoog: IR remote scan codes are translated through the keyboard physical-key mapping, causing scan code 97 to map to "Control Right"; the assertion fires because the metaState modifier bit for that key is not set. The proposed fix is to skip modifier-key synchronization when the input source is not a keyboard, which is detectable via platform APIs. A user-contributed workaround patches `hardware_keyboard.dart` to bypass modifier synchronization for non-keyboard sources, and another workaround intercepts the OK key in `MainActivity.dispatchKeyEvent` and re-dispatches it as `KEYCODE_ENTER`. No official PR was opened; issue is stalled with community workarounds only.

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {

comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug is specific to UniqueKey — using any other key type does not reproduce the focus-loss behavior, narrowing the root cause to UniqueKey's identity semantics forcing a full widget rebuild. Reproduced on stable (3.3.5) and master (3.5.0-pre) across Android and iOS physical devices. No fix, workaround, or linked PR was proposed; issue is stalled with no active investigation noted.

title: Constrained TextFields compensate for prefix/suffix when none exist
comment_summary: Comments confirm the `fixBelowBaseline` miscalculation persists on stable 3.3.5 and master 3.5. The workaround noted is that at least one app relied on the bug for visually-acceptable baseline placement. The proper fix requires both removing the spurious offset and also properly positioning the baseline under constraint. No PR; open and stalled.

title: TextFormField.onFieldSubmitted with TextInputAction.next fails inside a GestureDetector which requestFocus
comment_summary: Root cause: calling `FocusScope.of(context).requestFocus(FocusNode())` with an unattached FocusNode closes the input connection; when `TextInputAction.next` fires it tries to traverse focus, hits the detached node, and throws `'context != null'` from `ReadingOrderTraversalPolicy`. Logs show `IInputConnectionWrapper: beginBatchEdit on inactive InputConnection`. Affects Android (confirmed on stable 3.3 and master 3.6). Status: open, no fix or workaround offered.

title: Autofill on web is only activated after clicking twice on the TextField
comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: the web engine creates the native DOM input element dynamically and calls focus() programmatically, but browsers only show the autofill dropdown when the user physically clicks on the element — not when focus is set via script. Confirmed still reproducing through Flutter 3.19/3.21. A workaround noted is setting autofocus: true, which causes the field to show suggestions on first click. Another workaround is using Platform Views for a login form. Issue stalled with no fix.

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
Should print 'f1 pressed'

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
Nothing

<details>
  <summary>Logs</summar
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments show the issue is not consistently reproducible: one triager could not reproduce it on master while the reporter could. One commenter explains the likely cause is a "stuck key" (a key held down during focus switch leaves `_pressedKeys` in an inconsistent state). Another commenter clarifies that a related issue is a usage mistake requiring `Shortcuts` wrapping when multiple focus nodes are present, pointing to #109912. The issue is stalled with no fix and labeled as a potential false positive for some sub-cases.

title: [iOS] Support Text Selection Submenus
body: On native iOS, some items in the text selection menu may open submenus: Flutter doesn't support this. With https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/31412 ([design doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1hyay1TyUZB7DXzWw45-ruGMZM1yCMWEnC3DzOJYemuc/edit#)), Flutter will support much more customization of the text selection menu. Once that is finished, we should provide an easy way to do submenus, too. See also: * https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/35826, an issue that was fixed by adding support for overflowing text selection menus. Opened up support for a more dynamic iOS text selection menu that gets close to submenus.
comment_summary: Comments note that `contextMenuBuilder` now allows custom submenus. The native iOS context menu (via `SystemContextMenu`, added in #148265) currently has no customization support including submenus.

title: TextField TextInputType "name" overrides TextCapitalization "words"
body:
It appears that `TextField`'s `keyboardType` of `name` prevents the iOS keyboard from offering capital letters at the beginning of words, even when specifying `textCapitalization: TextCapitalization.words`.

Repro:
```
TextField(
  keyboardType: TextInputType.name,
  textCapitalization: TextCapitalization.words,
);
```

**Expected result:**
When the user selects the `TextField`, the keyboard pops up with uppercase characters.

**Actual result:**
When the user selects the `TextField`, the keyboard pops up with lowercase character.

This same issue might happen on Android but I haven't looked.

**Flutter version:**
```
Flutter 1.22.0-10.0.pre.9 • channel master • https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git
Framework • revision 8fa5c55e54 (5 weeks ago) • 2020-08-30 19:05:20 -0700
Engine • revision 1e302a111d
Tools • Dart 2.10.0 (build 2.10.0-76.0.dev)
```
comment_summary: Comments confirm the root cause: iOS's UIKeyboardType.namePhonePad (used by TextInputType.name) explicitly does not support auto-capitalization, so textCapitalization: TextCapitalization.words is ignored. Issue is iOS-only; Android works correctly. Three workarounds documented: (1) avoid TextInputType.name, (2) use an InputFormatter to force capitalization, (3) file a PR. Confirmed still reproducing through stable 3.29 and master 3.32 on iOS 18.4.

          child: Column(
            children: <Widget>[
              TextFormField(
                decoration: const Input
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the floating label of OutlineInputBorder is clipped when the TextField is placed at the very top of its container with no padding above it. A triager initially could not reproduce but was corrected by a commenter who clarified the issue occurs specifically with the first field when there is no top padding. Additionally, a commenter reported the same clipping occurs when wrapping an OutlineInputBorder field in a SizeTransition animation, while UnderlineInputBorder is not affected. Status is open and stalled.

title: Disabled TextField InputDecoration errorText allocates space but is not shown
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.3 and master 3.6. The error text space is allocated but not rendered when `TextField` is `enabled: false`. Status: stalled, no fix or PR.

title: KeyboardListener is buggy after focusing a TextField
body:
This is primarily an issue for physical keyboards without touch screens but it is acting weird even with touch screens. I posted this same question in a closed issue here but I didn't get any responses so I guess it's a new bug: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/11177

This repro is as simple as this _(but my repro is slightly improved to show focus and removing unused code)_:

1. `flutter create chromebook_keyboard`
2. Add `final _keyboardListenerFocus = FocusNode();` to the state class
3. Add `String _keyName = 'N/A';` to the state class
4. Override dispose() and call `_keyboardListenerFocus.dispose();` in the state class
5. Add the following to the 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug on Android (phones, tablets, Chromebooks with physical keyboards), and one comment confirms it does not occur on iPad with physical keyboards, making this Android-specific. A partial engine fix in flutter/engine#22340 restored delivery of `RawKeyEvent`s after a TextField was focused, but the soft keyboard still pops up on every hardware keypress after TextField focus. A workaround using `SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.hide')` on each keystroke was attempted but slows input; another workaround uses the `flutter_barcode_listener` package with the TextField set to `readOnly`. Multiple users reported that switching from Gboard to other keyboards (Samsung, TEC-IT Scanner Keyboard) eliminates the soft-keyboard pop-up, pointing to Gboard as a contributing factor. As of Flutter 3.29.1 on Android 15 the issue persists and remains unfixed.

title: CupertinoTextField Cursor position incorrect
body: The cursor position for CupertinoTextField is off-center. The top textfield is a UISearchTextField (type of UITextField) made in Swift. The bottom textfield is a CupertinoTextField made in Flutter. As seen above, the cursor position, relative to the first character, is about 1 px too distant. Another example (lying on top of each other): Kinda related: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/7034
comment_summary: Comments add that cursor position relative to the placeholder also differs from native iOS. @GaryQian was mentioned for context on how Skia determines cursor size.

title: Add type-specific Clipboard methods
body:
@justinmc As discussed in #60145...

Based on iOS 14's new [UIPasteboard APIs](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uipasteboard), we need to add type-specific methods to Flutter's [Clipboard](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/services/Clipboard-class.html) utility:

- `Clipboard.getImages`
Used most commonly in chat apps. Should implement `UIPasteboard` and `hasImages`. ([Docs](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uipasteboard/1829411-hasimages)) 
- `Clipboard.getNumbers`
Used most commonly for auto-verifications. Should implement `UIPasteboard` and `detectPatterns`. ([Docs](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uipasteboard/detectionpattern)) 
- `Clipboard.getStrings`
Used most commonly in journaling/editing apps. Should implement `UIPasteboard` and `hasStrings`. ([Docs](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uipasteboard/1829416-hasstrings))
- `Clipboard.getUrls`
Used most commonly for deep-linking and auto-population of URLs. Should
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments note that iOS 16 introduced a system alert whenever an app accesses clipboard data, making type-specific APIs (hasImages, hasURLs) critical to avoid prompting users unnecessarily. A third-party package `uipasteboard` was published as a workaround. A contributor offered to submit a PR if given direction.

title: Allow custom spacing between helperText, errorText and border on InputDecoration
body:
## Use case

There is currently no way of adding a custom spacing between `helperText`, `errorText` and `border` in an `InputDecoration`.

The look can be altered by changing the line height of the `helperText` and `errorText`, but this solution does not work for multi-line text and is just a work-around, not a true solution.

## Proposal

Allow custom spacing between `helperText`, `errorText` and `border`.
comment_summary: One comment notes Flutter has historically been slow to add features not in the Material spec, and `InputDecorator`'s layout is already complex. The request is considered reasonable but stalled.

title: Usage of form validators doesn't feel right - Proposal for better return type
comment_summary: Discussion concluded that changing the `validator` return type would be a breaking API change. The suggestion to use a `Result<S,E>` type was acknowledged as interesting but too big for a small change. A recommendation was made to file a design doc and add this to the big-breaking-changes issue (#24722). Status: proposal stalled, no action taken.

title: Active subrange within composing range should be visually highlighted in Flutter
body:
## Description

When inputting text in languages that use multi-step input (composing mode), there are two ranges involved:
1. The overall composing range
2. The active subrange within the composing range

This is common when inputting text using [ateji](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ateji) in Japanese, but occurs in general with longer composing ranges, person, and place names.

To support this, we'll need some visual highlighting of the text to make clear which range is the active subrange, as well as changes to the text input protocol so that the engine can specify this range to the framework. It seems unlikely to me that users will need to programmatically control the range via TextEditingController, so ideally the API surface should be as minimal as possible.

## Repro steps and expected behaviour

For example, when inputting the name 真惟佳 (Maika), one would go through the following steps:
1. Type the phonetic kana characters まいか.
2. Press shift-left arrow to restrict
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue affects Android (even without hardware keyboard, via Gboard's soft arrow keys), not just desktop. On Android the active subrange is shown with aqua-green highlight in native apps but Flutter shows no highlighting and the caret stays at the end of the composing region. An engine PR #49314 partially fixed macOS. Issue was reopened after that partial fix. Currently P1 with a note that it has had no monthly updates.

title: Linux: Emoji insertion via Ctrl-Shift-E incorrectly positions completions
body:
## Description

In https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/21897, I landed support for multi-step input methods, which also added support for unicode codepoint insertion via Ctrl-Shift-U and emoji insertion via Ctrl-Shift-E.

That said, the completion window position when not in composing mode (e.g. for emoji) is incorrect. 

To support this, I believe we'll need notify the GTK embedder of every change to the cursor position (even when not in composing mode), then remove [these lines](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/0747f2f4b1167a6fdfca0d04a8e4f0b862e7f130/shell/platform/linux/fl_text_input_plugin.cc#L324-L327). Removing those lines currently results in incorrect positioning of the IM window when composing mode is entered after committing a previous composing region.

## Expected behaviour
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/351029/96529318-b3911e00-1239-11eb-8183-c8215ffc047a.png)

## Current behaviour
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments discuss whether the cursor rect is currently sent on each keypress outside composing mode; ownership of that implementation was unclear between contributors.

title: Android with a physical mouse should still show text selection menu
comment_summary: Comments suggest using `TargetPlatform` to resolve the issue. Bot label comment is noise.

title: [Windows] TextField text being misplaced within its boundary when resizing window
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug (TextField text/fill misplaced when window is resized to small sizes on Windows) is reproducible on both stable 3.3 and master 3.6. The triager was unable to reproduce the exact misplacement seen by the reporter but observed visually abnormal output at minimum window sizes. No root cause was identified, no PR was linked, and the issue remains stalled.

title: Support text selection programmatically for the SelectableText widget
body:
Flutter currently does not provide a way to select text programmatically for the SelectableText widget. 

On Android we do that using the `Spannable` API, see https://stackoverflow.com/a/41760489/9185192
On iOS we a property to define a selection range, see https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uitextview/1618615-selectedrange

### Proposal

It would be great to have this capability in Flutter to help native developers easily migrate their apps to Flutter. I suggest to have this capability on `SelectableText` widget since it is the only one text visualizer that support text selection.
comment_summary: Comments note the proposal was labeled for the team. No discussion of implementation or priority.

Hi thanks for Flutter! I encounter a problem as follows: When the constraints (maxHeight) of `TextField` is smaller than the text size (or text line height), TextField will *shift everything up* so *the upper half cannot receive touch events and the lower half cannot be rendered*, which is quite abnormal. See the screenshots fo
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the shifting bug is reproducible on stable 3.3 and master 3.6 — `InputDecorator` moves its child upward when constrained height is less than the text size, causing the top portion to be outside the hit-testable and paintable area. No root cause analysis or fix was proposed in comments beyond confirming reproduction.

title: [web] TextField double caret on iOS
comment_summary: Comments confirm the double-caret issue is specific to old Safari (iOS 9.x) and does not reproduce on Safari 14 / iPad 8. The root cause is that Flutter renders its own cursor while the browser also shows a native cursor, and they get out of sync. No workaround or fix was offered; a question about whether hiding the browser cursor via `index.html` would help was raised but not answered. Status: open, old OS-version-specific, no PR.

title: TextField in NestedScrollView scrolls editing line out view port
comment_summary: Comments confirmed reproduction on stable and master via a provided video. The bug occurs when editing lower lines of a multiline TextField inside a NestedScrollView on Android—the current line scrolls out of the viewport. Issue #37721 flagged as possibly related. Status: triaged as P2, team-design, stalled.

title: TextFormField (and TextField) widgets do not wrap text correctly
body: Similar to the issues reported in #18761, the input fields in Flutter suffer from the same disfunction. Currently, the text input fields in Flutter do not wrap their input correctly. Instead of breaking a word in the middle and wrapping the remainder to the next line, Flutter is breaking the string at the word boundary closest to the end of the line, which is non buono. #### Flutter #### Chrome Web browsers correctly split the string at the character that would otherwise overflow. #### Code snippet ```dart TextFormField( decoration: InputDecoration(border: OutlineInputBorder()), minLines: null, maxLines: null, initialValue: 'https:// ...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug is distinct from #18761 (which is about read-only Text widgets), and the issue persists through at least Flutter 3.10.6 / Dart 3.0 on macOS. Multiple commenters reproduced it with long URLs in `TextFormField` using `FractionallySizedBox`. No proposed fix or PR exists; status is open and unresolved.

title: Volume keys can't be caught by focusScope with autofocus
comment_summary: Root cause identified as Android-version-specific: on Android 9+ devices (tested on Pixel 6 Android 13, Xiaomi Mi A2 Android 10, Samsung XCover6 Pro Android 13), Flutter's `FlutterView` does not initially have focus for hardware key dispatch, so volume and other hardware keys are not routed to the Flutter key event system until the soft keyboard is opened or a text key is pressed. Works correctly on Android ≤6 devices and is an emulator-specific non-issue. Multiple workarounds shared: invoking `TextInput.show`/`TextInput.hide`, using `WidgetsBinding` to autofocus a hidden TextField then immediately redirect focus, and a native Android workaround overriding `onKeyDown` in `MainActivity` or using `pview.requestFocus(); view.requestFocus()` in `onStart`.

title: TextField RTL input problem with LTR letters/numbers while obscureText is true
body:
