The characters are not written in the correct direction while typing (this issue appears only when obscureText is true).

The input value actually is submitted correctly with no problems and also if the password is made visible it is shown in the right way with no issues, the issue only is while typing so it makes it feel like if the password is written in a reversed direction.

**While typing the letters are written to the left not to the right as expected.**

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create an empty application.
2. Add a TextField  or TextFormField to the application.
3. Set textDirection: TextDirection.rtl
4. Set obscureText: true.
5. type something in English.


![screen-20201127-184354](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/30502213/100470646-51052a80-30e1-11eb-84a7-838968ba2232.gif)


### Expected behavior

To act the same like when obscureText: false.

### Flut
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Reproduced across many Flutter versions up through 3.3.x. The cursor visually appears on the left during typing but the submitted value is correct. Multiple related RTL issues linked (#47745, #50098, #54099). Status: stalled, long-standing RTL/obscureText regression affecting Android and iOS.

title: SelectableText with custom scrollPhysics inside SingleChildScrollView inconsistent scroll behaviour
comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: when AlwaysScrollableScrollPhysics is set on the inner SelectableText's Scrollable, setCanDrag(true) is called, preventing the outer SingleChildScrollView from receiving scroll events. A workaround is to set physics on the outer scrollable widget. No fix or PR has been proposed. Currently stalled.

title: Material text selection menu overflow buttons should be full-width
body: Tapping in the empty space to the right of "Custom button 2" should hit the button. Currently, it has no effect. ### To fix this: 1. Make the buttons full-width by setting the min and max width in their layout to the full width of the menu ([code](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/53119af105d2e8808e4e8be3675b6bdd3668f4b9/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/text_selection.dart#L472)). 1. Align the text in the button to the left or right depending on the text direction. Currently, TextButton always centers its child and can't align to the left or right.
comment_summary: Discussion suggests making `TextButton` support configurable alignment. PR #73894 added the `alignment` parameter but text selection toolbar overflow buttons still need to use it, and the parent layout needs to size to the largest intrinsic width of its children.

title: Selection toolbar/handler should disappear when it is opened and user clicks outside of it
body:
## Steps to Reproduce - Issue /1

1. Run the code bellow as mobile-web (or go to this deployed website: https://epic-thompson-badb9e.netlify.app/#/).
2. Select text in the text_field/selectable_text.
3. Tap outside of the text_field/selectable_text

**Expected results:** The selection toolbar/handler should disappear.
**Actual results:** Selection toolbar/handler is still visible and the only way to remove it is to select one of the options in the toolbar.

Note: while reproducing you can notice some unwanted behavior: double tap select text but not show selection toolbar. Let's focus on the actual issue.

![gif](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/33294549/101696283-308c8700-3a76-11eb-947c-efdfa4cb1556.gif)


## Side Note - issue /2

About the selection toolbar not disappearing reproduces on `stable` and on `master` channel. The toolbar not disappearing on mobile apps does not follow the Material Design rules: https://material.io/design/platform-guidance/android-
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: `SelectionOverlay` uses `Overlay`, which renders above all other widgets including AppBars, so there is no z-ordering mechanism to clip handles behind app bars. Confirmed on Android and iOS. One commenter proposed using `ScrollNotificationObserver` in `EditableText` to hide handles when they scroll out of bounds, and has a working prototype. No fix landed; issue is stalled.

title: Bug in Material text selection menu button layout when using narrow buttons
body:
In the case of text selection menu buttons that are narrower than the overflow button, it's possible that there is underutilized space in the text selection menu.

Currently, the algorithm lays out as many buttons as can fit until they overflow, then checks if removing the overflow button could allow everything to fit:

```
|------- width -------|
[o] [button 1] [button 2]
```
```
|------- width -------|
[button 1] [button 2]
```

Removing the overflow button [o] allowed both buttons to fit without needing an overflow menu at all.

However, if the overflow button is wider than an overflowing button, this algorithm will not work perfectly:

```
|------- width -------|
[overflow] [b1] [b2] [b3] [b4]
```

The algorithm will not remove the overflow button here because it assumes that the overflow button is narrower than b4, which is not correct.

This is unlikely to happen in reality because the overflow button is hardcoded to a small IconButton, but with the custom
...[truncated]

title: GBoard's contact suggest feature does not show up for Flutter text input
body:
Internal: b/348443241

GBoard allows users to enable contact suggestions which take effect when someone is entering a name or a phone number. It seems this works for Web and Android native fields but not for Flutter. From customer:

> We already tried `TextField` with keyboardType: TextInputType.phone, number, email, etc but still cannot see the suggestion chip.
comment_summary: Comments identify the fix: adding `autofillHints: [AutofillHints.telephoneNumber]` causes GBoard to show contact suggestions, because `keyboardType` is inferred from `autofillHints` but not vice versa. The fix is to infer autofill hints from `keyboardType`.

title: [Autofill] Switch to `SecSharedCredentials` on iOS, and explore how we can expose it in the framework
body:
## Use case

I work on a multi-platform e-commerce product that is available in several countries and has many associated domains. When using `AutofillGroup` as recommended by the [docs](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/AutofillGroup-class.html) to save login/signup credentials for the first time, they get saved in association with the wrong domain. 

This is problematic because it can cause confusion to customers. To illustrate, consider the example below.

- Users from Germany access `company.de` on Safari and register their credentials using the keychain (which correctly associates these credentials with the `.de` domain).
- These users decide to access the product via the Flutter mobile App. When they try to log in on iOS, the keychain correctly identifies that the App has the `.de` associated domain and suggests the previously registered credentials.
- Users accept the suggestion and get their username/password auto-filled. 
- However, when pressing on the submissi
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Issue repurposed to explore using SecSharedCredentials API on iOS for saving credentials to the correct associated domain. Assignee (@LongCatIsLooong) was auto-unassigned after prolonged inactivity with no status updates.

There's a community plugin for this but it has a complex API and becau

comment_summary: Only a stale comment asking for updates; no substantive new information.

title: RawKeyboard listener not working as expected when holding a key on Android Emulator
comment_summary: Root cause confirmed by a commenter's investigation: Android's `dispatchKeyEvent` in `FlutterView` emits paired down/up events on emulators, and this is Android emulator behavior — on real devices with a physical Bluetooth keyboard, key-repeat down events fire correctly and up fires only on release. The issue is emulator-specific, not a Flutter bug. A separate web-specific variant (key-up for held key when another key is pressed simultaneously) was noted as a distinct issue filed at #182775.

title: TextField is not working after backspace when using Frozen Keyboard
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on Android (Redmi 5, Samsung Galaxy S8 stock keyboard, various Samsung devices). The Frozen Keyboard app fails to call `endBatchEdit()` after dispatching a backspace keydown event inside a batch edit, leaving the `InputConnectionAdaptor` in a broken state; this was traced by @justinmc. A user-reported workaround is disabling the Unicode switch in the Frozen Keyboard settings. Engine PR #24500 was linked as a potential fix. The root cause is specific to the Frozen Keyboard's non-standard IME behavior; other apps are unaffected. Issue is stalled with no official fix landed.

title: Proposal to support Hex Keyboard format
body:
I would like an option for keyboard TextInputType to support hex value entry.

https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/services/TextInputType-class.html has many nice options for email, numbers, dates, names, street address, phone, etc.  Hex would be just numbers and A-F of course.

title: TextField adding new line when shift modifier not pressed (macOS)
body:
Issue on desktop-embedding, running a project for macOS. No combination of TextInputAction's provides the expected behaviour for creating new lines.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run `flutter create bug`.
2. Add a TextField with a textInputAction of either TextInputAction.done or TextInputAction.newline
3. Press enter without holding shift, a new line is created. Press enter with holding shift, a new line is also created. 

**Expected results:** New line is only created when the shift modifier key is held.

**Actual results:** New line is created regardless of modifier keys. Only TextInputAction.unspecified does not create a new line, but then it does not handle modifier keys either.

<details>
  <summary>Logs</summary>

<!--
      Run your application with `flutter run --verbose` and attach all the
      log output below between the lines with the backticks. If there is an
      exception, please see if the error message includes enough information
      to explain how
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug is reproducible on latest stable and master channels on macOS, with both `TextInputAction.done` and `TextInputAction.newline` failing to respect the shift-key modifier. A workaround using `RawKeyboardListener` with `TextInputAction.unspecified` was shared in the first comment. The issue remains open with no fix or PR referenced; the only triage activity was a bot note that the `triaged-desktop` label is irrelevant without a `team-desktop` label.

title: ☂️ Proposal: desktop context menu fidelity
body:
The desktop text selection toolbars, as introduced in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/73882 and https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/74286, have major limitations that make them not as good as native.  We should either find a way to use the native context menus, or we should work to fix each of these problems.

  * We can't draw the menu outside of the current window.  Also, the menu can't receive mouse events (like hover) unless the current menu is focused.
  * The only buttons available are the default mobile buttons (cut/copy/paste/select all).  We should support all of the same buttons that display natively.
  * It should be possible for Flutter devs to customize the menu.
  * The native Mac menu has a flashing animation when clicked.
  * Submenus should be possible and easy to implement.
  * Currently, only EditableText widgets can show the menu. Right-clicking elsewhere doesn't show it.  Fixing this probably requires refactoring TextSelectionControls to work wit
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments describe the gap between Flutter's desktop context menus and native ones. Key developments: @justinmc landed #107193 making `DesktopTextSelectionToolbar` and `CupertinoDesktopTextSelectionToolbar` public and fully widget-based, allowing developer customization. PR #107193 merged. Many of the original issues (customization, button set, widget-based API) are now addressed. Remaining items (native blur/glass effect, native menu integration) are noted but no further active work tracked.

  }
}
```
</details>

Steps to repro:
1. Run the sample app


2. Focus the text field by tapping on it.
3. Press <kbd>R</kbd> to hot restart the app. The software keyboard stays.
4. Type a few characters using the software keyboard without re-focusing the text field.
5. Focus the text field. The app throws the following exception:

```
Performing hot restart...                                               
Restarted application in 849ms.

══╡ EXCEPTION CAUGHT BY FOUNDATION LIBRARY ╞════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
The following LateError was thrown while dispatching no
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments identify the issue was caused by PR #72803 which was already reverted. The whole feature was potentially going to be rewritten, so the issue was kept open as a test case.

title: Number text field
body:
## Use case

Currently, it's very hard to develop a number text field. And I'm not talking about just allowing digits (and plus, minus and period/comma) but also limiting the number of those characters so that we can input only one period, only one minus (or maybe two in case of a scientific notation) etc.

## Proposal

Either a new `NumberTextField` or similar or an official `NumberTextInputFormatter` that works just like the `input[type=number]` and guarantees that `double.parse` will work for the text field's value.

I tried to implement this myself, but maintaining correct cursor position and selection is really hard for me https://gist.github.com/Albert221/8f3e68f34eac0783f84c9f869b7857be
comment_summary: Comments clarify the issue is not just about filtering digits but also enforcing number format rules (single decimal point, etc.). The StackOverflow workaround was noted as insufficient.

title: Support IME reconversion on Windows
body:
In https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/23853 we added support for IME-based input in the Win32 embedder. That patch did not include support for handling `WM_IME_REQUEST` events. In particular we should support:

* IMR_RECONVERTSTRING
* IMR_DOCUMENTFEED
* IMR_QUERYCHARPOSITION

See the documentation for the [WM_IME_REQUEST](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/intl/wm-ime-request) event, which links to information on the above three message types, for more details.
comment_summary: A commenter noted the triaged-desktop label was irrelevant. A community member suggested the pub.dev package `windows_ime_manager` as a potential workaround for managing IME on Windows.

title: Android Back gesture should dismiss text selection menu, not also navigate
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run `flutter create bug`.
2. Update the files by making a SelectableText widget:
```diff
diff --git a/lib/main.dart b/lib/main.dart
index d8a052651..7aca97c4e 100644
--- a/lib/main.dart
+++ b/lib/main.dart
@@ -95,3 +95,3 @@ class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
           children: <Widget>[
-            Text(
+            SelectableText(
               'You have pushed the button this many times:',
```

3. Run the app on Android, and long-press on the text. This will select part of the text and bring up the text selection menu.
4. Activate the system back gesture (i.e., swipe from edge, if on Android 10+ and have that enabled; or tap the system back button otherwise.)

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->

The text selection menu should disappear, and the app should otherwise remain as it is.

This is the normal behavior of the text selection menu on Android, and is a way that users are accustomed to dis
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments establish that Android behavior is inconsistent across apps: Gmail and Chrome navigate back while Google Search App, Google Maps, and Wikipedia dismiss only the menu. A test against a native Android Studio template app confirms that the standard native behavior is to dismiss the toolbar first. No PR was opened. The discussion also notes this parallels #72029 (tap-outside to dismiss) and that the behavior is preferable to dismiss-only to avoid inadvertent navigation loss.

title: Let RenderEditable use LineMetrics instead of assuming every line has the same height
body:
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/02d441ea55b328133c266991f43b0a1148edb63f/packages/flutter/lib/src/rendering/editable.dart#L864-L873

Vertical key movements may skip a line if that line is way shorter than its adjacent lines.
comment_summary: A commenter added a concrete example illustrating the expected caret behavior for up/down arrow key navigation across lines of varying length, where the caret should maintain its horizontal offset across lines rather than snapping to line end.

title: Missing cursor and paddings for TextFormField on certain linux distros
comment_summary: The issue is confirmed to be distro-specific: the cursor missing and widget overlap occur on Fedora 30 but not on Fedora 31, CentOS 7, or PopOS 20.04. The error logged alongside (`Could not get cmap table size`) is identified as a red herring unrelated to the cursor rendering. No fix or PR was proposed; the thread ended with a request to clarify which specific distro configuration triggers the problem.

title: EditableText calls onSelectionChanged even if the selection does not change.
body:
If you add a print statement in this method https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/934dfde42f3f2c8c7665f9874812f7c35d846ff4/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/text_field.dart#L1070

long press the textfield, you will see the print statement even if text selection does not change. This behavior is contradicted to the EditableText.onSelectionChange contract. Unfortunately, this is needed because textfield relies on this call to update the handle visibility.

We should fix this by refactoring out selection overlay of editable text so that text field does not need to rely on this callback, and then we should finally able to fix the EditableText.onSelectionChanged.
comment_summary: Comments note the issue was incorrectly closed (PR #87971 was reverted in #88183) and the TODO remains. Confirmed still reproducible on master.

title: RenderEditable shouldn't assume its selection is non-null and valid.
body:
e.g.: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/ff8f4717f798e890cf2fb7abc61fd04ac2b4e21f/packages/flutter/lib/src/rendering/editable.dart#L735-L905

Additionally, `TextEditingController` (and subclasses) should probably assert when an invalid selection is assigned to it.

title: Access to Shift-toggle Keys using LogicalKeyboardKey
body:
## Use case

The [`LogicalKeyboardKey` docs](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/services/LogicalKeyboardKey-class.html) state the following:

>A class with static values that describe the keys that [...] represent logical keys, which are keys which are interpreted in the context of any modifiers, modes, or keyboard layouts which may be in effect.

This works for most keys, however, there is this whole class of **logical** keys that is not covered:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19204050/108902927-9cfa3980-7614-11eb-93c2-20d1f90c3251.png)

The symbols at the top of these buttons (`!`, `@`, `#`, `$`, `%`, etc.) are all **not covered** by `LogicalKeyboardKey`.  

If I use e.g. a [`LogicalKeySet`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/LogicalKeySet-class.html) as in:

```
LogicalKeySet(LogicalKeyboardKey.shift, LogicalKeyboardKey.digit8)
```

In order to achieve the `*` symbol (multiplication/asterisk), this might work for an English keyboard layout, h
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments clarify convenience statics for shift-toggle characters (like `ampersand`) are missing but the underlying mechanism works via `LogicalKeyboardKey(0x26)`. Issue #77048 was opened separately for layout-dependent key mapping problems.

One commenter confirmed it also appears when using VoiceOver. No fix has been proposed; the issue has been re-routed to the iOS embedding team.

body:

However, I am not sure if this is the nature of how it is... but to me, something seems wrong here.

Here is another demo for Japanese!

```
[√] Flutter (Ch
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause as CanvasKit dynamically loading CJK fonts when unknown characters are first encountered; switching to the HTML renderer avoids the issue. The workaround of using a custom font family (bundled) also resolves it. Community notes this affects Hebrew and Arabic too, and persists through 3.32.4. The issue was marked P1 but has had no recent owner; status is stalled.

title: [web] Text selection toolbar doesn't show up on first interaction with SelectableText
body:
When running on web, the framework doesn't show the text selection toolbar. Instead, we rely on the browser to show its own.

SelectableText creates a text field under the hood to handle selection, etc.

The problem is the first interaction with the SelectableText happens before the underlying `<input>` has been created. So the browser doesn't show the context menu (aka the toolbar) until the user interacts again with the text.

**Upon first interaction**:

![Screenshot_20210302-095723](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1278212/109693473-8d13b580-7b3e-11eb-8a31-d286249583a0.png)

**After subsequent interactions**:

![Screenshot_20210302-095733](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1278212/109693542-a3ba0c80-7b3e-11eb-9b49-7452020f97ca.png)
comment_summary: Only one comment asking for updates; no fix or investigation.

title: [proposal][desktop] Tab character (\t) should be displayed as indentation in TextField
body:
## Tab Character \t should be displayed as indentation in EditableText, TextField and all implementations of Text widgets in Desktop
#### This is a common behaviour in many desktop applications

Flutter Channel: **master**
Platform: **Desktop, Linux**
Version: **2.1.0-11.0.pre.138**

When Tab key is pressed while the TextField is focused, in a Flutter Desktop Application, by default, the focused TextField is unfocused and the focus shifts to another widget. That is a problem in my case as I want the user to be able to enter tab characters. But I partially solved it by using a RawKeyboardListener to detect when the user presses Tab key, and then inserted a \t to the TextField at the current cursor position through its controller.
The problem is that, the \t character is displayed as a small space, almost half as wide as whitespace, which is counterproductive.
There should be an option to configure how \t is displayed, as it can be of multiple number of spaces' width, and it sho
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments develop a detailed proposal: add `acceptTabs` and `indentSpaceCount`/`tabWidth` properties to `TextField`/`TextStyle`, and provide `IndentLineFormatter`/`ReplaceTabFormatter` input formatters. A contributor shared working workaround code using `Actions`/`Shortcuts` to intercept Tab and insert spaces or `	`. A design doc was created (linked in comments). `@justinmc` committed to folding the feature into an ongoing text editing refactor, but no PR has landed and the issue remains open.

title: [web] <input> too big in date picker "input" mode
body:

title: Focus lost when interacting with keyboard
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug reproduces on macOS desktop but works fine on Flutter web and is still present on stable (3.7.x) and master (3.11.x). The root cause is that arrow key navigation at the edges of the custom widget traverses the global focus scope and moves focus to nearby interactive widgets (e.g., AppBar buttons) rather than staying within the widget. A workaround using FocusScope with onKey returning KeyEventResult.handled prevents traversal out of the scope, but at the cost of disabling Tab navigation out of the scope. Status is open and stalled.

title: [macOS] Selectable rich text with gesture recognizer should be focusable.
body:
In renderEditable, we break Selectable rich text into multiple semantics nodes if the platform is NOT macOS.

Flutter does not support text selection across multiple semantic node, but that is ok for mobile platforms because their assistive technology does not allow selection in selectable text, and they also does not use the text selection information from the semantics node.

This is not the case for macOS. The macOS will read the selection text range and announce it back to the user, and that works well if the selection is only in one semantics node. It does not provide a public API to implement selection across multiple semantics node. Therefore, we should force selectable text to send one semantics node to macOS until they have API to do selection across multiple semantics node.  


<details>
<summary> repro </summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/cupertino.dart';
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter/rendering.dart';
import 'package:flu
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Single comment links the issue to its originating PR #77730.

title: HID Input not being processed on iOS 13+
body:
We're tring to use a HID scanner to get input from barcodes. Prior to Flutter 2, we were able to do this on iOS devices, but after updating to Flutter 2, HID input isn't processed on iOS 13+ unless a `TextField` is selected (or possibly some other form of input receiver).

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a new Flutter 2 project.
2. Change the `main.dart` and `AppDelegate.swift` to what we have below.
3. Deploy the app to an iOS 13+ device.
4. Send HID input to the iOS device, (Bluetooth keyboard, HID barcode scanners).

**Expected results:** HID inputs are always caught by the AppDelegate.

**Actual results:** HID input is only caught if a `TextField` widget is selected.

Running these steps on Flutter versions prior to 2.0.0 will always get the HID input.

## Code sample

For the Flutter side, you only need a TextField widget:

<details>
  <summary>main.dart</summary>

``` dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(MyApp());
}

c
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the regression appeared in Flutter 2.0: HID keyboard input (via AppDelegate `keyCommands`) is only received when a TextField is focused, whereas pre-2.0 it was received regardless. The underlying cause appears to be that Flutter 2.0 changed how UIKeyCommands are handled in the engine. No fix; stalled.

title: [Autocomplete] Expose the latest TextEditingValue in optionsViewBuilder
body:
This requires updating the function signature for [AutocompleteOptionsViewBuilder](https://master-api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/AutocompleteOptionsViewBuilder.html), but it allows users to use the current text values to customize rendering of the options UI.

This would be a breaking change. However, there is currently a workaround for this at the Flutter app level by keeping track of the `TextEditingController` value yourself to customize rendering.

title: [chromeOS] TextField backspace doesn't work properly and text select cannot be selected with Japaense input
comment_summary: Comments add no new diagnostic information — a triager noted they lack the device to investigate, one commenter linked a related issue #78221, and another asked for updates. Status is stalled with no root cause, fix, or PR.

title: Wrong vertical alignment of text on TextFormField widget if suffix widget is used.
body: When I specify the `suffix` property for `TextFormField` the text in widget become not middle aligned by vertically. If searched on stackoverflow and found that [this issue](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/57659523/how-do-you-vertically-align-text-in-textformfield-with-a-suffix-in-flutter) is enough old. I don't mean _padding_ from left, I mean it is not aligned middle vertically. I tried to use `Padding` or `contentPadding` - it does not help.
comment_summary: Comments confirm the vertical text misalignment when using `suffix` (not `suffixIcon`) reproduces on stable and master, and persists as late as 3.32. Community workarounds: use `suffixIcon` instead of `suffix`, or set `suffixIconConstraints: BoxConstraints(minWidth: 22, minHeight: 22)`. No framework fix landed.

title: [Linux] keyboard stops working after using some backspacing
body:
Opening this issue to track the problem of keyboard not working on linux after using backspace a few times as asked by @dkwingsmt in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/44918#issuecomment-800822902...

> At the moment, I only know that the keyboard works and then stops working after using backspace a few times (only backspace keeps working) and that it occurs on Linux (I can only test on Linux and Windows)... The only way to get the keyboard to work is closing and opening the app again...

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17789155/111481683-f7695000-8711-11eb-8c01-cae2817e7bfd.mp4

On the above video, at 0:10 the keyboard has stopped already... The video was made on Ubuntu 20.04 in a laptop, but I have successfully reproduced the issue on Fedora 33/34 on a VM too...
comment_summary: Root cause partially identified: the Linux engine emits a warning message 'Event response for event id ... received, but pending event was not found' whenever keyboard input stops working. Reporter suspects it correlates with machines that have slower processor speeds (1.2–1.3 GHz). The issue is intermittent, harder to reproduce on VMs, and a separate commenter reports the bug also occurs with the space key causing inkwell effects instead of inserting text. No fix or PR proposed; stalled.

title: Windows word modifier inconsistencies
body:
On Windows, navigating through text with the arrow keys while holding control behaves slightly differently than other platforms, and Flutter misses this.  Windows jumps to the start of the next word in either direction, while other platforms jump to the end of words when moving right and the beginning of words when moving left.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run the app below on Windows, which is just a TextField with "word word word" in it.
2. Focus the field and put the cursor at the start.
3. Hold the word modifier key (ctrl on Windows, option on Mac) and press the right arrow key.

**Expected results:** "word |word word"

**Actual results:** "word| word word"

This is the correct behavior on all other platforms, but not on Windows.

<details>
  <summary>Code</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
 
void main() {
  runApp(MyApp());
}
 
class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  final TextEditingController controller = TextEditingController(text:
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed on Windows 10 master. Windows jumps to start of next word in both directions, while Mac jumps to end when moving right and start when moving left. No fix or PR.

title: Arrow keys in RTL move the wrong way
body:
The left/right arrow keys should move the caret left/right respectively, regardless of the text direction.  In Flutter they move in the wrong direction in RTL text.

The methods that handle this in RenderEditable should be updated to perform the correct behavior without being renamed.  ~~So for example, the method [_moveGivenSelectionLeft](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/8c44abc009a333b0494598c0cfda0fbc07358234/packages/flutter/lib/src/rendering/editable.dart#L735) actually moves the selection upstream right now, not necessarily left.  It should be modified to move the selection left like it says.~~ This is out of date, see below.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run the app below, which is just a TextField with some Arabic text in it, on a device with arrow keys.
2. Try to navigate with the left and right arrow keys.

**Expected results:** The cursor moves left with the left key and right with the right key.  Tested on TextEdit on a Mac after changing my keyboard to Arabic.
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Visual order (left = left, right = right) traversal support is acknowledged as a planned feature but not yet implemented. A commenter noted the existing Intent/Action system uses a `forward` boolean that may need to be extended to support left/right explicitly. Status: stalled, planned but not implemented.

title: Tab+Shift behaviour is not consistent with other web applications
body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

This is a very specific issue that might indicate a broad set of differences in key listening between Flutter and other applications.

If you have a list of focusable widgets and press Tab+Shift (in this specific order) then focus will jump to the next widget and will get back to the previous one.

That doesn't match any other web application. If you use the Tab+Shift combination there, the focus will get to the next input and stay there
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm pressing Tab first then Shift (Tab+Shift order) moves focus forward and then back, while the intended Shift+Tab (Shift first) moves focus backward correctly. The root cause is that Flutter's shortcut system does not account for key-press order when evaluating modifier combinations. A team member noted this was a known gap and changes were being considered. The bug is reproducible on latest stable and master on web. Status: open, no PR.

comment_summary: Confirmed on web/CanvasKit in multiple browser setups; right-clicking in the context of a SelectableText shows the browser's generic context menu instead of Flutter's copy/paste menu. The report links a similar issue #78877. No fix or PR mentioned; status is open.

title: SelectableText background offsetted from the selection by 1 pixel
comment_summary: The only substantive comment is a CC to @Renzo-Olivares. No root cause analysis or fix is discussed in comments. Status: open, stalled.

title: Up and down arrow keys miscalculate the cursor position in a TextField
comment_summary: Root cause: on web, arrow-key navigation is handled by the browser's engine rather than Flutter's `MoveSelectionUpTextIntent`/`MoveSelectionDownTextIntent`, and the browser's cursor positioning calculation diverges from Flutter's at certain font sizes/renderers. A workaround using `Shortcuts` to override arrow-key intents with Flutter's own `MoveSelectionUp/DownTextIntent` was provided and confirmed working. The issue was reportedly fixed around 3.7 but regressed in 3.10. The team noted it would likely be resolved by #120613 (multiline web text editing improvements).

title: [Text Selection] `TextSelectionDelegate` should not expose direct access to `TextEditingController`'s `TextEditingValue`
body:
Instead, it should operate on the text/selection from the previous frame as it is what the user sees.

Things that may prevent this from happening:
- Currently in the `TextSelectionControls` implementation we sometimes do a side-effect and try to observe it immediately: For instance, in response to certain gestures we first select a word and the text selection menu should show up with the "cut" and "copy" options.
comment_summary: Comments favor moving non-idempotent APIs (like `selectWord`) up to `EditableText` and exposing only idempotent APIs from `RenderEditable`.

title: ExpansionTile + TextField => undesirable behavior when backspace
comment_summary: Single doctor output comment confirming reproduction environment; no root cause or fix discussion.

title: [Web] Select word for suggestions box using "Tab" key but changes focus
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on web (Chrome on Mac) with Japanese keyboard input via IME; the Tab key inserts a suggestion and then moves focus to the next TextField. A user-contributed workaround uses `compositionstart`/`compositionend` DOM events to toggle `FocusNode.skipTraversal`, but a later commenter reported the workaround no longer prevents the issue on Flutter stable 3.19.6. No PR or official fix exists; issue is stalled.

The bug is absent in Chrome (web). The issue is an engine-level problem requiring communication of the cursor position to the OS IME, affecting all desktop platforms. No fix or PR was identified; the issue remains open.

A workaround using TextFormField's onChange callback is noted for text-only changes, and a DartPad showing a text-vs-selection check is linked.

title: LogicalKeyboardKey does not always match PhysicalKeyboardKey on Desktop(Windows)
body: Trying to grab scanner input, but key.logicalKey.keyLabel sometimes mismatch with physical, as if it still thinks the shift has been pressed. Sometimes there are % insted of 5, and randomly other presented numbers. So that forced me to use code like this, with substring physical key, instead simple key.logicalKey.keyLabel getter. ```void _keyEvent(RawKeyEvent key) { if (key.runtimeType.toString() == 'RawKeyDownEvent' && key.physicalKey.debugName != null) { (_keyTime.isRunning) ? _keyTime.reset() : _keyTime.start(); if (_keyTime.elapsedMilliseconds < _barTime) { var keyname = key.physicalKey.debugName ?? '0'; _barCode += keyname.substring(keyname.length - 1); if (_barTimer == null) { _barTimer = Timer( Duration(milliseconds: _barTime), () => _barProcessor(_b ...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the mismatch is reproducible on stable 2.0.4 and master 2.1.0 on Windows. The logs show that a barcode scanner sends shift+digit key sequences, which Flutter maps to shifted logical keys (e.g., `!` for digit 1) on the first event, then emits a second event with an unknown Windows key code (e.g., `Unknown Windows key code 49`) on the same physical key. No fix or PR referenced; status is stalled, triaged to team-windows.

Therefore, in my app, UITextView is displayed as PlatformView.

    ja-JP)

comment_summary: Root cause identified: physical keyboard input in UITextView platform views fails because Flutter 2.0 introduced a null check error in ShortcutManager.handleKeypress when no focus is set. PR #80302 was created to be more tolerant of apps with no focused widget. Confirmed fixed for the original reporter in Flutter 2.5.3. The issue also affected WKWebView and webview_flutter. PR #80302 landed.

title: Make RenderEditable and TextPainter's `text` field not-null (I guess it is a small mistake when migrating flutter to NNBD)
comment_summary: Comments from @LongCatIsLooong note that making `text` non-nullable may break builder-style usage and the framework itself sets it to null in two places. A PR was welcomed with the caveat that it might be a breaking change.

title: AutofillHints suggestion window and TextField glitches when pressing backspace when TextField is empty
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on latest stable and master. The flicker/jump is caused by the autofill suggestion window toggling when pressing backspace in an empty field with a number keyboard type combined with autofill hints. The problem also occurs (less severely) with non-numeric keyboards. No root cause analysis beyond the keyboard type + autofill hint interaction. No fix or PR filed; stalled.

title: ☂️  TextOverflow in TextStyle does not work for SelectableText (basically all editable text widgets)
body:
In https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/78927 I introduced `TextOverflow` in the `TextStyle` class but when applied in a `SelectableText` (or any editable text field) widget it does not work.

From my research I found out that all kind of overflow does not work for Editable widgets so maybe this can be worked in batches.

- [ ] Make `TextOverflow.fade` work for editable widgets. 
- [ ] Make `TextOverflow.ellipsis` work for editable widgets. https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/80435 

From my tests all other overflows seems to work as expected (please do your tests and share feedback).

title: [Web][TextField] canceling suggest dialog using ESC key, the dialog will close too
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on web (stable and master). On macOS desktop, the issue was fixed in master but not stable. Comments identify this as an ESC key event propagating through to the dialog when pressed during IME composition. Suggested that text fields should intercept ESC when composition is active. Status: web-only issue remains open; macOS desktop appears fixed in master.

title: Uppercase and case-insensitive LogicalKeyboardKey const values
body:
## Use case

`LogicalKeyboardKey` contains consts only for lowercase letters. The absence of uppercase values not only means that they need to be figured out but also incorrectly suggests case-insensitivity.

## Proposal

I suggest adding `LogicalKeyboardKey` const values for uppercase letters. Also, case-insensitive values as synonyms for both upper and lower case letters could be added to simplify defining case-insensitive shortcuts, etc.
comment_summary: Comments confirm the design intent: uppercase and lowercase share the same logical key under different shift states. However, a later comment notes the team decided to move forward with adding uppercase convenience statics, reopening the issue.

title: TextFormField -> TextEditingValue -> oldValue.selection and oldValue.composing are wrong with Gboard!
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on Android with `TextInputType.number` and Gboard with `autofocus: true`; the bug does not reproduce with SwiftKey or without autofocus. The root cause is that Gboard sends `oldValue.selection` already offset by one before the backspace is processed, causing `formatEditUpdate` to compute an incorrect cursor position. Confirmed on Android 11 and emulators. The issue was assigned to @Renzo-Olivares but the assignee was subsequently removed due to inactivity; currently stalled.

Occurs in both canvaskit and HTML renderers, suggesting a framework-level issue. CC'd to @LongCatIsLooong. Status: stalled, no fix or PR.

title: CupertinoTextField  in CupertinoNavigationBar with set focus node does not show keyboard after navigating back

title: TextInputClient in macOS doesn't update the editingValue except for inserting characters
body:
When creating a custom `TextInputClient`, macOS does not update the editingValue for the following keystrokes:

 - all  arrows 
 - shift + an arrow
 - delete
 - cmd+a
 - cmd+c
 - cmd+x
 - cmd+v

The last three were not tested since I'm unable to make a selection with either `shift + arrows` or `cmd+a`.  

## Steps to Reproduce

using the code sample provided below, run the following sequence: 

1. type the sequence `abcde`
2. press delete (nothing happen on macOS)
3. type the sequence `efg`
4. press left arrow key till baseOffset = 0 (nothing happen on macOS)
5. press `cmd + a` (nothing happen on macOS)
6. press `cmd + c` (nothing happen on macOS)
7. press delete (nothing happen on macOS)

**Expected results:**
I expected the following result which was produced with the same steps on the web platform.

```
value => TextEditingValue(text: ┤├, selection: TextSelection(baseOffset: 0, extentOffset: 0, affinity: TextAffinity.downstream, isDirectional: false), com
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: arrow keys and other editing operations for a bare `TextInputClient` on macOS are now handled at the `EditableText`/`Shortcuts` layer, not the engine, so a raw client without `EditableText` does not receive them. A maintainer noted the keys should be ported to `Shortcuts` soon. No confirmed fix or PR; issue stalled with suggestion to test on other desktop platforms.

title: Hide keyboard from system in input and editabletext
body:
When using any type of input, from a TexField to an EditableText, you cannot choose that the system keyboard does not appear, whether you focus on the input or select the text. I am trying to make a calculator and I cannot make my text editable because I cannot hide the system keyboard.

I propose to add the option to choose if you want the system keyboard to come out or not in order to make your own personalized keyboard, such as a calculator in my case.
comment_summary: A commenter clarified the engine tag was incorrectly applied — controlling keyboard visibility is handled at the widget layer via platform channels, so an API affordance could be added there without engine changes.

title: TextField becomes unresponsive in web browser after using cmd+f (ctrl+f on Windows) to search page

title: [Flutter Web] Clicked EditableText does not receive keyboard events when HtmlElementView was tapped before EditableText

-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- You must include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Run below Flutter app (compile for web) or see this Dartpad gist: https://dartpad.dev/71d8822a522da7f8d3e27e8bd7e33c35)

<details>
  <summary>Flutter Code</summary>

```
// ignore: avoid_web_libraries_in_flutter
import 'dart:html';
import 'dart:ui' as ui;
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  /
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm reproduction on Chrome desktop. The root cause is identified as the framework lacking a concept of blurring a text field without simultaneously focusing something else: when the user taps a platform view, the browser moves focus into the platform view but Flutter still considers the previous text field focused. The issue was escalated to P1 and noted as related to broader platform-view focus design, but has no fix or open PR and the assignee was removed.

title: Create CommandOrControl so ctrl+c in Windows == command+c in Mac
body:
LogicalKeyboardKey's `controlLeft` on Windows and Linux means ctrl (like ctrl+c), but on macOS control is something else entirely different. For things like ctrl+c, the correct is command+c, which is Windows' Windows key (`metaLeft`). This gets instantly confusing.

I've seen apps with shortcuts broken because the developer only tested on Windows/Mac and didn't realize control works completely different across both.

My suggestions: somehow we could have a LogicalKeyboardKey "correct/expected" control and meta so developers and users wouldn't be confused or even worry about it. This affects even Flutter Web. No one is going to do `if (Platform.macOS) metaLeft else controlLeft` because it is going to take too long for them to realize this is necessary. It seems like a bug, even though the logical key is 100% correct (from logical point of view).
comment_summary: Discussion converged on naming: `OsSingleActivator` or `AdaptiveSingleActivator` with fields `osControl`/`osMeta`. Electron's CommandOrControl activator was cited as inspiration. Flutter's existing default text editing shortcuts maintain separate maps per platform.

title: TextField fires onWindowShown event inappropriately on  Android platform.
body:
## TextField calls onWindowShown of IME inappropriately.

The following video shows an example where onWindowShown is called every time text is entered, causing input problems.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/58111771/120742708-621c7180-c532-11eb-8b3d-80cd3b2dea44.mov

I tried` flutter doctor -v`

```
[✓] Flutter (Channel stable, 2.2.1, on macOS 11.2.3 20D91 darwin-x64, locale ja-JP)
    • Flutter version 2.2.1 at /Users/chiakikajiwara/develop/flutter
    • Framework revision 02c026b03c (7 days ago), 2021-05-27 12:24:44 -0700
    • Engine revision 0fdb562ac8
    • Dart version 2.13.1
```
This behavior occurs in Flutter version >= 2.2.0.
And I have confirmed that this does not occur on iOS platform.
Thank you for your cooperation.
comment_summary: The root cause is that `showSoftInput()` is called on every keystroke in Flutter 2.2+ (confirmed via logs), whereas in Flutter 2.0.6 it was called only once on keyboard appearance — this triggers `onWindowShown` repeatedly in the IME. The issue is Android-only and is IME-dependent: it does not reproduce with standard English keyboards on API 28/30 emulators, but does reproduce with the Japanese flick-input IME ('flick' app). A triage comment closed the issue for lack of reproducibility, but was reopened after the reporter provided a Japanese IME repro; the issue was confirmed on both stable 2.2.3 and master. A commenter linked a potentially related issue #80218 and tagged @blasten; no fix or PR has been proposed, and the issue is stalled awaiting engine-side investigation.

title: Preserve `SelectableText`'s selection on setState
comment_summary: Team confirmed SelectableText loses selection on setState because it rebuilds; the suggested alternative is to use SelectionArea, which was introduced to replace SelectableText for most use cases. The reporter confirmed they had already worked around the issue by using SelectionArea in their package. Issue effectively closed by the workaround of migrating to SelectionArea.

title: Please design as public interface/Mixing instead of passing RenderEditable everywhere
comment_summary: Comments link to a related discussion (#80226) proposing a public widget with a `TextSpan` builder and `onChanged` field to decouple the controller from `RenderEditable`. The author clarifies the request is specifically about exposing a public interface/mixin rather than the broader widget proposal. The maintainer acknowledged the direction and noted existing refactoring momentum, referencing #84317 as potentially related. Status is open with no concrete PR.

title: Share code between RenderParagraph and RenderEditable
body:
In https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/83537, we realized that a lot of code is shared between RenderParagraph and RenderEditable, especially for WidgetSpan support.

As we consider adding WidgetSpan support for SelectableText as well (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/38474), this leads us to prefer code reuse rather than three extremely similar codebases to maintain.

We may be able to restructure it to inherit, or potentially a mixin, though I am still unclear how sharable the code actually is.

title: Autofocus not granted in bottomsheet
comment_summary: Comments confirm that `autofocus: true` in a non-modal `showBottomSheet` does not steal focus from the background `TextField`, while `showModalBottomSheet` does work correctly. The distinction is that modal bottom sheets prevent background interaction. Reproduced on Android and iOS on stable 3.29.3 and master 3.32.0-pre. Stalled with no fix.

title: [Web]: Editable text in TextFields not selectable if the text exceeds length of the TextFields.
comment_summary: Comments confirmed the issue on web and desktop: text in a TextField is not selectable by mouse/cursor/trackpad when the text exceeds the field's visible width. It was noted this may have been fixed in master 2.3.0-pre, but horizontal auto-scroll during drag selection remains unimplemented. A workaround of `scrollPhysics: NeverScrollableScrollPhysics()` prevents overflow scrolling but hides the extra text. No fix or PR for horizontal edge-scroll-during-selection; stalled with team-text-input.

title: [Keyboard] [iOS] [Dictation] During dictation, TextField onChanged is called after calling clear()
comment_summary: Reproduction confirmed on both stable and master on iOS physical and simulator. The root cause was traced in detail: when `setTextInputState:` is called to clear the field, it calls `[self.inputDelegate textWillChange:self]`, which causes `UIDictationController` to flush serialized dictation results via `insertText:`, re-inserting the text after it was cleared. No programmatic way to dismiss dictation or detect its state was found; a workaround using `Future.delayed(Duration(milliseconds: 10))` to call `clear()` a second time was suggested by a commenter. Status: stalled, engine-level issue with no fix.

title: Autofill Hints iOS: email field doesn't autofill if keyboardType is `TextInputType.emailAddress`
comment_summary: Comments narrowed the main repro: autofill suggestions disappear from the email field after tapping into the password field and back. Confirmed on iOS 14.6 on both stable and master. `AutofillGroup` was suggested as a workaround but reported as ineffective on iOS 16. A recent comment (iOS 17.6.1) could not reproduce the initial repro but a user still reports the field fills nothing when a suggestion is tapped. Status: stalled, intermittently reproducible, no PR.

title: Autofill Hints iOS: `obscureText: true` forces `AutofillHints.newPassword` to act like `AutofillHints.password`
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug persists on stable and master through Flutter 3.22. A user found that setting `autocorrect: false` alongside `obscureText: true` and `AutofillHints.newPassword` fixes the autofill hint on some versions, but others report this workaround does not work on iOS 16.2+ or Flutter 3.3+/3.7/3.22. Status: stalled, no fix or PR.

title: Autofill Hints iOS:`Choose My Own Password` option doesn't clear text field
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on iOS 14.6, 15.5, 16.2, and 17.5.1. The 'Choose My Own Password' option in iOS autofill does not clear the password text field. CC'd to @LongCatIsLooong, who indicated the keyboard should remove text from `UITextInput` when that option is selected. Status: open, stalled; no PR.

title: Autofill Hints iOS: `AutofillHints.newPassword` only recommends once on navigating
comment_summary: iOS-only. Confirmed on iOS 14.6 and later (14.7.1, 15.4.1). After navigating back from the create-account screen and returning, the AutofillHints.newPassword suggestion prompt appears but no longer auto-fills a strong password. A commenter noted the issue may originate in the engine; another observed that sending the app to background and returning causes the autofill to work. No fix or PR; stalled.

title: onKey handler getting called on multiple different focuses when the handler changes the focus on desktop
body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- You must include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Run `flutter create bug`.
2. Update the files as follows:
`lib/main.dart` should become:
<details> 
<summary>minimal code sample</summary> 

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter/services.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  // Th
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug reproduces on both stable (2.2.2) and master channels on Linux. The cause is that when `onKey` changes focus via `nextFocus()`, the key event propagates to the newly focused node and triggers another `nextFocus()`, resulting in focus jumping two fields instead of one. No fix or PR was linked. The issue appears stalled with only triage confirmation and a cc to @gspencergoog.

title: Let Autofill be Opt-out
body:
As per the request of the AwG team, we'd like to make autofill on Android to be opt-out to maintain parity with the existing Android Platform. In the long term, we might want to send all the information in the widget hierarchy to the autofill service. For now the following info of a text field should be sent to get a reasonable detection accuracy:

- [x] placeholder text (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/86312)
- [x] text
- [x] size & coordinates (should be implemented already)
- [ ] developer identifier (not sure how)
- [ ] input type (already available on android & iOS, should be trivial to add)
- [x] autofill type (current autofill is for text only)
comment_summary: The issue is kept open specifically to track adding input type information to the autofill data sent to Android; other checklist items were completed.

title: Textfield receives focus but keyboard doesn't remain open on iOS.
body:

     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- You must include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->
``` 
Keyboard disappears in Ios

```Works fine in Android```

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/38251726/123905964-f8588180-d990-11eb-84e8-393164ef022f.mp4
1. Run `flutter create bug`.
2. Update the files as 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Reproduction confirmed on iOS simulator and physical device (not Android). Comments confirm the keyboard disappears when switching focus via `unfocus()`/`requestFocus()` during a `PageView` page transition. An additional commenter reported the same on Android. No fix or PR identified; the issue was left without a concrete root cause or resolution.

title: Text fields constrained to small sizes confuse the focus traversal algorithm
comment_summary: Root cause narrowed down by @gspencergoog: the focus node's bounding rectangle goes to zero height when the container is constrained below approximately 35px, confusing the traversal algorithm. Confirmed on desktop (Windows); on web the broken traversal occurs regardless of window size. The workaround is to use a fixed height of 35px or more for the container. No PR exists; issue is stalled and was flagged for unassignment due to inactivity.

title: [Desktop][Web]: Caret is thick as compared to native
body:
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/46608407/124898667-9ab7db00-e009-11eb-863f-c851bd7c79a5.mov

flutter version: 
`
  Flutter 2.3.0-13.0.pre.15 • channel master • https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git
  Framework • revision 3fc408200e (7 weeks ago) • 2021-05-18 09:50:24 -0700
  Engine • revision 3cda7f5807
  Tools • Dart 2.14.0 (build 2.14.0-119.0.dev)
`
comment_summary: Comments confirm the caret is consistently thicker on macOS desktop and web compared to native macOS apps (e.g., Notes). The difference exists on both stable and master. No root cause or fix discussed; the issue description was updated to reflect the thickness discrepancy rather than padding.

title: Hero's height sizing issue when keyboard is opened
body: ## Steps to Reproduce ### In the first page: Put a TextField and an ElevatedButton inside an Hero widget inside a Scaffold widget. ### In the second page: Put an ElevatedButton inside an Hero widget (with the same tag of the first Hero) inside a Scaffold widget. ## Then: Focus on the textfield to display the keyboard and navigate to another page. During the navigation transition the keyboard will disappear. ## Expected results: I want my button play his Hero animation the same way as if we never displayed the keyboard ## Actual results: My button's height is oversized during the animation ## NOTE 1: If the Scaffold's property "resizeToAvoidBottomInset" is false (true by default) there is no problem but there is a risk for my textfields to be hidden by the keyboard. ## NOTE 2: Even if I set the property "fixedSize" of my button's style I had the the problem :/ ## Here is a screenshot during the transition:
comment_summary: Comments identified the root cause: `heroes.dart` uses `manifest.navigatorSize` (captured at hero flight start) to compute `RelativeRect`, but when the keyboard dismisses during the transition the actual Navigator render box size changes, causing the hero to be sized incorrectly. Removing the bottom/right constraints or using the live render box size partially mitigates the jump but does not fully fix it. Confirmed on stable 3.19.3; stalled with team-design.

title: Calling TextEditingController.clear() does not call formatEditUpdate of formatter
body:
Calling `TextEditingController.clear()` does not call `formatEditUpdate` of TextInputFormatter's.
This makes it difficult to keep the formatter state in sync with TextEditingController. 

Example case:

Some TextInputFormatter allows you to format the phone number in something like (555) 333-44-55, but it also has a method to get the phone in its original form without formatting like 555334455. There is also a field clear button that calls the method TextEditingController.clear(). When you click on this button, the text field will be erased, but this will not call the method formatEditUpdate, so it is still possible to get the value from the formatter. 

There is a way out to force the programmer to also call some cleanup method on the formatter itself, but it is quite difficult to keep track of this.
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue: `TextEditingController.clear()` directly mutates the value without going through `EditableText`'s formatter pipeline, so `formatEditUpdate` is never called. No workaround beyond calling a cleanup method on the formatter manually was suggested. Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.29.3 and master 3.32; no fix or PR proposed.

title: [iOS]TextField can't delete or insert charactor in the middle of pinyin
comment_summary: Confirmed reproduced on master: when text with an active composing underline is present and the cursor is placed in the middle of it, typing adds characters at the end rather than at the cursor position. Safari/UITextField handles this correctly. The issue is specifically about inserting within an uncommitted composing region, not just at any position.

title: TextField doesn't handle trailing space as characters if textAlign is TextAlign.right/TextAlign.center
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on stable and master by a triager (video provided); trailing spaces do not push text or break lines as expected when `textAlign` is `TextAlign.center` or `TextAlign.right`. An initial comment wrongly suggested the behavior was correct; subsequent comments and a video confirmed it is not. A bot comment noted a missing priority label. No root cause was identified in the comments and no fix or PR was proposed; issue is stalled.

title: [web] Detecting the click on "done" in the virtual keyboard in iOS
body:
At the moment, there's no proper way to detect the click on "done" in a virtual keyboard. All we receive is a "blur" event on the text field, which is indistinguishable from other "blur" causes.

We may be able to detect this by checking the new focus target. If it's `<body>` then the user most likely clicked "done" on the virtual keyboard. That is because iOS doesn't blur text fields when the user taps on random parts of the page. The only ways to blur are: click "done" or click some other focusable element (e.g. another text field).
comment_summary: Multiple comments asking for updates across several versions; no fix as of 3.3.2. The Done button on iOS Chrome does not reliably hide the keyboard. No workaround found.

title: Default text section shortcuts for some platforms are missing
body:
There are a number of text section shortcuts implemented by default on each platform, for example on macOS Control-A is "Move to the beginning of the line or paragraph.", and there are others that we do not currently implement.

*Because I use a non-standard keyboard, not all testing is currently complete.*

macOS(https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT201236):

- [x] Control-A: Move to the beginning of the line or paragraph.
- [x] Control-E: Move to the end of a line or paragraph.
- [x] Control-F: Move one character forward.
- [x] Control-B: Move one character backward.
- [ ] Control-L: Center the cursor or selection in the visible area.
- [x] Control-P: Move up one line.
- [x] Control-N: Move down one line.
- [x] Control-T: Swap the character behind the insertion point with the character in front of the insertion point.
- [ ] Control-Backspace: Delete backward by decomposing previous character (for example a multi-codepoint emoji).
- [x] Option–Left Arrow: Move the insertio
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm ctrl+T on Windows/Linux does nothing. `shift+ctrl+A` and `shift+ctrl+E` (extend selection to line start/end on macOS) were reported as also not working, with a screencast reproduction.

title: Mouse drag in TextField scrolls instead of selecting
body:

-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- You must include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->
1. Create a web app and add a TextField, make it maxLines to 20.
2. Input texts and click from middle of it.
3. Start to drag from there *Vertically*.
4. The textfield is scrolling instead of selecting the texts from the cursor.


**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
It should act like normal html textf
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed on macOS desktop in addition to web: vertical mouse drag inside a multiline TextField scrolls instead of selecting text. A related issue #59141 is linked. No fix or PR; status is open.

title: TextField accented word writing problem using soft keyboard on some iPad
comment_summary: The issue is device-specific: iPad Pro 12.9-inch 4th gen has a different software keyboard layout (with dedicated accent keys) compared to iPad Air, which produces the extra dead-key character before the accented letter. A commenter reports reproducing the Spanish version of the bug. No fix or PR; status is open.

title: BIDI text painting skipped tests.
body:
As part of the latest [skip test audit](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/86396), there are still several tests that are skipped in `text_painter_rtl_test.dart` that don't have a bug to track them. We can use this as a tracking bug for getting them turned back on or marked as intentional if that is the just behaviour we want.

The tests in question are:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/40739568ab80988d8f2671bf6ec43539ce983771/packages/flutter/test/painting/text_painter_rtl_test.dart#L37

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/4073

title: Consolidate TextEditingValues in RenderEditable
body:
Currently, EditableTextState has several aliases of the same value, and I propose that they should be consolidated into one name.  All of the aliases are:

  1. [textEditingValue](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/01dc9694be6461eb15bce4854c0253ef0362b1fb/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/editable_text.dart#L2524), which comes from the TextSelectionDelegateMixin [here](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/01dc9694be6461eb15bce4854c0253ef0362b1fb/packages/flutter/lib/src/services/text_input.dart#L818).
  2. [currentTextEditingValue](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/01dc9694be6461eb15bce4854c0253ef0362b1fb/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/editable_text.dart#L1730), which comes from the TextInputClient abstract class [here](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/01dc9694be6461eb15bce4854c0253ef0362b1fb/packages/flutter/lib/src/services/text_input.dart#L880).
  3. [_value](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/01dc9694be6461eb15bce4854c0253ef0362b1fb/packages/flutter/l
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments warn to watch out for different setters on the aliases before consolidating. A contributor started work then stopped.

title: Why is FormFieldValidator's value nullable?
body:
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/40e8620a27f1a608d4df232936a168f134d64d7c/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/form.dart#L279-L285

I find it less convenient to use `!` operator as I don't see a case when the `value` would be null for a field.

I'd be grateful for an explanation :)
comment_summary: Commenters clarified that the value is nullable because FormField is generic and can wrap nullable types such as tristate checkboxes (bool?) or dropdowns with no selection; the generic type parameter allows the developer to express nullability explicitly.

title: Do not populate the selection overlay when `EditableText` does not have focus
body:
Currently `EditableText._selectionOverlay` is populated in `_handleSelectionChanged`, which can be called during autofill.

Additionally when a long tap gesture first focuses a `TextField` and requests the toolbar to be shown, but since the primary focus does not change immediately we currently show the toolbar regardless of whether the text field has focus (otherwise long-pressing an unfocused text field won't show the toolbar). But if the text field ended up not getting the focus the toolbar will still show.

Maybe `showToolbar` should also set a private flag so when the text field is finally focused it shows the toolbar.

Corresponds to a `TODO` in `editable_text.dart`.
comment_summary: One comment from @justinmc agrees that imperative methods like `showToolbar` and existing flags like `_shouldShowSelectionToolbar` are both confusing. A PR reference was linked for the TODO in question.

title: TextSelection doesn't support TextAffinities for non-collapsed selection
body:
[TextSelection](https://master-api.flutter.dev/flutter/services/TextSelection-class.html) has a single [affinity](https://master-api.flutter.dev/flutter/services/TextSelection/affinity.html) property, and according to its docs, it only applies when collapsed: > If the text range is collapsed and has more than one visual location (e.g., occurs at a line break), which of the two locations to use when painting the caret. I think that this may be an oversight and a source of bugs, especially when using a keyboard. For example, these selections are different, but Flutter has no way to distinguish between the two:
extent TextAffinity.downstream extent TextAffinity.upstream Flutter treats them both as: 
This text wra|ps because it's
|too long.

This text wra|ps because it's |
too long.
comment_summary: Comments note that non-collapsed selections on macOS do not visually require affinity (rendered as highlighted rectangles). iPad with hardware keyboard was not tested. A suggestion was made to document explicit affinity assumptions when not collapsed.

title: [android]  keyboard type doesn't change on some keyboards
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

use two TextField with different `textInputAction`

one has `maxLines: null` and `textInputAction: TextInputAction.done`
another one has `maxLines: null` and `textInputAction: TextInputAction.newline`

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? --> action button on keyboard display correct action

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? --> action button on keyboard display the same action for both

`flutter doctor -v`

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

```

[✓] Flutter (Channel stable, 2.2.3, on macOS 11.4 20F71 darwin-x64, locale en)
    • Flutter version 2.2.3 at /Users/minhtran/fvm/versions/stable
    • Framework revision f4abaa0735 (7 weeks ago), 2021-07-01 12:46:11 -0700
    • Engine revision 241c87ad80
    • Dart version 2.13.4

[✓] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK version 31.0.0)
    • Android SDK at /Users/minhtran/Library/Android/sdk
    • Platform android-31, build-tools 31.0.0
   
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: the issue is keyboard-specific — it only reproduces with AOSP keyboard (emulator without Google Play) and SwiftKey, not Gboard. The framework correctly updates `_currentConfiguration` with the new action; the keyboard on screen simply does not update. A commenter confirmed native Android apps update correctly with the same keyboard, suggesting the problem is in how Flutter signals the IME about configuration changes. No fix or PR; stalled.

title: iOS Keyboard flickers when switching the TextFields which have obscureText set to true
comment_summary: Comments confirm the keyboard flicker on iOS when switching between obscureText fields is tied to iOS's autofill password bar above the keyboard, which resizes when focus moves between password fields. The behavior is confirmed in native SwiftUI apps as well, suggesting it is an iOS-level behavior rather than a pure Flutter bug. One commenter found the flicker disappears when setting autocorrect: false, while another noted it worsened with iOS 17 and autofillHints. A workaround of autofillHints: [AutofillHints.oneTimeCode] prevents iOS from showing the password bar but breaks password autofill. Reproduced on stable (3.29.3) and master (3.32.x); no fix has landed.

title: TabBarView does not trigger child 'deactivate()' method when swiped if TextField has focus
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

Add `TabBarView `and 3 children to it, one of them with a `TextField` and `autofocus = true`

Change tabs by swiping left/right.

Child `deactivate()` method is not called in the tab with the `TextField`, if it has focus, when swiping away from the tab.

If I call `FocusScope.of(context).requestFocus(new FocusNode());` on another tab, then `deactivate()` is called on the tab with the `TextField`.

Changing tab by swiping in not removing the focus from `TextField`.

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->

`TextField` should loose focus when hidden from view tree when swiping away from its tab.

Child `deactivate()` method should be called the same way it is when changing tabs by tapping on them.


<!-- Finally, paste the output of running `flutter doctor -v` here. -->

```
Doctor summary (to see all details, run flutter doctor -v):
[√] Flutter (Channel master, 2.4.0-5.0.pre.164, on Microsoft Windows [versÃ£o 10.0.19042.1165
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed on stable 2.2.3 and master: swiping away from a tab containing an autofocused TextField does not remove focus or call deactivate() on the tab widget, while tapping the TabBar correctly triggers deactivate(). deactivate() is called fewer times when swiping than when tapping. No fix or PR; stalled.

title: TextField labelText doesn't get hintTextStyle when labelTextStyle is non-null
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- You must include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. You can have a look at this little reproduction [https://dartpad.dev/?id=b401619acd87ab339da95a0c8e216e79&null_safety=true](https://dartpad.dev/?id=b401619acd87ab339da95a0c8e216e79&null_safety=true)

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
[The documentation for InputDecoration](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/InputDecoration/hintStyle.html) states that a TextField's `labelText` should get the `hintTextStyle` applied when the label is sitting on top of the input.
In the reproduction case, I expect the TextField label to be blue while TextField doesn't has focus or value.

**Actual results:**
`labelText` gets `labelTextStyle` values applied even while sitting on top of the input


I also had a quick look into TextField source, and found that in InputDecorator, `labelTextStyle` is merged into `inlineLabelStyle`, which explains why the 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Reproduced on stable and master. A contributor attempted a fix by merging hintStyle into inlineLabelStyle but it broke two existing tests. The fix was ultimately reverted via #90406 due to more image diff failures than expected. Status: closed then reopened as landed-but-reverted.

title: Proposal to increase the callback of each frame of the soft keyboard pop-up height for TextField
body: I hope that the callback for each frame of the soft keyboard pop-up height change can be increased for the TextField. It will be used to make some special animations when the TextField gets the focus. Thank you very much!
comment_summary: Comments identify the request as frame-by-frame keyboard height callbacks synchronized with the keyboard animation. @blasten suggested thread merging (similar to platform views) as a possible solution. No implementation planned.

title: Paint Control for Text Decoration
body:
## Use case

In our custom design, we want to have a thick underline, something along these lines:

```dart
TextStyle(
  decoration: TextDecoration.underline,
  decorationStyle: TextDecorationStyle.solid,
  decorationColor: underlineColor,
  decorationThickness: 12.0,
),
```

This, however, looks silly when the underline is drawn in the foreground:

| Wanted behavior | Current behavior |
| :---------------------: | :--------------------: |
| ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19204050/130544804-9324c764-deac-46de-b99e-66a4b09a3018.png) | ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19204050/130544839-2ea091f7-efe4-4701-90e8-25ce49dadb4d.png) |


## Proposal
