title: [desktop] [web] Cannot click drag to scroll PageView if child is SelectableText
body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

### Sample Code

<details>


```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

String loremIpsum = """
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Proin viverra lectus at dui tincidunt, faucibus iaculis sem ullamcorper. Proin auctor, nibh non molestie ornare, nisi lectus posuere metus, a facilisis magna lacus a ligula. Aliquam finibus, justo et finibus sagittis, dui ipsum accumsan nisi, congue pretium massa odio ut sapie
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug is a gesture conflict: `SelectableText` consumes mouse drag events across its entire bounding box, preventing `PageView` from receiving horizontal drag gestures for page-turning. Issue affects web and all desktop platforms; iOS native is unaffected. Custom `ScrollBehavior` with mouse drag support was tried but did not resolve the conflict — it only inverted the problem in Flutter 3.0 (page turns took over, making text selection impossible). Removing `SelectableText` in favor of `Text` restores page-turning. Stalled with no fix.

title: Text selection leads to scrolling in opposite direction
comment_summary: Confirmed on stable and master (Android physical device and emulator): dragging a text selection handle in a TextField inside a ListView causes the ListView to scroll in the opposite direction. No root cause or fix identified; no workaround provided. Issue stalled.

title: Add an option to dismiss keyboard on (reordering) drag start with ReorderableListView
body:
## Use case

When a (reordering) drag starts in ReorderableListView I want to dismiss the keyboard.

## Proposal

ScrollView already has a keyboardDismissBehavior property and ReorderableListView uses a CustomScrollView.  Maybe that property should be used, or a similar property added for reorderable.  Any keyboard dismiss behavior added for on drag (reordering drag vs list drag) start should still support dismissing on list drag.

Related #76170

Alternatively, it may be better to have a more general solution to dismissing the keyboard.

title: Make sure the `TextEditingValue` in the platform text input plugin and the framework converge after concurrent edits
body:
Currently platform text input plugins and the framework have their own local replicas of the document being edited by the user. A local update to one document will be sent to update the other (remote) document to keep both in sync, using a platform channel message. It is crucial that both replicas having the exact same state when all communications are finished, to make sure the platform (and the system services like IME, autofill, etc,.) and the framework are looking at the same document.

Our current strategy is to encode the entire document in each update message to the remote document and overwrite the remote document. To avoid sending the same document over and over (echoing), both the platform text input plugin and the framework have logic to track the last known state of the remote document and only send updates when the local document's state is different from the last known remote state. There's a (not so slim) chance the states of the 2 replicas diverge after concurrent edi
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comment from @Renzo-Olivares clarifies delta-based editing should retain `oldText` so mismatched states can be detected and discarded. Framework-to-engine updates still overwrite the full value, so this is not a current blocker.

title: Text Field obscure text cannot be used with  expands to true
body:
Putting an assert to check whether the text is a single line if it is obscured is a good idea when we think first. However, when we do like that we also avoid the use of "expands" property. For example, if I create a login screen with a password text form field wrapped with an expanded widget, I cannot fill the full size inside of the expanded with a text form field. The reason why I can't do it is the fact that obscured texts cannot be multiline and max lines should be null (cannot be 1) when expands is true. These two asserts lead to the problem and this problem leads to significant design issues especially when we are dealing with websites developed with Flutter Web since their size can be any.

Like it is mentioned in:

"the problem is that if i try to use a text field (obscure) that occupies the entire space of a container using Expands: true and max Lines: null, it will not work, only the text without obscure now.. :/"
_Originally posted by @FilipeAG in https://github.com/fl
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm this is by-design: `expands: true` requires `maxLines: null`, which conflicts with `obscureText: true` requiring `maxLines: 1`. The original author of the design acknowledged the use case of a tall single-line obscured field was not considered, and discussed potentially allowing `expands` with single-line fields. No fix or PR was proposed; issue remains open.

title: System UI overlay doesn't show up as promised when keyboard is enabled
body:

-->

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run `flutter create bug`.
2. replace the main.dart with 
```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter/services.dart';

void main() async {
  WidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized();
  await SystemChrome.setEnabledSystemUIOverlays([]);
  runApp(MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  // This widget is the root of your application.
  @override
  Widget build(B
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause is Android OS behavior: when the keyboard appears it forces system UI overlays back, and Android does not restore the previous UI state until 1 second after keyboard dismissal. The white bar is the system navigation area reappearing. Confirmed reproducible on Android API 29–30 emulators; not reproducible on API 33+. The documentation describes the expected behavior but the white space instead of the proper UI is the gap. No fix beyond upgrade recommendations was provided.

title: TextFormField with textAlign: TextAlign.right  whitespace doesn't show unless text is entered.
body:
I use TextFormField and property textAlign: TextAlign.right in my web application but I see prob when typing space, it don't show if don't have any text after it :(
 Please help!
comment_summary: Comments confirm the trailing whitespace rendering bug with `TextAlign.right` is reproducible on all platforms (web, macOS, Android, Windows) on stable and master. The space exists in the string but is not rendered because of text alignment trimming. A creative hack workaround (transparent TextField overlaid with a Text widget) is provided but is not suitable for production. The issue is missing a priority label as noted by a bot. Status: open, stalled, workaround available.

title: TextField with lots of text can hang app
comment_summary: Comments identify two contributing performance bottlenecks: (1) the entire text content is serialized and sent over the platform channel on every change, and (2) text layout of large content is slow. The `TextEditingDelta` / `DeltaTextInputClient` API was introduced as a potential path to delta-based updates, and engine PR flutter/engine#29036 improved macOS. As of the latest comments, all desktop platforms support deltas via a custom `DeltaTextInputClient`, but `TextField` has not been migrated to use it, and `TextInput.setEditingState` still sends the full text. Issue affects mobile and web in addition to desktop. Currently stalled with no concrete fix timeline.

title: [Android 12][Keyboard] Display ID mismatch found.
body:
Using Android 12 (API 31) with webview plugins such as [webview_flutter](https://pub.dev/packages/webview_flutter) or [flutter_inappwebview](https://pub.dev/packages/flutter_inappwebview) fail to show the keyboard with this exception:

```
[   +1 ms] V/InputMethodManager(10400): b/117267690: Display ID mismatch found. ViewRootImpl displayId=20 InputMethodManager displayId=0. Use the right InputMethodManager instance to avoid performance overhead.
[        ] V/InputMethodManager(10400): java.lang.Throwable
[        ] V/InputMethodManager(10400): 	at android.view.inputmethod.InputMethodManager.getFallbackInputMethodManagerIfNecessary(InputMethodManager.java:549)
[        ] V/InputMethodManager(10400): 	at android.view.inputmethod.InputMethodManager.restartInput(InputMethodManager.java:1843)
[        ] V/InputMethodManager(10400): 	at org.chromium.content.browser.input.ImeAdapterImpl.H(chromium-TrichromeWebViewGoogle6432.apk-stable-447211487:4)
[        ] V/InputMethodManager(1040
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause: on Android 12+, Virtual Display-based platform views use a `DisplayId` that doesn't match the `InputMethodManager`'s display, causing keyboard failures. Switching to Hybrid Composition (e.g., `SurfaceAndroidWebView` in webview_flutter, or `useHybridComposition: true` in flutter_inappwebview) resolves it. For `webview_flutter` >= 3.0.0, hybrid composition is the default. Issue is Android 12-specific and engine-level; no framework fix landed.

-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- You must include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Use `enterText` on a `TextFormField`. See that it works.
2. Disable and renable the form and see that `enterText` continues to work.
3. This time, disable the field and use `enterText`. See that does not change the text value, as expected.
4. Re-enable the field and see that `enterText` doesn't work despite being enabled 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug reproduces on stable 3.29.3 and master 3.32.0. The root cause is that calling `enterText` on a disabled `TextFormField` leaves the text input connection in an inconsistent state, so re-enabling the field and calling `enterText` again does not update the field value. No fix or PR is mentioned; status is open with triage confirmation.

title: Support oversized WidgetSpans in TextFields
body:
Original internal report: https://yaqs.corp.google.com/eng/q/7281814822109315072

Since TextFields assign their height via multiplying the number of lines by the preferred line height (determined by the TextStyle and StrutStyle), oversized WidgetSpans often have trouble fully rendering, resulting in clipped content and hit testing.

WidgetSpans rendering support was added to TextFields recently, but no new API was added to support the drastically different line heights and shapes WidgetSpans can have in a TextField.

We should provide additional API to allow this to be better controlled.

Clipping also causes unexpected rendering and hit testing behavior that should be better defined and in some cases fixed.
comment_summary: A commenter describes an accessibility use case: a WidgetSpan containing a math image is scaled down instead of being scrollable. A concrete test case was provided showing the WidgetSpan content is clipped and does not scroll horizontally when the TextField is narrower than the span.

title: [web] Option key shortcuts are broken on macOS
body:
## Option Key not detected

It turns out that the **Option (or Alt) ⌥** key on macOS is currently not working for [`ShortcutActivator`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/ShortcutActivator-class.html) on macOS **on web**.

That is, any `shortcuts` that use the alt key do not work. That applies both to `SingleActivator` as well as `LogicalKeySet`; [`LogicalKeyboardKey.alt`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/services/LogicalKeyboardKey/shift-constant.html) simply seems to be broken. This happens to our users on Chrome using Macbook.

---

```dart
SingleActivator(
  LogicalKeyboardKey.keyA,
)
```

Is detected but:

```dart
SingleActivator(
  LogicalKeyboardKey.keyA,
  alt: true,
)
```

is not detected on macOS on web. All the other modifiers still work.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run `flutter create bug --platforms=web`.
2. Update `main.dart` as follows:

<details><summary>Click to expand code snippet</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The Option/Alt key shortcut is broken on macOS web (Chrome and Safari) but works on Windows web and on macOS desktop. A commenter found a workaround: use `CharacterActivator('å', alt: true)` instead of `SingleActivator` with `alt: true`, which works if the keyboard layout produces a modified character for that key. Status: partially worked around, not fixed.

title: Supporting platform accessibility actions for custom keyboards
body:
## Use case
I'm trying to implement a custom keyboard and want to match accessibility actions of framework keyboards of Android/iOS but am unable to do so.

I'm using following `Semantics` for keys of custom keyboard.
```dart
Semantics.fromProperties(
  properties: SemanticsProperties(
    label: "some-semantics-label",
    keyboardKey: true,
)
```

### Expected Behaviour

**Android**
When Talkback is on, Android system keyboard doesn't use double tap to input the key instead when you tap on the key, its semantic label is pronounced by Talkback and the key is entered into the input field. On Flutter using `keyboardKey: true` on our custom widget does not reproduce similar behaviour and I'm unable to find a way to remove double tap behaviour on Android. 

Here's a video which demonstrates android's system keyboard accessbiility: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=y2tT4jDRfWs

On Native Android, we achieve this using following piece of code
```kotlin
ViewCompat.setAcces
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments clarify the implementation path: the `keyboardKey` trait was originally added for Fuchsia only and is not wired to iOS or Android. On iOS the fix is to set `UIAccessibilityTraitKeyboardKey`; on Android the fix is to call `AccessibilityNodeInfo.setTextEntryKey(true)`. Both are engine-side changes requiring plumbing from the framework. Status is open with no PR, awaiting engine work.

Delete button removes the last character.

title: Add keyboard input support for dates, times and durations to TextFormField/TextField
body:
Hi,
For desktop and web it is essential that the TextFormField widget natively supports the format to input a **date**, a **timeofday**, a **date and time** and a **duration** by **keyboard**.
On desktop, this type of data is only occasionally entered through an external dialog box. 
Thanks.
Regards,
Jose
comment_summary: Comments clarify the request is for desktop-style keyboard date/time entry (inline with validation on input), not a picker dialog. The team acknowledged the idea is reasonable. No PR or concrete plan.

title: TextSelection is lost when clicking on a button
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug affects web and desktop platforms but not mobile. On master at the time, selection values were correct numerically but the visible highlight was still dropped on button click. A team member identified this as standard browser behavior where clicking a button removes focus from the input element, and reassigned the issue to the framework team to investigate preserving selection on button click.

title: `dart:ui.LineMetrics` should include the line boundaries
body:
The information is currently manually stripped when constructing a `LineMetrics`:
https://github.com/google/skia/blob/a92b268f7d78e0069b2a61b22cb7ce615d12a60e/modules/skparagraph/include/Metrics.h#L39-L93

It will be much easier to implement vertical caret movements for up/down arrow keys with the line boundary information included. This corresponds to a todo item in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/90684 .
comment_summary: A commenter confirmed the need for TextRange in LineMetrics and described the current workaround: calling Paragraph.getPositionForOffset to derive line text ranges, which is inconvenient and indirect.

title: ☂️ Embedding keyboard states are not reset after hot restart
body:
We should add a hook to `onPreEngineRestart` that frees and recreates the platform keyboard manager. Since the keyboard manager is stateful, failing to reset its state can cause state instability or even crash the app after a hot restart (Shift-R in CLI.)


## Progress

- [x] Embedder: https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/28924
- [x] Linux
- [x] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [ ] iOS
- [ ] Android

## Reproduction
### Linux 

Reproduction, on an app with only a text field:
1. Start the app
2. Alt-Tab to the terminal
3. Hot restart
4. Switch back to the app.

There are two unexpected behaviors:

1. The text field no longer displays texts. (This behavior will disappear after another hot restart.)
2. The CLI reports the crash log. (This behavior persists after another hot restart.)

<details><summary>logs</summary><pre>
══╡ EXCEPTION CAUGHT BY SERVICES LIBRARY ╞══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
The following assertion was thrown during a platf
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Linux, Windows, and macOS embedding fixes were merged via engine PRs #28924, #28877, #28970. iOS and Android remain unresolved. The issue is marked with an umbrella symbol and the progress checklist shows iOS and Android as incomplete. Status: partially fixed, iOS/Android remain open.

title: [Proposal] Align prefixIcon with input text
comment_summary: Comments confirm this is a long-standing alignment issue not fixed by prior PRs (#28189, #91804, #91998). The core problem is that `prefixIcon` cannot be reliably aligned with input text baseline across all configurations (with/without label, outlined/underline border, different platforms). Multiple community workarounds were proposed: wrapping prefixIcon in a `Column(mainAxisAlignment: center)`, using empty `BoxConstraints` for `prefixIconConstraints`, or using `prefix` with `InputDecorator`. No framework-level fix landed; stalled.

title: Test Linux's FlView, and that Keyboard state is reset when engine restarts
body:
We could not find a way to unit test the `FlView` as of 10/5/2021 ([97f027](https://github.com/flutter/engine/tree/97f0274afd7a2da5af3de3e20bba1dab49f07411)), because most of its code can't be run headlessly. We should add a regression test for https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/28877 with the following step:

App: any app.

Steps:
1. Run with `flutter run -d linux`
2. Press ShiftLeft and hold
3. Hot restart the app
  - If run mannually, use the mouse to focus the terminal's window, then press R to hot restart. (You can release ShiftLeft now.)
4. Switch back to the restarted app, press any key.

Expected: the key press is processed normally.

Actual: The app crashes with the following log:
<details><pre>
══╡ EXCEPTION CAUGHT BY SERVICES LIBRARY ╞══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
The following assertion was thrown during a platform message callback:
A KeyUpEvent is dispatched, but the state shows that the physical key is not pressed. If this
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Reproduction confirmed on Linux (stable and master). The issue is that when a modifier key (e.g., ShiftLeft) is held during a hot restart, the engine resets but the Flutter framework still has the key in a pressed state, causing a `KeyUpEvent` assertion failure (`_pressedKeys.containsKey(event.physicalKey)`). The body notes a fix was made in the engine (#28877) but unit testing of `FlView` was not feasible headlessly. Status: the label-cleanup comment suggests ongoing triaging.

title: [Semantics] On TextField focused errorText is not read

## Steps to Reproduce

* create a form with multiple fields

* Navigate to other field than come back to email, It don't announce error message.

title: Semantics produces illegible errors in non-debug builds when test fakes text input
body:
When running an integration test that interacts with a TextField, multiple errors get thrown from the framework that cause the test to fail. Note that this only happens when not running with the default `--debug` flag (meaning running with `--release` or `--profile`) but we do not want to run our integration tests in `--debug` mode as we'd like the tests to accurately portray behavior in the "real" environment.

I first ran with `-d web-server` as instructed [here](https://flutter.dev/docs/cookbook/testing/integration/introduction#5b-web)

Terminal Command: `flutter drive --driver=int_test/util/flutter_driver.dart --target=int_test/my_int_test.dart -d web-server --profile --keep-app-running`
Terminal Output:
```
Running "flutter pub get" in flutter_int_test_on_web...            750ms
Launching int_test/my_int_test.dart on Web Server in profile mode...
Compiling int_test/my_int_test.dart for the Web...                 30.9s
int_test/my_int_test.dart is being served at http://l
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified by a Flutter team member: the test fakes text input via `tester.testTextInput` but uses real semantics, so when semantics tries to update the engine-side text field it finds nothing; in profile/release modes assertions are skipped so the code crashes rather than failing cleanly. Confirmed affected on web (Chrome/profile mode). Two proposed fixes: disable semantics in the test or don't fake text input; the framework could also produce a better error or fake text input in semantics when the test fakes it. Issue kept open to track the exception log for diagnosis; related to #89749.

title: TextField cursor display when the keyboard is closed should match the Platform
body:
In native development:

* In the **Android**, the text-field cursor should keep blinking when the soft keyboard is closed and the text-field has focus.
* In the **iPhone**, the text-field cursor should hide as soon as the keyboard is closed.

Flutter is only implementing the Android behavior. I'd like the `TextField` to implement this difference automatically, to match the Platform.

Please note: I know we can make `TextField(showCursor: false...)` to hide the cursor, but I can´t achieve that myself since I don´t know for sure when the keyboard is closed/open, and also some devices have physical keyboards.
comment_summary: A maintainer confirmed native iOS does hide the cursor when the keyboard is dismissed by scrolling, but not necessarily by tapping on non-tappable areas. A draft fix branch was created to only hide the keyboard (not unfocus) on iOS, but exact native behavior is complex. No PR was merged. Status: stalled, no fix.

comment_summary: Comments clarified that the crash no longer occurs on master, but Shift+CapsLock still produces a `KeyUpEvent` assertion error (`_pressedKeys.containsKey(event.physicalKey)`) and causes Backspace/Delete to stop working in TextField afterward. Root cause is that the custom IME shortcut key sequence sends a synthesized KeyUp for CapsLock without a prior KeyDown. A team member noted proper handling exists for Alt+Shift but not for user-customized shortcuts. Status: partial fix (no crash), residual keyboard breakage remains, no PR.

title: [Web][Windows]: RawKeyboard listener not working as intended on web (Ctrl + D opens bookmark)
comment_summary: Confirmed Windows-specific on web: Ctrl+D opens the Chrome bookmark dialog instead of triggering the Flutter shortcut. Ctrl+Q works correctly. Mac equivalent Cmd+D also opens bookmarks. One commenter workaround: calling RawKeyboard.instance.clearKeysPressed() at the right moment. The issue is that the browser intercepts certain keyboard shortcuts before Flutter can handle them; no framework-level fix proposed. Labeled as a new feature request; stalled.

body:

Documentation states `MediaQuery.of(context).viewInsets` is the following:

```
  /// The parts of the display that are completely obsc
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirmed reproduction on iOS simulator. Root cause is that `MediaQuery.viewInsets.bottom` reports a value larger than the actual keyboard height on iOS, causing a gap between the keyboard top and dialog bottom. Possibly related to #80253. Issue cc'd to @jmagman for iOS team. Status: stalled, triaged iOS, no fix.

title: [Proposal] First party support for input masks
body:
## Use case

Recently I was looking into adding an input mask to a TextFormField.
Similar to how [Qt does it](https://doc.qt.io/qt-5/qml-qtquick-textinput.html#inputMask-prop).

Example:
An input with mask `00-00`, where `0` allows digits and the `-` separator is 
prefilled at the correct position (index 2 in the text of the controller).
So an empty value would be '-' and not ''.

While looking into implementation options I didn't find anything in the SDK documentation.
I did however come across the following options:

- implementing a `TextInputFormatter` that handles a mask (not sure if this works and/or if this is flexible enough)
- using a package like `extended_masked_text` that provides this through a custom `TextEditingController`
This package does not support having the separators already prefilled.

## Proposal

I'd propose the following:

Add a `TextEditingController` or `TextInputFormatter` that can accept an input mask.
Or introduce a code lab that sho
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments suggest adding a codelab showing how to implement a TextInputFormatter mask. The documentation was noted as insufficient; `FilteringTextInputFormatter` is now too complex to serve as a reference example.

comment_summary: Comments identify that Sogou IME ignores composing-region position events during input but listens near commit events, causing the candidate window to appear at the previous field's position. The keyboard will start to appear. After the keyboard appears like this, it will keep on automatically popping up on navigating to new routes or popping and going back to previous ones.

This issue is only happening if there are buttons before a form, otherwise this is not happening.

To test the above theory go to 
https://wen-el-sahra.web.app/destination/BirthdayBachelor and select the first card named se and click book now and perform the above steps the keyboard won't appear.

2.  Go to https://wen-el-sahra.web.app/destination/Sunset use the search feature and search for something. Minimise and come back to the app and the keyboard will appear automatically. 

This behaviour may be device specific as previously a [similar issue](https
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Regression identified as introduced between Flutter 2.5.3 (working) and 2.6.x (broken) on Flutter Web/Android. A commenter bisected the root cause to PR #86312 (autofill changes), where autofill hints cause the keyboard to re-show after backgrounding and returning to the app; removing autofill hints from the TextField avoids the bug. The issue was routed to the web team. Workarounds include switching to stable 2.5.3, using Get.offAll to clear the widget tree, or using unfocus with proper autofill configuration.

title: Number keyboard on medium sized android tablets shows "calculator" buttons
comment_summary: A triager confirms the calculator-key layout on Android tablets and iPads is expected behavior: iOS iPad does not support `numberPad` or `phonePad` types, so `TextInputType.number` shows the extended numeric keyboard with operators. This is considered WAI by the framework; CC'd to @LongCatIsLooong for text input triage.

title: [Feature Request] Ability to copy WidgetSpan to clipboard
comment_summary: Workaround provided for text-containing WidgetSpans: use SelectionArea instead of SelectableText.rich to enable copying. No solution for image WidgetSpans or rich clipboard content.

title: Document "ghost run" and its interaction with `Paragraph.getBoxesForRange`
body:

>       // A "ghost" run is a run that does not impact the layout, breaking,
>      // alignment, width, etc but is still "visible" through getRectsForRange.
>      // For example, trailing whitespace on centered text can be scrolled
>      // through with the caret but will not wrap the line.
>      //
>      // Here, we add an additional run for the whitespace, but dont
>      // let it impact metrics. After layout of the whitespace run, we do not
>      // add its width into the x-offset adjustment, effectively nullifying its
>     // impact on the layout.

which implies `getRectsForRange` can return text boxes that are outside of the paragraph's bounding box if called on a ghost run. It's likely a common assumption that text boxes is always within the paragraph's bounding box so
...[truncated]

title: [Android 12] Textfield `onchanged` callback triggered twice on emulator

No fix or PR is mentioned; issue is labeled `team-android` and stalled.

title: Newline in Textformfield does not work on desktop
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Have a Textformfield
2. Build web app
3. Attempt to insert a newline in any web browser

**Expected results:** 

A new line

**Actual results:**

Not a new line

```dart
TextFormField(
    maxLines: 4,
    minLines: 3,
    controller: editingControllers['addr'],
    onChanged: (String val) => _changed(),
    textInputAction: TextInputAction.newline,
    keyboardType: TextInputType.streetAddress,
    decoration: InputDecoration(
        alignLabelWithHint: true,
        labelText: 'Address',
        hintMaxLines: 3,
        hintText:
            '124. Conch street\n00802 Bikini Bottom\nU.S. Virgin Islands'),
),
```
I have found similar issues on github, all of which were left unanswered and automatically closed by bots.
comment_summary: Comments confirm the root cause: TextInputType.streetAddress falls back to TextInputType.text on web/Linux, which does not allow newlines (only TextInputType.multiline does). The issue is confirmed on macOS desktop and Linux in addition to web. A related PR #90211 was merged but did not fix this; the issue remains open with no dedicated fix.

title: Device specific keyboard events on fuchsia
body:
Related: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/44918

Currently it seems on fuchsia some keys are not properly "normalized", for instance the backspace key is recognized by the framework as `LogicalKeyboardKey(42 | LogicalKeyboardKey.fuchsiaPlane)`, instead of `LogicalKeyboardKey.backspace`. 
Adding this issue as a TODO in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/93437.
comment_summary: A commenter indicated that some old text editing Intents in the framework are intentionally left in place until Fuchsia's codebase removes them, making this issue dependent on an upstream Fuchsia change.

title: No separation of hintText and suffixText with end-aligned TextField
comment_summary: Root cause traced to the cursor width adding an extra pixel of space between hint text and suffix text. A comment proposed negative cursorWidth as a workaround but noted an assert blocks it in debug mode. A Unicode narrow no-break space ('\u202f') is offered as a practical workaround. PR #93634 was started by @Reekoooo but not completed; the team called for someone to apply the review comments from that PR and open a new one.

title: Proposal to allow customization of the TextField toolbar
body:
**Use case:**

I have a simple TextField in my web app and I want to give the user custom options when they right-click on it (like 'set to default value'), this conflicts with the default TextField toolbar (copy, paste, etc). I have ways to show a custom ContextMenu that work fine in other cases, but the problem is the TextField consumes right-click events even if you disable the toolbar, so even if I add a GestureDetector, it doesn't notify me when the user right-clicks on the TextField.

I tried this:
`toolbarOptions: ToolbarOptions(
   selectAll: false,
   paste: false,
   cut: false,
   copy: false,
),`
on the TextField, and the toolbar no longer shows, but the right-click event is still consumed.

**Proposal:**

Either add a way to completely disable the toolbar, to allow custom handling of right-click events.
Or allow a way to add custom actions to the toolbar, and preferably some visual customization options.


Thanks for your time.
comment_summary: Comments confirm that setting all `toolbarOptions` to false disables the toolbar but right-click events are still consumed. A `_TransparentTapGestureRecognizer` hack was provided as a workaround.

title: [Web] RawKeyUpEvent is not triggered when both control and mouse click occurs simultaneously on macOS
comment_summary: Comments confirm that when Ctrl is held and a mouse click occurs on macOS in Chrome, the browser shows the right-click context menu, which causes the browser to swallow the Ctrl key-up event and Flutter never receives it. Confirmed on stable 2.5.3 and master 2.6. The issue was CC'd to @dkwingsmt. Status: stalled, no PR.

title: `KeyEventResult.skipRemainingHandlers` works differently on Desktop and Web.
body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

Give focus to the TextField and input text, arrow keys, backspace key, etc. with the keyboard.

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->

Text input, direction keys, and backspace key should work.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->

web : Text input is also possible, and the arrow keys and bac
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified by @gspencergoog: the TextField's FocusNode returns `skipRemainingHandlers`, which tells the focus system to stop propagating key events before they reach the top-level `WidgetsApp` `Shortcuts` widget, so arrow keys and backspace are consumed without being handled. Desktop behavior is considered correct by design; web is the platform that diverges from the intended model. No fix or PR was proposed; the thread concluded the platforms intentionally differ, and the reporter implemented a private workaround they did not share.

title: [Android] Can not input anything when press alt+tab with bluetooth keyboard
body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample <!-- (see "Code sample" section below) -->
2. Connect to bluetooth keyboard on Android device.
3. Press alt+tab

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->

When back to the app, it should be able to input text.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->

Ca
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirmed reproduction on both stable and master channels on Android with a Bluetooth keyboard. The root cause is that after alt+tab switches away and back to the app, the engine synthesizes a key-up event for Alt, but when a new key-repeat event arrives the framework asserts `_pressedKeys.containsKey` fails because the key was never recorded as re-pressed, leaving the keyboard in an unusable state. Non-Flutter apps do not exhibit this behavior. The issue was escalated to the framework team (@dkwingsmt, @HansMuller, @gspencergoog, @LongCatIsLooong) and remained open with no fix.

title: Windows app crashes when pressing "Insert" key.
body:
When I run a Windows app, and press "Insert" using the master channel, it crashes the app.

When it crashes, in the (Dart) exception, it gives the key event as:

```
RawKeyDownEvent#a2ba8(logicalKey: LogicalKeyboardKey#000ff(keyId: "0x16000000ff", keyLabel: "",
debugName: "Key with ID 0x016000000ff"), physicalKey: PhysicalKeyboardKey#70053(usbHidUsage:
"0x00070053", debugName: "Num Lock")) and its data: RawKeyEventDataWindows#f7e83(keyCode: 255,
scanCode: 57413, characterCodePoint: 0, modifiers: 0)
```

Which is weird to me, since it isn't the NumLock key, it's the Insert key.

The keycode for Insert should be 0x2d, not 0xff.

It's might be crashing because it looks at "NumLock" and thinks it needs to synchronize modifiers, but no modifier flags are set (because it's not a modifier).

Reproduction steps:

1) `flutter create --platform=windows foo`
2) `cd foo`
2) `flutter run -d windows`
2) Press "Insert" key.
3) Crash.

I was able to repro this with the b865e23
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Initially thought to be remote-desktop-specific but confirmed on native Windows 10/11 with Flutter 3.3+ for multiple keys (Insert, Shift, Alt-Left). Root cause: key state desync — KeyRepeatEvent or KeyDownEvent dispatched when _pressedKeys shows inconsistent state. Also reported on Android TV with a physical remote. Key fuzzing suggested. Affects stable 3.3.x through 3.16.x; triaged P4, team-desktop label removed during re-triage. No fix PR.

title: [Proposal][Desktop] Let "ESC" key de-select currently selected item
body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Use case

Users expect de-selection to work like it does in other programs.

Selected items (including selected text, widgets, and tab-focused items) all require the mouse to de-select, which is inconvenient. Other desktop programs typically allow de-selecting items with the <kbd>ESC</kbd> key.

## Proposal

Selected items should be de-selected when a user presses the <kbd>ESC</kbd> key. This includes:

- Selected text
- Select
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments discuss scope: ESC should deselect selected text, unfocus focused text fields, and cancel tab-based keyboard navigation. Related issues #98163 and #84416 cited. Discussion about matching platform-native behavior. WCAG 2.1 compliance raised as an accessibility concern. No PR or fix landed.

title: [Web][iOS][Safari]: Free space is shown at the bottom of screen when focussing a textfield
comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause: the issue only occurs when Safari's URL bar is positioned at the bottom (default since iOS 15). On iOS 14 or with the URL bar at the top, it does not reproduce. A physical device user reported that in PWA (Add to Home Screen) mode, the bottom of the app becomes unresponsive after any text field focus/unfocus. Assignee (@mdebbar) was unassigned due to inactivity. One later commenter could not reproduce it. Status unclear.

title: Text should remain foused when TextFormField caret position when off TextFormField width
comment_summary: Comments clarify the bug: when text is added programmatically via `TextEditingController` while the field is focused, the field does not scroll to keep the cursor visible if the cursor moves beyond the visible area. Reproduced on both iOS and Android on stable 2.8/master. Android partially works. The suggested workaround of re-setting `controller.selection` does not help because the underlying issue is that `EditableText` does not call `bringIntoView` after a programmatic text change. Labeled as duplicate of #11416 (closed) but reporter considers it distinct since field remains focused.

comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on macOS desktop and web (both renderers), not on Linux. Issue: caret width shrinks or goes off-screen at position 0 of a horizontally scrollable TextField. CC'd to @justinmc. Web-related label was later added. Status: stalled, no PR.

title: IME lifecycle not working well in TextField
body:
Hello! I am facing an issue when running my Android keyboard service in my Flutter app (or any Flutter apps), I found several issues opened on TextField but none matched my exact situation so I will try to detail it here:

## Steps to Reproduce
Android version: 9
Debugging an Android IME in any Flutter app, typing words and spaces in a `TextField`

**Expected results:**
The IME should follow a normal lifecycle

**Actual results:**
The IME `onStartInput` is called whenever a space is sent. 

I can try to provide more details if needed! Thanks!!
comment_summary:
Comments identify the root cause: Flutter's Android text input plugin calls `InputMethodManager.restartInput` whenever the framework changes the composing region, which happens when the built-in single-line input formatter removes `
` characters from the composition. This does not happen in native `EditText` because native editors don't apply such formatters. The issue is specific to `TextInputType.text` (not `emailAddress` or `visiblePassword`). No fix or PR proposed; stalled.

comment_summary: Comments narrow the issue to iOS 12 and 13 with the HTML renderer (CanvasKit does not work at all on those versions). No fix is provided; the triage links to the similar #92183. The issue is labeled P4 and held open due to lack of a device to reproduce on.

title: [web] TextField doesn't accept input after it is submitted even though it retains focus
comment_summary: Confirmed web-only regression introduced between Flutter 2.5.3 (working) and 2.8.x. After submitting via the Enter key, the TextField retains focus visually but stops accepting keyboard input; clicking a submit button instead avoids the bug because focus briefly moves. A workaround of wrapping requestFocus in a Future.delayed(10ms) was shared and confirmed effective for some users. Some users report the issue tied to the index.html bootstrap script and switching to the async engineInitializer.autoStart() pattern resolved it for them. Issue status is unclear — a team member noted they could not reproduce it in a later test with the original code sample.

title: [Windows Desktop] App does not read textfield characters when moving the cursor with arrow keys with screen reader enabled
body:
Hi,
I'm using `TextFormField`s on Windows with the [NVDA](https://www.nvaccess.org/) screen reader, and they're not behaving like text fields should.

For example, I get my characters echoed regardless of my character echo setting, and when I navigate through the contents of the text field with the arrow keys, nothing is read. The screen reader doesn't seem to know that character navigation is happening.

Arrow key navigation through text field content produces no speech output. Reproduced on stable 2.8.1 and master. The issue may also affect web (other UI elements not read there). Status: stalled, no PR, team-windows assigned.

title: HardwareKeyboard logicalKeysPressed returns previously pressed keys
body:

4. Press key `arrow left` and release
5. Check console output
6. See that the last keypress includes the key `k` when only the `arrow left` key was pressed

**Expected results:**

LogicalKeySet#4b191(keys: Meta Left)

LogicalKeySet#6c398(keys: Arrow Left)
```

**Actual results:**
```

LogicalKeySet#5c7f7(keys: Meta Left + Key K)
LogicalKeySet#6c398(keys: Arrow Left + Key K)
```

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
i
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirmed the bug reproduces on web (Chrome) but also on Windows desktop (with Alt key instead of Meta), not on macOS desktop, pointing to a framework-level key-state issue. A commenter noted a related web issue (#162977) where trigger keys are not released when the app loses focus. No fix or PR linked; stalled.

title: [IOS] Keyboard opens unexpectedly when navigating back to page with textfield.
comment_summary: Confirmed on physical iOS devices only (not simulator) across stable 2.8.1 through 3.10.5; swipe-back gesture triggers keyboard while hardware back button does not. Root cause: didStartUserGesture causes FocusNode from prior page to become primaryFocus. Workarounds: (1) NavigatorObserver calling FocusManager.instance.primaryFocus?.unfocus() in didStartUserGesture, (2) FocusManager.instance.primaryFocus?.unfocus() in GoRouter redirect. No fix PR; issue persists.

title: `suffixIcon/prefixIcon` alignment in an growing multiline TextField
body:
## Use case

I'd like to add prefix/suffix icons which are vertically aligned to the top or bottom of a multiline TextField. The TextField might have a fixed height `TextField(maxlines: 4)` or an auto-growing height with a limit: `TextField(maxlines: 4, minlines: 1)`. Think about a Telegram chat input box.

This feature has been requested in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/42651 with ideal example images:

![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1025241/94514883-f8252d00-022a-11eb-9d04-bfe5b7d13142.png)


![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/30778803/109836081-e110be00-7c54-11eb-985d-879da364133d.png)

And it has been closed by https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/91998 which add some doc how to do it. However, the PR doesn't work:

- The code snippet only covers simple one-line TextField situation.
- The technique given in the comment https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/91998#issue-1029071224 results in wrong paddings and alignments:

<i
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The community provided a workaround using a Stack with Positioned icons instead of prefixIcon/suffixIcon, but noted the hardcoded contentPadding is incorrect. An improved workaround using suffixIcon: SizedBox.shrink() with Positioned is shared. No fix or PR has been proposed; the issue is treated as a proposal/feature request. Currently stalled.

title: [Windows Web] Telex IME causes crashes
body:
I created the sample and reproduced this issue. If I use telex to type, it will throw this exception.
I think that it related to the unicode in the textfield

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4393681/147629539-8bab359a-5cb5-4fb0-a999-8fa02157cd15.mp4

d
comment_summary: Comments confirmed the crash is reproducible on both stable (2.8.1) and master channels when using the Telex Vietnamese IME on Flutter web (Chrome). The root cause is that the Telex IME synthesizes a key-up event for Space without a preceding key-down, causing a framework assertion failure in `HardwareKeyboard._assertEventIsRegular` (`_pressedKeys.containsKey` fails). After the assert fires, `_keyEventsSinceLastMessage` is never cleared, leaving subsequent key events unable to process. The issue is web-only — the Windows desktop target is unaffected. No fix has landed.

title: Use SingleChildScrollView to nest SelectableText, long press to select all at the front of the text, the copy option is displayed at the bottom but cannot be clicked
comment_summary: Reproduced on stable and master on physical devices (not emulators). The issue only triggers when long-pressing at the beginning/top of the text, not at the bottom. A question was raised whether it also reproduces with `SelectionArea` + `Text` instead of `SelectableText`. Status: stalled, no fix.

                  lastestInputLength = newValue?.length;
                },
                decoration: InputDecoration(
                    isDense: true,
                    contentPadding:
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug affects iOS Safari and Chrome on Android (web platform) on stable and master channels through at least Flutter 3.9, with the cursor failing to move to tapped positions in multiline TextFields. The root cause is identified as Flutter's inability to sync scroll state between the underlying hidden input element and the visible content, combined with inability to hide the native selection overlay on iOS (see #99918). Issue #120613 was linked as a proposed solution to address the underlying architecture. A native HTML element workaround was suggested. Currently stalled with no fix; still marked P1 but with no active assignee.

title: [Proposal]Tapping a virtual keyboard's next-button, when in a text-field should not switch focus to other widgets apart from TextField
body:
If a `TextField` has `textInputAction: TextInputAction.next`, a right-arrow will be displayed in the virtual keyboard in the Android phone.

If next to that `TextField` there is another `TextField`, pressing that right-arrow will move focus to that other `TextField`, which is the correct expected behavior.

However, if next to that `TextField` there is, say, a button; then pressing that right-arrow will move focus to that button, which makes no sense in a phone.

I don't understand why, in phones (Android and iOS), widgets other than textfields can have focus. There's nothing we can do with that focus.

It makes perfect sense in the desktop or web, of course.

So my questions are:

1) Does it make sense that widgets other than textfields can have focus, in phones?

2) Without having to manually add `ExcludeFocus` everywhere, how do I prevent focus of non-textfields in phones, so that pressing the abovementioned right-arrow will move focus to the next text-field? 

This 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Discussion clarified why non-textfield widgets receive focus on mobile: accessibility features and hardware keyboard navigation require it. The consensus is that pressing the virtual keyboard's next/done button should move focus only to the next text-capable widget, not to buttons. No framework-level fix proposed; FocusNode.skipTraversal is the available (but imperfect) workaround.

title: [Proposal] Multiline TextField should support both submit and enter a newline
body:
Related but closed: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/18439

The keyboard opened from this has a done button (on _most_ devices...), and visually has multiple lines. It however has no newline key anymore, so you can't manually enter newlines. See https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/18439#issuecomment-762781393.

```
TextField(
  maxLines: 5,
  keyboardType: TextInputType.text,
  textInputAction: TextInputAction.done,
),
```

The keyboard opened from this has a newline button, but cannot be submitted. Not only that, on iOS, you have no native back button and cannot close the keyboard as the user unless there is a another widget which requests focus which is on screen you can tap. On Android, the native back button will NOT fire `onSubmitted` or `onEditingComplete`, so while you can close the keyboard. Similarly, unless there is another way to change the focus, every time you do anything the keyboard will re-open with this TextField focused. See https://github.c
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug on iOS and Android; users share workarounds involving Overlay + keyboard-visibility plugin to display a floating "Done" button on iOS. No official fix or PR is mentioned; the issue remains open and stalled with the workaround as the only practical approach.

title: Test key simulator doesn't work with uppercase characters
body:
I'm using `WidgetTester`'s `sendKeyDownEvent` and `sendKeyEvent` to simulate hardware interaction with a custom text field implementation. For example, typing "Hello, World!" into the text field using individual hardware keyboard keys.

It looks like the keyboard simulator for tests does not support uppercase characters:
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/5a862ebecdb14ad389cdce9cd7f79470ece27a7f/packages/flutter_test/lib/src/event_simulation.dart#L31

What I would expect is that if the shift key is currently pressed in the simulator, then the resolved character would be uppercase. This is also true for the various non-alpha-numeric characters on the keyboard as well.

Flutter version:
```
Flutter 2.8.1 • channel stable • git@github.com:flutter/flutter.git
Framework • revision 77d935af4d (2 weeks ago) • 2021-12-16 08:37:33 -0800
Engine • revision 890a5fca2e
Tools • Dart 2.15.1
```

Here's the workhorse code for how I'm attempting to use the simulator to enter text:

...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the root cause: the simulator converts all keys to lowercase per a TODO in the code. The intention was to deprecate old simulation code once the new KeyEvent-based system was ready.

title: [MacOS] LogicalKeyboard involving shift key throws error: Physical key for LogicalKeyboardKey not found in macos physical key map
body:
When I try to press the exclamation logical keyboard key using `WidgetTester`, I get the following error:

```
Error: 'package:flutter_test/src/event_simulation.dart': Failed assertion: line 243 pos 12: 'result != null': Physical key for LogicalKeyboardKey#00021(keyId: "0x00000021", keyLabel: "!", debugName: "Exclamation") not found in macos physical key map
```

Flutter version:
```
Flutter 2.8.1 • channel stable • git@github.com:flutter/flutter.git
Framework • revision 77d935af4d (2 weeks ago) • 2021-12-16 08:37:33 -0800
Engine • revision 890a5fca2e
Tools • Dart 2.15.1
```
comment_summary: Comments confirm the assertion fails on both stable and master when simulating shift-key combinations (e.g., `!`, `*`, `(`) on macOS in tests. The root cause is that `KeyEventSimulator` has no macOS physical-key mapping for logical keys that require Shift; the workaround is to specify `physicalKey` explicitly (e.g., `PhysicalKeyboardKey.digit1`) or simulate Shift+key separately. A broader fix tracking logical-key remapping for shifted keys was tracked in #100456. Status: open; the workaround is documented in comments.

title: [TextField]`backgroundColor` covers/hides cursor and text selection
comment_summary: A PR #96276 was submitted to fix this. A comment notes this is a duplicate of #79168 and is blocked on #39420. Comments as of 3.32.2 confirm the issue persists. No confirmed landed fix; status is stalled and still open.

comment_summary: Root cause clarified: pasting multiline text loses line breaks on web when `keyboardType: TextInputType.text`; using `TextInputType.multiline` preserves them. A fix would require always using a `<textarea>` element even for single-line cases. Confirmed affected on web (Chrome and macOS desktop); macOS app unaffected. title: WidgetSpan on first line of TextField is positioned incorrectly
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug: a `WidgetSpan` as the sole first child of a `TextField`'s `TextSpan` renders outside the TextField bounds; adding any preceding `TextSpan` corrects the layout. Reproduced on macOS stable 2.8.1 and master 2.9. No root cause analysis, fix, or PR in comments; stalled.

title: [Desktop] Pasting large run of non-whitespace text consumes many GB and freezes the app
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample
2. Download large text files from [The Large Corpus](http://corpus.canterbury.ac.nz/resources/large.zip)
3. Paste the contents of E.coli into the text field

**Expected results:** Application does not hang

**Actual results:** Application hangs and memory usage skyrockets:

macOS:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/72562119/148201841-f5ec3308-22a9-4e85-9498-5dca6e710b33.png)

Windows:
![large_text memory](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/72562119/148202679-b4d930d1-1cc4-4f83-bb45-8095b2141333.png)

Pasting a similar amount of text (e.g. world192.txt which is 2.5MB and bible.txt which is 4MB) with spaces and newlines doesn't cause this (editing is painfully slow though, similar to https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/90063).


<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause: pasting a large run of non-whitespace text (e.g., genome data) causes runaway memory usage because Paragraph rendering tries to lay out one enormous word without line-break opportunities. The issue is distinct from #90063 (general slowness) because the memory blowup is unique to whitespace-free content. Confirmed on macOS and Windows desktops on stable/master. Was mistakenly closed as a duplicate and reopened. Labeled P1 but no fix; stalled with stale-label bot comments.

title: [iOS Web] TextField does not advance to next input field via up/down arrow
comment_summary: Comments confirmed the issue is specific to iOS browsers (Safari, Chrome, Edge, Opera, DuckDuckGo) when `TextInputType.number` is used — the up/down navigation arrows are disabled. Android web works correctly. The issue was initially closed as a duplicate of #73835 but reopened as distinct. A 2025 update confirmed the bug persists on stable 3.35.7 and master 3.38.0. Status: open and stalled.

title: Flutter add-to-app fragment inserts unnecessary bottom padding when keyboard is open
body:
Internal: b/258263557

## Description

If a flutter fragment is imported into an android app & the fragment is not full screen (e.g. a screen with bottom navigation bar), opening a keyboard adds an additional padding in flutter.

![Android without flutter](https://i.stack.imgur.com/Gptqdm.png)
![Flutter fragment in android with keyboard closed](https://i.stack.imgur.com/Qpau0m.png)
![Flutter fragment in android with keyboard open](https://i.stack.imgur.com/0rluDm.png)

Bigger images

 1. https://i.stack.imgur.com/Gptqd.png
 1. https://i.stack.imgur.com/Qpau0.png
 1. https://i.stack.imgur.com/0rluD.png

Image three shows the padding between the keyboard and the text field. It is clearly coming from flutter (as it is white). It is always the same height, as the distance from the end of the fragment to the end of the screen.

We know of `resizeToAvoidBottomInset`, but using this will cut off text fields and won't scroll the active one into the view, which is what we want.
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause: when a Flutter fragment does not occupy the full screen height (e.g., native bottom navigation bar present), `Scaffold` adds bottom inset padding equal to the distance from the bottom of the fragment to the bottom of the screen, causing extra whitespace above the keyboard. Affects Android (confirmed on stable 2.8 and master) and also iOS. The extra padding is confirmed via Flutter Inspector to come from Flutter's Material scaffold, not the host Android app; Cupertino does not exhibit the behavior. No fix has been landed; using `resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false` and manually handling `MediaQuery.viewInsets.bottom` is a partial workaround.

title: AutofillGroup breaks autofocus of TextField on route change
comment_summary: Root cause identified: `TextInput.finishAutofillContext` is called several frames late during route transition animation, after the autofocused new field already has focus. No single-field commit API exists on Android (the Android `commit` API affects the entire Activity). Proposed fix: hook into route's `willPop` or listen to `Route.isCurrent`. Assigned to @LongCatIsLooong, later unassigned due to inactivity. Status: stalled, unassigned.

title: Change RawAutocomplete and Autocomplete initialValue attribute type to T?

title: TextInputConnection updateConfig does not update action button
body:
Software keyboards have an "action button". It often shows a message like "Done", "Search", or shows a newline icon. This button can be configured as needed by an app. Flutter provides an API to configure this button when the keyboard first opens, as well as an API to change the button after the keyboard is already open.

Flutter's API to change the button after the keyboard has opened doesn't work.

Here's a Flutter snippet that provides an initial keyboard configuration and shows the keyboard:

```dart
_inputConnection = TextInput.attach(
  this, // or whatever you have that implements TextInputClient
  TextInputConfiguration(
    enableDeltaModel: true,
    inputAction: TextInputAction.newline,
    // possibly other configurations
  ),
);

_inputConnection!
  ..show()
  ..setEditingState(/* pass in some initial editing value */);
```

Once the keyboard is visible, an app should be able to change the action button using the following code, but the button doesn't 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirmed the bug is reproducible on both Android and iOS on stable (3.29.3) and master (3.32.0-1.0.pre.514). The `TextInputConnection.updateConfig` method sends the updated configuration to the platform but the soft keyboard's action button does not visually update while the keyboard is already open. No root cause beyond the method not triggering a keyboard reload was identified. No PR or fix exists; status is open.

title: [Windows] tablet mode can't hide soft keyboard when unfocus
body:
**1. run sample in windows tablet mode**
flutter create --sample=widgets.FocusNode.unfocus.1 mysample

**2. focus on a textfield, then open the soft keyboard**

**3. click unfocus**
 
the soft keyboard should auto hide, but not.
comment_summary: Windows tablet mode soft keyboard does not dismiss on unfocus. Confirmed to affect Windows 10 and 11. Multiple community workarounds shared: SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.hide'), FocusManager.instance.primaryFocus?.unfocus() in onTapOutside, RouteObserver to hide on navigation, and pub.dev package flutter_osk. No fix landed; stalled.

title: TextEditingController Textspan's recognizer raising error on initializing text
comment_summary: The root cause is that `buildTextSpan` is called during the widget attach phase (semantics configuration) before the widget is fully mounted, and a `TapGestureRecognizer` created inside `buildTextSpan` triggers an assertion `readOnly && !obscureText` in `RenderEditable.describeSemanticsConfiguration`. Confirmed on Android, iOS real device, and iOS Simulator across Flutter 2.8 through 3.24. A workaround is to defer the controller text assignment via `Future.microtask(() => controller.text = ...)` in `initState`. Issue remains open and stalled with no PR.

title: Flutter Web on Windows; Using Windows Clipboard History Freezes Application With Pasting Text into TextFormField
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

2. Search for Clipboard
3. Enable Clipboard History as seen in this image.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/79998776/151980347-c1d29316-3f92-4b73-8860-558a3bb996de.png)

4. Create a new Flutter application.
5. Replace main.dart code with the below Code.
6. Selected Chrome as the device
7. Copy some text into your clipboard
8. Run in the application using Debug mode (Start Debugging in VS Code)
9. Using the mouse, click the TextFormField
10. Press Windows Logo Key + Alt.
11. Click the text you copied into the clipboard
12. **App will freeze.**

If you repeat the above steps and run the application without debugging, assert messages will appear in the terminal Debug Console.

**NOTE:** Windows Clipboard History feature works normally when running on Windows and Andriod. 

**Expected results:** 
Text is pasted into the TextFormField

**Actual results:** 
Chrome a
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: Windows Clipboard History emulates Ctrl+V via a synthetic keyboard event where KeyboardEvent.code is empty and KeyboardEvent.location is 0 (no location); Flutter's keyboard binding asserts that modifier keys must have a valid location, causing the assert failure and freeze in debug mode. Related issue #98460 tracks the empty-code problem. No fix mentioned.

title: IME deltas on Android trigger input method restarts when they shouldn't
body:
I'm integrating the relatively new IME deltas in Flutter into a document editor. I'm finding that on Android (not iOS), that it's essentially impossible to keep Android from restarting the input method due to changes to the composing region.

For example, I have a blob of text. I place the caret in the text. Then I press and hold the backspace button on the Android software keyboard. The expected behavior is that delete deltas keep getting reported until all the available text is gone. In reality, at random times, the Flutter Android embedding decides that it doesn't like the composing region and forces the input method to restart. This abruptly causes the deletion to stop.

The deletion problem is exceptionally visible when you place the caret at the very end of the text. In that case, the deletion only makes it 1 character before the input method restarts. This essentially means that if a user wants to delete a bunch of text, including the last character in the text, then that us
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause: the Android embedding restarts the IME whenever the composing region changes, which was a prior fix to a different bug. One commenter notes the framework needs a way to signal that a composing change originated from a key event so the IME restart can be skipped. A GIF from @Renzo-Olivares shows the basic text client working correctly, suggesting the issue may be specific to document-editor use cases. Status: stalled.

title: Programmatic control of text undo/redo
body:
Recently, undo/redo (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/96968) was added to Flutter's text editing.  It is contained within EditableText privately, but some developers may want to be able to control its behavior programmatically.

Currently, the only way its behavior can be affected by the developer is by using Shortcuts and Intents to affect its use of UndoTextIntent and RedoTextIntent.

CC @YeungKC
comment_summary: Comments from @Renzo-Olivares confirm ongoing work to allow programmatically pushing state to the undo stack, which would cover this issue. Assignment was later removed due to no updates.

title: Discussion: Should char messages be redispatched on Win32?
body:
Message redispatching is a mechanism used in add-to-app scenarios that allows messages not handled by Flutter to be handled by outer native applications. On Win32, a key press associated with text input can occur in two forms:

1. `WM_KEYDOWN`, `WM_CHAR`
2. Standalone `WM_CHAR`

For example, when pressing `DeadKey^` followed by an uncombinable character, say `Digit1`, the messages for the second press will be

1. `WM_KEYDOWN(Digit1)`
2. `WM_CHAR('^')`
3. `WM_CHAR('1')`

Where the second message follows the first keydown message, while the third message is a standalone one.

Currently, in the first form, whether the char message will be redispatched follows that of the key down message, which further depends on whether the entire key down event is handled by Flutter framework, while in the second form, the char message is never redispatched.

This leaves inconsistency between the handling mechanism of char messages.

Options to fix this are:
1. **Never redispatch char 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Only a bot comment about an irrelevant label; no substantive new information.

title: Redispatched messages should follow the order of first arrival
body: Message redispatching is a mechanism used in add-to-app scenarios that allows messages not handled by Flutter to be handled by outer native applications. This is meant to make the Flutter app feel "transparent", but the current mechanism does not keep the order of arrival. Some tests are marked with a TODO with this issue.

**Expected results:**
After step 5

title: [Windows][HardwareKeyboard] Incorrect Caps Lock status when starting the app with Caps Lock On
body:
[EDIT] Changed the issue title and please refer to the [comment](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/98377#issuecomment-1040906016) below.

The issue is that if you start the app with Caps Lock enabled, flutter reports the lock mode off and once you disable it, the lock mode is reported as on (present in the `HardwareKeyboard.instance.lockModesEnabled`)

Another separate issue is that on Dartpad, HardwareKeyboard stops calling the handler after the very first event, regardless of the type of event, so this issue can't be tested on Dartpad.

On MacOS the issue is not present, but, the lockMode is not added until an event happens, however, the status is reported correctly (unlike windows which shows reversed behavior, on when off and off when on).

[OLD COMMENT FROM HERE] *************************************

When I was working on my Mac, I found a way to detect caps lock, it is a bit weird, but it worked. Basically I add a listener to `RawKeyboard.instance` that checks 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: on Windows, HardwareKeyboard.lockModesEnabled reports the inverted Caps Lock state when the app starts with Caps Lock already enabled because the engine does not query the initial lock state from the OS on startup. The issue also affects Linux. On macOS, the lock mode isn't added to lockModesEnabled until another key event fires. A related issue #98533 is linked. Comments confirm the bug persists through Flutter 3.19 and that lockModesEnabled also fails to update when Caps Lock is toggled while the app window is not focused. No fix or PR proposed.

title: [Win32, Keyboard] Fix synthesization of toggle keys from phase 0 to 3
body:
The synthesization algorithm on Win32 is broken if it was last observed at phase 0 (pressed 0 toggled 0) and currently phase 3 (pressed 1 toggled 0). 

Expected: 3 events are synthesized (down, up, down)

Actual: 1 event is synthesized (down)

This has been noted as a TODO for a unit test, `SynthesizeModifiers`.

Original report follows.

---

I found an issue with TextField, I'm not sure if it's intended or not.
After entering text in the TextField, if you select the text in the middle, the cursor should move to that position.
However, if you add two exclamation marks after the text and select the middle of the text, the cursor does not move to that location, but selects up to that location.

This issue was found in both English and Korean.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Enter arbitrary strings in the TextField.
2. Finally, enter two exclamation marks.
3. Click in the middle of the text.
4. Instead of moving the cursor to that position, it is selected from the back to the selected point.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/26322627/154611611-48731563-9fa1-4270-b1d7-7aebfe5e0a1c.mp4


<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

<!--
    
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments narrow the root cause to older Samsung Keyboard versions (below 5.x, i.e., Android 10 and older) emitting a `ShiftRight` key-down event with non-zero `metaState` when the user switches to the symbols pane, but never emitting the corresponding key-up, leaving Flutter thinking Shift is held and causing all subsequent taps to extend the selection instead of placing the cursor. @gnprice provided detailed key-event logs confirming the synthesized shift event, and @LongCatIsLooong and @dkwingsmt identified that a forced `metaState` synchronization before text events could fix it. A user-level workaround of disabling Samsung Keyboard swipe/cursor controls or switching to 'No swipe gestures' resolves it for some; a Dart-side workaround of injecting a synthetic shift-up event also works. The issue is assigned to @LongCatIsLooong but has had no recent status updates and no PR; a late comment links a similar reproduction using Gboard's shift-to-capitalize feature, suggesting the bug is broader than Samsung-only.

                      data: RawKeyEventDataWindows(
                          keyCode: 65,
                          scanCode: 30,
                          characterC
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Team confirmed simulating key events via RawKeyboard.handleRawKeyEvent only fires framework-side events and does not work for actual text input because input requires cooperation between the engine and framework. Suggested workaround for Windows is to use the win32 package's FFI to send real OS-level key events. A cross-platform simulation API was acknowledged as technically feasible but not prioritized.

title: [Windows] On screen keyboard pops up abnormally on touch screen tablets
body:
screen keyboard pops up abnormally on the Surface tablet

It will not pop up for the first time. You need to manually click the keyboard icon on the taskbar. After that, any click on the position on the software will evoke the on-screen keyboard.

Sample demo video: [video](https://github.com/yh4922/flutter_keyboard_test/raw/main/assets/1.mp4)

Sample code repository: https://github.com/yh4922/flutter_keyboard_test

Hardware platform
```
Processor	Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-8350U CPU @ 1.70GHz   1.90 GHz
Installed RAM	8.00 GB
System type	64-bit operating system, x64-based processor
Pen and touch	Pen and touch support with 10 touch points
```
comment_summary: Affects Windows touch-screen devices (specifically Surface tablets and other Windows devices with touch capability) where the on-screen keyboard pops up on every touch after a TextField has once been focused. The root cause identified in comments is that when a TextField is disposed or focus is lost, the engine still holds an active IME/input connection on Windows, causing the OS touch keyboard to appear on any subsequent touch. Multiple workarounds involve placing an autofocus transparent ElevatedButton on every screen to redirect focus; FocusManager.instance.primaryFocus?.nextFocus() also works as a workaround while unfocus() does not. Issue is over two years old with no engine-level fix landed.

title: Mouse Middle click to paste doesn't perform any action.
comment_summary: Clarified that on Linux, middle click pastes the current X11 selection (highlighted text), not the clipboard, and Shift+Insert pastes from the clipboard — these are distinct behaviors. On macOS there is no native middle-click paste. The framework tag is sufficient; engine involvement not needed since events are being received.

title: [MacOS] Trailing whitespace in multiline TextField overflows and doesn't go to newline.
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Use a multiline TextField.
2. Input a lot of whitespace in the content tail.
3. Whitespace is overflow and not switch to a newline (Tap Ctrl + A to select all).

**Expected results:** Auto switch to a new line.

**Actual results:** Whitespace is overflow.

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
const SizedBox(
    width: 400,
    height: 200,
    child: TextField(
      maxLines: null,
      keyboardType: TextInputType.multiline,
      decoration: InputDecoration(
        border: InputBorder.none,
        filled: true,
        fillColor: Colors.pink
      ),
      expands: true,
    ),
  )
```

</details>

<details>
  <summary>Logs</summary>
<!-- Finally, paste the output of running `flutter doctor -v` here. -->

```
[✓] Flutter (Channel stable, 2.8.1, on macOS 12.0.1 21A559 darwin-arm, locale
    zh-Hans-CN)
    • Flutter version 2.8.1 at /Volumes/AOSP/flutter
    • Upstream repository https://github.com/flut
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm this is distinct from mobile behavior: on macOS/desktop, trailing whitespace in a multiline TextField does not trigger line wrap, unlike Sublime Text or other native editors. On iOS/mobile, whitespace also does not wrap, but subsequent characters start on a new line. Selection extending horizontally outside the field when selecting text with trailing whitespace is noted as a separate bug. Attributed to Skia-level text layout behavior. No fix; stalled.

title: Autocomplete<T> missing features desktop and web users expect
body:
