1. With the Autocomplete options displayed clicking the option with the mouse selects the option, and focus is placed in Autocomplete text box with the cursor at the end of the text.  _PERFECT BEHAVIOR_.

2. With the Autocomplete options displayed using the up and down arrow keys different options are displayed.  Then pressing ENTER on an option, selects that option.  BUT, focus is not visible.  The user needs to press the TAB key once to get focus on the text box. _PROBLEM_

The two above behaviors should be the same, but they are not.  Can this be fixed so that when the ENTER key is pressed the Widget behaves the same as being clicked?

3.  If the user enters text in the Autocomplete and presses ENTER or TAB, the text is accepted but the onSelected callback is not invoked. I understand why this behaves like this but at a minimum, a text entry callback should be added so that developers would be advised when the user entered text without selecting an 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed pressing ENTER to select an autocomplete item loses focus on the text field. Related to #98199. No PR landed. Labeled as feature request.

title: Undo/redo should coalesce changes correctly by platform
body:
Support for text undo/redo was added in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/96968, but the coalescing isn't exactly correct on each platform.  In that PR, we used a simple time-based throttle on all platforms ([code](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/fac90f44baf42558de44af86169346cbf7585e58/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/editable_text.dart#L4344-L4347)), but the actual behavior is much more nuanced and slightly different on each platform ([some analysis](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/24222#issuecomment-868930150)).  Ideally, Flutter should perfectly match the behavior of each platform.

By coalescing, I mean where the editor separates one undoable chunk of text from the next.

## To reproduce

  1. Run any app with a text input, such as the one given below.
  2. Type one or two lines of text.  Enough that it takes you a few seconds to finish.
  3. Press ctrl/cmd + Z to undo a few times and notice how the text is grouped.

Expected behavior: The amount 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments contain a detailed platform-by-platform analysis of native vs. Flutter undo/redo coalescing behavior on macOS, Linux, and Windows. Key findings: on Linux, native undo is word-by-word while Flutter is more granular; on macOS, native coalescing is time-based; on Windows, the pattern is complex. Redo (Ctrl+Shift+Z) was noted as not working on Linux in Flutter. Stalled with no fix or PR.

title: Distinguish the key with same scancodes but different logical key.
body:
## Use case
This issue is extracted a common issue from #97582 and #88646.

In some cases, keys with same scancodes (both is zero) will be pressed at the same time with different logical key.
This is caused by emulated shortcut key on Windows.
- On Windows (both win32 apps and web pages on Chromium), pasting from Win+V will emulate Ctrl+V but both scancode is 0.
- On web on Chromium on Windows, the emoji item in the context menu on textarea will emulate Win+period but both scancode is 0.

In #97582 and #88646:
This assertion https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/ab89ce285f76a3fd3a9328ec863f5623cc5ac8bc/packages/flutter/lib/src/services/hardware_keyboard.dart#L444-L447
is fails because PhysicalKeyboardKey uses the pyhsical key to remain pressed keys: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/ab89ce285f76a3fd3a9328ec863f5623cc5ac8bc/packages/flutter/lib/src/services/hardware_keyboard.dart#L392

## Proposal
- The PhysicalKeyboardKey should distinguish these keys.
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue occurs when scancode is 0 (Windows) or the code is empty/Unidentified (Chromium web). A Chromium bug was filed.

comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue is that the gesture detection region for text selection extends to the label and helper text areas outside the visual text input border. A proposed fix of wrapping the editable text with a GestureDetector was ruled out because _RenderDecoration uses a custom RenderBox. The longer-term fix is applying global selection to TextField, per @chunhtai. An adjustDragOffset utility is shared as a potential building block.

title: [Keyboard] Synchronize keyboard state on defocus and refocus
body:
We should find ways to synchronize keyboard state when the app loses focus (maybe) and regains focus (definitely). Otherwise apps that display based on keyboard state (instead of key events) might confuse users when the app regains focus.

State:
- [ ] Windows
- [ ] Linux/GTK
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Web
- [ ] Android 
- [ ] iOS 
- [ ] Fuchsia
  - Probably already done. Fuchsia sends sync/cancel events to mark key events when unfocused.
comment_summary: Multiple users confirmed the bug causes modifier keys (Shift, Cmd, Ctrl) to remain stuck as pressed after the app loses and regains focus, breaking shortcuts and multi-select interactions. One user noted that modifier state should also be reported on mouse events (like HTML MouseEvent) so key state is accurate even when the app is not focused. Issue was downgraded from P1 to P2 due to no active work. A web-specific follow-up issue #162977 was filed. Affects Windows, Linux, macOS, and web.

title: [Android]WidgetsBindingObserver.didChangeAppLifecycleState is triggered by selecting an Autofill option
body:
## Issue When I select an autofill option from the keyboard in Android (see screenshot), `WidgetsBindingObserver.didChangeAppLifecycleState` is triggered. The application state shouldn't change because an input has been inserted into a TextField.
Screenshot
Code sample - [Download the sample app](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/files/8168727/textfield_bug.zip). This is just a `flutter create my_app` listening to WidgetsBindingObserver. - Run with a Android device/emulator with Autofill activated (on Android: Settings -> Password & accounts -> Autofill service) - Tap on the TextField and select a candidate (see 1st screenshot) - `WidgetsBindingObserver.didChangeAppLifecycleState()` is triggered (a print is done on the console in `_MyHomePag ...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm Android-only reproduction — iOS does not trigger the lifecycle state change. The lifecycle transitions observed are `inactive` then `resumed`, triggered by the Android autofill service gaining and releasing focus when the user selects an autofill suggestion. Issue affects apps that use `didChangeAppLifecycleState` for session management, causing unintended logouts. No fix or PR mentioned; issue remains open.

          return Column(
            mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
            children: [
              TextField(
                textCapitalization: capitalization,
                decoration: InputDecoration(
                  labelText: '$capitalization',

...[truncated]
comment_summary: A comment from @jonahwilliams identifies the root cause: `textCapitalization` is used when creating the native text field; it may not be possible to update it without tearing down and recreating the field. Both Android and iOS engine references were provided.

title: [Windows][Desktop]: textfield caret position is not consistent when traversing Chinese text
body:
Below problems occurred on windows 10 desktop version.
- put a long Chinese article which consists of multiple paragraphs into a textfield, and try to move caret by using keyboard. And soon you will find some weird caret position problems. E.g. the caret positions will suddenly go to unexpected positions etc. This might be relevant to the next problem.

- If mouse is used, soemtimes you just cannot click into the blank line in between the paragraphs. Seems if the newline is pasted from outside or read from db then it cannnot be clicked into. If the newline is originally made in textfield, no problem.

- When you try to select a specific piece of text segment by mouse, the caret will sometimes quickly move over the selected segment leaving nothing selected.

- When I copied a article from a text editor (like emeditor) to textfield in flutter, all the characters become blocks. Then If I selected carefully from the very first character to the last and then paste into textfield, the
...[truncated]
comment_summary:
Root cause identified in comments: the caret and selection issues with Chinese text on Windows are caused by `
` line endings — replacing them with `
` resolves the caret problem and the blank-line click issue. Pasting text with special/non-standard characters renders them as blocks. Confirmed not reproducible on macOS. Status: stalled, no PR; workaround is to normalize line endings to `
`.

title: Autofocus should delay request of focus until the material page is at the forefront
comment_summary: Comments identify the scenario: a page not at the forefront has an autofocus TextField, and when the underlying page rebuilds and re-adds a TextField with autofocus, it steals focus from the top page. Proposed workaround: wrap background pages with ExcludeFocus. Also reproduces inside a PageView when multiple pages have autofocus TextFields. @gspencergoog was CC'd. Status: open, stalled.

title: Ability to change the keyboard language programmatically
body:
I have a page with multiple textfields and some of the textfields require the user to change the current language from arabic to english and vice versa to insert the proper input 

So I have searched and figured out this feature is not supported yet
comment_summary: Comments confirm Android has a native API (EditorInfo.hintLocales, API 24+) but iOS has no public API. A detailed feasibility analysis concludes a plugin alone is insufficient because the language hint must be set at the engine level (TextInputPlugin on Android, FlutterTextInputPlugin on iOS). Windows has been solved via FFI by one commenter. Issue remains open with no PR.

use `` flutter create --sample=material.TextField.2 mysample``

## Doctor 
```txt
$ flutter doctor
Flutter assets will be downloaded from https://storage.flutter-io.cn. Make sure you trust this source!
Doctor summary (to see all details, run flutter doctor -v):
[√] Flutter (Channel stable, 2.10.3, on Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.17763.2565], locale zh-CN)
[!] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The bug is confirmed on Windows with Microsoft Pinyin/Japanese IME: pressing Right Shift (or Left Shift in some configurations) triggers a `KeyUpEvent` assertion because the IME absorbs the key-down and only emits the key-up, causing `_pressedKeys` to have no record of the physical key. Multiple commenters confirm it affects Windows 10 (pre-19042 and later) and Windows 11 with Microsoft IMEs but not Sougou IME or Windows Server 2022. One user reports it was introduced in Flutter 3.3 and not present in 3.0.5, and that the Flutter 3.4 beta appeared fixed. The issue is referenced alongside #99465. Currently stalled with no merged fix.

title: [Desktop] Multimedia key events not reported to HardwareKeyboard
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue extends beyond macOS to Flutter Web (reproduced on 3.22.3), where media keys (play/pause) are not detected by `HardwareKeyboard`. No fix, PR, or workaround is mentioned. Status is open and stalled.

title: [Web][iOS]: Text field selection with `obscuredText: true` has grey selection
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Use a browser on any iOS device and navigate to https://www.flutterfolio.com or https://app.novospace.com.
2. Type something in the password field.
3. Double-tap the password text to select it.

**Expected results:**
Only the written text should be selected and nothing else.

**Actual results:** 
There is a weird visual grey-highlighted layer in addition to the text selection layer.

Note: This doesn't reproduce on android emulators but I did not test on a real device. 

<details>
<summary>Screenshots</summary>

|flutter folio|my project|
|---|---|
|![IMG_8851](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/41455764/157713014-7fa94310-7e7e-4899-8297-5624cb9a7956.PNG)|![IMG_8852](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/41455764/157713030-706d6c30-0d23-4d8c-a18f-de4d5a20492e.PNG)
|

</details>
comment_summary: Root cause partially identified: iOS Safari ignores the ::selection CSS pseudo-element used to style selection highlights. The selection background color is linked to the caret-color property (WebKit takes caret-color's alpha and uses it for selection background, making it grey when caret-color is transparent). The assigned issue has had no active updates; team notes a larger proposal (#120613) would address it. Workaround: not available without engine changes.

title: [IOS] When the flutter creates the flutterviewcontroller through initwithengine, the external keyboard does not respond
body:
【IOS】When the flutter creates the flutterviewcontroller through initwithengine, the external keyboard does not respond. We found that the flutterviewcontroller is created through initwithengine ， _engineneedslaunch is yes, addinternalplugins will not be called, resulting in the keyboardmanager being nil, and the response chain will no longer pass events down.
flutter version:2.5.0
comment_summary: Confirmed: when FlutterViewController is created via initWithEngine, _engineNeedsLaunch is NO so addInternalPlugins is not called, leaving keyboardManager nil and breaking external keyboard input. Soft keyboard works but physical/Bluetooth keyboard does not. Team requested a minimal reproducible sample.

title: [IOS] Long press delete key on physical keyboard does not delete textfield characters in continuation
body: 【IOS】Long press the button and the external keyboard does not respond to continuous input
comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: Flutter only processes a key event on press+release, so long-pressing delete on a physical keyboard on iOS does not continuously delete (unlike native iOS). Confirmed reproduced on stable and master. CC'd to @justinmc; question raised about whether desktop platforms handle key repeat but mobile does not. Status: stalled, no fix or PR.

title: The DefaultTextEditingShortcuts implementation should be reusable
body:
Currently, [DefaultTextEditingShortcuts](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/default_text_editing_shortcuts.dart) handles dozens of text editing shortcuts for EditableText, from complex stuff like undo/redo all the way down to simple things like the delete/backspace key.  The implementation that handles these text operations is hidden privately [in EditableText](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/95276ccc8265e1eb0d29772f932cb3d5201779cd/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/editable_text.dart#L3204).  We should expose that logic somehow so that it can be reused by developers.

### Why

Any user that is implementing their own text editor that is not based on EditableText currently has to reimplement all of this behavior on their own.  This is a huge burden and likely to result in bugs and platform inconsistencies.  With the recent completion of the [TextEditingDeltas project](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/87972), we are encoura
...[truncated]

title: Flutter throws an exception when typing something while holding selection endpoint
comment_summary: Root cause is a race condition on Android: when a user types while holding a text selection handle, the framework sends an out-of-bounds selection range to the engine's `TextInputChannel.TextEditState` constructor, throwing `IndexOutOfBoundsException`. iOS is also affected with an `!_isDraggingEndHandle` assertion failure in `SelectionOverlay._handleEndHandleDragStart`. The crash is confirmed present in Flutter 3.10.7, 3.22.0, and is hitting production users with ~1.7k occurrences per 30 days reported. No fix or PR is mentioned; issue is assigned to `team-android` and stalled.

title: [web on macOS] "A KeyUpEvent is dispatched, but the state shows that the physical key is pressed on a different logical key" after using Cmd+digit then Option+digit
body:
Seeing this on latest `master` today. There are a few other open issues with this exception, although it's not clear if they're the same problem (this is web on macOS). Below is a simple app that just prints when 1, ctrl+1, shift+1, option+1, cmd+1 are pressed. The following actions trigger the exception when running in Chrome:

- Load the app
- Press `1` and ensure `1` is printed to the dev console (if not, try pressing `<tab>` and then try again)
- Press cmd+1 (`cmd+1` should be printed)
- Press option+1

It doesn't happen if you don't press `cmd+1` before `option+1`, or if you wait a while (eg. > 1sec) after pressing `cmd+1` before pressing `option+1`.

```
Error: Assertion failed:
_pressedKeys[event.physicalKey] == event.logicalKey
"A KeyUpEvent is dispatched, but the state shows that the physical key is pressed on a different logical key. If this occurs in real application, please report this bug to Flutter. If this occurs in unit tests, please ensure that simulated ev
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug is reproducible on both stable (2.10.3) and master on macOS web (Chrome). The root cause is that macOS remaps the logical key for modifier+digit combinations (e.g., cmd+1 produces `¡` for option+1 release), so Flutter's `_pressedKeys` table records the wrong logical key and the assertion fires on key-up. Additional commenters report the same assertion with Korean IME (space/shift trigger it), Windows Emoji picker (web on Windows), and Apple Magic Keyboard with no special key combos. One commenter notes the exception renders the keyboard stuck. No fix or PR mentioned; issue is stalled and open.

title: [Keyboard] Analyze keyboard layout and enforce mandatory logical keys
body:
We should change the algorithm of deriving logical keys on Web, macOS, and Linus, so that they're more intuitive to use.

This aims to resolve the following problems:
- Shift-Digit keys are broken. For example, physical `Shift-Digit1` produces logical `ShiftLeft + exclamation`. Related:
  - https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/90167
  - https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/96022
- Digit keys are broken on French layout. For example, physical `Digit1` produces logical `ampersand`.
- Letter keys are broken on Russian layout. For example, physical `keyA` produces logical `ф`. Related:
  - https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/59065

Although it is still technically possible to define shortcuts for such scenarios, doing so requires platform- and language- dependent code, which is both unintuitive and cumbersome.

The goal can be demonstrated by the following table:

| When pressing physical keys...| on layout | Previous logical keys | New logical keys |
|---|--
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Only a bot comment about an irrelevant label; the body describes completed work (macOS, GTK, Web, Android done; iOS remaining).

title: Poor app performance with multiple TextFields inside a Table
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on Android and web (both canvaskit and HTML renderers), but not on iOS or macOS. The HTML renderer performs worst. Performance traces were shared. No root cause identified in comments. Status: stalled, no fix or PR mentioned.

title: MacOS text input plugin should be properly incorporated into the view hierarchy and the responder chain
body:
Today the macOS text input plugin is a subclass of NSTextView which must be in the view hierarchy for it to properly interact with the OS input system.

Moreover the macOS text input plugin calls multiple API that are only supposed to be invoked by the system (`-[NSTextInputContext active]`, `-[NSTextInputContext deactive]`, `-[NSResponder becomeFirstResponder]`, etc.). Setting up the view hierarchy could potentially remove these incorrect API usages.

This issue covers the long-term strategic fix for https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/100272, which was fixed by a short-term workaround.
comment_summary: One irrelevant comment about image picker rotation; no substantive new information.

title: [add-to-app][Android]: App doesn't rebuild widgets if keyboard is opened on older android versions
body:
## Description
If you add a flutter fragment to an existing app, the behavior after the keyboard was opened differs between android versions (8/9 vs. 12). While Android 12 rebuilds the widgets when the keyboard is opened, Android 9 doesn't. Behavior clearly observable by watching the `print` statement of the `viewInset.bottom`: Shows different values when keyboard is opening on Android 12, while showing nothing on Android 9.

This leads to the problem, that the lower text fields will not scroll into view on android 9, because they are still hidden behind the keyboard.

Android 8
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31889204/159942386-3a97005f-7f3c-4a75-8261-69655498957c.mp4
Android 12

title: [iOS][iPad]: Tapping on textfield couple of times stops opening the system keyboard.
comment_summary: The bug is iPad-specific (not iPhone) and related to Scribble handwriting recognition. A commenter identified that commenting out engine-side Scribble lines in FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm fixes the issue. The bug worsens over time: after triggering, all widgets on the screen become unresponsive, the layout distorts, and the app requires a full relaunch. Still reproducible 3 years after filing with no fix.

title: Text Editing Gestures/Intents/Actions
body:
Currently, gestures related to text editing are handled by `TextSelectionGestureDetectorBuilder`, with no way to override these gestures practically without using `IgnorePointer` which blocks all default gestures defined on the text editing widget. The goal here is to build something analogous to the current keyboard shortcuts -> intents -> action system, with defaults that can be practically overwritten by the user. 

☔️ To complete this issue we need:
  - [ ] All pointer-related text editing interactions ported to Intents/Actions, like tapping, long pressing, etc. #75004
  - [x] Support selection all text on triple click #63576
  - [x] Support double tap drag gesture to select a block of text on iOS #64550
  - [x] Single tap on text field to display toolbar #48434
  - [x] Hide selection toolbar when moving caret/handles #62193
  - [ ] Selection toolbar/handle should disappear when clicking outside of it #72029
  - [x] Textfield context menu does not show after focus switch #
...[truncated]

title: [Android]Avoid clipboard reading permissions on XiaoMi MIUI 13
body: I'm developing an app that doesn't really need the clipboard permission, but I can't get rid of the system alert of clipboard reading. ## Ways I have tried to fix the problem - switch to master channel - run `flutter upgrade` - set `enableInteractiveSelection` flag to `false` in TextField widget Nothing has yet worked on my device nor m ...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirmed the clipboard-reading alert is reproducible on Xiaomi MIUI 12.5 (public dev version) and affects Flutter 3.16.6, with the stack trace pointing to `getPrimaryClipDescription` inside `PlatformPlugin.clipboardHasStrings` being called dozens of times per second. The root cause is that even though `hasPrimaryClip`/`getPrimaryClipDescription` was implemented (engine #27215, framework #87678) to avoid reading clipboard contents, some Xiaomi/MIUI devices still trigger the security alert for those calls. A workaround was proposed (always return `ClipboardStatus.pasteable`) and a PR was filed (#179840) to reduce the loop calls from `didUpdateWidget`. The team discussed whether to always show the Paste button on Android as an alternative but no fix has landed.

title: [Web]Composing Candidates has incorrect vertical offset from the focused Textfield
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on web only (not macOS desktop) after removing focus from the field and refocusing in the middle of existing text. The wrong offset is used for the composing candidates box after refocus. No root cause or fix identified in comments. Currently stalled.

title: Error: "Attempted to send a key down event when no keys are in keysPressed" on Pressing Select or Enter on a TV remote
body:
I'm using **onKey** and **onKeyEvent** to listen to tv remote control keys
but I have this error when press **SELECT** or **ENTER**

```
Attempted to send a key down event when no keys are in keysPressed. This state can occur if the key event being sent doesn't properly set its modifier flags. This was the event: RawKeyDownEvent#23bc9(logicalKey: LogicalKeyboardKey#0050c(keyId: "0x10000050c", keyLabel: "Select", debugName: "Select"), physicalKey: PhysicalKeyboardKey#700e4(usbHidUsage: "0x000700e4", debugName: "Control Right"), repeat: false) and its data: RawKeyEventDataAndroid#92581(flags: 8, codePoint: 0, plainCodePoint: 0, keyCode: 23, scanCode: 97, metaState: 0)
'package:flutter/src/services/raw_keyboard.dart':
package:flutter/…/services/raw_keyboard.dart:1
Failed assertion: line 685 pos 7: 'event is! RawKeyDownEvent || _keysPressed.isNotEmpty'`
```
When I use **RawKeyboardListener** I have same issue but it call the method **onKey**


**Flutter Doctor**
Doctor summary
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The root cause is that Android TV and IR remote devices send the Select key mapped to the physical key `Control Right` without setting modifier flags, violating Flutter's assertion in `raw_keyboard.dart` that requires keysPressed to be non-empty before a key-down event. The issue is confirmed on Android TV devices (TX 3 mini, Linux devices with custom IR remotes), Windows with USB barcode scanners (Shift Left key triggers same assert), and Android on Linux. A workaround via a linked comment on issue #65233 involves using the `onKey` method on FocusNode instead. Currently stalled with no fix landed.

title: [Proposal] Expose `padding` for multi-line `TextField`
body:
## Use case

I have a Flutter desktop app with a full bleed `TextField` with infinite `maxLine` contraint. The text comes too close to the edges of the window, and I'd like to pad the content. 

I can pad the `TextField` on the left and right with a `Padding` widget, but I can't pad the top and bottom without creating an unsightly top and bottom gap during text scroll.

## Proposal

Please expose something akin to [`BoxScrollView`'s `padding`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/BoxScrollView/padding.html) property to multi-line `TextField`.
comment_summary: Discussion clarified that contentPadding in InputDecoration already exists but shows padding during scroll (not the desired behavior for full-bleed multi-line fields). The proposal is for a proper scroll-aware content padding similar to BoxScrollView.padding. The issue was assigned to and then abandoned by @pedromassango; @justinmc also expressed interest. Currently unassigned and stalled.

title: Unable to focus TextField widget after scrolling
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on iPad (not iPhone) on stable and master channels; the issue is specific to scrolling vertically on a focused TextField when keyboardDismissBehavior is set to ScrollViewKeyboardDismissBehavior.onDrag. A commenter identified the likely cause as a pointer event not being released, leaving the engine believing two active pointers exist and causing the scroll/gesture system to lock. Two PRs partially fixed the issue but it remained reproducible on iPad simulator after they landed. Still reproducible as of stable 3.19.5 and master; assignee was eventually removed after no status updates.

title: Add integration tests for IME input
body:
Our text input integration testing doesn't cover IME-based input such as for Chinese, Japanese, and Korean. At the moment, this is covered by non-platform-specific unit tests.

We should include tests that:
1. Set the input method to an IME-based input method.
2. Send a sequence of key events such that are handled by the IME.
3. Verify the text editing contents, in particular, the composing region.

A few things to test:
* Ensure arrow cursor movement is restricted to the composing region when composing (in Chinese/Japanese)
* Test composing in Flutter, then switching to a non-Flutter textfield, composing there, and switching back to flutter. See https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/31879.
* Ensure backspace handling is correct while composing.
* Ensure Escape key handling is correct.
* Verify keyboard navigation of candidates menu is possible (and not captured by Flutter).
comment_summary: Discussion surfaces ideas including writing a custom IME with a testing interface, testing composing across focus switches, backspace/Escape behavior, and candidate menu navigation. @LongCatIsLooong suggested a custom IME approach since third-party IMEs like Samsung Korean behave unpredictably.

Video of the issue comparing behavior on iOS Safari, OSX Safari, and OSX Chrome:
- Inserting characte
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Issue confirmed reproducible on iOS web (Safari and Chrome, which share the same engine on iOS) on stable 2.10.5 and master 2.13. Root cause noted as `DefaultTextEditingStrategy.handleChange` responding to a DOM event that resets cursor position when `readOnly=true`. A comment notes the behavior is inconsistent with native web `readonly` inputs. Assignee was eventually unassigned after no status updates. Issue is still open and stalled.

title: Reliable initialization of keyboard and pointer bindings
body:
We should review the messaging mechanism of various bindings (keyboard and pointer) and make them reliable. The current process, where the embedding blindly sends events to the framework, might cause message loss at initialization and state instability, especially during hot restarts.

## Analysis

Take keyboard as an example. Currently, when the app is started,
1. The Flutter view creates both a keyboard manager and a dart VM.
2. The keyboard manager immediately starts listening to system key events and send messages through the embedder API, `FlutterSendKeyEvent`.
3. It takes a short while for the dart VM to boot and run through all static statements, such as assigning a listener to the framework API `onKeyData`.
4. If a key event happens between the creation of the keyboard manager and the initialization of the framework, since the listener is still null, the message is lost.
 
Since the keyboard system only sends events, not the entire state, packet losses are critical an
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments debate whether to use unbounded message buffers, send full state instead of deltas, or add a framework-initiated synchronization message. @stuartmorgan argues for keeping APIs in embedder.h for ABI stability (the engine is shipped as a DLL). The discussion is architectural; no concrete fix PR is linked and the status is stalled.

title: [Windows][Accessibility]: Text field input is read inconsistently while adding, deleting and while using shift / arrow keys.
body:
Text fields are basically unusable, and it is often to write text in some other program, then paste it into Flutter apps.

I could have sworn there was already an issue for this, but I am unable to find it, so sorry if this is a duplicate.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create an app with the below code.
2. Run `flutter run -d windows`.
3. With a screen reader (such as [NVDA](https://www.nvaccess.org/download)), enter some text, navigate through the text with the arrow keys, try and find an arbitrary point to add more text, ETC.
## Problems

- Moving through the text field with the arrow keys (or home and end) speaks nothing (#95556).
- When typing new characters, the text of the field is spoken in its entirety.
- When deleting text with the backspace or delete keys, the text is spoken in its entirety.
- When selecting text with shift and the arrow keys, nothing is spoken.

## Logs

<details>
<summary>Logs</summary>

```
[  +44 ms] executing: [C:\Users\chris\flutter/]
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on Windows stable (2.10.5) and master by a triager. A comment pinpoints the root cause to the `accessibility_bridge_delegate_windows.cc` ignoring `DOCUMENT_SELECTION_CHANGED` events and suggests `EVENT_OBJECT_TEXTSELECTIONCHANGED` as the correct Windows event. NVDA is identified as the correct screen reader for testing; Narrator is misleading. One commenter notes NVDA still reads entire field contents on every change as of 2023.1. The issue has been open for over 3 years with no fix; Windows team added it to Q4 planning but no PR has landed and the issue is stalled.

title: EditableTextState should provide deltas to use with updateEditingValueWithDeltas when the user cuts or copy/pastes text
body:
## Use case

I'm currently experimenting with `DeltaTextInputClient` and it works wonderfully for my use case (bridging Quill's Delta format with Flutter's Editable). However, I ran into some issues during development:

- Pasting text does not trigger delta updates, therefore the `DeltaTextInputClient` has no idea that new text has been added
- Same for any action that uses `userUpdateTextEditingValue`, including cutting

Currently, as a workaround for cutting text, I've copied and pasted the source file for `EditableText` and made the method `EditableTextState._replaceText()` public, and in my `DeltaTextInputClient` I override that method and construct the delta:

```dart
@override
void replaceText(ReplaceTextIntent intent) {
  final delta = TextEditingDeltaReplacement(
    oldText: intent.currentTextEditingValue.text,
    replacementText: intent.replacementText,
    replacedRange: intent.replacementRange,
    selection: intent.currentTextEditingValue.selection,
    c
...[truncated]
comment_summary: A commenter (likely @Renzo-Olivares) acknowledges the request, agrees delta support for cut/paste actions is desirable, and links to related #100260 and a PR #104198 exploring a solution. Status: acknowledged, pending further work on delta text input.

title: [Android] After focusing on a text field, no printable key events can be received ever
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue also reproduces on Zebra TC52 and HP Chromebook x360 (Flutter 3.10.5), not only ARC++. One commenter notes that switching to another app and back temporarily restores key dispatch until the next TextField focus. The root cause is that the IME intercepts printable key events after a TextField is focused and does not release that interception, so `FlutterView.dispatchKeyEvent` is never called for those keys. No fix or PR. Stalled.

title: [Web]: TextField `undo` option from selection bar is not available after the TextField loses focus
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Execute `flutter run -d chrome --web-renderer html` on the code sample <!-- (see "Code sample" section below) -->
2. Delete some text from the TextField. At this point, one can undo changes.
3. Make another change in the TextField, and then click on the page outside the TextField, so that it loses focus.
4. Click back into the TextField. Undo is not available any more.

**Expected results:**
I expect to be able to undo changes to the TextField regardless of whether it lost focus or not.

**Actual results:**
One can undo changes as long as focus hasn't been lost, but not once focus has left the TextField.

**Notes:**
This only happens when built for web; desktop is fine. Inspecting #shadow-root, I suspect the HTML renderer is recreating the `<input>` element (or one of its ancestors) when the TextField receives focus, thereby losing the undo history from the original `<input>`.

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'packa
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments clarify that the issue affects both HTML and CanvasKit renderers on web, not just HTML. The root cause identified by comments is that the web engine creates a new `<input>` element in the shadow DOM each time a `TextField` loses focus, discarding the browser's native undo history. A proposed fix from the engine team (@yjbanov) is to cache `<input>` elements and reuse them rather than recreating them. Status is stalled with no linked PR.

When handwriting in a TextField using Apple Pencil, the first letter is always entered in uppercase. Is there a way to input the first letter in lowercase?
Even if I turn off auto-capitalization option in settings, it still type in uppercase.
Tested on iPad 12.9inch 4 and iOS 15.4.1 version.

comment_summary: Confirmed on iPad Air 5 (iPadOS 17.5.1) with Flutter beta 3.27 and stable 3.22.3: Apple Pencil Scribble always capitalizes the first letter regardless of the case drawn. CC'd to @LongCatIsLooong; no fix or PR mentioned.

title: Implementation of `EditableTextState._scheduleShowCaretOnScreen`
comment_summary: The reporter proposed a concrete code fix using `getBoxesForSelection` to scroll to the selection extent rather than the caret rect. A comment noted the fix only covers mouse-drag selection, not selection handle dragging, and outlined further changes needed in `TextSelectionOverlay` and `_TextFieldState._handleSelectionChanged`. PR #102992 was created and merged, fixing the desktop case, but the mobile case was not fixed. Status: partially fixed (desktop), still open for mobile.

title: [Selection] Reimplement SelectableText with SelectionArea
body:
SelectableText is currently a read only EditableText which has several issues, such as the selection is bounded in a single selectable text, and it may be costly in terms of memory usage.

The idea is reimplement SelectableText with SelectionArea + text, but we should make sure they are feature on par before we do that.

several issue with SelectionArea:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/111370
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/111021 （fixed）
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/110594 (won't fix)
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/104703 (fixed)
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/104603 (fixed)
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/104594
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/126652 (fixed)
comment_summary: Comments note all mentioned blocking issues have been fixed. However, programmatic multi-line selection with `selectable: true` is still not possible via `SelectionArea`.

title: [macOS][Desktop]: Entering a compound character crashes Mac app
body:
I'm working on IME support for Super Editor. At the moment, if I try to enter a `ü` in an empty paragraph, the entire Mac app crashes.

It's likely that this problem is the result of something that we're doing in Super Editor, however, I'm thinking that shouldn't be possible to completely crash the app. So something should probably be updated on the Flutter side, too.

The key presses to generate a `ü` are:
* opt+u
* u

Here's a snippet from the Mac crash report that results from this crash:

```
Process:               example [34359]
Path:                  /Volumes/VOLUME/*/example.app/Contents/MacOS/example
Identifier:            com.example.example
Version:               1.0.0 (1)
Code Type:             X86-64 (Native)
Parent Process:        dart [34037]
Responsible:           studio [337]
User ID:               501

Date/Time:             2022-05-24 21:18:59.972 -0700
OS Version:            Mac OS X 10.15.7 (19H2)
Report Version:        12
Anonymous UUID:    
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: super_editor's `currentTextEditingValue` sets an invalid `TextEditingValue` with a composing range outside the text bounds, which the engine does not handle gracefully and instead crashes. PR #104711 was created to wrap method call handlers to surface such errors. The framework fix was landed to prevent invalid data from reaching the embedding, and the remaining work is adding bounds-checking in the macOS embedding to prevent crashes from malformed values, tracked as a lower-priority follow-up. Status: framework fix landed, macOS embedding fix still pending.

title: iOS TextField with TextInputType.number and AutofillHints.creditCardNumber shows the wrong keyboard
comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause: when iOS has a creditCardNumber autofill hint combined with a non-default textInputAction (Flutter defaults to UIReturnKeyDone), iOS falls back to the alphanumeric keyboard. Workaround confirmed: setting textInputAction: TextInputAction.unspecified restores the numeric keyboard. An engine PR (flutter/engine#17493) was filed but the issue persists on 3.22.0; status is open with a known workaround.

title: AutofillHints.oneTimeCode not works on flutter web on iOS
comment_summary: The issue is specific to Safari on iOS; Chrome on iOS does work for OTP autofill. A user reported it worked in Flutter 3.13.0 for Safari, but others still report it not working. Status: conflicting reports, appears partially resolved in 3.13.0 but may regress.

title: Clipboard dartdoc should have some hints on how to support it in tests
body:
Using the Clipboard APIs directly in widget tests can result in confusion since the platform channel calls are just dropped on the floor. The dartdoc https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/services/Clipboard-class.html could leave more breadcrumbs on what to do there. 

Such as a hint to consider using MockClipboard from https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/packages/flutter/test/widgets/clipboard_utils.dart and testing it like https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/packages/flutter/test/material/text_form_field_test.dart#L19.
comment_summary: A commenter noted that MockClipboard is not public API, and suggested that providing a public test affordance for clipboard behavior would be a useful addition.

title: Unexpected backspace behavior via IME with composing character
body:
There's an unexpected behavior when pressing `backspace` after a character that's being composed.

Steps:

1. Place a caret in a text field or any other IME text entry widget
2. Press `opt+u` to create a composing character: "¨"
3. Press `backspace`

Expected behavior: the composing character should be gone, the text should be empty: ""

Actual behavior: the composing character stays. It takes a 2nd backspace to delete it: "¨"

The expected behavior can be seen here in a GitHub text entry.
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on macOS stable and master. The issue is that pressing backspace on a composing character (e.g., opt+u diacritic) requires two presses to delete it. Also noted to affect regular Flutter TextField, not just super_editor.

title: [iPad][physical keyboard] Cursor will jump back to 2 characters before the next character appears
comment_summary: Reproduced on stable and master with an iPad + physical keyboard + Japanese IME. Root cause analysis by @LongCatIsLooong: a specific keyboard relies on undocumented `UITextInputController` behavior that temporarily shifts selected text to insert composing text at the string start; Flutter's `-[UITextInput insertText:]` doesn't replicate this hack and the fix could break other text interactions. Status: stalled, engine-level iOS issue.

title: TextField toolbar button text do not match platform iOS and macOS text in Japanese
body: ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Change your locale to Japanese 2. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample 3. Tap text field **Expected results:** for example, it should be `すべてを選択` like iOS native todo app
comment_summary: Root cause identified: Flutter uses Material/Cupertino translations generated via Translation Console rather than Apple-platform-native strings; Android uses '翻訳 切り取り コピー 取り付け' while iOS users expect '翻訳 カット コピー ペースト'. Fixing Cupertino ARB strings would address iOS/macOS; a separate string set is needed for Windows/Linux/Android since the underlying string is shared. No fix PR linked; stalled.

title: Multiple Rebuild issue on opening a keyboard
body:
Hello, there's an issue with flutter 2.10.5 when opening keyboard and using `responsive_builder`. It will call `build` and `didChangeDependencies` multiple times. This is not happening in 2.8.1. 

**2.8 video below**:

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19194749/171528794-777f5898-1077-4cad-9fce-fe83235788bc.mov



**2.10.5 video below**

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19194749/171528700-e5351981-514f-43a3-a7e8-e24b06257e6b.mov


You can use this sample demo:
https://github.com/marctan/demo_rebuild
comment_summary: Root cause identified as `MediaQuery.of(context)` triggering a rebuild on every frame during keyboard animation, because MediaQuery emits a change notification per frame as the keyboard slides in/out. This was introduced as a regression relative to Flutter 2.8 — on iOS, rebuilds went from 2 times (2.8) to 20+ times (3.x). PR #97928 was cited as the intended fix, and PR #114459 (Callum's) was mentioned as a follow-up solution. Workarounds include using `MediaQuery.sizeOf(context)` instead of `MediaQuery.of(context)`, using a `Builder` widget to scope rebuilds, or using `LayoutBuilder`. Issue persists as of Flutter 3.22.2 with users reporting janks on complex screens.

title: [Web] Refactor TextEditingDelta implementation
body:
The TextEditingDelta implementation on web currently continually updates an `editingDeltaState` during the process of text-input (beforeInput, onInput, compositionupdate), until we have arrived at a final `editingDeltaState`. Having it spread across many handlers can make it difficult to make updates to the `DefaultTextEditingStrategy` without breaking TextEditingDeltas. An example of this is flutter/engine#33590 which adds composing region tracking, but broke TextEditingDeltas at first (later resolved).

Proposal:
Use the lastEditingState and the newEditingState to determine the state of a TextEditingDelta, and centralize the logic into `inferDeltaState`.

title: [Firefox] Composing down to a single character from multiple characters does not replace the composing text until a new event fires.
body:
Composing region on web for firefox using delta model. This issue is very specific and a little hard to pin down.

If the amount of text inputted during composing is more than one character, and it composes down to one character, the text inputting does not replace until the next keypress. Here is a list of "triagable" cases:

- Type 'ni' into the composing keyboard, then select the composition. This will _not_ replace properly, as it is more than one character (ni.length == 2) and composes down into a single symbol.
- Type 'n' into the composing keyboard, then select the composition. This _will_ replace properly, as it is one character composing down into one character.
- Type 'ni ho' into the composing keyboard, then select the composition. This _will_ replace properly, as it is more than one character composing down into more than one character.


## Steps to Reproduce

1. Execute `flutter run` on the delta model sample [found here ](https://github.com/flutter/samples/tre
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: Firefox sends a null composing region in the same message as the final committed text, while Chrome sends two separate updates. This causes composing text (e.g., 'ni') to not be replaced by the composed symbol until the next keypress. Related to engine PR flutter/engine#33590 but confirmed not fixed by it.

title: The isKeyPressed function in RawKeyboardListener behaves differently for korean language
body:
I'm implementing a shortcut, but the `isKeyPressed` function in RawKeyboardListener behaves differently depending on the language of the user's keyboard. I don't know if this is a bug or not, but I want to create a shortcut that behaves the same regardless of the user's language.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample.
2. Connect the Bluetooth keyboard to your iOS or iPad.
3. When entering `E` or `e` in English, it works as `is KeyE pressed = true`.
4. When entering the Korean `ㄷ` corresponding to `E` on the English keyboard, it works as `is KeyE pressed = false`.

**Expected results:**
I would like to receive the same `event.isKeyPressed(LogicalKeyboardKey.keyE)` result as `true` whether I type `ㄷ` on a Korean keyboard or an English keyboard when I type `E`.

The physicalKeyboardKey recognizes the Korean word `ㄷ` as Key E. However, the `isKeyPressed` function returns `false` because it checks whether they are the same using LogicalKeyboardKey int
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: `RawKeyboard` (legacy system) maps Korean characters to different logical keys than their physical key equivalents, so `isKeyPressed(LogicalKeyboardKey.keyE)` returns `false` for Korean input. A maintainer stated no breaking changes will be made to `RawKeyboard` and recommended migrating to `HardwareKeyboard`/`KeyboardListener` and using `Shortcuts`/`SingleActivator`. A follow-up test showed that even with `Shortcuts` and `SingleActivator`, the Korean key `ㄷ` (physical Key E) does not trigger the shortcut; the maintainer acknowledged this as a separate problem to investigate.

title: Clipboard on Android 13: Allow defining copied content as sensitive
body:
Internal: b/242213606

## Use case

Copying data into the clipboard on android 13 will show the user a notification. There is a new API to define clipboard data as sensitive: https://developer.android.com/about/versions/13/behavior-changes-all#copy-sensitive-content

## Proposal

Expand API for `ClipboardData` to define a property `isSensitive` and pass it through to the android APIs.
comment_summary: Comments confirm the proposal is reasonable. The team suggested enabling this by default when `obscureText: true`. A third-party plugin `sensitive_clipboard` was published as a workaround. A related iOS request (#103163) was linked.

title: Child is drawn above a border while animating
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

[Dartpad](https://dartpad.dev/?id=1a26608234fe8f03e025c94649f45a8f)
I know it really isn't visible here ; it is however jumps to the eye on mobile. Here's a [screenshot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5080204/172926821-0cebd5ad-2282-466c-b4dc-084a539cbb9a.png) I took where you can see two white pixels on the right side of the border, which come from the TextField. Screenshot taken as I was spamming the right-side arrow (used to open/close the widget), try it yourself but you'll need to squint your eyes.

I'm mainly here to understand if this is intended behavior from Flutter, and if so, what is the standard way to go about solving this particular issue.

**Expected results:**
The child isn't drawn above the specified border of the container.
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue reproduces on Android and all platforms during animation when widthFactor is between 0 and 1. Suspected root cause is anti-aliasing at clipped boundaries. Team deferred investigation, suggesting the user ask on Stack Overflow. No fix or PR proposed.

title: the selection highlight should expand for the full width of the input.
body: ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample 2. Tap `TextField` and Tap `Select All` **Expected results:** the selection highlight expands for the full width of the input like native app.
comment_summary: Root cause identified: the selection highlight is computed from TextBox rectangles that only cover the actual text width, not the full line width. A commenter investigated using BoxWidthStyle and LineMetrics but found that LineMetrics.width does not include trailing whitespace, making a complete solution require changes to paragraph.cc in the engine. A proof-of-concept code snippet was posted but noted as imperfect. No PR proposed; issue stalled.

title: Support gamepad on Web
body:
Gamepad on Web uses the special [gamepad API](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Gamepad_API/Using_the_Gamepad_API). We should support it. 

Also, Web supports multiple gamepads. Is keyboard the best way to support them?

title: Flutter TextFields on Chrome and Edge (but not Windows or Firefox) receive multiple onChange events from Japanese input
body:
When programatically shifting focus between TextFields as part of an TextField's onChange event triggered by Japanese IME input, the change event seems to be incorrectly received by multiple TextFields. The behaviour is not consistent between Windows and Firefox on one side, and Chrome and Edge on the other.  The behaviour can also be avoided by forcing a delay in the input handling code, indicating a possible race.

## Steps to Reproduce
A simplified application has two TextFields, "left" and "right". Each has an onChanged handler. Left's onChange handler prints the received text to the console, and if the input text was "t" or "れ", gives focus to right.  Right's onChange handler prints the received text to the console.

DartPad: https://dartpad.dev/?id=484f57a4221afe3ffced3614a23f1316

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
1 - I clear both fields and paste "t" into the left field. Focus shifts to the right field, the left field shows "t":
![image](https://
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm reproduction on stable and master channels, with Chrome and Edge affected but not Windows native or Firefox. The issue is that TextField's onChange event triggered by Japanese IME input is incorrectly received by the wrong FocusNode after a programmatic focus shift. No fix or PR has been proposed; status is open and triaged.

title: App crashes and disappears when typing on the keyboard

comment_summary: Comments identify the crash as occasional and similar to #97789 (also a `KeyboardKeyEmbedderHandler` crash). Suspected trigger is QQ Chinese IME on Windows. Regression introduced between versions 2.5.3 and 2.10.5.

title: [Windows] Keyboard shortcuts stop working after modifier key repeat
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample
2. Type some text with spaces, press Ctrl+Z to undo (works OK)
3. Press any modifier key (e.g. Ctrl) or a combination of modifier keys (e.g. Shift + Win) and hold for a short while
4. Press arrow keys to move around text, Ctrl+Z again to undo more edits and other shortcuts

**Expected results:** Keyboard shortcuts work
**Actual results:** Shortcuts stop working and the following errors are shown in debug mode:

<details>
<summary>Errors</summary>

```
Launching lib\main.dart on Windows in debug mode...
Connecting to VM Service at ws://127.0.0.1:64705/23jNOxPBevw=/ws
Unable to parse JSON message:
The document is empty.

════════ Exception caught by services library ══════════════════════════════════
The following assertion was thrown during a platform message callback:
A KeyRepeatEvent is dispatched, but the state shows that the physical key is not pressed. If this occurs in real application, please 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirmed the issue occurs on Windows when a modifier key is held long enough to generate repeat events. The root cause was traced by a commenter: the engine sends a synthesized key-up before a key-repeat event (due to Windows IME interaction), so the framework receives a key-repeat for a key it believes is not pressed, hitting the `_pressedKeys.containsKey` assertion in `HardwareKeyboard._assertEventIsRegular`. After the assertion fires in debug mode, `_keyEventsSinceLastMessage` is not cleared, permanently breaking subsequent shortcut handling until app restart. The bug only manifests in debug builds (the assertion is stripped in release). No fix has been filed.

title: TextField cursor jumps to beginning when the textfield contains`\r\n\r\n`
body:
我当前的Flutter版本为3.0.3

_translated using Google Translate_ 

My current Flutter version is 3.0.3

![VeryCapture_20220624230911](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/30982989/175564379-aeaa3e67-23f4-40bf-9af0-8460e64360c0.gif)
comment_summary:
Root cause identified: the bug is specific to text containing 

 (Windows-style double newlines), not 

. The cursor jumps to the beginning when clicking or arrowing onto the blank line between the 
 pairs, likely due to incorrect caret position calculation in TextPainter. Confirmed on Android and Linux. No fix or PR has been proposed.

title: Cursor disappears after TextField receives focus and executes setState.
comment_summary: Root cause identified by @LongCatIsLooong: Container color changing from null to non-null restructures the widget tree (inserts ColoredBox), causing TextField to be removed and re-inflated; the new EditableText does not handle being given an already-focused FocusNode in initState. Workarounds: replace null color with Colors.transparent, use GlobalKey on TextField, use ColoredBox instead of Container. Assigned to @xu-baolin who stated intent to fix but made no progress; unassigned. No fix PR.

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample <!-- (see "Code sample" section below) -->
2. Click on the text field on the home screen. <!-- describe steps to demonstrate bug -->
3. See keyboard up and down and up again on the second screen<!-- for example "Tap on X and see a crash" -->

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed on both Android and iOS, though the keyboard bounce is more visible on Android due to faster animation. Root cause per @gspencergoog: the focus system does not recognize that focus will survive the Hero transition, so the keyboard dismisses and re-shows. The active workaround is delaying `focusNode.requestFocus()` by ~350ms to let the Hero animation complete before requesting focus. No PR or official fix exists; issue is stalled.

title: [Keyboard] Support stateful shortcut activators
body:
Currently all shortcut activators are stateless (despite having a "session"). The following features might require stateful activators to implement:
- Long pressing a key.
- Tap a shift with no key events between its down and up.
- Ctrl-A, Ctrl-D.

title: How to share focus with popovers and other sub-tasks
body:
There's a conflict in text-based focus behavior in Flutter and I'm wondering if there's an intended approach to this problem.

Typically, when entering text into something like a form field, the moment focus moves away from the field, the selection is gone. For example, you'll see a caret and type text into a "name" field. Then, focus moves to an "email" field. At this point, there is no caret or selection in the "name" field any longer, and there is a caret in the "email" field. However, this focus behavior is not universal.

When composing text, there are a number of occasions in which some kind of popover might appear. For example, a popover to select text styles, enter a URL for a link, or select a compound character. In these situations, the popover includes focusable elements, and the user should be able to move focus around those elements. But that focus change should **not** impact the selection within the text field. Additionally, the moment the popover disappears, focus s
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Discussion confirms Flutter has no built-in mechanism for shared/retained focus across overlay widgets whose focus nodes cannot be placed as descendants of the editor's focus node in the widget tree. @LongCatIsLooong explains `hasFocus` is true as long as a descendant has focus, but overlays break that hierarchy. @gspencergoog confirms there is no focus-stack concept and manual `FocusNode` parenting outside `Focus` widgets is fragile. PR #113655 added optional `parentNode` to `Focus`/`FocusScope`, but subsequent comments show `DropdownButton` still steals focus because it forces its own route. Status: partial fix landed in #113655; `DropdownButton` in overlays still broken; stalled.

title: [Android] Autofillhints keeps showing on other apps keyboard
body: When you change from a flutter app to another one on Android, the autofill hints of the focused inputs keeps showing on the other app: ## Steps to Reproduce I implemented a basic form that contains one text input with AutofillHints.email and opened it on a physical Motorola G20 with Android 11 in debug mode: Tap on input: Switch to another app and try to use the keyboard, in this case, I switched to Telegram: The hints show as if the text input was still on the screen (the same happens if AutofillHints.password is used). If you click on the hint, the apps automatically switch to the flutter app and a ...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed Android-only (iOS does not reproduce). The bug requires the autofill service to already have stored credentials; commenters struggled to reproduce because they lacked pre-saved autofill data. A Samsung Galaxy S23 Ultra tester could not reproduce the autofill hint display at all, suggesting device/autofill-service-specific behavior. No fix or PR mentioned; issue remains open.

title: [Flutter Web] The keyboard animation is abnormal when the Web closes the keyboard on Android
comment_summary: Reproduced on stable and master on iOS (physical device iPhone 7) and Android (Chrome). The issue is more pronounced on iOS. No root cause was identified. Status: stalled, no updates from team despite user pings.

title: Code quality improvements for text input deltas
body:
I'm creating an IME simulator in the flutter_test_robots package so that developers can simulate the kind of input that's used in document editing.

I found some code quality issues that made it difficult for me to get the simulator working. I recommend improving these details.

Please see:
 * Where Flutter handles delta JSON in the channel: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/packages/flutter/lib/src/services/text_input.dart#L1803
 * Where Flutter deserializes the delta JSON: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/packages/flutter/lib/src/services/text_editing_delta.dart#L65

Issues:
- **Provide a `toJSON()` for text editing deltas:** I'm sending deltas through the platform channel so that I get maximum correctness in the IME simulation. `TextEditingDelta` has a `fromJSON()` method, but no `toJSON()` method. Moreover, the `fromJSON()` method is very complicated. I found it impossible to read. So I made a guess about how I'm supposed to map values in the JSO
...[truncated]
comment_summary: A commenter (@Renzo-Olivares) acknowledged the issues and committed to adding toJSON(), cleaning up fromJSON(), addressing silent failures via #107426, and clarifying composing/selection terminology. The public constant naming proposal was accepted by @justinmc. The issue was later unassigned from @Renzo-Olivares with no status update.

title: How do we simulate text editing deltas?
body:
I'm working on an IME simulator so that developers can test against delta-based text editing in widget tests.

I think we may be missing an important tool to make this happen. Please let me know if I'm missing something.

To send a delta, we must send the current text, called `oldText`. We also need to know the current selection to determine what some of the delta values should be. How do we obtain this information so that we can assemble a delta?

**Options:**

1. Implement an entire IME in Dart. This is a huge undertaking and I don't think it should be necessary.
2. Wrap the existing channel handler with a custom handler and intercept the messages about selection and text. I don't see any way to do this because I don't see how to get a reference to the current channel handler, which means I can't wrap the current channel handler with my own decorator.
3. Use a `Finder` to locate a widget that implements `DeltaTextInputClient` and query the information. This approach works. 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the limitation remains after 3 years. The only workaround requires implementing `DeltaTextInputClient` directly with significant boilerplate. No public API exposes the current TextInput connection's client state to test tooling.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample <!-- (see "Code sample" section below) -->
2. Apple pencil sliding input results cannot be absorbed into the input box.

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3472623/177976869-b9d8b894-c27b-4584-b1ff-f90df9d08c77.mp4

comment_summary: Confirmed on master 3.1.0 with an iPad and Apple Pencil. The webview_flutter plugin does not support Apple Pencil (Scribble/handwriting) input on iOS. A commenter also reported that flutter_inappwebview's InAppBrowser works with the pencil but InAppWebView does not. No fix or PR has landed; issue remains open and stalled.

title: TapDown in a SelectionArea causes text field to lose focus
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue is only on web and desktop (macOS, Windows, Linux); Android and iOS are not affected. The root cause is that SelectionArea requests focus on tap-down, briefly stealing focus from the child TextField before restoring it on tap-up. @chunhtai suggests requesting focus only after a drag starts. No fix or PR merged; status is open.

title: [Form] `AutovalidateMode.onUserInteraction` validates Textfields without interaction
comment_summary: Comments clarify that `AutovalidateMode.onUserInteraction` on a `Form` validates all fields on any interaction with any field, which is the original design (confirmed by the PR #59766 test). A commenter proposes a new mode `AutovalidateMode.singleOnUserInteractionForm`, but ultimately the decision was to change the behavior of the existing mode to only validate the interacted field. A partial patch exists in PR #120830; a follow-up fix was planned pending PR #180822. Status: has partial patch, waiting for follow-up PR.

title: WidgetSpan wrong caret position
comment_summary: Root cause identified: `WidgetSpan` is treated as a single character by the engine text APIs, but the `TextEditingController` stores the multi-character source string, causing a position mismatch. The framework PR #80185 attempted a 'replacements' API but never landed; #83537 also touched this area. Workaround confirmed: insert zero-width-space (`​`) placeholder `TextSpan`s for each additional character the widget replaces so positions align. Team states this is not on the near-term roadmap.

title: The autofill dialog can appear in the wrong place
comment_summary: Root cause identified as Gboard version-specific behavior: reproducible only with Gboard 9.4, not with Gboard 8.0. Since Gboard 9.8 (released ~2020) no longer shows a floating autofill dialog at all, the issue is moot on modern Gboard versions. iOS was noted to always show autofill suggestions in a fixed position on the keyboard. Status unclear; question raised about whether it still warrants a fix given obsolete Gboard versions.

title: TextInputClient/DeltaTextInputClient has different behaviors on MacOS and Windows
body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation

      * https://flutter.dev/

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->
On macos, `updateEditingValueWithDeltas` was invoked after `TextInputConnection.setEditingState`.
On windows, no `updateEditingValueWithDeltas` invoked after `TextInputConnection.setEditingState`.

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample <!-- (see "Code sample" section below) -->
2. Click text to call  `TextInputConnection.
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm that `TextInputClient` exhibits the same cross-platform inconsistency as `DeltaTextInputClient`. The root cause is a macOS-specific behavior where the platform sends `updateEditingValueWithDeltas` callbacks in response to `setEditingState`, while Windows does not. One commenter suggested the Windows behavior is more reasonable. No fix or PR was proposed. Issue appears stalled.

title: Missing default context menu buttons on each platform
body:
By default, Flutter's text selection toolbar shows cut, copy, paste, and select all. However, on native platforms, other buttons appear by default. | Platform | Screenshot | Missing | Related Work | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Android | | Translate, Share, Web Search | Share: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/138728
Custom buttons: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/139361 | | iOS | | Select, Replace, [Translate](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/150392), [unit conversion](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/111672) | Look up: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/130532, ht ...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments flag that third-party app-specific context menu buttons (e.g., AnkiDroid) are tracked separately in #107603. The iOS Translate button can now likely be added using the Translation API announced at WWDC24. Android's ACTION_PROCESS_TEXT mechanism for third-party buttons is confirmed possible per #107603. Linked PRs: Look Up #130532, Search Web #131898, Share engine #44554.

title: Flutter should respect custom system-wide text selection toolbar buttons
body: On some platforms, it's possible to write an app that adds a custom button to the system-wide text selection toolbar. For example, here the Android app AnkiDroid has added the "Anki Card" button to my text selection toolbar, and it appears in any app, such as the Google app here. Flutter should respect these added buttons, so that in any Flutter app, the buttons that the user has added to their device show up in the default text selection toolbar just like in any native app. ### Scope This issue does not include allowing Flutter apps to add system-wide buttons. It's about the reverse problem. ### Platforms | Platform | Behavior | Related Work | | --- | --- | --- | | Android | Apps can add custom system-wide buttons ([relevant article](https://dev.to/bigaru/providing-custom-text-selection-actions-in-android-1akc)). Is there a way for Flutter t ...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirmed that on Android, an app must declare a `<queries>` block in its manifest referencing `android.intent.action.PROCESS_TEXT` to enumerate third-party text-action providers; without it, no results are returned. A community member (@gualse) implemented a working proof-of-concept plugin. Engine PR #44579 was filed by @bleroux for the Android implementation, and a separate framework tracking issue #139361 was created. Confirmed that iOS has no equivalent of Android's `PROCESS_TEXT` API, and Jetpack Compose's `TextField` also lacks this feature. The team agreed this belongs in Flutter proper (not flutter/packages) and that the manifest `<queries>` entry should be added to the `flutter create` template. The Linux/macOS Services menu was noted as a potential analog for macOS.

title: The screen shrinks when the keyboard pops up in Chrome OS
body:
We are facing issue which is that the screen shrinks when the keyboard pops up in Chrome OS making the screen unresponsive and unusable . The issue is also occurring in the text field section of the flutter gallery [Flutter Gallery](https://gallery.flutter.dev/#/demo/text-field)

[Sample Video](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/108711688/178908974-de25a7c4-d9da-41bc-b61a-8bdd5f86bc27.mp4)
comment_summary: Confirmed Chrome OS-specific. Additional reproduction code provided using Card widget with multiple TextFormFields. Works differently on Chrome OS vs Windows tablet mode. No root cause or fix provided.

title: Getting GestureRecognizers to respect each other
body:
In super_editor, we're trying to add support to `SuperTextField` on desktop to allow arbitrary gesture interactions on top of the text field. For example, an app should be able to implement `ALT + LEFT CLICK` to open a context menu.

Currently, this is a challenge because we can't seem to prevent both the app-level gestures and the `SuperTextField` internal gestures from both responding to user interactions. I have a PR that illustrates our attempt to do this: https://github.com/superlistapp/super_editor/pull/692

FYI - There's a test in the PR that's currently failing. That should demonstrate the issue.

I think we need the insight of the Flutter team to determine if there's presently a mechanism that would allow these two `GestureRecognizer`s to cooperate in the gesture arena, or whether this use-case is impossible with the current gesture system.
comment_summary: Discussion did not produce a concrete solution; stalled on needing a reduced test case not dependent on custom recognizers.
