| Linux | ![out](htt
...[truncated]
comment_summary: OverlayPortal PR #105335 noted as potentially making this fix easier. A developer also requested exposing a selectionOverlayTransitionBuilder to allow custom toolbar fade animations, noting the current private implementation makes customization very difficult.

title: Implement iOS keyboard track pad selection for TextField widget
body:
## Use case
On iOS system fields and in natively developed iOS applications, one can press and hold the space bar to go into trackpad mode, then put another finger on the trackpad to be able to select specific texts (this is the replacement of 3D touch selection) 

With Flutter, the keyboard only transforms in the trackpad to move the cursor, but it is *not* possible to also select text. 

## Proposal
Flutter should be able to select text in a textfield in trackpad mode, as demonstrated in the sample video below: 


https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8842565/179538899-b6af75dd-fe72-4fed-ae26-b938ea8ae222.MP4


## Additional information 
More detailed descriptions on the usage in iOS can be found online, for example [here](https://www.igeeksblog.com/how-to-use-iphone-keyboard-as-trackpad/).
comment_summary: Comments note this is related to #89228. The video was re-uploaded confirming the feature request is specifically about text selection (not just cursor movement) in trackpad mode.

title: A KeyRepeatEvent is dispatched, but the state shows that the physical key is not pressed
body:
# Edited by Moderator

Most users report that this issue exist only on v3.3.x or earlier.

**Please only report if this error exists on v3.4.0 or later**. 

When reporting, **please make sure** to add your `flutter doctor -v` result as well as reproduction steps. Thank you.

------


<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. In Windows 10, Execute `flutter 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The root cause is that on Windows, holding a modifier key (Ctrl, Shift, Alt, Caps Lock) causes the engine to send a synthesized KeyUpEvent followed by a KeyRepeatEvent, but the framework's `_pressedKeys` map no longer contains the key, triggering the `_assertEventIsRegular` assertion in `hardware_keyboard.dart` line 535. Confirmed affected on Windows 10 and 11 (debug mode only; release builds are unaffected per comment #27); one commenter also reported it on Android 7.1.1 (comment #53) and another on Windows with third-party software injecting Ctrl+C events (comment #56). The issue was introduced after Flutter 3.0.5/3.0.0 and was fixed in the engine via PR #36129 (landed in Flutter 3.4.0-34.1.pre / 3.5.0 master), but comments in 3.7.0 and 3.19.5/3.19.6 indicate regressions. A community workaround is to comment out the `_assertEventIsRegular` call in `hardware_keyboard.dart`; no official fix was cherry-picked to the 3.3.x stable branch.

title: Can't move caret within composing region on iOS, though it appears to move
comment_summary: Comments confirm the root cause: after moving the cursor within the composing region via the floating cursor (long-press space), the iOS platform text input plugin continues treating the cursor as being at the end of the composing region. A cast error (`int` to `double`) also appears during floating cursor updates. The issue affects both Chinese pinyin and Japanese kana keyboards, not English. Moving the cursor outside the composing region commits the text (different from native behavior). A related issue #122490 was filed for the native snap-back behavior. A UIKit app reproducing the bug was created, and a radar was filed with Apple since the fix may require a secret UITextInput method call. Status: stalled, waiting on Apple/engine.

title: [Desktop] Incorrect backspace handling with combined Khmer Unicode characters
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample
2. Using a Khmer Unicode keyboard, type កុំ (K + U + Shift + M) into the text field and pressing Backspace to delete each character

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->

Pressing backspace should only remove one character at a time.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->

Pressing backspace deletes all of the combined characters (only on Desktop).

The incorrect backspace behaviour on Desktop seems to apply to any combined characters, e.g. កី (K + Shift + I), តើ (T + ;), as it seems to consider them as one character.

_(I haven't had the occasion to test on any mobile platform.)_

I've also attached a demonstration video below:

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6465682/180672657-61c947ed-0023-431f-a232-074fc6da8c46.mov

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

<!--
      Please create a minimal reproducible sample that shows the problem
      and attach i
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirmed reproduction on macOS and Windows (desktop platforms) but not on mobile or web. The root cause was identified by a commenter: Flutter's framework uses `CharacterRange.at()` (grapheme cluster boundary) for backspace deletion after PR #115900, which deletes the entire grapheme cluster (e.g., consonant + vowel in Khmer) instead of one code point at a time. The proposed fix direction is to delete by code point rather than grapheme when there is no active composing range, and delegate to the IME only when a composing range is active. A discussion noted that on iOS, the keyboard manager decomposes precomposed Hangul syllables and deletes one jamo at a time, suggesting platform delegation is preferable. The assignee (LongCatIsLooong) was removed after no status updates; PR #115900 is cited as the introduction point.

title: AutoFillHints missing Android's AUTOFILL_HINT_2FA_APP_OTP
body:
## Use case

Android autofill property has AUTOFILL_HINT_2FA_APP_OTP but flutter only has AutoFillHints.oneTimeCode that resolves to AUTOFILL_HINT_SMS_OTP in Android. There isn't any AutoFillHint that resolves to AUTOFILL_HINT_2FA_APP_OTP.

## Proposal

Create a new AutoFillHints item or config to resolve to AUTOFILL_HINT_2FA_APP_OTP.
comment_summary: Comments provide a string workaround (`'AUTOFILL_HINT_2FA_APP_OTP'`). Assignment removed due to no progress. Android triage asked about the iOS equivalent hint.

title: Enable text selection handles on desktop platforms with touch screen
body:
Currently text selection handles are only shown on mobile platforms via Material or Cupertino widgets.

Handles should also be enabled on desktop platforms with touch screen e.g. Windows and Linux laptops, similarly to what Word does:

![text-handles](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/72562119/181909280-cd669558-ab07-4661-a681-98acb97d9c60.png)
comment_summary: Team welcomes a PR; existing mobile text selection handle code could likely be reused for desktop touch screens. A contributor asked whether to detect touch vs. mouse input and whether changes should apply to all widgets using `TextSelectionControls`.

title: Implement mouse context menu on Android
body: It turns out that Android has a different context menu (text selection menu) when using a mouse vs. when using tap gestures. If you right click on a text field on native Android, this is what you get: Flutter doesn't currently implement this menu at all! We should distinguish between mouse and tap gestures on Android and display the correct menu. ### Open questions Is there a separate menu when using a mouse on iOS too? Related: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/106586

title: Input Decorator overflows the parent widget vertically
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run https://dartpad.dev/?id=60403a776f027d75ccd29aa2421342b7

**Expected results:**
The InputDecorator in red should be inside the parent widget without any overflow.

**Actual results:**
On web (screenshot) we can see on the top and bottom a small overflow. On android, this is slightly different but still buggy.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/18555491/182391056-e103feb2-5a7f-43c3-9ceb-e9b180412fbc.png)

I have not find a workaround yet. Might be a similar issue with another approach https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/82709.
comment_summary: Comments confirm the overflow occurs on desktop (macOS) and web but not on Android or iOS. Workaround: set `visualDensity: VisualDensity.standard` in `ThemeData`; the overflow is caused by density-offset adjustments in `InputDecorator`. Possibly a duplicate of #82709. Unassigned after previous assignee did not make progress; open for contribution.

title: [add_to_app] Keyboard doesn't show when `TextField` has `autofocus: true`
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Execute `cd add_to_app/multiple_flutters/multiple_flutters_module && flutter pub get` 
2. Execute `cd add_to_app/multiple_flutters/multiple_flutters_android && ./gradlew clean installDebug`  or installRelease
3. Open App -> "NEXT" button
4. TextField not active IME

add_to_app:
```
W/InputMethodManager: Ignoring showSoftInput() as view=io.flutter.embedding.android.FlutterView{2205904 VFE...... .F....ID 0,0-1080,2210 #1} is not served.
W/InputMethodManager: Ignoring showSoftInput() as view=io.flutter.embedding.android.FlutterView{2205904 VFE...... .F....ID 0,0-1080,2210 #1} is not served.
```

If I create a flutter application from AndroidStudio on flutter sdk stable 3.0.5:
Debug mode is ok (log below), release also failed with reason `SHOW_SOFT_INPUT`

```
W/InputMethodManager(13319): Ignoring showSoftInput() as view=io.flutter.embedding.android.FlutterView{c1acb50 VFE...... .F....ID 0,0-1080,2210 #1} is not served.
D/DecorView[](13319): onWi
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: the Android InputMethodManager rejects showSoftInput() calls because the InputConnection between the FlutterView and the current IME is not established when autofocus fires (which happens in a post-frame callback). This is specific to add_to_app; standard Flutter apps work in debug mode. The issue may be related to #58498. The assignee was removed after a long period with no updates; status is stalled.

title: MaterialApp scrollBehavior does not override TextField's scroll behaviour
comment_summary: Comments clarify that this is by-design: `EditableText` explicitly enables scrollbars for multi-line text fields regardless of the app-level `ScrollBehavior`. The team acknowledges the documentation is misleading. A workaround is provided: wrap the `TextField` with a `ScrollConfiguration` using a custom `ScrollBehavior` that overrides `buildScrollbar` to return the child directly. A request was made to add a `scrollBehavior` parameter to `TextField`/`TextFormField`. Status: stalled, doc update proposed, workaround available.

title: Better textFormField label placement customization when focused and when value input- vertical floatingLabelAlignment feature.
body:
## Use case

I want some fine tuned, detailed customization for my textFormField. Currently, when the field becomes focused (and permanently when a value is input) the floating label floats up to the border & cuts it. The label sits vertically in the centre of the border and  the label cuts the border. While this does look okay for some field configurations, such as some rectangular fields, it looks horrible for others. Specifically mine looks gross. I have a beautiful design from my designer and I want to keep with this.

This is a problem for me and my design, not specifically a problem with the textFormField.

Haven't found a package that does this but, here is some code that does solve this problem. It is a bit of a hack because it requires external state and container wrapping logic to exteriorly handle a textFormFields border outline. This is definitely NOT OPTIMUM and I do NOT want to have to use this solution. The full SO post can be found [here.](https://stackoverflow.co
...[truncated]
comment_summary: A contributor (lastmeta) worked on PR #130039 to add a `floatingLabelGap` parameter to `InputDecoration` but was blocked by repeated pushback; the contributor stated they abandoned the effort. Community members shared workarounds: a custom `InputBorder` subclass that sets `isOutline = false` (avoiding the label-on-border gap) and a `Stack`-based approach using a borderless `TextField` with an external `Container`. PR #130039 appears stalled/abandoned.

title: canvas.drawLine() does not paint correctly on Samsung Galaxy Note 20 Ultra
comment_summary: Issue not reproduced on Samsung Tab A7 (Android 11/12) by triagers, but reporter confirms it persists on GT-I9500 (Android 5.0.1) through version 3.3.0. A related issue (#145872) on a different Samsung device was closed in favor of this one, so the issue remains open; the GT-I9500 is discontinued but the rendering defect via drawLine on older Samsung hardware is kept under investigation.

title: [iPadOS] When using an external keyboard to input Chinese, the associative word expansion and left and right selection cannot be controlled through the arrow keys on the keyboard
body:
Expected results：
1 - Control the expansion and up-down selection of associative words through the up and down keys of the hardware keyboard, similar to the control effect of the plus and minus keys
2 - Control the left and right selection of associative words through the left and right buttons of the hardware keyboard
3 - Click the delete key of the hardware keyboard, when the content of the input box is deleted, the associative words are updated synchronously

example video:

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/16076050/187617499-a35aed2d-7646-4349-ad10-ad3d7ea1e1ba.mov

Actual results：
1 - The expansion of associative words and the selection of up and down can only be controlled by the plus and minus buttons
2 - Unable to control associative words with up and down keys on hardware keyboard
3 - Only the content of the input box can be deleted, the associative words cannot be updated synchronously

example video:

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1607605
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirmed the bug still reproduces on stable 3.3 and master 3.4; affects both iPhone and iPad (not just iPad). The issue is that hardware arrow keys don't navigate IME candidate lists for Chinese input on iOS/iPadOS—only the `+`/`-` keys work. No fix proposed; issue is 3+ years old with no activity. Status: stalled, no PR, open.

title: Long SelectableText is not actually selectable in ListView
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue also affects `SelectionArea` on both beta and master channels on iOS and Android. When "Select All" is used inside a `ListView`, only the currently visible text is selected. A simplified repro confirms the selection resets when the list scrolls. No fix or PR referenced; status is open and stalled.

title: TextField to support samsung spen handwritting input
body:
## Use case

On Samsung Galaxy Devices with an SPen the TextField does not allow Handwriting input.
I read (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/61278) that this is already implemented for Apple Pencil.

## Proposal

I suggest to support handwriting here too. 

## Findings

Many apps I got on my tablet does not support this at the moment. 
But e.g. the Google Play Store app or Outlook does support this.

![20220905_201454](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/880759/188499609-750e0419-bd01-46c9-b691-9f67deea8c78.gif)
comment_summary: Comments suggest this may be fixed via the Scribe handwriting feature (#115607). A PR reviewing Apple Pencil support in the engine was cited as a reference.

title: Hide right click menu when app loses focus
comment_summary: Comments confirm the context menu stays visible through 3.24 on macOS when the app loses focus by clicking the top menu bar (app lifecycle does not change to inactive in that case). A contributor investigated and found the issue is not a focus or lifecycle problem but that specific macOS events (clicking menu bar, dragging window) do not trigger context menu dismissal. PRs welcome per a maintainer comment but none submitted; stalled.

title: Textspan's tapGestureRecognizer has unexpected behaviour when the textspan is out of the textfield visible area after the first layout
body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample (see "Code sample" section below) 
2. Like the code shows below, I want to insert custom string (like "[ hello ]" "[ world ]" ) or common text into a textfiled by override "buildTextSpan" in TextEditingController. 
3. And I add recognizer to each textSpan. But I find  that the textspa
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug reproduces on Flutter 3.3/3.4 stable and master. The issue is that TapGestureRecognizer on TextSpans that are scrolled out of the initial visible area of a TextField return incorrect hit positions — tapping out-of-view spans fires callbacks for spans that were originally visible. Investigation was deferred to @justinmc, @LongCatIsLooong, and @Renzo-Olivares with no fix proposed.

title: Remove TextSelectionDelegate from SelectableRegionState
body:
SelectableRegionState currently [mixes in](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/652ff7a5e6684fb3fe94f89cfd23a8445f5943e5/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/selectable_region.dart#L241) TextSelectionDelegate in order to receive text selection toolbar functionality, however, this functionality is being remove from TextSelectionDelegate in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/107193.  After the deprecations in that PR are removed, it should be possible to remove TextSelectionDelegate from SelectableRegionState altogether.

### Why remove TextSelectionDelegate?

TextSelectionDelegate provides functionality that is related to editing text and which SelectableRegion doesn't use ([example](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/652ff7a5e6684fb3fe94f89cfd23a8445f5943e5/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/selectable_region.dart#L869)).  Text selection should be separate from text editing.

CC @chunhtai
comment_summary: Comments note `TextSelectionDelegate` can be removed once deprecations from PR #107193 expire. A commenter planned to open a PR then unassigned themselves. The issue was incorrectly closed with #107193.

title: iOS 16 text selection toolbar menu unit converter
body:
The iOS 16 text selection menu has a built-in unit converter:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/389558/190458753-dcfb6922-0fb1-4651-845c-3728d15ca477.png)

### Flutter implementation

This is very similar to the ["email button" demo](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/107193/files#diff-c37e45d149cf86a6565f1eb32f738d5faaf4170b8bd4f1f6421651f0af2f1bfa) that I wrote with the new context menu system.  If we can write something that correctly identifies convertable strings and their conversions then it should be easy to implement in the context menu.

### Reference
  * An article that mentions this feature:
    - https://www.macstories.net/stories/ios-16-the-macstories-review/14/#content
  * Issue about all missing context menu buttons:
    - https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/107578
comment_summary: One comment asks whether native iOS exposes any public API for the unit converter to avoid duplicating logic in Flutter.

title: Provide text deltas to TextEditingController
body:
Text deltas became available recently, but to my understanding, it is available only if you create a text editor with `DeltaTextInputClient`. I think it is too much for most developers to implement their own editor.

## Use case

I've created a package with a `TextEditingController` that styles text according to some rules as you type. It works, but the longer the text is, the slower text changes become visible because it takes more time to parse the text.

If I have to implement a text editor to achieve the above, the API of my package must be changed. In addition, thinking about the fact that even the Flutter's standard TextField created by awesome people from the Flutter team still has bugs and is far behind native text input components in terms of quality, I doubt that general developers including me can create good editors on our own.

## Proposal

Is it possible to make deltas accessible from `TextEditingController`? It will become possible to debounce parsing by instea
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments express strong agreement with the proposal; maintainers of packages overriding `TextSelectionGestureDetectorBuilderDelegate` (tightly coupled to `EditableTextState`) also find the current API a maintenance burden. Issue stalled with no plan.

title: FocusNode.skipTraversal is set unexpectedly through Focus widget
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample <!-- (see "Code sample" section below) -->
2. Focus on the text field. Click + button twice. **Press tab to change focus.**

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
Text field is traversable.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
Text field is not traversable.

**Root cause:**

I believe the issue is here: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/18a827f3933c19f51862dde3fa472197683249d6/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/focus_scope.dart#L607-L612

Should be:

```
        if (widget._skipTraversal != focusNode.skipTraversal) {
          focusNode.skipTraversal = widget._skipTraversal;
        }
        if (widget._canRequestFocus != focusNode.canRequestFocus) {
          focusNode.canRequestFocus = widget._canRequestFocus!;
        }
```

Could someone confirm if my finding is correc
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified in the issue body: `Focus.didUpdateWidget` unconditionally copies `skipTraversal` and `canRequestFocus` from the widget to the node even when they haven't changed, corrupting state set by `descendantsAreTraversable`. The reporter proposed a guarded copy fix and offered to send a PR. A maintainer confirmed the proposed fix direction and invited a PR with tests. No PR confirmed merged; status unclear.

title: TextFormField focus consumes enormous system resources on simulator & iPhone

- [Here](https://docs.flutter.dev/coo
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments identify two distinct causes: iOS cursor opacity animation runs continuously while focused (related to #108187 and #59327), and debug mode exaggerates the issue — release/profile mode should be used for performance measurement. A workaround was added exposing `cursorOpacityAnimates: false` on `TextField` to eliminate the CPU spike with minimal visual difference. Issue remains open with no fix landed; some commenters report crashes with Impeller that were redirected to separate issues.

title: Provide an API to detect if a TextPosition is located at a soft word wrap
body:
Currently it is not possible to detect if a TextPosition is located at a soft word wrap. We should provide an API, most likely through `RenderEditable` that gives this information. This is necessary to accomplish the use case below.

## Use case
On iOS we want to be able to toggle the toolbar if we tap on the same `TextPosition` regardless of the `TextAffinity` when our tap is not at a soft word wrap. When our tap is at a soft word wrap we want to hide the toolbar if the `TextAffinity` has changed and toggle the toolbar if it hasn't.

## Proposal
Provide an API through `RenderEditable` that tells us if a given `TextPosition` is located at a word wrap.

title: Outdated text received in a `TextEditingDeltaNonTextUpdate`
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Checkout the demo repo: https://github.com/angelosilvestre/ime_investigation 
2. Run the app on Android
3. Tap "Attach to IME"
4. Press the new line button on the software keyboard
5. Type a
6. Press Backspace quickly 3 times

**Expected results:** Flutter should send a `TextEditingDeltaNonTextUpdate` with the current text (Ab)

**Actual results:** Flutter sends a `TextEditingDeltaNonTextUpdate` with the previous text (Abc)

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>
Available in the demo repo.
</details>

<details>
  <summary>Logs</summary>

```
 [ +29 ms] I/ViewRootImpl@f8f1298[MainActivity](26103): MSG_WINDOW_FOCUS_CHANGED 1 1
[   +2 ms] D/InputMethodManager(26103): prepareNavigationBarInfo() DecorView@a6127ac[MainActivity]
[   +1 ms] D/InputMethodManager(26103): getNavigationBarColor() -16711423
[   +1 ms] D/InputMethodManager(26103): prepareNavigationBarInfo() DecorView@a6127ac[MainActivity]
[   +1 ms] D/InputMethodManager(26103): 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug is Android-only and device-specific: reproducible on Samsung Galaxy M51 (Android 11, Gboard) but not on Pixel 7 Pro. The root cause is excessive `_syncWithOS` calls that bounce editing state back to the engine even when changes originated in the engine, causing stale text to appear in `TextEditingDeltaNonTextUpdate` and triggering an assertion (`range.end is out of text of length`). The suggested fix was to limit `_syncWithOS` calls to cases where the framework is directly mutating editing state; applying this in super_editor's own IME code reduced occurrences. The issue is currently stalled with no open PR in the Flutter repo and the assignee was removed.

title: Wrong position of a cursor pointer in web view where the web app is built with flutter web
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible with both canvaskit and html renderers when viewing Flutter web inside a WebView or mobile browser. The cursor and selection handle positions are misaligned, with the handle appearing at the obscured-mode character positions even in non-obscured mode for password fields. Double selection handles are also observed. No root cause or fix was identified in comments. Currently stalled.

title: Create fuzzing tests for text input
body:
Similar to https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/113571

We've definitely had issues in the past around invalid/unexpected values supplied in text input bridge. We should add fuzzing tests.
comment_summary: A commenter clarified the intended scope: fuzzing tests should send arbitrary random data (invalid JSON, missing fields, unexpected values) from the framework side to the text input channel to catch crashes or misbehavior.

title: Improve the performance of the EditableTextState / text input synchronization
body:
Currently every frame the `EditableTextState` will calculate and send state to the text input in the following locations:
1) https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/9064bf674b544a8d472ee231cb69b9f9b841d843/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/editable_text.dart#L3213
1) https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/9064bf674b544a8d472ee231cb69b9f9b841d843/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/editable_text.dart#L3225
1) https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/9064bf674b544a8d472ee231cb69b9f9b841d843/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/editable_text.dart#L3189

In the use case defined in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/59327 this represents a significant portion of the ui thread's time.  I implemented a naive approach that dropped the frequency of the updates and that lead to a 28% decrease in CPU usage (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/113722).  @LongCatIsLooong and Hixie determined there is a better way to be more reactive for sending updates so that is preferable to the naive dro
...[truncated]

title: Using the Japanese prompt window on Windows works incorrectly on Windows.
comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause as an interaction between Japanese IME (Windows prompt window) and `inputFormatters` using a regex decimal pattern: when the IME commits a candidate, the text is duplicated. A commenter suggests the fix is to include `composing: newValue.composing` when building the new `TextEditingValue` in the input formatter. Reproduced on both stable 3.3.5 and master. Status: open, no PR, fix direction identified.

title: Assert triggered in focus traversal: RenderBox was not laid out
comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause: during tab focus traversal, `ReadingOrderTraversalPolicy.sortDescendants` collects focus nodes from all `Navigator` pages including inactive ones; since inactive pages are not laid out, their `RenderBox.size` assertion fires. The fix is to exclude inactive Navigator pages from focus traversal. A commenter confirms the original reporter may have worked around it differently and the issue may still be open. Status: root cause identified, no fix merged.

title: Text cursors should deterministically show in goldens tests
body:
Every few months a change to text editing will cause the text cursor animation to shift. This requires goldens to be updated to trivially either show or hide the cursor.

For larger customers, this can be a pain as it can impact several thousand goldens.

By default, text cursors should always show in goldens.

\cc @justinmc @Piinks
comment_summary: A commenter surfaced that EditableText.debugDeterministicCursor already exists as an API but suggested it should be made the default behavior to resolve the golden test instability.

title: TextField selection handle wrongly appear and can be interacted even if it should be occluded and not interacted
comment_summary: Root cause identified in body (selection handle lives in root Overlay above all content). Comments confirm reproduction and discuss the expected use case — a custom on-screen keyboard overlaying the text field. No workaround provided. Status: stalled, no fix or PR.

title: Text selection gesture callbacks should use details objects directly instead of saving state in RenderEditable
body:
Currently `RenderEditable.handleTapDown` is called when `onTapDown` is fired. The `globalPosition` from the `TapDownDetails` is stored in `RenderEditable._lastTapDownPosition`. This stored `globalPosition` is used in `onTapUp` when `RenderEditable.selectPosition({ required SelectionChangedCause cause })` is called. Internally it uses the position captured during `onTapDown` to select the position. `onTapUp` provides a `TapUpDetails` object with the global position that we can use to select the position we want.

`RenderEditable.handleSecondaryTapDown` is called `onSecondaryTapDown`. The `globalPosition` from the `TapDownDetails` is stored in `RenderEditable._lastSecondaryTapDownPosition`. This stored `globalPosition` is used in `onSecondaryTap` when `RenderEditable.selectWord({ required SelectionChangedCause cause })` is called. Internally it uses the position captured during `onSecondaryTapDown` to select the word. `TextSelectionGestureDetector` has access to `onSecondaryTapUp` whic
...[truncated]

title: Flashing text selection handle in the magnifier on Android
comment_summary: Comments confirm the selection handle flashing in the Android magnifier. The likely cause identified is a race condition in the BackdropFilter rendering, occurring intermittently. It was noted that `shouldDisplayHandlesInMagnifier` is already `false` on Android but handles still flash through. Additional related issues noted: toolbar positioned too close to the field (#separate issue) and handle clipped by keyboard (#120551). Issue was considered for shipping behind a feature flag but no fix landed. Stalled.

title: [Web]: Pointer acts incorrectly when typing at TextField wrapped in Stack
comment_summary: A commenter clarified this is distinct from the general cursor-override issue (#31952) and provided additional reproduction: the incorrect text-cursor is shown over stacked widgets only when the TextField is focused, not when unfocused. Reproducible in both CanvasKit and HTML renderers on web. Confirmed still present in 3.24.3 (October 2024). No fix or PR; stalled.

title: Add benchmarks for text scrolling / text input
body:
Currently when a text field is focused, the framework sends a relatively large amount of data (especially on iOS) to the platform text input plugin. This is probably not currently measured / monitored by any benchmark as the data transmission is usually only triggered on text layout changes or when the text field scrolls.  

Since a text entering/deleting benchmark will have to include the time spent interacting with the OS text input system it could be very flaky but it will still be great if we can get a warning when a change renders text input unusable.

title: [iPadOS]Pressing tab key doesn't cycle through conversion window when using physical keyboard
comment_summary: Reproduction confirmed on iPadOS stable and master. A commenter identified the root cause: Flutter's keyboard shortcut handling intercepts the Tab key before the platform can handle it for IME candidate cycling. title: Keys don't map correctly when using "Dvorak - QWERTY ⌘" layout on macOS
body:
![Screenshot 2022-12-03 at 1 11 46 AM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/156888/205433468-f2e26625-38ed-46bb-b76f-560993c0714f.png)

There's a keyboard layout on macOS called "Dvorak - QWERTY ⌘". It's a Dvorak keyboard layout and the "v" key is where the "." key is for QWERTY. So in a normal Dvorak keyboard, ctrl-v, cmd-v etc all work by doing a combo with the "." physical key. But that layout is a bit annoying because one handed operations like ctrl-c, ctrl-v etc become two handed operations since c and v are on the right side of the keyboard. 

So "Dvorak - QWERTY ⌘" solves that by being a Dvorak layout most of the time, except when the command ⌘ modifier is held. Then for that duration, all keys remap to the QWERTY layout. So cmd-c is still on the "c" physical key (which normally maps to "j" on Dvorak). 

That works in all AppKit apps but doesn't work in Flutter. The macOS menu bar is actually highlighting Edit->Paste each time I press cmd-v, but there's no pasting in 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause: the "Dvorak - QWERTY Cmd" layout has a second layer where keys remap to QWERTY when command is held, but Flutter's mapping algorithm does not handle this two-layer behavior, receiving cmd-j instead of cmd-c. No fix proposed.

title: Document why KeyboardListener needs a FocusNode
body: The documentation could be a bit more explicit about why KeyboardListener needs a FocusNode and what is the expected interaction between them.
comment_summary: Comments from @gspencergoog clarify that `KeyboardListener` does not strictly require a `FocusNode`; it could use `HardwareKeyboard.addHandler` instead. The FocusNode allows participation in the focus tree so descendants can handle events first.

title: Make shortcuts portable across different keyboard layouts
body:
Flutter shortcuts defined with `SingleActivator` or `LogicalKeySet` are not keyboard layout agnostic. For example, the classic zoom-in shortcut `Control`+`+` only works with such keyboard layouts (e.g. Finnish) that do not require the `Shift` modifier to be pressed to access `+`. If the system has e.g. US English keyboard layout in use, the shortcut has to be defined as `Control`+`Shift`+`+` instead. A single shortcut definition does not work with both keyboard layouts.

The "logical" shortcut is `Control`+`+` and what is typically presented in GUIs. Application developers should not need to know that in some keyboard layouts, the "physical" key combination to activate the shortcut includes an "extra" `Shift` modifier.

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Switch to Finnish keyboard layout
2. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample <!-- (see "Code sample" section below) -->
2. Try pressing `Control`+
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the proposal: SingleActivator is not keyboard-layout-agnostic. @gspencergoog explains that CharacterActivator should be used instead (e.g., CharacterActivator('+', control: true)) for layout-agnostic shortcuts, as it activates on the produced character rather than the physical key combination. Qt and GTK+ handle this correctly. Issue is labeled as a proposal; no framework change planned.

## What the code sample does
I am trying to build a text editor on top of the TextField. The code sample format's the text within a text field, adding a line break at every 10th character.

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample <!-- (see "Code sample" section below) -->
2. Simultaneously press multiple keys on the keyboar
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The issue was confirmed on iOS simulator and likely on iOS release mode. The bug manifests when multiple keys are pressed simultaneously just before a line break would be inserted by the `onChanged` formatter — some characters are dropped or the formatter fails to apply. The root cause appears to be a race between rapid key input and the formatter modifying the controller state. No fix proposed; status is stalled.

title: Add a property to text rendering widgets that allows for adding a tooltip when the text is overflowed
body: In many cases for text widgets, we do not want to show a redundant tooltip (e.g. "This is my text widget with words" with a tooltip of "This is my text widget with words". But it would be useful to show a tooltip with the full content when the text is ellipsized (e.g "This is my text widg..." with a tooltip "This is my text widget with words". It would be useful if there was a `tooltipOnOverflow` property on `Text`(and likely on other text widgets with the `overflow` field).
comment_summary: Comments agree the feature is desirable. A compositional approach via `Tooltip` with an `onTriggered` callback checking `isOverflowing` was proposed, but both the callback return type and the `isOverflowing` property are missing. No PR.

title: [android] Animation when opening keyboard is unsynchronized
body:
This issue is the android version of [#105687](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/120555) (which is fixed by https://github.com/flutter/engine/commit/a5ff3629dfecd81d7322c40de7edaab24ee3d21f).

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/53510751/3437e46b-b620-420a-af8b-d69b4ad0dcd6

The keyboard animation is jittery most of time. 
Notice that the issue is much evident and occurs often on real device running in release mode.

As pointed out in the iOS fix, issue is:
"if setViewportMetrics called **before** framework's vsync process callback (rebuild & layout & paint), will cause jitter keyboard animation.So this PR is to keep the time when updateViewportMetrics call to framework side legal".

Demo code here:
https://github.com/delfme/flutter-native-text-input-repo

With this fix, flutter would finally provide a native-like keyboard animation on both ios and android 🚀
comment_summary: Comments confirm Android keyboard animation jitter on real devices in release mode; root cause is a vsync timing mismatch where `setViewportMetrics` is called before the framework's vsync process callback. The analogous iOS fix used `engine/pull/37604`. An Android-side contributor notes no clear equivalent Android engine hook was found. #168768 was resolved by #176418 but it is unclear if that covers this specific timing issue. Community continues to report the issue through 2025; labeled duplicate of #168768 but kept open pending confirmation. Status: stalled.

title: [Autofill] TextInput.finishAutofillContext() doesn't bring up the iOS system prompt
comment_summary: Root cause explained in comments: iOS UIKit has no public API to trigger the save-password prompt programmatically; it fires automatically when the view is removed from the hierarchy, which is why the prompt appears on navigation away rather than on `finishAutofillContext()` call. Confirmed still broken on stable through 3.27.4; one commenter cannot reproduce on iOS 26.2 but still fails on iOS 18.6. `AutofillContextAction.cancel` is confirmed non-functional — it still triggers the save prompt on dispose. Team note: developers must call `finishAutofillContext(save: false)` themselves when leaving the screen without a successful login.

title: Fragile padding and anchor math for toolbars
body:
For TextSelectionToolbars and SpellCheckSuggestionsToolbar, there is a Padding widget in the Overlay that helps the toolbars naturally position themselves with respect to the device's screen padding.  Usually this is included via an app's Scaffold, but inside of an Overlay it's not there.

Currently, the Padding widget seems to include padding that's related to the toolbar (`_kToolbarContentDistanceBelow`), and the toolbar's anchor also compensates for screen padding that should be done via Padding (`mediaQueryData.padding.top`).

Instead, ideally the Padding would set all of the device screen padding and the anchor would just position itself within it.  That would make the positioning logic much easier and more inline with how Flutter usually handles this.

### References

 * SpellCheckSuggestionsToolbar PR: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/117221
 * TextSelectionToolbar code: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/9102f2fe0bd26db6074ac4a17785296cd341ecb9/packages/f
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue is still valid; the author self-assigned but had not worked on it for 2.5 years and removed the assignment.

title: Problems with TextInputAction.send TextInputType.multiline
body:
很多三方的键盘[例如搜狗]， 设置之后之后

但是发送键能正确使用， 换行键点击之后就无法换行了。 是一个空格的效果。

However, the Send key can be used correctly. After clicking the Line Feed key, line feed cannot be performed. Is the effect of a space.
Is there a way to be compatible with both types?
Chat tools like WeChat and QQ can be used together with line breaking and sending.

## Steps to Reproduce

`TextField(
          keyboardType: TextInputType.multiline,
          enableInteractiveSelection: true,
          maxLines: 4,
          minLines: 1,
          textInputAction: TextInputAction.send,
          onChanged: (value) {},
          onSubmitted: (value){},
)`



![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/29450740/209492215-e4e28dea-ddfe-479e-aa32-f311df3af1fd.png)

https://user-images.githubusercontent.c
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue: with TextInputType.multiline and TextInputAction.send on iOS, the Sogou (and similar Chinese third-party) keyboard's line-feed key inserts a space instead of a newline. A commenter traced the root cause to Flutter's engine detecting newlines via monitoring for \n in input, but the Sogou keyboard inserts two carriage returns (\r) instead; SkParagraph treats \r as a soft line break. A workaround FilteringTextInputFormatter was proposed to replace \r with \n. Status is open.

title: TextInputConnection should let objects listen for IME connection closure
body:
An IME connection is established with these pieces: `TextInput`, a `TextInputConnection`, and a `TextInputClient`.

```dart
final myConnection = TextInput.attach(myInputClient, myConfig);
```

`TextInputConnection` has a `bool` that reports `isAttached`, but there's no way to listen for a change to this attachment.

The `TextInputClient` has a method called `connectionClosed`, which, I assume, represents a notification that the IME connection has closed. However, policies about when to open and close a connection are usually independent from the policies that apply text input messages from the platform. Therefore, the use of `TextInputConnection` should be able to exist independently from the code that implements `TextInputClient`, and this requires that `TextInputConnection` receive a notification when its connection is closed.

```
Flutter 3.7.0-1.2.pre • channel beta • https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git
Framework • revision c29b09b878 (9 days ago) • 2022-12-19 18:30:
...[truncated]
comment_summary: A PR #118283 was proposed to change the notification flow from TextInput to TextInputConnection to TextInputClient. Separate discussion about IME candidate window positioning stuck in upper-left corner on Windows/macOS when using custom TextInputClient.

title: InputMethodManager / InsetsController log messages on each typed character on Android
body:
It appears that with every character typed we send a request to the platform to show soft keyboard.

**Expected results:** No logs shown on each typed character

**Actual results:** Every character typed produces the following log:

D/InputMethodManager(32033): showSoftInput() view=io.flutter.embedding.android.FlutterView{c4be627 VFE...... .F...... 0,0-1080,2214 #1 aid=1073741824} flags=0 reason=SHOW_SOFT_INPUT
D/InsetsController(32033): show(ime(), fromIme=true)
```

Here's the call stack:
```
I/flutter (32033): updateEditingValue
I/flutter (32033): _formatAndSetValue
I/flutter (32033): _handleSelectionChanged
I/flutter (32033): requestKeyboard
I/flutter (32033): _openInputConnection
D/InputMethodManager(32033): showSoftInput() view=io.flutter.embedding.android.FlutterView{c4be627 VFE...... .F...... 0,0-1080,2214 #1 aid=1073741824} flags=0 re
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirmed the issue: every character typed calls `requestKeyboard` → `_openInputConnection` → `showSoftInput()` on Android, producing noisy logs. @danagbemava-nc confirmed reproduction. Commenters reported related symptoms (keyboard popping up then hiding, requiring many taps to focus). No root cause analysis or fix mentioned in comments; issue remains open and stalled.

title: Add debug tooling for focus and IME connections
body:
As I'm working with user interfaces that have more complex focus and IME interactions, I'm finding it difficult to track associated changes.

I think it would be helpful to be able to visualize the focus path, from root, to the widget with primary focus. For example, all widgets in the focus path might be given a border and a tiny number in the corner that shows the tree distance from the root. The widget with primary focus could have a special color.

Also, I'd like to be able to track changes to the IME connection. For example, is an IME connection open right now? Ideally, I'd also like to know which widget owns that IME connection. That later part is more difficult, because there's no requirement that a widget own the IME connection. So maybe that requires an opt-in API. Nonetheless, when debugging issues at moments where one widget's IME connection closes and another is supposed to open, it would be helpful to have some quick toggle-able visual indicators through Flutter Inspec
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Flutter run key f to dump focus tree was added in PR #123473. A DevTools issue (#5326) tracks adding focus tooling in DevTools. debugDumpFocusTree and debugFocusChanges boolean were noted as existing tools.

title: [Flutter Web][Android Chrome] ListView freeze after scroll in IFrame
comment_summary: Narrowed to Flutter web in an iframe on Android Chrome physical devices. After scrolling in a ListView or SingleChildScrollView the scrollable area loses gesture hit-testing; sibling widgets outside the scroll area remain interactive. Workaround: remove Flutter from the iframe entirely. No fix PR; stalled with no ETA.

title: TalkBack includes labelText and hintText into text field length
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.3 and master; on master the character count announcement was removed entirely (a different regression). Issue affects Android (TalkBack). CC'd to @chunhtai and @hangyujin. Status: open, stalled, no fix.

title: iOS obscureText input security features
body:
## Use case

It seems that since probably iOS 13+ the system actively protects users from showing obscured text like passwords and related stuff like keyboards in screenshots, recordings, screen sharing etc. I could not find some official paper with specifications, but from testing and sources like https://www.reddit.com/r/iphone/comments/eqox55/if_you_go_into_your_passwords_and_try_to/ (screenshot), https://discussions.apple.com/thread/251035283 (video), https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/116777, etc. I figured out:

for screenshots, recording, screen sharing on iOS the apps while obscureText=true:
1. hide keyboards
2. hide content of obscureText (passwords) inputs
3. immediately when the state of the input changes (aka user clicked on "eye" icon etc), it switches

This works for all native apps I tested including settings, Twitter, Instagram, ...

It seems that Flutter only supports the 1. thing, aka keyboard is hidden while.

## Proposal

Explore more about the
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause discussed: native UIKit apps create a UITextField, set isSecureEntry=false, and grab its layer to hide content from screenshots/recordings. Flutter needs to implement similar layer-based hiding. Keyboard hiding while obscured already works, but password content hiding and dynamic switching do not.

title: Flutter web application wierd behaviour regarding keyboard. (Android web broswer & IOS Web Broswer)
comment_summary: Triage confirms the white-space flicker after keyboard dismissal reproduces on master (3.7.0-26.0.pre.15) on both iOS and Android (HTML and CanvasKit renderers); the lag is described as taking a couple of seconds for the UI to update. The issue was filed January 2023 and commenters note it is still unresolved as of early 2024, citing lack of web team attention. No fix or PR referenced; status is stalled waiting on team-web.

title: Embedding flutter web app using an iframe causes zoom issues with keyboard input in IOS Safari
comment_summary: One commenter provides a JavaScript workaround for the index.html that adds a `touchstart` listener and prevents double-tap zoom specifically for iPad/iPhone user-agents. A second commenter resolves the zoom issue by adding `maximum-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no` to the viewport meta tag. No root cause analysis or PR is mentioned in the comments; the issue has a workaround but no framework-level fix.

title: SingleActivator doesn't recognize shortcuts with  some 'alt  + key' combinations
body:
## Use case

A SingleActivator created as:
```
SingleActivator(
   LogicalKeyboardKey.keyG,
   meta: true,
   alt: true,
)
```
doesn't work. Because `alt + G` produces `©`,  and SingleActivator uses `RawKeyEvent` under the hood, the activator expects the logical key for `©`, not `G`. 

A workaround for this, would be to use the `CharacterActivator` as:
```
CharacterActivator(
   '©',
   meta: true,
   alt: true,
)
```

but this doesn't cover the case where you want to use the actual G key.


## Proposal

Create a new type of activator that uses the new `KeyEvent` class, which will do the right thing.
comment_summary: Comments confirm the `CharacterActivator` workaround is functional but platform-dependent. A proper fix requires an activator using `KeyEvent` (layout-agnostic) rather than `RawKeyEvent`. Cross-platform portability of such shortcuts was noted as the core problem.

I have been using [maestro](https://maestro.mobile.dev/) for testing my apps and using accessibility info in `maestro studio` but this can be accessed in normal `adb` `uiautomatorviewer` or any other tools on top of that.

1. Try Flutter Gallery app built with Flutter 3.3 
2. Try Flutter Gallery app built with Flutter 3.7
3. 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: Flutter 3.7 changed the text field's accessibility label from `text` to `hint` on Android, which `uiautomatorviewer` and tools built on UIAutomator cannot query via `AccessibilityNodeInfo.getHintText()`. An internal Android bug `b/267640877` was filed. Maestro worked around the issue on their side via PR. Status: upstream Android UIAutomator limitation; workaround exists in Maestro.

title: TextInputClient updateEditingValue giving back collapsed selection on Android emulator
body:
I'm trying to write a Rich Text Editor, but have problems with the text input connection. I'm using `ExtendSelectionByCharacterIntent` to expand my selection to an uncollapsed one, but always get back the collapsed from remote. I've managed to create a little sample that should explain the problem...

I'm using the Android emulator on Android Studio.

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample
2. Press Shift+RightArrow to expand selection
3. See console log

**Expected results:**
[log] Updating remote with current value:
[log] TextEditingValue(text: ┤Dummy value├, selection: TextSelection(baseOffset: 0, extentOffset: 1, isDirectional: false), composing: TextRange(start: 0, end: 5))
[log] Received update from remote:
[log] TextEditingValue(text: ┤Dummy value├, selection: TextSelection(baseOffset: 0, extentOffset: 1, isDirectional: false), composing: TextRange(start: 0, end: 5))

**Actual results:**
[log] Updating remote with current value:
[log] TextEditingValue(text: ┤
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug reproduces on stable and master when using `ExtendSelectionByCharacterIntent` on Android (emulator and physical devices). The Android IME sends back multiple `updateEditingValue` calls that collapse the selection after the client sets an uncollapsed one; this also occurs with the delta API. No fix or PR is mentioned. A follow-up comment noted the bug also affects Korean backspace with the delta model. Status: stalled, awaiting further investigation.

title: Allow `MenuAnchor` created menus to traverse the submenu via keyboard.
body:
Currently, if you create a menu using `MenuAnchor`, and you tab to the button, you can activate it, but you can't traverse to the submenu using the keyboard.  This is because the submenu is in the overlay, and the button isn't handling the key events.

I'm not quite sure what the fix for this is, because it may be that the button itself has to add a shortcut for the arrow and tab keys to get to the submenu. Or maybe it just needs to focus the first item in the submenu when activated.
comment_summary: Arrow-key traversal into MenuAnchor submenus works on non-web platforms but not on web because web shortcuts dispatch ScrollIntent for arrow keys instead of DirectionalFocusIntent. Tab traversal into menus is broken on all platforms. A workaround with custom Actions/DirectionalFocusMenuAction was provided. PR #177159 (draft) was filed by @davidhicks980 and is being finalized.

title: [Android] Add spell check devicelab test with custom IME
body: The Android spell check devicelab test is currently [flaky](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/113710) because Gboard learns the misspelled words used in the test due to the [federated learning](https://support.google.com/assistant/answer/11140942#federated_learning&zippy=%2Cfederated-learning) technique it uses. In order to fix the flakiness, a custom IME should be created and used in the test with the `adb shell ime` command to select it.
comment_summary: PR #120144 removed the integration test that this issue was intended to de-flake. To implement the custom IME workaround, that removal would first need to be reverted, making this issue blocked on that decision.

title: [iOS] Show spell check toolbar when caret touches misspelled word
body:
On iOS, a spell check toolbar containing suggestions for misspelled words should show when the caret moves to touch a misspelled word (and only if it is misspelled). Thus, to follow the behavior of iOS, the ~~Cupertino style~~ spell check suggestions toolbar should show when the caret moves to touch a word, and disappear when the caret moves away.

Fixing this depends on https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/119537. Part of https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/34688.
comment_summary: Comments confirm the behavior should be platform-wide on iOS (not just for Cupertino widgets). A video demonstration was provided.

title: Make keyboard testing more user-friendly when physical keys are missing
body:
Users often want to test keys that are not directly available on a US keyboard, such as the `Zoom In` key or the `+` key. When the user uses these keys, like

```
await tester.sendKeyDownEvent(LogicalKeyboardKey.zoomIn);
```

The current testing framework throws an exception:

> LogicalKeyboardKey#4cf4c(keyId: "0x10000050d", keyLabel: "Zoom In", debugName: "Zoom In") not found in android physical key map.

This error is frustrating since it does not tell the user how to fix their testing code, despite that their demand feels valid.

 The workaround is to send an arbitrary existing physical key, such as

```
await tester.sendKeyDownEvent(LogicalKeyboardKey.zoomIn, physicalKey: PhysicalKeyboardKey.plus);
```

(Ideally we should allow arbitrary dummy physical key, such as `PhysicalKeyboardKey(0x123456)`, but a real physical key is required to generate the raw key data, which we should really deprecate soon.) The physical key does not matter since none of the code should 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Only a bot comment about an irrelevant label; no substantive new information.

title: [iOS] Determine and implement behavior for toggling spell check suggestions toolbar visibility for non-touch gestures
body: On iOS, a tap gesture may be made in different ways, e.g. via a tap or trackpad or stylus, and these gestures [respond to a tap gesture differently](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/329f86a907503568df9731112a2157a2c9722e97/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/text_selection.dart#L2189). The non-touch/unknown gesture will show the spell check suggestions toolbar in the same way that native iOS does after https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/119537 is completed. This issue is to track the implementation for other gestures.
comment_summary: Comments show a support side-thread about spell check not working; reporter was redirected to file a separate issue (#158929). No progress on the actual tracking issue.

title: Extending to paragraph/or word boundary on MacOS should default to the `downstream` position when at a word wrap
body:
When using keyboard shortcuts on MacOS
* shift + option + up/down to extend to the next/previous paragraph boundary
* shift + option + left/right to extend to the next/previous word boundary

And the caret is positioned at a word wrap initially, and the selection is extended, when returning to the original position (collapsing at the base when the selection inverts) the resulting selection should to default to the `downstream`  position.

Current Behavior:
The selection inherits the `affinity` from `selection.base.affinity`.

Expected Behavior:
The selection defaults to a `downstream` affinity.

Expected|Actual
---|---
<video src="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/948037/215957886-fbafa1e7-8b3a-4df4-8403-f88dc84e372d.mov" />|<video src="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/948037/215958318-7da3618b-2362-4791-97f9-7c3c19e97b33.mov" />
comment_summary: Comments point to PR #116549 discussion for context.

title: Flutter 3.7 breaks TextField widgets that wrap MaterialApp.router
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->
After updating to Flutter 3.7, when a TextField gains focus, the keyboard appears but then immediately is dismissed. This issue was originally reported [here](https://github.com/wiredashio/wiredash-sdk/issues/262). I have confirmed that this issue does not occur in Flutter v3.3. This bug only occurs when a Router is used and only seems to occur for TextFields that are outside the `MaterialApp` in the widget tree. It is _not_ specific to the [wiredash](https://pub.dev/packages/wiredash) package. I've tested it with [flappy](https://pub.dev/packages/flappy) and the same thing happens.

1. Download the [Wiredash sample repo](https://github.com/percula/wiredash_test)
2. Run the sample on an iOS or Android device. There is no need to provide the Wiredash `projectId` or `secret`.
3. Tap the FAB to bring up the Wiredash interface
4. Try to type in the `TextField`

**Expected 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments identify the regression as introduced by flutter/flutter#109702, which removed the embedded `FocusScope` from `Navigator`, causing `WidgetsApp`/`MediaQuery` rebuilds to request focus and dismiss the keyboard on TextFields outside `MaterialApp`. The fix commit is a45727d81360b278cc51c5a9ae429529038cf069, which was present on master but not yet cherry-picked to stable at time of discussion. @gspencergoog provided a workaround of wrapping the app in a `FocusScope`. Users on Flutter 3.10 and 3.16 still report the issue, and some report remaining stalled on Flutter 3.3 to avoid it; no dedicated cherry-pick confirmation appears in the thread.

title: The operation of converting from Japanese strings to symbols on certain Android devices is bad
body:

-->
Using TextField on Samsung devices, the behavior when converting from Japanese to 2-letter symbols is bad.
The same phenomenon did not occur when I used Google Gboard, so I think this is a problem specific to Samsung's software keyboard .


## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample <!-- (see "Code sample" section below) -->

...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: when the Samsung keyboard selects a suggestion like `[]`, it sends a batch edit containing (1) `commitText` replacing the composing text and (2) a `KEYCODE_DPAD_LEFT` key event to position the cursor inside the brackets. Key events are not currently treated as part of a batch edit, so the left-arrow arrives at the framework before the text replacement, causing state corruption. Analysis was documented in a shared Google doc. Issue was investigated by @Renzo-Olivares and handed off to @LongCatIsLooong for a more thorough fix requiring a non-trivial refactor. Status: assigned then unassigned, no PR landed, stalled.

title: [android] Textfield text selection handlers get covered by keyboard
comment_summary: Comments confirm this is Android-specific; a triager compared with Telegram (handles above keyboard) vs. iOS apps (handles can go below keyboard). A Flutter engineer and an Android engineer both concluded drawing over the keyboard is not straightforwardly possible from the Flutter view layer, and that native Android also allows handles to hang off screen. No fix planned; the suggested workaround is to keep the text field away from the screen edge.

title: [Android] Support Samsung spell checker for spell check on text input
body:
Currently, spell check on Android does not support the Samsung spell checker by default as it only currently supports the Gboard Latin-based IME (see the [check for this](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/main/shell/platform/android/io/flutter/embedding/android/FlutterView.java#L1469)). Besides modifying this check, support for the Samsung spell checker will require changes to the spell check logic; https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/119868 and https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/117167#issuecomment-1407247309 describe the case where the spell check results of the Samsung spell checker are being improperly interpreted.

Thus, to support this spell checker:

- [ ] Modify the `nativeSpellCheckServiceDefined` engine parameter to be true when the Samsung spell checker is used.
- [ ] Ensure that the Samsung spell checker results can be used to correctly render text.
comment_summary: Comments note `nativeSpellCheckServiceDefined` may already be true for Samsung, and PR #40924 was filed to suppress incorrect behavior as a short-term fix.

title: [web] ☂️ Enhancing Flutter Web's text editing system by using Platform Views
body:
# Objective
One of our core goals is to make Flutter Web feel more idiomatic and natural on the web. A key part of this is to align the behavior of our text inputs to be more consistent with how text inputs behave on the web. This has major implications given how many impactful features branch out of a simple text box (spellcheck, autofill, etc) that web users are conditioned to expect to work.  So when text inputs don’t work on Flutter Web apps exactly the way they do elsewhere on the web, it has a glaring impact on UX.  

# Problems

### Clashing styles
We currently delegate all of the rendering of text inputs to the framework rather than the browser.
* When an `<input>` element is created, we apply CSS to make these elements and aspects of these elements (content, selection caret, selection highlight, etc) invisible.  By resetting the browser’s default styling, we enable Flutter to have full control over the look and feel of our text inputs.
* This has the potential to cause some is
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments debate whether delegating text rendering to platform views is too large a departure from Flutter's pixel-perfect rendering philosophy. The team clarified the plan is not to drop TextField but to integrate platform views inside the current system, letting the browser handle content rendering, selection, carets, and context menus while the framework handles surrounding layout. Rich text editor maintainers (super_editor, Fleather) note their projects use only TextInputConnection so they would not be broken, but concern remains about cases needing custom rendering. A formal design proposal was published at flutter.dev/go/web-native-text-editing. The assignee was removed after a long period with no updates, and a community member noted multiline textfields remain unusable on web iOS and Android.

title: MacOS TextField does not work if window style is changed

title: [iOS] - Dictation deltas don't respect app-side content updates
body:
On iOS, when using dictation, the IME deltas that are dispatched to apps are delivered in a way that causes the app to get out of sync with the dictation system.

I've described the issue as it presents in Super Editor: https://github.com/superlistapp/super_editor/issues/1005

The summary of the issue is: The dictation system sends us one delta at a time, but the dictation system has already assembled multiple deltas that depend on each other. In cases where multiple deltas relate to each other, we're supposed to get the whole set of deltas at once. Instead, we get the deltas one at a time, which is supposed to let us immediately send our own app-side updates to the IME to alter the content (if we choose to) before the next delta comes in. But the next delta that comes in is based on the content of the first delta, which is now out of date.

Flutter version:
```
Flutter 3.8.0-12.0.pre.57 • channel master • https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git
Framework • revision 480c54c370 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The core technical finding from comments is that on iOS, dictation sends individual deltas sequentially, but each delta assumes the previous delta's result without waiting for app-side modifications, making it impossible for apps like super_editor to inject corrections between deltas. Renzo-Olivares investigated iOS dictation APIs and found no reliable way to detect dictation start/end on iOS 16+. Engine PR #43267 (flutter/engine) was identified as providing a positive improvement, with one user confirming significantly better behavior after it landed in beta, though extra spaces were still being added. The issue remains partially open with the assignee removed due to inactivity.

In 3.7.3, the regression worsened — the left arrow key now also deletes text preceding the composing range, not just text inside it. A separate sub-issue about the macOS error sound on arrow key press was requested to be filed separately. No PR or fix referenced; stalled.

title: [iOS ]When external keyboard on iPad resizeToAvoidBottomInset resizes scaffold by keyboard/dictionary popup
comment_summary: Root cause identified as the iOS keyboard notifications treating the floating shortcuts bar as "docked" on iOS 16 but as "floating" on iOS 15, causing the Flutter view to resize for the bar's height. @vashworth confirmed this is not intended and needs a fix. A comment references the relevant engine code in FlutterViewController.mm. Team iOS later flagged that fixing this requires an engine-side change to provide a different viewInset for the popup.

title: Flutter form has issues with TextFormField getting focus when voiceover is on

6. The result is not consistent. The TextFormFields randomly get/don't get focus

The same can be reproduced on the flutter gallery demo app
https://gallery.flutter.dev/#/demo/text-field

comment_summary: Comments confirmed the VoiceOver focus issue is web-only (works on iOS and Android). A workaround was identified: navigating outside the web page and back restores focus. A later commenter observed that typed text appears in the accessibility node but not in the visible TextField, indicating a disconnect between the accessibility tree and the rendered text field. No fix or PR linked; stalled with team-web.

title: Editable Text after clear has issue with buffer korean input
body:
$ flutter --version
Flutter 3.7.4 • channel stable • https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git
Framework • revision b4bce91dd0 (3 days ago) • 2023-02-21 09:50:50 +0800
Engine • revision 248290d6d5
Tools • Dart 2.19.2 • DevTools 2.20.1

I have created a main.dart with the minimal steps to produce this issue but basically this might be related specifically to the way the Korean Keyboard has to build each character in a syllable. In a single syllable, the character can be made of 2 to 5 korean letters equivalent to the english alphabet. In the example syllable 광 (gwang), it is made up of 4 letters  (ㄱ ㅗ ㅏ ㅇ) but the keyboard has to keep a buffer of collected letters to build up to the full character. When the clear is called without clearing the buffer and breaking it, it seems to cause weird input until you deselect and re-select the Editable Text widget.

In the following modified Flutter Demo main project, you enter the text and when it is equal to the text to enter, the clear is 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause confirmed in comments: the Windows (and Linux) IME keeps an internal composition buffer; calling TextEditingController.clear() without first breaking the composition (unfocus/refocus) leaves stale buffer state that injects spurious characters on subsequent input. The issue reproduces on Windows and Chrome-on-Windows but not macOS or Chrome-on-macOS. The workaround is to call focusNode.unfocus() before clearing, then requestFocus() in a post-frame callback. A later comment confirms the bug also affects Linux. Status is open with a known workaround.

3. Scratch words or draw the vertical line between characters.

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
When scratching words, then it is removed from TextFormFie
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The issue proved difficult to reproduce: one triage member could not reproduce on iPad Air M1 with 2nd gen Apple Pencil, and another could not reproduce on iPad Pro with 1st gen Pencil on iOS 16.1.1 or 16.3.1. The original reporter confirmed the bug persisted on master on an iPad Air 3rd gen (non-M1) with 1st gen Pencil, suggesting a device-specific factor. A later comment identified a new related variant where using `supportedLocales` breaks Scribble with `maxLines: 1`, while using `locale` directly works; emoji also fail with a console error `[PencilTextInput] ResultCommand: Could not process result replacing range because it is out of bounds`, pointing to a possible extended grapheme cluster handling bug in the Scribble PRs (#97437, engine #24224). A final comment from the reporter on 3.19.9 stated the original issue is fixed.

title: url_launcher: After returning from launchUrl, there is a gap/whitespace at bottom of Flutter app if the other app opened the keyboard
comment_summary: Root cause identified as incorrect keyboard frame data from UIKeyboardFrameEndUserInfoKey when the Flutter app is in a small Stage Manager window near the top of the screen. Comments link to specific engine checks at FlutterViewController.mm that fail to correctly determine whether the other app opened the keyboard. The issue is iOS 13.0–13.3 specific and related to the checks documented in a Google doc on keyboard inset cases.

title: Can not disable spellcheck for TextField in FireFox
body: ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample (open it in FireFox browser) 2. Tap on TextField **Expected results:** - Browser doesn't highlight anything
comment_summary: Root cause identified: Firefox enables spellcheck on `textarea` elements by default while Chrome disables it, causing inconsistent behavior. Flutter does not set the `spellcheck` HTML attribute on the underlying textarea, so Chrome silently disables it but Firefox enables it. The proper fix requires Flutter to control the `spellcheck` attribute via `SpellCheckConfiguration`; long-term this needs engine-level work. No workaround exists from pure Dart; the JS workaround of setting `spellcheck="false"` directly works but requires a plugin.

title: Enabling Textfield.obscureText without does not disable autocomplete until the textfield loses and regains focus
body:
[Touch Bar](https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/use-the-touch-bar-mchlbfd5b039/mac) by default shows typing suggestions. This happens also for Flutter `TextField`s which have `obscureText` set to `true`. 

`obscureText` is usually set for input fields with sensitive content, such as passwords and pins. Showing this content in clear text could be considered to be a security risk.

This issue can be reproduced on MacBooks with Touch Bar or on MacBooks without Touch Bar with  Touch Bar simulation turned on (XCode -> Window -> Touch Bar menu).

## Steps to Reproduce
1. run the attached sample code
2. write in the text field
3. click the eye icon to reveal the text
4. click out of the text field and back to the text field
   - now you see your the clear text in the text field and in the touch bar
5. click the eye icon to obscure the text
   - now the text in the text field is obscured
6. continue typing (don't click anywhere else)

**Expected:** the touch bar does not s
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified by @cbracken: changing obscureText while a TextInputConnection is open does not call TextInput.updateConfig to sync with the embedder, affecting all platforms. Engine PR #39460 fixed the initial-obscureText case but not mid-session toggling. Workaround: set AutofillHints.password (activates Android Autofill Service, which may be undesirable). Proposed fix: sync all TextInputConfiguration fields to engine on any change; @cbracken had a macOS engine implementation ready. No cherry-pick to stable planned.

title: [local_auth] [Windows] TextField does not accept any input after local authentication
comment_summary: Root cause is that the local_auth Windows plugin opens a UAC-style biometric dialog (via `IUserConsentVerifierInterop::RequestVerificationForWindowAsync`) which takes focus away from the Flutter window without restoring it. A commenter proposes calling `SetFocus(child_content_)` or sending `WM_ACTIVATE` after authentication completes. Workaround using the `window_manager` package (blur, focus, setAlwaysOnTop toggle) is confirmed working by multiple users. No official fix has been merged; issue is stalled waiting on the Windows team or local_auth plugin owners.

When using `TextField(textInputAction: TextInputAction.done)`, if you run your app in the Android emulator, the Enter key on the on-screen keyboard will change into a checkmark. **Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->

Sinc
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: using `keyboardType: TextInputType.multiline` suppresses the `done` action from the physical Enter key, even when `maxLines: 1`. This is reproducible on both stable and master. The issue may be intentional behavior, but documentation should clarify that `TextInputType.multiline` prevents Enter from triggering `onSubmitted` regardless of `maxLines`. Status: open, stalled; no PR.

title: Moving cursor while composing should stay in composing region
body:
When there's an active composing region for a multi-step IME (like for Chinese or Japanese), if the user moves the cursor (aka the caret) around, it should stay clamped within the composing region. This is what iOS and Android do for native text inputs. (With a few exceptions, that is; details below.)

This applies to moving the cursor by tapping inside the text field, and on iOS applies also to using the "floating cursor" feature by long-pressing the space bar and dragging around.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Go to a text input field in a Flutter app, such as the code sample below, on iOS or Android.
2. Select (for example) a Japanese kana keyboard. Type some text, for example "あかさあかさあかさ". Hit the big "確定" key on iOS, or "enter" key with Gboard on Android, to end composing and confirm your input.
3. Put the cursor in the middle of the text you've typed. Type some more text, for example "たなたな". Leave the input in a still-composing state.
4. Try to move the cursor beyond the comp
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments add detailed documentation of the expected native behavior: iOS always clamps the cursor to the composing region (with one edge case where tapping after the region sometimes cancels composing), and Android/Gboard shows the cursor in the wrong visual position but internally keeps it within the composing region. No fix or PR mentioned.
