That's a problem, because sometimes only the appearance of the keyboard makes the screen scrollable. The disappearance of the keyboard upon scrolling then makes it impossible to scroll TextFields into view.

This only happens for web apps. When I build the same app for the iPad, the TextField keeps its focus when scrolling.

##
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirmed the bug on web for both Android and iOS touch devices; the dismissal occurs specifically during fast or in-progress scrolls. A workaround was found: wrapping the form in `TextFieldTapRegion` prevents the unintended focus loss. Multiple commenters confirmed the workaround works. No upstream fix or PR linked; stalled with team-web.

title: [iPad] GestureDetector gets stuck in a "pressed" state if pressed when the keyboard is hiding
comment_summary: Reproduction confirmed on iPad only (not iPhone). The bug is that when a button is pressed while the keyboard is dismissing, the `GestureDetector` gets stuck in pressed state and the `onPressed` handler never fires. The issue persists as of Flutter 3.38.9. No root cause identified in comments; status is stalled.

title: Resolve Text Input Code Divergence
body:
I was looking to use `CupertinoTextField` on iOS and `TextField` on Android for my app. The API differences between the two widgets for seemingly similar concepts feel rather unnecessary. Here are some examples:

- To add a piece of hint text, I need to supply that through the `decoration` property on `TextField` but the `placeholder` property on `CupertinoTextField`. 
- The `decoration` property on `TextField` takes an `InputDecoration`, but the same property on `CupertinoTextField` takes a `BoxDecoration`.
- To add a prefix icon, I can use the `prefixIcon` property on `InputDecoration`, which I then pass to the `decoration` property on `TextField`. However, I need to use the `prefix` property on `CupertinoTextField` to achieve a similar result. 

Such differences make it difficult to 1) reason about how to use `CupertinoTextField` based on my knowledge about `TextField`, and 2) minimize platform-specific code when showing one of the two widgets based on the OS. 

It could be 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Discussion includes a DartPad demo showing M3 TextField customized to look like CupertinoTextField. Spellcheck API on iOS is noted as broken with TextField (word suggestions overflow). Team members express interest in synchronizing TextField and CupertinoTextField APIs or building a Material-based adaptive TextField.

title: Keyboard type switches back during typing
comment_summary: The root cause was identified: when a `TextInputFormatter` modifies the composing region, the framework signals the Android `InputMethodManager` to restart the input connection, causing the keyboard to switch back. Confirmed on both emulator and physical devices (Gboard and Samsung keyboards). The pattern requires typing enough characters to cross a block boundary in the formatter. A workaround is `keyboardType: TextInputType.visiblePassword`. Assigned to @LongCatIsLooong but subsequently unassigned after inactivity; stalled.

title: [Android] Keyboard not showing at startup on TextField has `autofocus: true`
comment_summary: The issue is Android-specific and confirmed not reproducible on iOS. Comments narrow it to Samsung devices (S10+, S20 Ultra, A52S, A54, S21fe) running Android 12–13, with logs showing the IME reporting `servedView != view` when the keyboard is requested at cold-start. Later comments confirm the bug also manifests in release builds but not debug builds, and that a hot-restart or a second run resolves it, suggesting a race condition during app startup before the view is fully served to the IME.

title: CJK word boundaries
body:
Moving the cursor by word does not produce correct results when the text contains Chinese characters.  Currently, it seems to move character-by-character, but instead it should move word by word.

| Incorrect Flutter behavior | Correct Mac behavior |
| --- | --- |
| <video src="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/389558/213824760-b7b35d2b-f762-4dfa-92c3-c1d6de71617e.webm)" />| <video src="https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/389558/226982482-9a99d1ba-ddf4-420b-adaf-1af56d906b2a.mov" /> |

### Steps to reproduce

  1. Run an app with a text input, such as the one below.
  2. Enter some Chinese text containing words that are more than 1 character in length, such as the example text below.
  3. Press control+arrow left/right (or option+arrow left/right on Mac).

Expected results: The cursor moves left/right to the nearest word boundary.
Actual results: The cursor moves by one character.

<details>

<summary>Example app</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flut
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue also affects Japanese text identically. Platform breakdown: Flutter web already handles CJK word breaking correctly (uses the engine's `word_breaker.dart` which implements full Unicode TR29 rules); macOS/Windows/Linux move only one character at a time. Commenters discuss using the engine's web `WordBreaker` as a shared implementation, and CLDR-based script-property rules (e.g., `ja.xml` Hiragana/Ideographic rules) as a dictionary-free approximation for CJK. A commenter notes that newer ICU data tables adopted for desktop may make proper handling more feasible, and that the same problem affects word selection by double-tap/long-press on mobile. No PR; status is open and stalled.

title: Triple tap gestures in a TextField are blocked by selection handles overlay in some situations
body:
# Problem: 
On certain platforms like iOS, when the selection handles overlay pop up on a `double-tap`, a third tap might be prevented from being detected by the `TextSelectionGestureDetector` if the consecutive tap occurs directly where the selection handles might appear.

## Flow of a triple-tap not on a word edge:
When double tapping a word, the word is selected. When this happens the selection handles overlay is shown. This Overlay contains a GestureDetector that handles dragging and tapping on the selection handles.

If a triple tap is attempted, the same sequence occurs and the word is selected and the selection handle overlay pops up on the second tap. On the third tap the paragraph is selected.

## Triple-tap at a word edge issue:
If a triple tap is attempted at the edge of a word, where the selection handles overlay pop up on a double tap, they may block the TextSelectionGestureDetector below it from receiving the third tap. The TextSelectionGestureDetector will not r
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the user-visible impact is essentially 'triple-tap does not work' especially on short words or single characters. Reproducible by triple-clicking a single letter on iOS. Two proposed solutions involve using IgnorePointer or a pass-through widget during double-tap to allow the third tap to reach TextSelectionGestureDetector.

comment_summary: Issue is specific to Chromebook Android (Lenovo Duet); pressing the Control key generates a Num Lock key event alongside it. Confirmed not reproducible on standard Android phone or with a physical keyboard on a non-Chromebook device. A later comment reports the same issue on a Chromecast keyboard. No resolution or root cause confirmed; stalled waiting for Chromebook device access.

title: SelectableText maxLines should behave the same as Text
comment_summary: A triager confirmed the bug on stable 3.7.8 and master 3.9. `SelectableText` uses `EditableText` internally, so `maxLines` reserves the full height rather than fitting content, unlike `Text`. Workaround is to use `SelectionArea` instead. Issue kept open as a real bug; likely to be fixed only when `SelectableText` is deprecated. Status: stalled.

title: Merge different strategies for building TextSpans with spell check results
body: As of https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/123481, there will be two separate strategies used to build `TextSpan`s used for text field widgets with spell check results marked. These should be merged for efficiency and clarity.
comment_summary: One comment identifies the two strategies (`_buildSubtreeWithComposingRegion` and `_buildSubtreeWithoutComposingRegion`) that could be merged by dropping composing region consideration and using only cursor position.

title: Selection cursor over WidgetSpan
comment_summary: Comments confirm the selection cursor (text cursor) still appears over `WidgetSpan` children even when wrapped in `SelectionContainer.disabled`, reproduced on master as of stable 3.7 and master 3.9. The root cause is that the outer `Text` widget wraps the entire content (including `WidgetSpan`) with a `MouseRegion` that sets the text cursor. Proposed fix: have `SelectionContainer.disabled` wrap its child with a `MouseRegion` to override the cursor; a workaround is to wrap the `WidgetSpan` child with a `MouseRegion(cursor: SystemMouseCursors.basic)`. Status: open, no PR, workaround available.

5 !!!!!!  issue: you will see cursor does not end up where the  tap occurred. bounces to other places, with underlines and confused attempts at hi-lighting I guess

6 this is just one environment to demo this iss
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: both Flutter and the underlying <textarea> element update the selection simultaneously on scroll, causing the cursor to jump to the pre-scroll tap position rather than the current one. A fix for Flutter Web on Android/Chrome was merged via engine PR #41202, resolving cursor placement on Android Chrome. The iOS/Safari fix remained outstanding and was tracked as part of a larger effort at issue #120613; as of the latest comments, long-press with the magnifier is the only reliable cursor placement mechanism on iOS. Issue is partially fixed (Android) and still open for iOS.

title: Not able to fetch placeholder test using appium inspector during automation testing
body: I am using flutter 3.7.6 version and Appium Inspector Version: 1.22.3-4.20220515.3 , While we are trying to fetch the text placeholder from any of the text field than no value is coming. It was available in the earlier version (up to 3.7.4). Below is the image of error for your reference.
comment_summary: Comments confirm the regression on Android in Flutter 3.7.6+: Appium/UIAutomator2 can no longer read hint text from TextFields because Flutter intentionally moved the hint text to the `hintText` accessibility property (as opposed to `value`) to fix TalkBack behavior, but UIAutomator2 does not expose `hintText`. The team determined the real fix requires UIAutomator2 to expose `hintText`, and an issue was filed against UIAutomator2. A Flutter-side workaround wrapping `TextField` in a `Semantics` widget with `container: true`, `explicitChildNodes: true`, and a `label` was provided and confirmed to work.

title: Integrate inputAccessoryView capabilities on iOS/iPadOS/macOS/tvOS
body:
## Use case

On iOS/iPadOS/macOS/tvOS, it is possible to make use of the `inputAccessoryView` property of `UIResponder` (https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uiresponder/1621119-inputaccessoryview) to attach a custom view on top of the OS keyboard.

This is the way to correctly attach an element natively on top of the keyboard and produce a native look&feel.

## Proposal

Since the framework itself is providing the `TextField` and `TextFormField` widgets, which will trigger the keyboard to appear if focused, the implementation of the native component should include the property `inputAccessoryView`.
comment_summary: Comments argue the `keyboard_actions` package workaround produces poor UX in Cupertino apps. A request was added to also expose iOS `inputView` (not just `inputAccessoryView`). The issue was reopened after initially being closed.

title: `DesktopTextSelectionToolbar` doesn't support keyboard navigation
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on macOS desktop. The discussion explores using parentNode on Focus widgets to keep the TextField focused while toolbar buttons also receive focus, but @gspencergoog and @justinmc found that Shortcuts traversal always starts from the parent Focus, preventing key events from reaching the child. The technical approach of using an unfocusable bridge Focus widget with parentNode was proposed. Currently stalled with no PR.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run code sample
2. Write text into `TextFormField`
3. Error: Show error even `autovalidateMode` on TextFormField is disabled

### Expected results

When `TextFormField` has the property `autovalidateMode` with value **AutovalidateMode.disabled**, the field won't show the error, event with property `autovalidateMode` with **AutovalidateMode.always** or **AutovalidateMode.onUserInteraction** on `Form` parent widget

This would be _follow the Flutter's inherited widget convention were the innermost takes priority_ (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/107350#issuecomment-1295694555)

### Actual results

When `Form` parent widget has property `autovalidateMode` with **AutovalidateMode.always** or **AutovalidateMode.onUs
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug is reproducible and explain why a simple fix is not straightforward: `AutovalidateMode.disabled` is the default value for `FormField`, so checking for it to skip validation would break all `FormField` widgets wrapped in a validating `Form`. Comments conclude that adding a new `AutovalidateMode` enum value (to explicitly opt out of parent `Form` validation) is the correct approach. No PR linked; issue is open.

title: PageView shows old page data if TextField inside its pages still keeps the focus while navigating between pages
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [x] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Introduction:

Pageview shows old page data. 

When I make a list of pages for a pageview, where page 2 is dynamically chosen based upon the choice on page 1, flutter updates page 2 according expectations. 
However, when you add a textfield to both page2A and page2B (as in the provided example code) and fill it with some text there is an issue where old data is shown. 

### Steps to reproduce:
1. Run the example
2. Swipe to page 2
3. Put in some text into the text field (for example the text page2A) and do not press the keyboard 'done' button. 
4. Swipe back to page 1
5. Toggle the checkbox to toggle page2A vs page2B
6. Swipe to page 2
7. Find your old text typed at step 2 in a different page / widget. 

In my application the problem is even
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments identified that the bug only triggers when navigating away from a page while a TextField still has focus (keyboard not dismissed) — pressing the keyboard Done button before swiping prevents the issue. Confirmed on Android (Samsung Galaxy S22/S9/Note II, Nokia G21, Android emulators) and iOS (iPhone 7, SE 3rd gen, iOS simulator). Two workarounds exist: (1) call `FocusManager.instance.primaryFocus?.unfocus()` in `onPageChanged`, or (2) assign a distinct `TextEditingController` to each page's TextField. The root cause is Flutter reusing widget state across PageView pages when focus is retained, causing the text state of the old TextField to leak into the new page's widget.

title: Global text scaling shouldn't be automatically applied on `WidgetSpan`s as scale transforms
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

With the following `TextSpan`, if the ambient text scale factor is `2.0`, the text "INTERRPUTION" will be 2.0x the size of the surrounding text, 
because text widgets in the framework automatically scale `WidgetSpan`s by the ambient text scale factor (2.0x) by applying a 2x scale transform.  See the [Screenshots or Video](#screenshots-or-video) section
 
```dart 
  MediaQuery(
    data: MediaQueryData(textScaleFactor: 2),
    child: Directionality(
      textDirection: TextDirection.ltr,
      child: Center(
        child: Text.rich(
          TextSpan(
            children: <InlineSpan>[
              const TextSpan(text: 'a '),
              TextSpan(text: 'pebble'),
              const TextSpan(text: ' in the '),
    
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause: WidgetSpan applies the ambient text scale factor as a scale transform to its child, so any Text widget inside the WidgetSpan is scaled by textScaleFactor twice (once by the transform, once internally by the Text widget). Community workarounds provided: a NonScalingWidgetSpan class that wraps with textScaleFactor: 1/ambient, and discussion of a future WidgetSpan.noAutoScale flag. Team discussion concluded that images in WidgetSpan should not auto-scale since textScaleFactor is only for text. No fix or PR landed.

title: Can't scroll left or up with iOS floating cursor (dragging spacebar to move cursor)
body:
Originally reported by @Curvel as part of #89228.

**Repro:**
1. Have a `TextField()`. (Probably an `EditableText` will do.)
1. Use iOS.
1. Type a bunch of text into the input, so that it doesn't fit and has to scroll.
1. Long-press the spacebar, then drag around the keyboard to move the cursor (the "floating cursor" feature; Apple calls it "Turn the onscreen keyboard into a trackpad").
1. Try to move the cursor to the edges of the input, to scroll through the text.

**Expected:**
The text scrolls. This is the native iOS behavior.

**Actual:**
If you move to the end of the visible text — the bottom in a multiline input, or the right/end in a single-line input ­— then it scrolls. But if you move to the beginning — the top in a multiline input, or the left/start in a single-line input — then it doesn't.

### Further context
This is the behavior I see on a recent version from master/main, on iOS 16.

Originally reported in 2021 in #89228, so it isn't new. It's possible d
...[truncated]
comment_summary: One comment identifies the root cause: Flutter only sends positions of on-screen characters to the engine; when the field scrolls during the floating cursor gesture, selection rects become incorrect. A fix would require updating `EditableTextState._updateSelectionRects` and measuring performance impact.

title: [Web]: Incorrect behavior related to TextField maxLines = 1 property
comment_summary: The root cause was identified as a side effect of #119583, which made Flutter select all text on Tab focus to match web browser behavior; this also fires when `requestFocus()` is called programmatically. Confirmed web-only; mobile and desktop behave correctly. A distinction between tab-triggered and programmatic focus is not available through the focus API. Workaround: use `autofocus: true` on the `TextField` instead of `requestFocus` in `initState`. Stalled with team-web.

...[truncated]
comment_summary: Reproduction confirmed on Android and iOS (macOS/web selection handles not visible there). The root cause is that `CompositedTransformFollower` (used for selection handles) inherits the full transform including rotation from its `CompositedTransformTarget`, so handles appear misplaced on rotated text. A commenter found that wrapping the rotated widget in a `CompositedTransformTarget` with a `LayerLink` directly inside the `Transform.rotate` makes the handles appear correctly, though the reporter considered this a workaround rather than the proper fix. Status: open, no PR.

title: [web] - Password managers can't autofill until after focusing on inputs due to lazy-loaded inputs
body:
On the web, password managers have the ability to autofill forms either automatically, or manually through context menus and their chrome extensions. 

Currently, autofill via password managers in Flutter only work through context menus.  This is because input elements are only created after being focused on in our current text editing system. This prevents password managers from filling out forms automatically or through their chrome extensions until after an input in the form is focused on.  

One potential solution being explored to resolve this is https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/120613.
comment_summary: Engine PR #42268 (landed in Flutter 3.13) partially resolved autofill timing; Bitwarden can now autofill via keyboard shortcut, context menu, and extension when a field is focused. Autofill via extension click while no field is focused still fails.

title: Scribble throws `RenderBox was not laid out: RenderFractionalTranslation#23d29 NEEDS-LAYOUT NEEDS-PAINT` when home & initial route are specified
body:
The following error occurs when focusing a `TextField` widget using the Apple Pencil on iPad. The error is not raised when tapping with a finger on the field to use the virtual keyboard. Scribbe does not work when this error is raised.

<details>
<summary>logs</summary>

```
════════ Exception caught by services library ══════════════════════════════════
The following assertion was thrown during method call TextInputClient.requestElementsInRect:
RenderBox was not laid out: RenderFractionalTranslation#23d29 NEEDS-LAYOUT NEEDS-PAINT
'package:flutter/src/rendering/box.dart':
box.dart:1
Failed assertion: line 1966 pos 12: 'hasSize'

2

Either the assertion indicates an error in the framework itself, or we should provide substantially more information in this error message to help you determine and fix the underlying cause.
In either case, please report this assertion by filing a bug on GitHub:
  https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/new?template=2_bug.md

When the ex
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: `isInScribbleRect` is called on TextFields in inactive routes (when both `home` and `initialRoute` are set, two route instances exist and the inactive one's TextField is queried). The fix is to disable Scribble for TextFields that cannot be interacted with. Assigned to @LongCatIsLooong who later indicated it is likely a framework issue; the assignee was eventually removed due to no activity.

title: Previous route in stack gets rebuilt on every frame when virtual keyboard appears/disappears
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new project with the provided code sample
2. Run the project;
3. Click on red container. You will be navigated to the screen with a form;
4. Click on the form in order to bring it in focus/click Done on the keyboard to remove focus;
5. Look inside the console, First screen is being rebuilt on every frame when keyboard appears/disappears.


Seems like using MediaQuery inside the first screen is somehow broken or I'm not using it correctly

### Expected results

I expect the following results:
 **first screen to not rebuild since keyboard is appearing/disappearing on the second screen**

### Actual results

read above

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

* First screen
```dart
imp
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments clarify this is expected behavior per Flutter's InheritedWidget semantics: MediaQuery.of(context) creates a dependency that triggers rebuilds on any viewInsets change (keyboard animation). PR #114459 introduced selective MediaQuery aspects (MediaQuery.sizeOf) as a mitigation. Suggested workarounds: use MediaQuery.sizeOf/viewInsetsOf or LayoutBuilder. Team member suggested closing as won't-fix with possible docs update; issue stayed open with user follow-up through Flutter 3.24.0.

title: Keyboard flickering while moving cursor pointer in Split screen mode

title: When TextField enters Chinese in Windows 10, the candidate list will flicker
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

It is found that when running the Flutter program on Windows 10, when entering Chinese into the TextField component, there will always be flickering shadows in the candidate list of the input method. I have tried other input methods and the same problem occurs.

### Expected results


Under normal circumstances, such flickering shadows should not appear.

### Actual results

Under normal circumstances, there should be no afterimage flickering in the input method candidate list

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

main() {
  runApp(const MaterialApp(
    home: MainPage(),
  ));
}

class MainPage extends StatelessWidget {
  const MainPage({Key? key}) : super(key
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified in desktop triage: the IME candidate list is repositioned asynchronously and sometimes loses a race condition, causing it to appear at 0,0 for one frame before jumping to the correct position. Proposed fix: only reposition the candidate list when the user commits a character or the cursor moves to a new line, not after every keystroke. A web user also reports Chinese IME not following the cursor. Status: stalled, Windows-specific, proposed approach agreed upon but not implemented.

body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

2. Dart 3.0.1

In the old version, when I input Chinese pinyin, it will be triggered
The new version does not work

### Actual results

In the old version, when I input Chinese pinyin, it will be triggered
The new version does not work

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
[Paste your code here]
```

</details>


### Screenshots or Video

<details>
<summary>Screenshots / Video demonstration</summary>

[Upload media here]

</details>


### Logs

<details><summary>Logs</summary>

```console
[Paste your logs here]
```

</details>


### Flutter Doctor output

<details><summary>Doctor output</summary>

```console
[Paste your output here]
```

</detail
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments established that `isComposingRangeValid` behavior is inconsistent across platforms: on iOS it correctly returns `true` only during CJK composition and `false` after candidate confirmation, but on Android it behaves differently depending on whether the user types on the keyboard grid vs. selects from the suggestion bar. On Android with Gboard, typing characters returns `true` even for English, while selecting suggested text returns `false`. A commenter confirmed this is a regression introduced in 3.10.1–3.10.5 specifically for Apple and Google CJK keyboards. A PR (#130081) was filed to address this. A workaround using `WidgetsBinding.instance.addPostFrameCallback` to check `isComposingRangeValid` one frame later was shared but reported as not reliable. A linked issue (#174159) was referenced for a newer related problem.

title: TextField is slow with long text with CanvasKit
body:
## Details

With CanvasKit, setting a `TextEditingController.text` to a string with 270k characters takes 90 seconds including the render.
With HTML renderer, it is takes less than a second although still noticeably slow.

This test app progressively sets the text to 1000, 2000, 3000, 4000, 5000, 6000, 7000, 8000, 9000, and 10000 lines (each 27 characters long) and measures the time before interactive again after each change.

With different renderers, it shows the following time in seconds (ran with `--release`):

```
Lines	1000	2000	3000	4000	5000	6000	7000	8000	9000	10000
HTML	0.111	0.154	0.188	0.211	0.252	0.394	0.447	0.423	0.44	0.491
Canvas	0.806	2.448	5.308	9.267	23.366	32.874	46.939	59.016	73.45	90.266
```

To produce this series, click "1-10k" button.
To measure a single change, click any of the other buttons.

In this video, the text field is set to 3000 lines, which takes almost 6 seconds:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/44893228/9a5b5dc3-8d9e-45
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirmed reproduction on stable/master with CanvasKit on Chrome; Firefox is unaffected. Root cause identified as `Intl.Segmenter` being extremely slow on Chrome—iterating word segments over 3k lines takes ~1 second, scaling quadratically to 15 minutes for 100k lines. The issue is engine-level and was suggested to be tracked via a Chrome bug report. Status: stalled, no PR, previously labeled P1 with no active assignee.

title: Text and TextField with exact same text do overlap on iOS and Android, but not on Web
comment_summary: Comments confirmed reproduction on Android, iOS, macOS, and web (both renderers). Root cause identified as TextField not accounting for characters with diacritics/accents—the text field's bounding box does not encompass the full glyph height, unlike the Text widget. Web offset is a separate rendering difference. Labeled P2 for consistency across platforms. Status: triaged, no PR, stalled.

title: Document intended semantics behavior on each Flutter widget - whether standalone or building block
body:

- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

**Context:** I ran into `TextField` merging its semantics into the parent, resulting into an incorrect semantics tree. This has already been filed as an issues at: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/26336.

**Issue:** One of the challenges I run into with Flutter component documentation is that it's not clear for each widget whether it is intended to be
- **standalone** (and provides `Semantics(container: true)`) or
- a **building block** (provides `Semantics`, but without `container: true`, intending to be merged with its parent)

This has to be determined by trial and error or digging to various abstractions in the code. Sometimes it's clear from the examples given, but this is confusing for cases like `IconButton` whose
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Discussed with @chunhtai who agreed the documentation of standalone vs. building-block widget semantics behavior is needed but has no strategy for a systematic rollout. A code sample was provided showing TextField merging its semantics node with a sibling RichText node (linked to existing issue #26336). Status is stalled; no actionable plan.

Text boxes should still scroll into view when the app is embedded inside a div.

### Actual results

Text boxes remain hidden when app is installed inside a div.

Root cause is that when the Flutter app is embedded in a div (not body), the scroll-into-view mechanism for text fields breaks on iOS when the virtual keyboard opens. Status: triaged for web team, no fix, stalled.

comment_summary: Reproduced on stable. The issue is specific to Safari on iOS physical devices and only occurs when `TextFieldTapRegion` is present; without it the behavior is normal. The issue was assigned to @mdebbar but the assignee was later removed due to no progress. Status: stalled, unassigned.

title: `ensureSemantics()` makes TextField font related properties in dev tools empty
comment_summary: Confirmed on latest stable/master web: when ensureSemantics() is active, the computed font-size in the hidden input element is 13.33px regardless of TextStyle.fontSize. Root impact identified by commenters: iOS Safari auto-zooms inputs with font-size < 16px on focus, causing undesired zoom. The Flutter Inspector shows correct font size; only the DOM attribute is wrong. No fix or PR; stalled.

title: [Web] Unable to type in TextField when ensureSemantics is enabled for accessibility
comment_summary: Two distinct sub-issues identified: (1) `Navigator` placed after `TextField` in the widget tree prevents the engine from ever receiving a semantic tree update containing the text field, so no DOM input element is created on web; a fix PR #138446 was merged for this but was later reverted in #142141. (2) A separate mobile-web issue involving `InputDecoration` with `errorText`/`suffixIcon` and `autofocus: true` causing typing to be impossible on mobile browsers even with semantics enabled; tracked separately in #141975. The engine PR flutter/engine#55152 was suggested as a possible partial fix but a user confirmed reproduction on version 3.41. Issue was autoclosed by bot and reopened; currently still broken with no complete fix.

title: `SelectionArea` supported gestures should have feature parity with `TextSelectionGestureDetector`
body:
`SelectionArea` should have the same supported gestures as `TextSelectionGestureDetector`. This is a pre-requisite for migrating `TextField` from `TextSelectionGestureDetector` to `SelectionArea`. Some of these gestures are also needed for re-implementing `SelectableText` with `SelectionArea` https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/104547.

**Platform Common:**
| TextSelectionGestureDetector | SelectionArea | isEditable/isNonEditable |
| --------------- | --------------- | --------------- |
| Double tap to select a word | https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/149295 ✅ | Both |
| Double tap + drag to select word-by-word | https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/149295 ✅ | Both |
| Double click to select a word | https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/124817 ✅ | Both |
| Double click + drag to select word-by-word | https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/124817 ✅ | Both |
| Triple tap to select a paragraph | Implementation is there but needs to be enabled. Static text behav
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Discussion notes opportunities to reuse logic from EditableText. Several gesture PRs landed. Remaining unimplemented gestures include iOS-specific cursor drag, long press to move cursor, and some Android/Linux behaviors. Agreed to watch for code-sharing opportunities.

title: Provide TextField named constructors for different input types
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

TextFields are very verbose to build, even if you want a simple functionality. 

For example, if I want only numbers to be filled I need to manually set the type of keyboard and a TextInputFormatter making it very verbose and polluting the widget's `build` method.

### Proposal

It would be awesome if we could have natively different named constructors for common input types such as:

- TextField.number
- TextField.email
- TextField.password

And so on, such as we have different `input` tags in `html`. This would make it a lot easier and more intuitive to use and read.
comment_summary: One comment suggests implementing named constructors as a pub.dev package (potentially via a Dart extension) rather than adding them to the framework.

comment_summary: Comments confirm the tab traversal edge behavior for element embedding remains broken specifically for the element-embedding case, while it works correctly for iframes. A team member confirmed the iframe fix exists and the element-embedding case needs its own implementation. On master, behavior improved due to multi-view work. No dedicated fix or PR filed; stalled but noted as improvement in progress.

title: TextInputFormatter formatEditUpdate's oldValue has different selection.base.offset on android and ios
comment_summary: Root cause identified: on iOS, the text input plugin receives `setSelectedTextRange` before `deleteBackward`, which sends an `updateEditingState` to the framework with a selection range pointing at the character to be deleted — this state becomes `oldValue` in `formatEditUpdate`, so `oldValue.selection.baseOffset` reflects the post-deletion offset rather than the pre-deletion cursor position. Confirmed as iOS-only; Android behaves correctly. No fix or PR; stalled.

title: Broken selection on TextField if canRequestFocus: false
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug (magnifying glass stuck visible) reproduces on Android and iOS with `canRequestFocus: false` on stable 3.10.5 and master 3.12.0-pre. A related PR #130164 removed the `canRequestFocus` parameter in favor of using FocusNode directly, but did not fix the underlying buggy behavior. The original use case was disabling text selection without disabling suffix widgets. One commenter reported the issue could not be reproduced on 3.27.4. Stalled/possibly fixed.

Very simply: parameterize the 4.0 ha

title: [Web] When useMaterial3 is true in ThemeData, setting StrutStyle to TextField breaks Japanese input.
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

Run the code sample in Chrome.
Then enter Japanese.
Japanese input breaks when StrutStyle is set to TextField when usematerial3 in ThemeData is true.

### Expected results

Pressing the 'A' key will input the Japanese text 'あ'.

### Actual results

Actually, "ああ" is typed. I am unable to enter the first character in the textfield correctly.

}

class MyApp extends StatefulWidget {

...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause narrowed by the reporter: the bug occurs only with the canvaskit renderer + `StrutStyle()` + `useMaterial3: true`; the html renderer and no-StrutStyle cases are unaffected. The IME issue specifically affects multi-keystroke Japanese input (e.g., 'ka' = k+a) as the first character, on both Windows and macOS. title: TextField using custom SpellCheckService messes up its contents
comment_summary: Comments confirmed reproduction on Android, iOS, web, and macOS. Root cause is a bug in `buildTextSpanWithSpellCheckSuggestions` that does not handle out-of-order or overlapping `SuggestionSpan` lists, corrupting the rendered text. A separate issue #132270 was filed for spell check not triggering until typing begins. @justinmc plans to fix the ordering/overlap bug and add a custom-service example. Status: stalled, no PR yet.

title: Flutter needs a way to test real IME text input behavior
body:
Super Editor is a Flutter-based document editing toolkit. Text manipulation is fundamental to everything we do. Specifically, we rely on text editing deltas, which are the standard tool for any non-trivial text editing use-cases.

Flutter lacks any mechanism to test real IME delta behavior. We've discovered that it's critical for us to be able to test real IME deltas for content changes.

Super Editor users are routinely filing bugs against our text manipulation behavior. These bugs are nearly impossible for us to diagnose and test against, given the current state of Flutter's IME system, including testing tools.

IME deltas are determined by a series of details:

1. User behavior with the software keyboard
2. The keyboard's decision about how to represent a change with deltas
3. Flutter's decision about how to organize and/or alter the deltas that the keyboard reports to it

Flutter lacks integration testing tools to simulate user behavior with the keyboard, and therefore 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments discuss whether Robolectric or adb-based coordinate tapping could simulate real IME behavior, with the adb approach flagged as flake-prone. A related Super Editor issue is linked showing real-world impact. No concrete proposal or PR emerged; the issue remains open as a feature request with no planned implementation.

title: SelectableText: `userUpdateTextEditingValue` calls `_scheduleShowCaretOnScreen` when it should not
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Use case

I have a SelectableText widget where I need to select text programatically using `userUpdateTextEditingValue`. The issue arises when I need to deselect the text by passing `TextEditingValue.empty` that in turn triggers `_scheduleShowCaretOnScreen(withAnimation: true);` causing a scroll jump. 

### Proposal

I would suggest that we check the TextEditingValue passed in and in case it is empty, the `_scheduleShowCaretOnScreen` will not get executed as there is really no need for it and this way, the unwanted scroll jumps are prevented.

Something along those lines (note ***):
```
void userUpdateTextEditingValue(TextEditingValue value, SelectionChangedCause? cause) {
    // Compare the current TextEditingValue with the pre-format new
    /
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the scroll-jump is still reproducible on Linux when clearing selection via `userUpdateTextEditingValue(TextEditingValue.empty, ...)`. The root cause is that `_scheduleShowCaretOnScreen` fires even when the resulting selection is empty/invalid. A contributor (vongrad) agreed to open a PR but never did, and was eventually unassigned. The issue remained open after re-verification that the bug still occurs on master.

title: TextInputFormatter breaks dictation on Android
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Focus the TextField
2. Start the dictation feature on the virtual keyboard (I've tested with GBoard and SwiftKey and found the same issue)
3. Say something in the mic

The problem has been reproduced on 3 different android devices, one of them being an Honor 6X BLN-L21 API 24

### Expected results

The dictated text should be displayed in the TextField, with the TextInputFormatter modifications applied. (Here the text should be upper-cased).

### Actual results

Given the dictated sentence: "Hello this should be working"

With GBoard: only the first word is displayed in the TextField, upper-cased: "HELLO".
With SwiftKey: the first word, then the first two words are displayed, upper-cased: "HELLO HELLO THIS".

The rest of the dicta
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed on both Android and iOS. Root cause noted: the asynchronous communication between the engine and framework means that when a TextInputFormatter transforms text (e.g., uppercases it), the composing region sent back to the IME is inconsistent, causing dictation to commit and restart after each word. The framework sends a modified editing value that the IME treats as a commit trigger. A synchronous text input update path would likely fix this but is a long-term effort. No short-term fix identified; stalled.

comment_summary: Narrowed to physical Android + Chrome devices; emulators and iOS Safari unaffected. When resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false, Chrome Android resizes the browser viewport outside Flutter's control, pushing content up. Workaround: use kIsWeb ? 0 : MediaQuery.of(context).viewInsets.bottom as bottom padding (partially mitigates). title: [iOS] Keyboard partially overlaps TextField after native dialog closes
comment_summary: Comments confirmed the regression is iOS-specific (not reproducible on Android, where the keyboard is dismissed before the image picker appears). The bug occurs only when dismissing the image picker via Cancel or Add buttons — dismissing via pull-down gesture does not trigger it. Reproduction was confirmed on iPhone 7 (iOS 15.7.2), iPhone 13 Pro Max, and SE; not reproducible on iPhone 14 Pro simulator. The root cause was bisected to commit 9aa2ea150c and attributed to engine PR #37719. `MediaQuery.viewInsets.bottom` shows a different value (233px vs 260px) after the buggy dismissal. Setting priority to P1 as a regression. A workaround is to call `focusNode.unfocus()` before opening the picker and then request focus again afterward.

comment_summary: Confirmed Windows-only; macOS does not reproduce it. A commenter identified that the bug occurs specifically with CRLF (Windows-style) line endings in pasted text — pasting text with Unix LF line endings works correctly. The cursor rendering is the buggy part: the cursor is rendered at the start of the text when it logically sits on a CRLF newline, but typing proceeds correctly from that position, indicating a visual rendering bug rather than a text-model bug. No PR was linked and the issue remains open for investigation.

title: TextField decoration documentation/assertion bug
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Use Material `TextField` inside a custom theme, outside of `MaterialApp` and any other Flutter-provided wrappers
2. Set `TextField.decoration` to `null` as [per documentation](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField-class.html) **only having it non-null** requires you to have a `Material` ancestor:

   > If [decoration](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField/decoration.html) is non-null (which is the default), the text field requires one of its ancestors to be a [Material](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/Material-class.html) widget.

    Which is understood as having `decoration` value to be `null` allows you not to have `Material` ancestors

### Expected results

Everything works and no erro
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause: the assertion requiring a `Material` ancestor fires unconditionally regardless of the `decoration` value, contradicting the documentation. @justinmc investigated and found that removing the assert allows `TextField` with and without `decoration` to work under `CupertinoApp` and `WidgetsApp` when proper localizations are provided. A fix was proposed (removing the Material ancestor assertion) and assigned to @Renzo-Olivares, but the assignee was eventually removed due to inactivity. Stalled.

title: Strange scroll behavior of ListView which has TextFormField with maxLines 2
comment_summary: Confirmed iPad-specific: does not reproduce on iPhone or Android. The issue involves scrolling with one finger outside a multi-line TextFormField while keeping a second finger on the field, causing a gesture-arena lockup. No fix or PR; status is open and was previously assigned to @Renzo-Olivares then unassigned due to inactivity.

title: Proposal: Support Copy and Paste keycodes
body:
### Use case

It's great to be able to use global keyboard shortcuts for copy and paste that work in GUI apps as well as the terminal. Using Control-C and Control-V often doesn't work because they conflict with bindings within terminals.

A good solution for keyboards they support it is to use the `XF86_Paste` and `XF86_Copy` keycodes. (As they are known on Linux) Keyboards with custom firmware to make these keycodes easy to use are increasingly popular. As long as your apps support it, you can then just always use the "Copy Key" and the "Paste Key". 

Several apps and terminals already support  these keybindings as default options for copy and paste. 

### Proposal

Add support for the `XF86_Paste` and `XF86_Copy` keycodes with text inputs as alternates for copying and pasting.
comment_summary: Comments confirm XF86_Paste/XF86_Copy keys likely need to be in the keycode database and in the default key bindings for copy/paste intents.

title: No good way to get line metrics for `Text`/`TextField` based widgets.
comment_summary: Root cause identified: the user's `TextPainter` does not use the same `TextStyle` as the `TextField` (which applies material theme styles), causing layout width mismatches. A team member (@dnfield) initially proposed using `controller.buildTextSpan(context: context, withComposing: false)` as a fix but later confirmed it does not reliably carry the correct styles. The confirmed workaround is to manually replicate the exact `TextStyle` the `TextField` applies (e.g., resolving from the material theme). @LongCatIsLooong mentioned a planned framework-level API exposing immutable text layout metrics via a callback (e.g., `onTextLayoutChanged`) but noted a `TextEditingController` cannot be the right place since one controller can serve multiple fields with different styles. Currently stalled with no PR.

title: Numpad enter doesn't work on tests for windows platform
comment_summary: Reproduction confirmed on stable 3.13.2 and master. The error is that `LogicalKeyboardKey.numpadEnter` is not present in the Windows keyCode map used by `KeyEventSimulator`, causing the test assertion to fail. No fix or PR was proposed; status is stalled.

title: Multiline TextField text selection randomly changes on right click Web
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a multiline TextField
2. Enter some text that goes beyond it's view port and makes it scrollable
3. Do some random scrolling and text selection and right click on web to see the issue

### Expected results

1. When right clicking on a part of text that is not selected or cursor is in another position, the cursor should move to that position
2. When right clicking on a selected text, selection should stay unchanged

### Actual results

1. Cursor moves to another position
2. Selection changes

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const App());
}

class App extends StatelessWidget {
  const App({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: right clicks on web are handled by the browser's native context menu via the underlying hidden textarea/input, and the scroll position of that hidden element is not synced with the Flutter framework's rendered scroll offset. So right-clicking on visible "Line 8" actually right-clicks "Line 3" in the hidden element, causing the selection to jump. A PR was developed but broke for complex multiline values (#135159). This is no longer a blocker for #120613. Status: stalled, no landed fix.

title: [web] Typing compound characters generates unexpected deltas on Safari
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the sample code with `flutter run -d web-server --web-port 8080 --web-hostname 0.0.0.0` 
2. Access the page on safari with `http://localhost:8080` using a brazilian keyboard (ABNT2)
3. Press "Attach to IME"
4. Type `~` (or press OPTION + U on an english keyboard)

### Expected results

Flutter generates a single delta or a single call to `updateEditingValueWithDeltas` with two deltas.

### Actual results

Flutter calls `updateEditingValueWithDeltas` with an insertion delta and then calls `updateEditingValueWithDeltas` again with a non-text delta.

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter/services.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(
    const
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause: Safari/WebKit reports an `TextEditingDeltaInsertion` for a dead-key (e.g., `~`) with an uncollapsed selection, then a separate `TextEditingDeltaNonTextUpdate` to collapse it; Flutter's delta system calls `setEditingState` between these two calls because the editor's selection differs from the IME's, which prevents the follow-up non-text delta from arriving and breaks IME composition. Affects web/Safari only. Renzo-Olivares was assigned to investigate a fix (batching the two deltas), but was later unassigned due to inactivity. Status: stalled.

title: Unexpected padding for TextField input when first child TextSpan has a non-zero letterSpacing
comment_summary: Comments confirm the unexpected left-side padding (half of `letterSpacing`) on the first character also occurs in `RichText`/`Text` widgets — it is standard behavior where `letterSpacing` is split half-before and half-after each glyph. One commenter noted that with SkParagraph the behavior is different (no leading space). No fix or PR; triaged to design/engine team.

title: [Web] Position emoji picker when using multiple styles
comment_summary: The root cause was identified: `setCaretRect` is a no-op on web, and the browser positions IME panels based on the element's style (font family, size, transform). When multiple text styles are used, the browser cannot infer the correct caret position. A commenter confirmed this is a fundamental styling limitation — Flutter can express more styles than CSS — and that fixing it requires significant design work. Status: accepted with no clear solution path.

title: [Web] Unable to detect if a native IME panel is opened
body:
In super_editor, we handle arrow key events to move the selection around the text. However, doing that prevents the user from interacting with native input controls, like emoji pickers and japanese input panels:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/7597082/42386c6d-2baa-4b13-82da-35aac3c4f9c8

To let users interact with those panels, we need to let the key events bubble up to the OS. However, we do want to handle the key events when there isn't an IME panel open.

We need a way to determine whether or not an IME panel is being displayed. Even though an IME panel is opened, the `FocusNode` still has primary focus.

Is there a way to check if there is an IME panel opened?


### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the sample code using Japanese keyboard
2. Type some letters to trigger the IME panel, like "s" and "a"
3. Press DOWN ARROW 

On the `_onKey` method, we'd like to know whether the IME panel is open.

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'pac
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments note that for composition-based IME panels (e.g., Japanese), checking `_currentEditingValue.composing.isValid` is a usable proxy for whether the panel is open. The `super_editor` team adopted this workaround. However, a follow-up report noted it breaks when the selection is at the right edge of text because the composing region is invalid even though the panel is open. No framework-level API proposed or PR linked; stalled.

title: [Web] Re-enable safari tests that utilize `InputEvent.inputType`
body:
Some tests that utilize `InputEvent.inputType` have been skipped on Safari. Safari only recently added support for setting `inputType` on Safari 16, more context on this issue [here](https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/44693#issuecomment-1704308730) . The CI is currently running on the tests on Safari 15 so tests fail. These tests should be re-enabled when the CI is rolled to Safari 16.

affected files: `text_editing_test.dart`
tests: `Supports deletion at inverted selection`, `Supports new line at inverted selection`.

PR that introduces tests: https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/44693

title: TextField.scrollPadding does not affect scrollable and scrollable decorations
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

To reproduce 
1. Copy the code from the sample and run it
2. Type multiple letters till the scrollbar shows up
3. Scroll up and down to see the scrollbar out of the bounds of the textfield

The scrollbar is clipping out of the bounds of the textfield when the border is curved and to resolve this, scrollbar padding should work but it doesn't. 

P.s [Proposal] Providing an option to position the scrollbar in the center would also be a great add. (Not related to issue, Just asking from a unique design pov)

### Expected results

Two options
1. Even if the the scroll padding doesn't work, the scrollbar should be within the bounds of the textfield (like putting them in a row within a container) rather than stacking it above it.
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm `TextField.scrollPadding` is unrelated to the scrollbar position — it does not pass padding information to any scrollable part of the TextField, making the parameter name misleading. The external `Scrollbar` widget has no knowledge of the `scrollPadding` value; `RawScrollbar.padding` is the correct API to control scrollbar inset. No fix or PR mentioned; issue remains open with discussion about whether `ScrollBehavior.buildScrollbar` could be used as a workaround for mobile platforms.

title: [Desktop app]Can not restrict candidate list on password field
comment_summary: Comments identify the issue as desktop-specific (macOS/Windows): on a password field, Chinese IME candidate list still appears and the input method is not forced to English mode, unlike native apps (Qt, Objective-C). Web behaves correctly (Chinese input disabled). Android and iOS also behave correctly. The bug was labeled desktop but no fix or PR was proposed. Stalled.

### Expected results

Context menu doesn't appear.

### Actual results

- Context menu appears.

- There is a grey selection box that is being displayed.
- It looks like two magnifiers are being displayed

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter/services.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyAp
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: the `contextmenu` event is not supported on iOS Safari per MDN docs, so `element.addEventListener('contextmenu', ...)` with `preventDefault()` cannot suppress the native context menu on mobile. Confirmed affected on both iOS (Safari) and Android (Chrome). No fix proposed; a commenter noted this may be related to native input work by @htoor3. Status: stalled, no PR.

title: UndoHistoryController does not function properly in languages like Japanese where characters are confirmed when typed.
comment_summary: Comments explain that for non-Android platforms, Flutter prevents coalescing during CJK composition to avoid interference, so `undo` during active composition has no effect and the initial composing state is not saved to the history stack. On Android, a separate embedder-side issue causes `restartInput` to be called after `undo`, adding unexpected history entries and causing blinking. Renzo Olivares had a proof-of-concept fix that caused other regressions; issue is stalled with no active PR.

title: [iOS] [Accessibility] [VoiceOver]  - when tapping `next` on virtual keyboard, the screen reader should read the next field
comment_summary: Comments confirm the VoiceOver announcement gap is iOS-only; Android does not have the issue. Reproduced on both stable 3.13.4 and master. The issue was CC'd to @hangyujin for iOS accessibility input, and is labeled for both design and iOS teams. Status: stalled, no fix or PR, assigned to team-design.

title: Better support for variable fonts as animated icons
body:
### Use case

Currently one just can implement animated (monochrome/colored) icons by variable fonts using something like,

1. Download https://github.com/Typogram/Anicons/raw/master/webfonts/AniconsColorGX.ttf and add it to `fonts/AniconsColorGX.ttf` in your project.
2. Add the following in your `pubspec.yaml`
```
    - family: AniconsColorGX
      fonts:
        - asset: fonts/AniconsColorGX.ttf
```
4. Use the following code,

```dart
Text(
  'G',
  style: TextStyle(
    fontFamily: 'AniconsColorGX',
    fontSize: 30,
    fontVariations: [
      FontVariation('TIME', _controller.value * 99 + 1)
    ],
  ),
)
```

<details>
<summary>The complete code which has animation controller part also</summary>

```dart
class HomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  const HomePage({super.key});

  @override
  State<HomePage> createState() => _HomePageState();
}

class _HomePageState extends State<HomePage>
    with SingleTickerProviderStateMixin {
  late Animati
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Single comment suggests registering a font variation axis in the axis registry before adding to Icon.

title: [web] - Fix text input overlay alignment
body:
## Problem
On web, the framework renders text inputs and we overlay transparent DOM `<input>` to support features like native context menus.  The problem with this is that we have an overlay misalignment that leads to a lot of selection bugs, especially on mobile and with multiline inputs.  Additionally, this misalignment prevents us from enabling spellcheck. 

Examples:
* https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/79305
* https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/134024
* https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/124483
* https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/99918 (partially - iOS selection can't be made transparent.  However, this issue causes the iOS selection overlay to appear in completely different locations than Flutter's selection overlay)

## Solution
If we can get the input to overlay more accurately over the framework-rendered text, we can solve some of these issues.  

There are a few different components to getting this fixed:

1. `line-height`: we don't 
...[truncated]

title: getLocalRectForCaret Returns inconsistent results based on what the last key press was
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

* Create a EditableText widget
* Assign it a global key with an EditableTextState
* Wrap the text field with an https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/Overlay-class.html (I used flutter_portal because it makes it easer to manage, however has no influence on the issue so it's not package related)
* Add a listener to the textcontroller
* Just refresh state in the add listener event
* Create a 20px high (arbitrary to match font size) see-through red box and add it in the portal to be shown over the text field
* Get the getLocalRectForCaret from the editable and use the rect to draw the horizontal line

* type into the text field on the first line until you reach the second line
* Keep tying on the second line and somewhere in the middl
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified by commenter: `TextEditingController` listeners fire before layout, so the underlying `TextPainter` still holds the old text and `getLocalRectForCaret` returns stale coordinates from the previous layout pass. Reproduced on Android (incorrect position) and iOS/web/macOS (different incorrect behavior). Workarounds: (1) use `WidgetsBinding.instance.addPostFrameCallback` to read caret position after layout, or (2) force-update `renderEditable.text` with `buildTextSpan()` before calling `getLocalRectForCaret`. Status: open, no fix proposed.

title: Word Candidate Window has problem when typing Chinese with hardware keyboard
comment_summary: Confirmed on latest stable and master. The issue is that arrow and tab keys used for Chinese IME candidate selection are consumed by Flutter's key handling and not passed to the IME candidate window, affecting use with physical keyboards (Magic Keyboard, Bluetooth). No fix or PR; status is open.

title: Keyboard dismisses after widget tree change
comment_summary: Confirmed on stable 3.13.5 and master: when the widget tree is restructured (widget wrapped in a new parent) while a TextField has focus, the keyboard is dismissed even though the FocusNode retains focus. Root cause: EditableText does not re-establish the input connection when a FocusNode with existing focus is assigned. Team noted a GlobalKey should be used when moving a field in the widget tree; marked P3. No fix or PR.

title: Framework->Embedder syncing of scrollable widgets to allow smooth IME animation
body:
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/62876 tracked the embedder->framework syncing to allow the insets to dynamically change with the keyboard mid-animation when the keyboard is shown/hidden.

Framework->syncing would allow keyboard to smoothly animate away as the widget is scrolled.

Also, on simple screenshots we can see some white space from bottom to the `TextFie

comment_summary: Comments identify two distinct cases: (1) keyboard opens but FAB jumps to top of screen and TextField has wrong insets, similar to #56039; (2) keyboard opens but TextField disappears, related to #124205. Confirmed reproducing on stable 3.13.6 and master 3.16. A commenter suggested setting resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false for Android web and applying iOS-specific unfocus handling. An additional report shows the FAB jumping on Android when dismissing a bottom sheet after keyboard use. Issue stalled.

title: iOS copy/paste bar is available by 3-tap gesture even after TextField has been destroyed
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

From my understanding this was introduced sometime between 3.7.9 and 3.10.x

1. Launch app in iOS
2. Use three fingers to tap at the same time (nothing happens)
3. Go to screen with a CupertinoTextField

I would not expect the 3-tap gesture to show the iOS menu in step 7. 

### Actual results

The 3-tap gesture shows the iOS menu even when there is no TextField present.

<
...[truncated]

title: `RawKeyEvent` and `RawKeyboard`, et al should be deprecated and removed
body:
## Description

Several years ago, we embarked on a project to improve the keyboard key handling for Flutter.  The new system has been in production use for a while now (at least a couple of years).

However, the legacy interfaces are still present, and on the framework side, this makes for a confusing story for our users, and complicates the code. On the engine side, we are still producing and handling both kinds of keyboard events, which complicates the code and is less efficient.

This document describes the steps that need to be taken in order to complete the deprecation and removal of the old “Raw” APIs and code.

The migration guide for this work has been [published](https://docs.flutter.dev/release/breaking-changes/key-event-migration).

## Affected APIs

The APIs that need to be deprecated and eventually removed are:

- [x] `RawKeyboard`
- [x] `RawKeyEvent`
- [x] `RawKeyDownEvent`
- [x] `RawKeyUpEvent`
- [x] `RawKeyEventData`
- [x] `RawKeyEventDataAndroid`
- [x] `RawKeyEventDat
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Deprecations were committed (#136677), starting the one-year countdown. Comments document migration pain points: `RawKeyEventDataLinux.scanCode` has no equivalent in the new API (a user worked around it with a manual USB HID→scan-code mapping); modifier-mask-based keybinding logic must be rewritten using `HardwareKeyboard.isMetaPressed` etc. A web-specific issue was raised where `KeyboardListener` does not fire events (filed separately). A game-dev team reported a blocking issue (#162305) on web and requested the removal be delayed; a macOS regression was also reported (#148936). The `simulateKeyDownEvent`/`simulateKeyUpEvent` and `KeyEventSimulator` items remain unchecked. Status: deprecations landed; removal timeline slipped past Q4 2024 due to blocking issues.

title: Android: keyboard opens when focusing on button while moving with "tab"
body:
Internal: b/371244390

### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open a page that contains a textfield
2. Use "tab" to jump to the textfield which will focus it and open up the keyboard
3. Press "tab" again, this will move the focus to the next element below the textfield -> Keyboard will be closed
4. Press "tab" again, this will move the focus to the next element which is also a button
5. Keyboard will pop up again, even though it shouldn't
6. Close the page and move around the initial page -> Keyboard will pop up again

(if you jump out of the textfield using tab and then use the arrow keys to move to the next widget (an not tab) this issue is not happening)

### Expected results

The keyboard should only open when the user focuses a textfield

### Actual results

The key
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm reproduction on physical Android devices with an external keyboard (Realme 6, Pixel Tablet) and also on emulator. Root cause analysis from a commenter: `View.onFocusChanged()` in Android calls `notifyFocusChangeToImeFocusController(true)` on any focusable view gaining focus; if the window has IME focus, `ViewRootImpl` eventually shows the keyboard. This is deep in the Android view system and cannot be intercepted from the SDK. Android team marked it P2 and deprioritized for Q4. Still reproducible on Flutter 3.29.3. No fix or PR; status is stalled.

### Steps to reproduce

3. See how the text style has been applied to the child of WidgetSpan.


### Expected results

b and d in the sample app result in the same visually.

### Actual results

Translucent colors is displayed darker in WidgetSpan.

I'm not sure if this is a bug, but I found it very unintuitive. I couldn't find documentation related to this. It is also inconsistent that the workaround that works for `background`/`backgroundColor` does not work for `decorationColor`.


### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(App());

class App extends StatelessWidget {
  final _style = TextStyle(
 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue is present on both stable and master. The root cause is that a `WidgetSpan`'s child `Text` widget is rendered on top of the parent `TextSpan`'s background paint layer, causing translucent background and decoration colors to visually compound. One commenter noted the behavior is expected given the rendering order but acknowledged it is confusing. No fix or PR; stalled pending design team input.

title: Browser warning when focus is given to text field in autofill group
comment_summary: The root cause was identified in comments: when an `AutofillGroup` contains two inputs with the same autofill hint (e.g., two `newPassword` fields), Flutter assigns the same `id` attribute to both hidden DOM elements, causing the browser warning about non-unique IDs. A commenter proposed decoupling the `id` attribute from the autofill hint or appending an incremental suffix, and noted that duplicate autofill hints are a valid use case (e.g., shipping + billing forms). No PR submitted; status is open.

...[truncated]

title: [Android] Keyboard input failure on physical Android devices after screen lock/unlock
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Execute flutter run.
2. Focus TextField. Keyboard appears. Type something.
3. Lock android screen by pressing Power button.
4. Unlock android screen.
5. Type something again.

### Expected results

Keyboard input occurs in TextField

### Actual results

Keyboard input is not performed and is restored only after re-focusing on the TextField

This problem occurs only on physical devices.
List of devices on which this was noticed:
1) Readme 9A (Android 10, MIUI Global 12.0.17)
2) Nokia 5.3 (Android 11)
3) ZTE Smart 20 blade

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidge
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Issue confirmed Android-only; does not reproduce on iOS. Reproduction is device-specific: confirmed on Realme 6 (Android 11) and Realme X2 Pro but not on Pixel 3A (Android 12), Galaxy A5/J7, or Pixel 7 Pro (Android 14/15). A commenter identified that on affected devices `InputConnectionAdaptor.closeConnection` is called twice (on lock and again on unlock) instead of once, and the IME sends extra `getTextBeforeCursor`/`getExtractedText` calls on an inactive InputConnection. A working workaround using `WidgetsBindingObserver` to unfocus/re-focus via the app lifecycle was provided. Investigation stalled after the test device lost USB connectivity.

title: Typing with Pinyin-Simplified Input Source while selecting text, the navigation box is positioned incorrectly
comment_summary: A commenter confirmed reproduction on macOS and web. The root cause was identified as using the selection base offset instead of the selection start offset; a fix landed in #137863 and was confirmed working on master. However, the original reporter demonstrated via video that the fix only addressed horizontal positioning and the bug persists when the TextField widget itself changes size/position as text is typed (e.g., when centered and with many selected lines), which the patch author acknowledged as a narrower remaining case with lower priority.

Input loses focus and nothing happens.

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample (very simple autofillgroup with 2 inputs)</summary>

```dart
           Au
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The root cause is the app going to the background (e.g., to open a password manager or complete biometric auth) causes the focused `TextField` to lose focus, and the autofill service cannot restore values to an unfocused field on return. Affects Android confirmed across multiple devices and Android versions; iOS autofill has a separate related issue #148475. A community workaround using `AppLifecycleState` listeners to re-request focus on inactive state was widely confirmed working. An engine bisect identified commit `04e284a49dc9` as the bad commit; the revert landed in Flutter 3.22.0, fixing the Android case, but the issue was reopened with reports of remaining iOS problems and some users still seeing it on Android 3.24+.

title: Disable paste action in TextField when using three-finger gestures on iOS
body:

### Use case

Raising again the issue discussed in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/132191.
There is a way of preventing Copy/Paste by configuring (f.e.):

 ```
TextField(
       enableInteractiveSelection: false,
       contextMenuBuilder: (_, __) => const SizedBox.shrink(),
        // Other properties...
)
```

However, it is still possible to paste in the TextField using keyboard shortcuts or three-finger gestures on iOS.


### Proposal

As proposed by the issue: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/132191
A parameter that will only allow keyboard input in the TextField and block paste action.

Also noted that three-finger hold shows the toolbar. Issue re-labeled from design to iOS team. No fix or PR; status is open.

```
import 'package:flutter/cupertino.dart';

...[truncated]

title: IOS Text Field with Emojis Input Selection Issues

Using `MaterialTextSelectionControls` explicitly works around the issue. Status: stalled after investigation; assignee eventually removed.

title: Spell checking should be according to current selected language in keyboard, not locale of device.
body:
- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Locale of device should be ru_RU
2. Write english words with english keyboard.

### Expected results

1. Locale of device is ru_RU
2. When typing in english keyboard, spell checking should also be in english. And correct english words should not be underlined with red

### Actual results

Correct english words are underlined with red.

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
Localizations.override(
            locale: Locale(
                "ru",
                "RU"),
            context: context,
            child: TextField(
              autocorrect: true,
              enableSuggestions: true,
              style: TextStyle(
                  fontSize: 17,
                  height: 1.3),
              spellCheckConfi
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue is iOS-specific: when the device locale is Russian (or any non-English locale) but the keyboard is switched to English, Flutter's spell checker uses the device locale (Russian) rather than the active keyboard language, so correct English words are flagged. On Android, spell checking follows the keyboard language as expected; on iOS it does not. A native iOS Notes app comparison confirms this is a Flutter regression from correct behavior. @camsim99 suggests investigating `TextServicesManager`/`SpellCheckerInfo` on Android for the correct approach; no fix or PR has been filed. Currently stalled and assigned to both `team-ios` and `team-android`.

title: TextField doesn't report "up" event in onTapOutside
comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: PR #136291 changed onTapOutside to only fire when the TextField has focus, so by the time the pointer-up event arrives, focus is already lost and the callback is not called. The design decision was intentional but users argue it prevents distinguishing taps from drags. Team discussed reverting #136291 or adding onTapUpOutside/onTapDownOutside callbacks. PR #156110 was referenced as a potential fix; no confirmation it resolved the issue.

title: `FocusNode.nextFocus()` doesn't seem to focus the next node
comment_summary: Comments reveal that FocusNode.nextFocus() moves focus to the node after the currently focused node, not after the given FocusNode — matching documented behavior. User expected it to advance relative to the specified FocusNode regardless of current focus. Issue confirmed specific to TextField (TextButton behaves differently because buttons don't request focus on click). Assigned to @gspencergoog then unassigned due to no progress; reflects API-design gap rather than a clear bug.
