...[truncated]
comment_summary: The root cause was identified as how Flutter handles the size transition when returning from an external activity (the image picker) back to the Flutter app; it is not specific to image_picker itself. Confirmed on stable 3.13.9 and master 3.17. Workarounds shared: unfocus the text field before opening the picker and refocus afterward, or close the keyboard after image selection. No fix or PR linked; stalled with team-framework.

title: Crash while type some characters and double click in textfield: 'NSInternalInconsistencyException', reason: 'Expected a valid text range.'
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. type "hdjdd" 
2. double click textfield
3. crash

work in iOS 17.0.0 well. While update to 17.1.1 crash.
### Expected results

no crash

### Actual results


*** Terminating app due to uncaught exception 'NSInternalInconsistencyException', reason: 'Expected a valid text range.'
*** First throw call stack:
(0x1a277c870 0x19aa97c00 0x1a1ce6d18 0x10af72f54 0x1a5560928 0x1a555ec90 0x1a5557724 0x1a55573ec 0x1a55539ac 0x1a54d37e0 0x1a4bc3c2c 0x1a4bc3b10 0x1a551b738 0x1a54d34a0 0x10af71a78 0x10af79d04 0x10b4fe1c8 0x10af6b590 0x10a678f50 0x10a67ab34 0x10a68afec 0x10a68aba0 0x1a26c720c 0x1a26c3f18 0x1a26c3668 0x1e5a8e5ec 0x1a4ae02b4 0x1a4adf8f0 0x104ae49cc 0x1c5116dcc)



### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</su
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments pinpoint the crash to `FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm`'s `text(in:)` returning an invalid range when iOS predictive suggestions propose a shorter word than the currently typed text, combined with a double-tap to select. The crash occurs on iOS 17+ physical devices (confirmed on iPhone 13 Pro Max, iPhone 11 iOS 17.1.2, iPhone 15 iOS 17.2.1) but was not reproducible on simulators. A commenter notes the crashing assertion in `text(in:)` was introduced by engine PR #16496 to fix an a11y bug, and the correct fix is to return `nil` for ranges containing `NSNotFound` instead of throwing. Workarounds reported include `autocorrect: false` combined with `keyboardType: TextInputType.name`. One commenter notes the crashing assertion is removed in Flutter 3.30.0, and another confirms they can no longer reproduce on Flutter 3.32.7.

title: [EditableText] value should be selectable via double click on web in edit mode
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue on web, macOS, and Android — `EditableText` does not support double-tap or drag-to-select because it intentionally omits gesture support; `TextField`/`CupertinoTextField`/`SelectableText` are the full-featured alternatives, or `TextSelectionGestureDetectorBuilder` can be used to add gesture support manually. Issue #120613 was noted as the tracking issue for web double-tap selection. A workaround using `GestureDetector` with `onDoubleTap` to programmatically select all text was provided.

title: [EditableText] should have a `selectionForegroundColor` parameter
[iOS] TextField gets hidden by the keyboard due to out of sync animation
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

This issue has been reproduced on old iphone models (tested on iphone 7 with iOS 15.7.9)

Sample code here:
https://github.com/delfme/flutter-native-text-input-repo/tree/3ef4459c0aad47b8d74d90340096e27844a18b9f




### Expected results

Bottom inset should move in sync with keyboard native animation.

### Actual results

There is a 4s animation delay, and during this time textfield is covered by the keyboard, see video from this comment (tested on iPhone 7, iOS 15.7.9) https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/138845#issuecomment-1825131443



### Code sample

Sample here: https://github.com/delfme/flutter-native-text-input-repo/tree/3ef4459c0aad47b8d74d90340096e27844a18b9f

### Screenshots or Video

https://private-user-images.github
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The severe 4-second animation delay seen on iPhone 7 (iOS 15.7.9) was no longer reproducible on newer stable/master after the original report, though a residual jerk on the chat input view when the keyboard appears remains. A commenter identified the fundamental root cause: Flutter receives keyboard height changes via events (viewInsets) but lacks frame-by-frame timing information from the OS animation, so layout updates are out of sync with the native keyboard animation curve. A detailed proposal for engine-side per-frame keyboard height updates via CADisplayLink (iOS) and WindowInsetsAnimation.Callback (Android) was posted. Issue remains open and was re-pinged to @LongCatIsLooong.

title: [Documentation] enableSuggestions: false breaks non-English keyboards that required suggestions to work
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Start with the default counter flutter app
2. Add in the following `TextField` in the body:
```dart
TextField(enableSuggestions: false)
```
3. Set the system keyboard to be Gboard and download a few non-English keyboards like Korean and Chinese.
4. Focus on the TextField. Try to hit the globe button to switch the keyboard language and notice that it can only alternate between English and QWERTY, even though other keyboards are available.

In the Flutter doc, `enableSuggestions` is defined to be:
> Whether to show input suggestions as the user types.
This flag only affects Android. On iOS, suggestions are tied directly to autocorrect, so that suggestions are only shown when autocorrect is true. On Android autocorrection and sugges
...[truncated]
comment_summary: A triager confirmed the issue on both stable and master on Android emulator. iOS still allows switching keyboards even with enableSuggestions: false, making behavior platform-inconsistent. One commenter noted this is correct behavior on iOS but not on Android. Currently stalled with no active assignee.

title: TextField with `TextAlign.right` throws `Failed assertion: line 206 pos 12: 'isValid': is not true.`
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

Create a CupertinoTextField object with TextAlign.right, enter a text with several letters, and press the up and down keys to go to the beginning or end of the text.

### Expected results

The cursor moving at the beginning or end of the text.

### Actual results

This message:
```
══╡ EXCEPTION CAUGHT BY SERVICES LIBRARY ╞══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
The following assertion was thrown during method call TextInputClient.performSelectors:
'package:flutter/src/rendering/editable.dart': Failed assertion: line 206 pos 12: 'isValid': is not
true.

Either the assertion indicates an error in the framework itself, or we should provide substantially
more information in this error message to help you
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified by @LongCatIsLooong: the VerticalCaretMovementRun.isValid check fails because computeLineMetrics returns a different List instance each time when textAlign is not .left, making isValid always false. The bug also reproduces with regular TextField (not just Cupertino), confirmed via #165979. Still reproducible on master. Currently stalled with no active assignee.

title: EditText box in TLHC handles resize and offset changes poorly
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

Filing this issue coz comments are disabled on the original one: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/112712
We thought we found a way to use TLHC as a substitute for HC when wrapping a native textfield.
The solution suggested by @johnmccutchan (see here https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/112712#issuecomment-1807704679)
indeed resolves the issue related to Magnifier lense, but a new issue originated as a consequence of that fix.

Basically, due to that `override`, the surface gets rendered with some delay and the platform view bumps when its position is changed (in our case the inputted text bumps when a new line is added).

Please do not discontinue HC as long as a fix for this is found. We've no alternative to it and we'v
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause as a lack of synchronization when the TLHC platform view is resized: the old image reader is torn down and a null image inserted before the new reader is populated, causing frames to drop during the gap. Jonahwilliams provided detailed analysis of the three-thread sequence (UI, Platform, Raster). Engine PR #49201 was attempted and eliminated dropped frames but introduced image stretching as a new artifact. The user reported that Hybrid Composition (HC) meanwhile developed new regressions with janky screen animations and screen-wide flickering when HC visibility changes. Currently stalled with assignee removed; users remain on HC as an imperfect workaround.

title: TextField with TextAlign.end imposes unintended behaviour when character before last one is a space.

### Steps to reproduce

- Create the demo app and add a Textfield with the alignment to the right

``` dart
TextField(
   textAlign: TextAlign.end,
),
```

- Enter Text like "21 %". Important! Enter a space between the number and the %.
- Unfocus
- Refocus
- Push the backspace key
- Observe behaviour as described below

### Expected results

Intended behaviour is, that the Textfield should now contain: "21 " (Space as last character). If we push backspace again, the 1 should be deleted.

### Actual results

The cursor jumps behind the 1. The field shows "21" iso "21 " (21 SPACE). If we push backspace again, nothing happens and "21" remains to be shown iso "2".

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
TextField(
   textAlign:
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the trailing-space rendering bug occurs not only on Windows but also macOS, web, and Android. The space character is actually present in the text (pressing backspace twice confirms this), but is not rendered because `TextAlign.end` trims trailing whitespace in the display. A workaround using ` ` (non-breaking space) instead of a regular space is provided. A commenter suggests this is a duplicate of issue #40648. Status: open, possibly duplicate of #40648, workaround available.

title: [iOS][Web] Widgets are pushed up when long pressing on the TextField
comment_summary: Confirmed iOS browsers only (Safari and Chrome on iOS); not reproducible on Android Chrome. Long-pressing a TextField causes a phantom keyboard-height white area to appear below the page on subsequent interactions. A commenter noted an additional repro without long-press when inside a bottomSheet on some mobile web devices. Root cause suspected to be incorrect delay in input placement when long-press focuses the field. No fix landed.

title: [web] Native text editing on web using platform views
body:
### Document Link

https://flutter.dev/go/web-native-text-editing

### What problem are you solving?

The current text editing system doesn't support a lot of core text editing functionality on the web (e.g. autofill, spellcheck, [and more](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/120613)).  This document proposes using platform views so that we can implement a more native text editing experience on the web for our users.
comment_summary: Comments note the investigation is still ongoing with no timeline. A customer reports being forced to consider alternatives after 1Password 8 started being affected.

Type rtl text to multiline textfield :

بابا آب داد

### Expected results

The letter to the right of the cursor should be deleted.
(It should be noted that when I copy the text from above and paste it in the text field, everything works correctly.)

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/32570964/b3d09305-3b20-4388-b938-2ce451217a79



### Actual results

But when I type the rtl text , the letter on the left side of the cursor is deleted.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/32570964/31f9ee2d-3aeb-441a-8568-97dcf7fdefe7



### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/mat
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirmed reproduction on Windows with RTL (Persian/Farsi) multiline text that was manually typed (not pasted); pasting the same text works correctly. The root cause was identified as a caret placement issue: when text contains CRLF-style line endings from manual typing, the cursor offset reported by the framework is incorrect for RTL text in multiline mode on Windows. A commenter linked this to issue #140739 and suspected a connection to CRLF handling. Another commenter noted the bug also manifests with custom fonts on Android (wrong caret position and incorrect word selection). A user shared a workaround that manually inserts `\r\n` instead of `\n` on Enter. The assignee (LongCatIsLooong) was removed after no status updates.

title: [Windows][Web] Unexpected deltas when using Japanese keyboard
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on Windows web (Chrome) but not on Windows desktop or macOS Chrome. The unexpected delta behavior occurs when the app calls setEditingState to resync after the IME updates, causing a second delta insertion at the wrong offset. Currently stalled; assignee was removed due to no status updates.

title: TextField: The container does not disappear from autocorrection
comment_summary: Reproduced on both latest stable and master on iOS 17. A team member (@hellohuanlin) was pinged and suggested it is related to #137666. The root cause is described as a native iOS 17 highlight/selection layer that persists on screen after the text field is removed or the keyboard is hidden. Status: triaged as P2, likely related to #137666, no fix.

title: Textfield obscureText interaction exception
comment_summary: Comments confirm the keyboard dismiss/reappear behavior when switching between a non-obscure and obscure TextField is device-specific, reproducible on HarmonyOS 4, Vivo Android 12/13, but not on standard Android emulators or Xiaomi Android 13. The root cause on affected devices is that a security keyboard layer (preventing screen recording of passwords) grabs focus when the obscure TextField is focused. Two workarounds were posted: using `Future.delayed` to re-request focus after a delay, and calling `FocusScope.of(context).unfocus()` in an `onPointerDown` Listener wrapper. One user reports the issue resolved in Flutter >= 3.22.0, but others on physical devices report it still occurs in 3.35.3.

title: Whether focus exists has nothing to do with whether the keyboard is open
body:
### Steps to reproduce

Related issues:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/123523

Related Pull Requests:

https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/41500
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/123929

There should be no correlation between whether the keyboard is open and whether focus exists. Should keep focus after keyboard was closed.

related comments:
https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/41500#issuecomment-1852785103
https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/40746#issuecomment-1819189977


### Expected results

Keep focus when keyboard was closed.

https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/30322203/228413196-29c57bb0-3220-495b-9e73-f58777de440f.mp4?jwt=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJnaXRodWIuY29tIiwiYXVkIjoicmF3LmdpdGh1YnVzZXJjb250ZW50LmNvbSIsImtleSI6ImtleTEiLCJleHAiOjE3MDMxNTg1NDEsIm5iZiI6MTcwMzE1ODI0MSwicGF0aCI6Ii8zMDMyMjIwMy8yMjg0MTMxOTYtMjljNTdiYjAtMzIyMC00OTViLTllNzMtZjU4Nzc3ZGU0NDBmLm1wND9YLUFtei1BbGdvcml0aG09QVdTNC1ITUFDL
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm this is a regression introduced by engine PR #40746 in Flutter 3.16, where closing the keyboard now also removes focus from the TextField (not the case in native Android). A Java-level workaround was shared: subclass `TextInputChannel` and override `onConnectionClosed` to be a no-op. Engine PR #49366 was merged to address the iOS side. A commenter noted difficulty re-showing the keyboard programmatically after this change; workaround using `SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.show')` was provided.

title: when using 3-set korean keyboard, textField ignores ' ' - word spacing (space bar key) on Windows
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on Windows stable 3.16.5 and master with 3-beolsik Korean IME: the spacebar key press is swallowed and produces no space character in the TextField; the key event is detected but not resolved. Affects Windows only; not reproducible with 2-beolsik. Still open as of 3.24.3 with no fix or PR.

title: Allow Autocomplete and RawAutocomplete to distinguish onFocus and onTap events
body:
### Use case

Currently both `Autocomplete` and `RawAutocomplete` widgets trigger the `optionsBuilder` callback when widget becomes focused and then a dropdown with the list of options is immediately being shown after that.

However the widget could get focus in a several different ways:

* tap on the widget (both on mobile or web/desktop)
* using keyboard and tabbing through the form fields (this is especially relevant on web/desktop)

For the latter, it is not always desirable to have options immediately shown. Especially with an async `optionsBuilder` that may make a network call.

### Proposal

It would be great to have an option in both `Autocomplete` and `RawAutocomplete` widgets to control onFocus vs onTap behaviour.

Additionally it would make sense to have `AutocompleteNextOptionIntent` and `AutocompletePreviousOptionIntent` to trigger the `optionsBuilder`, so we could have custom UI (e.g. a dropdown chevron) or custom action shortcuts (e.g. Ctrl-Down as in common Win
...[truncated]
comment_summary: PR #146995 (adding settable focusNode and textEditingController to RawAutocomplete) is proposed as a partial solution, with a DartPad proof-of-concept showing a chevron-triggered dropdown. The original reporter rejects the approach as relying on non-public internal behavior; the thread is stalled, with the feature tagged for @victorsanni's related work on controlling when the options view shows.

title: When entering Korean, the text cursor is behind single space.
comment_summary: Comments confirm the cursor-position bug is Windows-specific (not web or macOS) and Korean-specific (Japanese and Chinese do not exhibit it), first appearing around 3.16. The Korean IME builds characters in-place rather than buffering like CJK IMEs, causing the composing cursor to be drawn one position ahead. Commenters link it to a previous related issue (#121376). No PR exists; the issue has been open for over two years with multiple commercial users affected and no workaround. Status: stalled.

title: Add 'validator' handler at Form level to support cross field validation logic
body:
### Use case

Most non trivial form data entry requirements will include the validation of data across multiple input fields.   Currently, there is no simple and effective way of adding form level validation using the Flutter 'Form' and 'xxxxxFormField' widgets. 

### Proposal

Add a 'validator' handler at the 'Form' widget level.

The validator method would be passed an array of global key objects,  representing the global keys set up for each data input form field.  Like... 

`Function String? (List<GlobalKey> keys);`
comment_summary: Comments clarify the proposal: cross-field validation error messages should appear at form level, not under a specific field. No existing API supports this.

title: Key events for macOS keys "rewind" and "fast forward" not received
comment_summary: Only routing and cc comments; no substantive new information.

title: Flutter on Linux desktop: Neo2 Keyboard Layout not all keys recognized (missing e.g.: Arrows, Delete, Insert, ...)

In all other applications keys are handled correctly (gtk/Qt/...)

All keys of layer 4 (M4 + Key) are working correctly

comment_summary: Comments clarify the Neo2 keyboard layout issue: the modifier keys M4/Mod4 (like AltGr) on a physical ISO keyboard should produce arrow/control key events when combined with WASD/ESDF, but Flutter on Linux desktop does not recognize them, while Flutter on web and other native Linux apps work correctly. Reproduction requires a physical ISO keyboard with an AltGr key; the triager's ANSI keyboard cannot reproduce it. Status: stalled, no PR, team-linux assigned.

title: WebView text input is hidden by soft keyboard
comment_summary: Issue confirmed Android-only; works correctly on iOS Flutter apps and native Android WebView. Root cause discussion points to Scaffold not correctly calculating keyboard inset height in time — one commenter observed the height calculation succeeds when a debug breakpoint delays keyboard ejection. A JS-based workaround using `flutter_keyboard_visibility` + `document.activeElement.scrollIntoView()` was shared. One user noted `flutter_screenutil` 5.9.0 with `useInheritedMediaQuery: true` resolved the issue for them (related to #126585). No official fix; issue remains open.

title: Support Radius.elliptical for OutlineInputBorder
comment_summary: Comments confirm the Radius.elliptical workaround exists but is suboptimal. The issue has been open for over 2 years with no progress.

title: Text selection in a reversed ListView in combination with a text input is buggy

The text is not selected until you reach the original position of the selected tex

title: Flutter text field (on android) shows plain text character when max char limit reached and user attempts to insert in the middle of the string.
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Use android  device
2.Add a TextField 
3.Switch to the password entry box
4.Enter the maximum length and then switch to another place
5.Found plaintext displayed

Original title: Android TextField reaches the maximum password value, switching to any position and entering a new character again should not take effect, but the location cursor and text is clearly displayed in the password box

### Expected results

The maximum length cannot be entered, and the plaintext cannot be displayed

### Actual results

4.Enter the maximum length and then switch to another place
5.Found plaintext displayed

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
TextField(
        //文字默认小写
        textCapitalization: TextCapitalization.none,
        style: TextStyle(
          color: FColor.text33,
          fontSize: widget.fontSize ?? 18.fsp,
        ),
        //是否能够复制粘贴
        enableInteractiveSelection: true,
        fo
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on Android only (not iOS) on both stable and master. The bug only occurs with obscureText: true. Despite the text appearing to be inserted visually, the underlying text does not change (onChanged does not fire) and the character count remains at maxLength. No fix or PR has been proposed.

title: TextEditingController is not firing event when it already has a value on "From Mail" is isued
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Receive a One Time Password through email
2. Start typing in your input field to add one or more characters
3. Then use the smart sugggestion that is shown above the iOS keyboard that proposes to autofill the code (in our case 6 characters)
4. Nothing happens, but the code from the email is shown under quotes as the first suggestion in the iOS keyboard hint bar.

### Expected results

I would expect that the "From Mail" functionality resets the TextEditingController and fires the event with the value. 

### Actual results

The TextEditingController does not fire any change. Debugging the code, I found that neither `set text(String newText)` nor `set value(TextEditingValue newValue)` are called.

### Code sample

Unfortunately I don't know how to provide a sample that allows to mock the "Fill from Email" hint on iOS.


### Screenshots or Video

<details open>
<summary>Video demonstration</summary>


https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/6661557/d28
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug: when a `TextField` has pre-existing text and the iOS 'From Mail' / OTP QuickType autofill suggestion is tapped, the `TextEditingController` receives no notification and the field is not updated. Native iOS apps handle this correctly. Reproduced on stable 3.16 through 3.19 on iOS 14–17. The issue was assigned to `@LongCatIsLooong` but subsequently unassigned due to inactivity. Status: closed by bot for insufficient repro info, but community confirmed the bug is reproducible with the provided sample.

title: Focus issues when embedding a Flutter app inside an iframe
body:
### Steps to reproduce

I've been investigating an issue with Cmd+C not always correctly working to copy text from embedded DevTools. I think I've finally tracked down what's happening, and I think it may be a Flutter issue. I can reproduce what seems like the same bug using DartPad without any VS Code/Flutter.

The issue seems to be that when some SelectableText is selected (by clicking and dragging or double-clicking), focus is not changed, so pressing Cmd+C is then handled by whatever else had focus at the time. A single left-click first does move focus and then things work correctly.


### Expected results

Selecting text with a drag or double-click should move focus so that pressing Ctrl+C copies the selection.

### Actual results

Focus does not change, so Ctrl+C is handled by another part of the application (in this case the DartPad editor, but when embedded in VS Code, it's the VS Code editor).

### Code sample

Here's an example testing on DartPad using the fo
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: `SelectableText`/`EditableText` uses an offscreen `<input>` HTML element as the web embedder backing, and focus-related interactions with this element prevent focus from leaving the Flutter iframe. `SelectionArea` (which does not use `EditableText`) was confirmed as a workaround by @justinmc. The behavior changed on master (original copy-on-drag now works) but a new regression was introduced where clicking into the iframe prevents focus from ever returning to the parent page. DevTools filed follow-up issues (#8929, #8939) and is migrating from `SelectableText` to `SelectionArea`. Assignee removed due to inactivity.

title: Textfield focus can become unattainable on an embedded flutter application in Safari
body:
### Steps to reproduce

The minimal code to reproduce the issue can be found [here](https://github.com/pearlcertification/safari_embedded_bug).

Steps to reproduce:
- Run the application and load the URL in Safari
- Click in the text field and type something
- Keystrokes will update the contents of the text field
- Click outside the embedded widget
- Click in the text field again and type something
- Keystrokes will _not_ update the contents of the text field

You can get around the issue by clicking inside the embedded application (but not in the text field) and then clicking back in the text field. After that, the text field contents will again update on each keystroke.

### Expected results

Typing in a text field should always update the contents of that text field in an embedded Flutter application (or any Flutter application).

### Actual results

After following the steps to reproduce, the text field no longer updates while the user is typing.

### Code sample

<detail
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Issue is confirmed to be Safari-only (Chrome and Firefox do not reproduce). After deleting the text field contents, clicking outside, and clicking back in, keystrokes no longer update the field. A partial improvement was seen on master but the issue was not fully resolved. The reporter applied a JS interop workaround to force HTML-side focus. The issue is noted as related to #137443 which is expected to fix it.

title: EditableText blocked PointerEvent after dismissed in IOS with apple pencil supported
comment_summary: Issue only reproducible on iOS 15.x and 16.x physical devices with Apple Pencil support but not iOS 17+. Requires Chinese (Pinyin) IME input to trigger. iOS layout constraint errors logged after multiple tap attempts. Comments suggest related issue #138395. Team could not reproduce on simulators or iOS 17 devices. Status: P3, stalled; labeled device-specific.

title: [macOS] Japanese-kana input with live conversion disabled produces weird characters
comment_summary: Reproduced on macOS stable and master and also on web (CanvasKit renderer). The issue is that when live conversion is disabled, the delta model receives a replacement with invalid characters (e.g., `^_`) before the correct text is re-inserted. Related issue #149379 was flagged as a possible duplicate. Currently stalled with no fix or PR.

title: TextField add a onCtrlV callback
body:
Hello!

The Flutter textfield are powerful, but still not able to fullfill all usage scenarios.

For instance, on desktop, if users wanna paste rich content such as image in textfiled. There are plenty pacakages such as super_clipboard, pasteboard etc provides ability reads images bytes from clipboard.

Is there a way to make textField have a callback on paste to deal with images?

(PS: contentInsertConfiguration not work at this usage)
comment_summary: Comments clarify that intercepting paste via PasteTextIntent and an Actions widget wrapping the TextField works on non-web platforms, but Ctrl/Cmd+V shortcuts are disabled on web (confirmed in the source). The right-click context menu also does not use intent-actions. No fix proposed for web paste interception; stalled.

title: Updating TextSelectionOverlay causes it to hide and reshow
body:
### Steps to reproduce

 1. Run the app below (an app that makes a change to a property of TextField that gets passed to TextSelectionOverlay).
 2. Drag the selection in order to show the magnifier.

### Expected results

Changes to the magnifier appear smooth, without flashing or hiding/showing.

### Actual results

The magnifier is alternately hidden and shown each frame.

This didn't used to work at all until https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/142463, but that PR still needs to recreate the overlay on each change, so it leads to stuttering like shown in the video.

This is an egregious example because it's better to animate the magnifier in magnifierBuilder. I'm wondering if anyone will encounter this bug in the real world. If you do, please leave a 👍 reaction to this issue.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'dart:math' as math;

import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.16.9 and master. Root cause: each rebuild of TextField with a changed magnifierConfiguration recreates the TextSelectionOverlay, causing the magnifier to hide and reshow every frame. Assigned to @Hixie who noted partial progress but has been distracted; issue has received stale-assignment bot pings. No PR landed.

title: Hardware keyboard handlers are invoked when TextField keyboardType is set to number, phone, dateTime etc. on mobile device.
body:
### Steps to reproduce

Running the below code will suffice.

Tested on OnePlus Nord 2T (Android 13), Samsung Galaxy Tab S6 (Android 12), Samsung Galaxy Tab Active 2 (Android 9) using both Gboard and the in-built keyboards.

### Expected results

If the keyboardType is set to `TextInputType.text` or `TextInputType.datetime` or `TextInputType.name` or any **text-forward** type, the hardware keyboard listener doesn't get invoked. The other keyboard types should exhibit the same behaviour.

### Actual results

When the keyboardType is set to `TextInputType.number` or `TextInputType.phone` or any **number-forward** type, the hardware keyboard listener is invoked on the digit buttons, and if it returns true, the input is not propagated. This is easily worked around, but the behaviour with the different keyboard types is not consistent, and will cause time wasting debug sessions.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutte
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause explained: Android software keyboards for numeric/phone types send simulated hardware key events (unlike text-type keyboards), so `HardwareKeyboard` listeners fire and — if they return `true` — consume the input before it reaches the text field. This is keyboard-app-dependent behavior not under Flutter's control, consistently reproduced with Gboard and AOSP on Android 9–13. Team says documenting the inconsistency is the appropriate response; no framework fix planned.

title: Under certain conditions TextInput does not correctly handle backspaces on Android
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug is Android-only (iOS and web work correctly). Root cause identified in discussion: on Android with `enableSuggestions: false`, the underlying `InputType.TYPE_TEXT_VARIATION_VISIBLE_PASSWORD` flag causes the IME to handle backspace via a hardware-key path (`DeleteCharacterIntent`) rather than the `MethodChannel` text-update path, so a custom `TextInputClient` never receives a `updateEditingValue` for delete. The workaround is to intercept `DeleteCharacterIntent` via `FocusableActionDetector`. A separate but related bug (#143364) was filed for number-entry issues. Status: open, workaround documented.

title: [iOS] Unexpected delta deletion range with a Korean keyboard when the IME replaces a character
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the sample code on a physical iOS device
2. Using a Korean keyboard type "ㅅㅛ"
3. Check the logs

### Expected results

The following deletion delta should be generated:

```dart
TextEditingDeltaDeletion(
  oldText: '. ㅅ',
  deletedRange: TextRange(start: 2, end: 3),
  selection: TextSelection.collapsed(
    offset: 2,
    affinity: TextAffinity.downstream,
  ),
  composing: TextRange(start: -1, end: -1),
)
```

### Actual results

The following deletion delta is reported (the deleted range start at offset 1 instead of 2):

```dart
TextEditingDeltaDeletion(
  oldText: '. ㅅ',
  deletedRange: TextRange(start: 1, end: 3),
  selection: TextSelection.collapsed(
    offset: 2,
    affinity: TextAffinity.downstream,
  ),
  composing: TextRange(start: -1, end: -1),
)
```

Background: In the super_editor package, we always send the text editing value to the IME prepended with the string `". "`. We do this to detect the user pre
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the wrong deletion range is reproducible on stable 3.16.x and master when typing Korean on a physical iOS device. The issue is specific to iOS (not Android or web): iOS sends a batch of deltas where the deletion range includes the invisible prefix characters (`". "`) used by super_editor to detect backspace-at-beginning. No fix or PR is mentioned in comments. Status: open, confirmed iOS-only, assigned to text-input team.

title: [TextField] alignLabelWithHint does not have any effect when used with UnderlineInputBorder
comment_summary: Comments confirm reproduction on stable and master. Analysis explains that `alignLabelWithHint` was originally designed for vertical alignment only; its horizontal effect on `OutlineInputBorder` was added later via PR #115409 without changing the parameter semantics. Fixing `UnderlineInputBorder` to respect `alignLabelWithHint: false` the same way as `OutlineInputBorder` would require a breaking change because `false` is the default. The proposed solution is a new `InputDecoration` parameter, or subclassing `InputBorder` with `isOutlined: true` to use the outline positioning logic. Issue is stalled; no PR linked.

title: `TextField` widgets in Flutter DevTools are being treated as new password inputs

Chrome thinks that the `TextField` is a new password text field and the Chrome password manager popup is displayed.

comment_summary: One triage comment notes Chrome appears to auto-suggest passwords for empty-typed inputs in form tags with `aria-autocomplete="list"`. Further investigation was noted as needed; no fix landed.

title: Crash when requesting focus for a TextField located below a sliver of infinite height
comment_summary: Comments confirm the crash reproduces on Android (stable 3.16 and master). The crash occurs in two stages: first, `NaN` is serialized when `setEditableSizeAndTransform` is called on a TextField not yet in the viewport (because the preceding sliver has infinite height); second, the viewport layout asserts `correctedOffset.isFinite`. A commenter argues this is an invalid widget tree (a sliver of infinite height followed by another sliver is unreachable by scrolling) and suggests an assert should be added via PR #141671. A later comment reports inability to reproduce on stable 3.27.2 on Android. Status: disputed validity; no fix landed.

title: Attempted to send a key down event when no keys are in keysPressed, when using barcode scanner
body:
When I scan a barcode with my barcode scanner I get this error.

```
======== Exception caught by services library ======================================================
The following assertion was thrown during a platform message callback:
Attempted to send a key down event when no keys are in keysPressed. This state can occur if the key event being sent doesn't properly set its modifier flags. This was the event: RawKeyDownEvent#22ace(logicalKey: LogicalKeyboardKey#df100(keyId: "0x200000102", keyLabel: "Shift Left", debugName: "Shift Left"), physicalKey: PhysicalKeyboardKey#ed430(usbHidUsage: "0x000700e1", debugName: "Shift Left"), repeat: false) and its data: RawKeyEventDataWindows#b7606(keyCode: 160, scanCode: 42, characterCodePoint: 0, modifiers: 0)
'package:flutter/src/services/raw_keyboard.dart':
Failed assertion: line 695 pos 7: 'event is! RawKeyDownEvent || _keysPressed.isNotEmpty'


Either the assertion indicates an error in the framework itself, or we should provid
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The barcode scanner emits a Shift Left key-down event with modifiers: 0 (no modifier flags set), which violates the Flutter assertion that keysPressed must be non-empty before a key-down event. The root cause is a mismatch between what the barcode scanner reports and what Flutter's raw keyboard logic expects. @dkwingsmt confirmed familiarity with similar issues and noted entangled keyboard logic makes investigation difficult; no fix has landed and the issue is stalled.

title: [Android] - Tapping TextInput unfocuses it on Tablet emulator
comment_summary: The issue is specific to the Pixel C emulator (API 29). On the device, TextInput receives a TextInputClient.onConnectionClosed message from the platform channel after the user taps, causing unfocus. The reporter confirmed they switched to a different emulator as a workaround. @LongCatIsLooong was CC'd for triage. Status unclear; possibly emulator-specific.

title: Input always move the cursor at end of text selection
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new project with basic template with `flutter create input_test`.
2. Paste the code below at `Code sample` section.

3. Scroll to the end of page, select any word and try to move the select cursor over the text above looks like on the video attached.

### Expected results

1. When selection moves the scroll position in reverse mode (moving to top on this case) the cursor should be placed on the first character of selection, avoiding this strange scroll flick.
2. Otherwise, the cursor must be placed at the last character of selection (seems correct in this case).

### Actual results

When selection moves the scroll position in reverse mode (moving to top on this case) the cursor always retorn to the last character of input, causing a strange scroll, especially when the distance between the start and end of selection is very large or has reached the top

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed Android-only (iOS does not reproduce). Root cause identified: `_bringIntoViewBySelectionState` in `editable_text.dart` is called on every selection change including handle drags, causing the view to scroll back to the selection end instead of following the drag. The fix is to skip `bringIntoView` when the selection change is caused by a handle drag. Labeled as good first issue. A contributor has confirmed reproduction on current `main` and is investigating a fix with a regression test.

title: When `validator`of `TextFormField` widget returns an `AppLozalizations.of(context).translationKey` value, error message does not refresh with locale change
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a Form in your Flutter App using a `TextFormField()`.
2. Localize your Flutter app according to [the docs](https://docs.flutter.dev/ui/accessibility-and-internationalization/internationalization).
3. Build simple form validation according to [the docs](https://docs.flutter.dev/cookbook/forms/validation), by returning a localized string via the `validator` (e.g. `AppLocalizations.of(context).myTranslation`).
4. Test the app using `flutter run`, and provide invalid input and trigger validation.
5. This will show an according error message displayed in the current app locale.
6. Trigger a locale language switch in your app (we're currently doing this using a provider to whose value the `locale` property of the `MaterialApp` / `CupertinoApp` listens to).

### Expected results

The locale should be switched in the entire app, to the language to which the locale switch occurred. 

### Actual results

Every string present in the app via `AppLoc
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug is reproducible on stable (3.19.3) and master. The root cause is that FormField stores the validator's return value as a string and does not re-run validators when locale dependencies change; only calling formKey.currentState.validate() again refreshes the error text. A commenter proposed fixing this by re-running validators when dependencies change and adding a BuildContext parameter to FormFieldValidator. A community workaround was shared: a custom BaseForm widget that watches for locale changes via didUpdateWidget and calls validate() in a post-frame callback. No official fix has landed; status is open.

title: SelectionArea doesn't apply to TextFields
comment_summary: Comments explain the root cause: RenderEditable has its own independent selection implementation that does not participate in SelectionArea's selection. @Renzo-Olivares was working on unifying the two systems, which would fix this for free. Status: open, work-in-progress on unification.

title: `TextField.minLines` documentation is unclear about its `maxLines` interaction
body:
Link to the documentation: https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField/minLines.html

> If this is null (default), text container starts with enough vertical space for one line and grows to accommodate additional lines as they are entered.

This doesn't seem to be true. When `maxLines` is set to a non-null value, `minLine` is effectively set to the same value as `maxLines`. It seems the real meaning of `null` here is to defer to the `maxLines` parameter (which makes the default a bit strange, shouldn't the two parameters be orthogonal?).

This is hinted in the `maxLines` documentation: https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField/maxLines.html

> The input's height is large enough for the given number of lines. If additional lines are entered the input scrolls vertically.
> `const TextField(maxLines: 2)`

but I wasn't able to find anything that directly spells out that interaction when `minLines` is null but `maxLines` is non-null.
comment_summary: One comment suggests `minLines` could default to `1` instead of deferring to `maxLines`, but this would be a breaking change requiring a dart fix.

title: [Windows 11] Paste from clipboard history is broken
body:
### Steps to reproduce

In Windows 10+, you can open your [clipboard history](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/tips/clipboard-history) using `Win + v`. This shows a popup with a list of previously copied content. Clicking on an item in this list should paste it. 

*Steps to reproduce*
On a Windows 11 machine (this does not repro on Windows 10):

1. Create a simple text field
2. Open the Windows clipboard history using `Win + v`
3. Click on an item to paste it

![image](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/75003816/848f2725-7b9a-4d6b-9176-7ab0f8f6c1ae)


### Expected results

The data should be pasted into the textfield

### Actual results

i'm seeing empty text field.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
  runApp(MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: Center(
          child: Padding(
            padding: const EdgeInsets.all(8.0),
            child: TextFormField(),
          ),
        ),
    
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: Windows 11's clipboard history popup synthesizes Ctrl+V key events using a non-standard physical key ID (0x1600000000 instead of the real Control Left scan code), which the Windows embedder does not correctly map to standard Flutter key events, so the paste shortcut is never triggered. Confirmed Windows 11-specific; works on Windows 10 and web (though web throws an assertion error). A community workaround that intercepts PlatformDispatcher.onKeyData and remaps the synthetic key codes was shared. The fix requires changes in the Windows embedder (engine-side). Status is open and stalled.

title: [ios]Support secure paste for iPad hardware keyboard shortcut (CMD+V)
body:
## Problem: 

When user type CMD+V, Flutter app shows paste warning, but native app does not show the warning. (related: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/103163)

## Why flutter apps trigger this warning?

The path is quite complex with multiple steps: 
1. User press CMD+V
2. iOS calls `FlutterTextInputView`'s key press [event handlers](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/23fc488a6c33b7633ecff4b27713782fc4dcc640/shell/platform/darwin/ios/framework/Source/FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm#L2219-L2238)  
3. The keyboard event is [eventually relayed to FlutterKeyboardManager](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/23fc488a6c33b7633ecff4b27713782fc4dcc640/shell/platform/darwin/ios/framework/Source/FlutterViewController.mm#L1863), which sends to the framework
4. Framework handles the keyboard event and [request access to clipboard](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/2d422a326120714355d06aa713f809c9aea84651/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/editable_text.dart#L2483) from th
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: CMD+V goes through Flutter's keyboard handler which programmatically accesses the pasteboard (triggering paste warning), while native apps use private iOS APIs unlocked by default handlers. Removing custom handlers allows default paste without warning but may break KeyboardListener widget. Labeled P3 as iPad hardware keyboard-only.

title: nested `FormField`'s produce inconsistent state when propagating via `onSave`
comment_summary: Root cause identified: `Form.save()` iterates a `Set` of fields in non-deterministic order, so the outer `FormField`'s `onSaved` may run before the inner `TextFormField`'s `onSaved` calls `state.didChange()`, leaving the outer field stale. A commenter suggested using `Form.onChanged` or doing all saving in the parent's `onSaved` as workarounds. No PR filed; issue remains open awaiting team input on correct behavior.

title: Spellcheck code uses context across an asynchronous gap
body:
### Steps to reproduce

I enabled spellcheck for `TextFormField`:
```
final spellCheck = kIsWeb
        ? null
        : SpellCheckConfiguration(
            spellCheckService: DefaultSpellCheckService(),
          );
```
and published to GooglePlay to get a pre-launch report

### Expected results

No exceptions are thrown

### Actual results

Crashlytics recorded "crashes" with the following stacktrace:
```
Fatal Exception: io.flutter.plugins.firebase.crashlytics.FlutterError: Null check operator used on a null value. Error thrown .
       at State.context(framework.dart:958)
       at EditableTextState.buildTextSpan(editable_text.dart:5071)
       at EditableTextState._performSpellCheck(editable_text.dart:3843)
```
Quick review of the code lead me to the
```
final List<SuggestionSpan>? suggestions = await
        _spellCheckConfiguration
          .spellCheckService!
            .fetchSpellCheckSuggestions(localeForSpellChecking!, text);

      if (suggestions =
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause confirmed as a use-build-context-synchronously violation: spellcheck code uses a BuildContext after an async gap, causing 'widget has been unmounted' crashes in production Crashlytics logs. Comments provide a reliable reproduction sample (append/delete a character, wait a second). One commenter applied a user-side workaround filtering the specific exception in FlutterError.onError. Labeled for further investigation; no fix PR linked.

title: Samsung Pass unfocuses TextField
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Write a Flutter app that uses an `AutofillGroup` with one ore more `TextField`s.
2. Make sure Samsung Pass is set up on your device. If it is not installed, here is the link: https://apps.samsung.com/appquery/appDetail.as?appId=com.samsung.android.samsungpass&cId=000005295090
Follow these instructions to set up the Samsung Pass (see the How to set up Samsung Pass section): https://www.samsung.com/latin_en/support/mobile-devices/how-to-set-up-and-use-samsung-pass-on-your-galaxy-phone/
3. Run the app on a Samsung device with Samsung Pass on
4. Fill the `TextField`s

### Expected results

Samsung Pass popup is shown, but does not unfocus the TextField

### Actual results

After the very first letter in the TextField is entered, Samsung Pass popup is shown, the TextField is unfocused, so user should tap the TextField again to continue filling the form.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flu
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue is not limited to Samsung Pass: Kaspersky, KeePassDX, ProtonPass, and the C2 Password browser extension all trigger the same unfocus behavior on Android. One commenter extends the scope to Flutter Web, where password manager autofill via browser extension causes fields to lose focus on Windows (Chrome), macOS (Chrome/Firefox), and Android (Chrome). The issue is not reproduced in native Android apps, indicating it is Flutter-specific. Status: open, no fix or PR linked.

title: api.text_magnifier.text_magnifier.0_test golden image is flaky
body:
See image history here: https://flutter-gold.skia.org/search?corpus=flutter&include_ignored=false&left_filter=name%3Dapi.text_magnifier.text_magnifier.0_test&max_rgba=0&min_rgba=0&negative=true&not_at_head=false&positive=true&reference_image_required=false&right_filter=&sort=descending&untriaged=true

Each differently colored dot is a unique image result.
We are skipping this test for now as it is keeping the tree red generating unique images as commits are landing.
comment_summary: PR #144351 was filed to disable the flaky golden test. A commenter noted the flakiness appears to be with the text rendering itself, not the magnifier widget. The disabling PR was merged as a workaround while the root cause remains unresolved.

title: [iOS] text field loses focus after Look Up text selection
comment_summary: Comments identify this as likely a regression: the original look-up feature author confirmed it worked correctly at merge time, implying a subsequent change broke re-focus. The root cause is linked to app-lifecycle focus resets introduced in #142930, partially reverted via #148612, but the text field still loses focus on master. A connection to shake-to-undo (#150522) was noted, as the native text field keeps the input connection open after losing focus to receive undo commands. PR #173846 has a related comment. Status: assigned (okorohelijah), PR activity noted but issue still open.

title: Arrow key navigation gets stuck at the end of RTL text using Samsung Keyboard
body:
```dart
const text = 'ی';
final controller = TextEditingController(text: text * 10)..selection = TextSelection.collapsed(offset: 10); // Selection is placed at the logical end of the text.

TextField(textDirection: TextDirection.rtl, controller: controller, autofocus: true),
```
## Steps to reproduce
1. wait for the text field autofocus
2. switch to the Samsung keyboard
3. press <kbd>←</kbd> to move the cursor to the start of the text (since flutter currently uses logical order for arrow key navigation).

## Expected behavior:
the cursor moves to the right

## Actual behavior:
the cursor does not move.
comment_summary: Root cause identified: Samsung keyboard ignores the left-arrow key when the cursor is already at the visual left end of RTL text, because Flutter uses logical order for arrow key navigation while Samsung keyboard uses visual order. Gboard is not affected. On native, arrow key navigation moves in visual order which Flutter does not currently support; a commenter notes this is a long-standing architectural gap.

title: In Desktop platforms, TextFormField ignores the HOME, END, and arrow keys on the Numpad.
comment_summary: Root cause: Flutter does not define shortcuts for Numpad navigation keys. PRs #145146 and #145464 were merged to add NumLock-aware shortcuts and numpad navigation for Linux. A Windows fix was prepared but blocked by #98377 (Windows NumLock state reporting bug). The issue is fixed on Linux, still broken on Windows. A side effect was reported where the Linux fix broke numpad number input in web apps on Linux browsers.

title: [web] Autofill forms aren't updated properly
body:
In the web engine, when we receive a `TextInput.updateConfig` message from the framework, we don't properly update the autofill form and its fields.

Details:
- When the new config is [received](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/351470ce31cb4852b826d3ed361369191768f1f8/lib/web_ui/lib/src/engine/text_editing/text_editing.dart#L2145-L2147), we create a [new `EngineAutofillForm`](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/351470ce31cb4852b826d3ed361369191768f1f8/lib/web_ui/lib/src/engine/text_editing/text_editing.dart#L978-L981).
- That new `EngineAutofillForm` [creates its own `<form>`](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/351470ce31cb4852b826d3ed361369191768f1f8/lib/web_ui/lib/src/engine/text_editing/text_editing.dart#L206) element and all the fields.
- The new `<form>` element isn't appended to the DOM because we only append during initialization (e.g. [here](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/351470ce31cb4852b826d3ed361369191768f1f8/lib/web_ui/lib/src/engine/text_editing/t
...[truncated]
comment_summary: One triage comment proposes calling `placeForm()` explicitly when `updateConfig` is received as a fix for the bug.

title: Add support to macOS text replacements
body:
### Use case

MacOS supports configuring system-wide text replacements on the settings:

![CleanShot 2023-08-17 at 13 02 51@2x](https://github.com/superlistapp/super_editor/assets/31278849/bc09f8b3-8468-4924-89cb-4e1809735050)

After typing a configured replacement, the text is highlighted and a popover is displayed, so the user can accept the replacement:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/7597082/6ab8ffa8-163d-43c1-b5d3-00dfb9bf5dfc

Flutter `TextField` doesn't seem to support that:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/7597082/2e9a9299-5c22-42eb-83dc-8db4b5e56d0c




### Proposal

Implement support for that feature.

title: PopupMenuButton click area is wrong when placed in an InputDecoration
comment_summary: Comments identified that the hit-testing problem is not specific to `PopupMenuButton` but also occurs with `TextButton` in an `InputDecoration` label. The root cause hypothesis is that `InputDecorator`'s layout of the `label` does not account for its transform during hit testing. Confirmed on stable 3.19.3 and master 3.21. No fix or PR linked; stalled.

A live demo was provided at kaonick.github.io. The issue is that the browser's focus/input handling is disrupted by the cross-frame iframe context. No fix or PR proposed.

class

comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on physical iPad devices (iPadOS 17.2+) but not on simulators. The bug only triggers after the magnifier appears during a long press, and it freezes silently with no console errors. A commenter linked related issues #142294, #138395, and #136244 suggesting a shared root cause where a native view blocks touch events after the magnifier is shown. title: RawAutocomplete widget does not allow to invoke its intents/actions
body:
### Steps to reproduce

When the `RawAutocomplete` widget is created with the `fieldViewBuilder` parameter. The context passed to the `fieldViewBuilder` does not have access to the previous/next item intents registered by the `RawAutocomplete`, so the following call fail to find requested intent: `Actions.invoke(context, const AutocompleteNextOptionIntent());`

### Expected results

It would be really really useful if the `Actions.invoke(context, const AutocompleteNextOptionIntent());` call worked, so we could use it to activate the autocomplete dropdown using an UI action (e.g. IconButton) or map own keyboard shortcuts to these actions.

### Actual results

Currently the call is failing with "Unable to find an action for an Intent with type AutocompleteNextOptionIntent in an Actions widget in the given context." error. The complete example below also throws this error in the web-based DartPad.

I'm guessing this is because the parent context is passed to the `fieldViewBuilder`, but 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug reproduces on both stable 3.19.3 and master 3.21. The root cause is that `RawAutocomplete` passes the parent context to `fieldViewBuilder`, which is outside the `Actions` widget that registers the autocomplete intents; wrapping with a `Builder` would fix it. No fix or PR linked; open.

title: Scrollbar does not reach the bottom of the textfield
comment_summary: Comments confirm the scrollbar-not-reaching-bottom bug is limited to mobile (iOS and Android); macOS is unaffected. The root cause is traced to `ScrollbarPainter._totalTrackMainAxisOffsets` using `padding.vertical` instead of 0. Two workarounds are shared: wrapping with `SafeArea` (partial fix) or wrapping the `Scrollbar`/`ListView` with `MediaQuery.removePadding(removeBottom: true)`. Status: open, no PR, workaround available.

title: Korean input keyboard in android phone adding spaces between characters not creating proper text
comment_summary: Root cause isolated: setting `_textController.text = v` inside the onChange callback (reassigning the controller reference rather than setting its text property) disrupts composing state on Android with Korean input, but not on iOS. After the Gboard update this is not reproducible via the original code; the issue only manifests with the misuse pattern.

title: App Background Image Resizes with toggled keyboard in screen pushed above `CupertinoTabScaffold`
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Place any sample background image within `assets/imgs/bg.png` in a flutter dummy project.
2. Run the provided Code Sample in a simulator (e.g. iPhone 15).
3. Switch among the tabs by clicking on them and tap into the text field -> Keyboard shows up, Background img stays fixed in the bg.
4. On any tab screen, tap on the button `Push To Upper Screen (Normal Push)`. 
5. Tap into the text field in the pushed screen, and you'll see that the background img resizes.
6. Leave that screen and tap the button `Push To Upper Screen (Rootnav Push)`. 
7. Tap into the text field in the pushed screen, and you'll see that the background img DOES NOT resize.

### Expected results

I actually expected the outcome of 5. to be the same as 7. (but with the bottom navigation bar displayed below). I'm unsure if this is actually a bug, as it makes it impossible to use a non-resizing background image in your app as soon as your app uses pushed screens, together with a `Cup
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments clarify the behavior: a normal `Navigator.push` inside a `CupertinoTabScaffold` nests the pushed route inside the tab scaffold, so the scaffold's `resizeToAvoidBottomInset` resizes everything including the background container. Using `rootNavigator: true` avoids the resize because it pushes above the tab scaffold. One commenter provides a workaround using `resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false` plus `EdgeInsets.only(bottom: MediaQuery.viewInsets.bottom)` padding on the container. The team member who triaged considers it expected behavior given the navigation architecture.

title: Custom `TextInputControl`s should be able to perform `TextInputActions`
body:
### Use case

We are building a custom on-screen keyboard using `TextInputControl` and while typing works great, there seems to be no way to perform a `TextInputAction`, since it only seems to go through the MethodChannel in `TextInput`.

Thus, we currently can't implement a "Submit" button on our custom keyboard.

### Proposal

Similarly to `TextInput.updateEditingValue(editingState)`, a method like `TextInput.performAction(textInputAction)` could be exposed, that calls `performAction` on the currently connected `TextInputClient`.
comment_summary: One comment acknowledges the gap: there is no public API to trigger a `TextInputAction` from a custom keyboard, and the team needs to investigate whether native Android/iOS expose such an API.

Investigation paused as @LongCatIsLooong is working on a broader prototype for iOS text input. Status: under investigation, no PR yet.

title: Flutter 3.19.4 | Android Specific | `enableSuggestions = false` on `TextField` causing emoji button to disappear.
body: ### Steps to reproduce Use a `TextField` and set `enableSuggestions` to `false`. ``` TextField(enableSuggestions: false) ``` This will result in emoji button, which opens emoji keyboard, to disappear. Not sure if Emojis have anything to do with suggestions. #### Note: Tried similar code on my older Flutter 3.13.4 version, and `enableSuggestions` have no impact. It doesn't even remove suggestions box. Seems like a bug back then and the fix must have also caused this side effect. ### Expected results Emoji button should not disappear when suggestions are turned off `enableSuggestions: true` | `enableSuggestions: false` :-------------------------:|:-------------------------: | ### Actual results ...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause: the fix for #71679 (engine PR #46037) added `InputType.TYPE_TEXT_VARIATION_VISIBLE_PASSWORD` alongside `TYPE_TEXT_FLAG_NO_SUGGESTIONS` to suppress suggestions on Samsung/GBoard devices, but `VISIBLE_PASSWORD` disables emoji input and Chinese IME input. The team confirmed that no Android `InputType` flag combination simultaneously disables suggestions and preserves emoji/CJK input even in native apps, making a clean fix non-trivial. Status: open, waiting for a flag combination solution from the community.

title: Support focusNode and textEditingController parameters for Autocomplete (as passthrus to RawAutocomplete)
body:
### Use case

It can be useful to have the ability to create (and control) the `focusNode` and `textEditingController` of an Autocomplete widget.  The underlying `RawAutocomplete` widget supports accepting these as optional parameters.


### Proposal

I propose to adding the `focusNode` and `textEditingController` parameters supported by the `RawAutocomplete` widget to the material Autocomplete widget and passing these through to the `RawAutocomplete` constructor.

I understand that I can re-implement the Autocomplete widget myself, and I have done this on multiple occasions - and I am sure that I am not alone.
comment_summary: PR #146995 was a partial fix with outstanding feedback. A comment confirms a fix has been merged (resolved).

title: Missing SemanticsNode of TextFormField's validator text when using `tester.pump`
body:
### Steps to reproduce

I found this odd behaviour in testing `TextFormField` validation. 

I created a small app with one `TextFormField` and one submit `ElevatedButton`. When you submit with empty `TextFormField`, you'll get an `errorText` (of `InputDecoration` of `TextFormField`) saying "validation error".

I wrote a test for that by pumping the app widget, then tapping submit and doing either `pump` or `pumpAndSettle` then checking if "validation error" exists both on screen and in semantics.

The test passes for finding "validation error" on screen but the issue is finding semantics does not pass when using `pump` and it does not show in semantics tree. Although it passes using `pumpAndSettle` but documentation discourages from using `pumpAndSettle` in general.

Is it a bug? Is `pump` suppose to show `errorText` on screen but not on semantics tree?

### Expected results

When I use `tap` then `pump`, test `find.bySemanticsLabel` should pass for the `errorText` cases 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug: after `tester.tap` + `tester.pump`, the `errorText` of `TextFormField` is visible on screen but its semantics label is absent from the semantics tree; it only appears after `pumpAndSettle`. The issue is reproducible on stable 3.19 and master 3.22. No root cause analysis or fix proposed in comments beyond reproduction confirmation.

title: Raw web keyCode cannot be extracted from KeyEvent
body:
### Description

We have migrated our keyboard handling from Flutter 3.16 to 3.19 according to [this migration guide](https://docs.flutter.dev/release/breaking-changes/key-event-migration#migrating-your-code-that-uses-rawkeyevent). After the migration we can't get the keyCode from the KeyEvent as we did before.
We're using Flutter for a webos application and for that we need to get the keyCode from the `RawKeyEventDataWeb` to distinguish between the special keys coming from the [LG magic remote](https://webostv.developer.lge.com/develop/guides/magic-remote).

### Expected results

KeyCode like 413 for stop

### Actual results

We're getting the `usbHidUsage`: `0x01700000000` and the `LogicalKeyboardKey` `unidentified`

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
Focus(
      onKeyEvent: (FocusNode node, KeyEvent event) {
        if (event is RawKeyDownEvent && event.data is RawKeyEventDataWeb) {
          final keyCode = (event.data as Ra
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments from @gspencergoog clarify that the new `KeyEvent` API intentionally does not expose platform-specific keycodes (like web `keyCode`) to be platform-agnostic. The correct fix is to add mappings for the missing WebOS remote keys in the keycode generator database. A commenter investigates and finds that WebOS remote key events provide only a `keyCode` with an empty `code` field, which the current mapping system cannot handle. A request to add scan code fallback was raised. Expanding scope: Android TV and Fire TV users also need raw keycodes. Status: stalled, no PR, requires mapping addition in keycode generator.

title: Make Autocomplete show a custom options view while `optionsBuilder` future is being resolved
comment_summary: One comment links to related issue #108258.

}
enum ColorLabel {

Comments clarify entries do show if the user scrolls up; the panel is actually rendered but clipped by the keyboard boundary. Comparison with Android Compose ExposedDropdownMenuBox shows the native behavior places the list above the keyboard. No workaround or PR; stalled.

title: [iPad] Apple pencil input is not properly captured by the textfield toolbar
comment_summary: Confirmed reproduced on stable 3.19.6 and master. Comments identify the root cause as the toolbar visually shifting or showing different items when the Apple Pencil interacts with it — the pencil appears to register hits on different toolbar buttons than intended. Investigated in `CupertinoTextSelectionToolbarButton` (wraps `CupertinoButton` in a `GestureDetector`); needs a physical Apple Pencil to confirm whether stylus taps are properly handled. Status: under investigation, no PR.

title: [web] TextField is completely white while editing when using Netwrix Password Secure extension
comment_summary: Root cause identified: the Netwrix Password Secure browser extension (available for Chrome and Firefox) interferes with Flutter web's text rendering, making the text transparent and the background white while editing. The issue does not reproduce with a plain HTML input element, indicating Flutter's DOM text input implementation is vulnerable to the extension's CSS injection. This is categorized as the same class of issue as #125125, to be addressed by the web text input redesign tracked in #139379.

The original intent of `navigationMode` was to support TV/D-pad input. A community workaround plugin `native_textfield_tv` was published. Stalled with no framework fix.

title: Text field loses focus when changing Gboard language on Android
comment_summary: Root cause traced to Android's keyboard-switching dialog sending a `hideSoftInputFromWindow` signal, but commenting out those engine calls did not fix it, suggesting the cause is subtler platform behavior. Reproducible on Android 31–34 but not Android 30. Two commenters reported it stopped occurring on their Pixel 7 after upgrading to Android 15, but confirmed it still reproduces on Android 14 when using the 'Change keyboard' dialog (not the globe key). Stalled; no fix or PR.

The only time I observed it working 100% of the time was when we had a similar structure to ***1.

Also I am confident that we have the apple site association set-up correctly as well. This was also tested on a real IPhone 15 pro max, IOS version 17.4.1.

1. Copy provided code onto a page.
2. Try in
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm reproduction on stable 3.22.0 and master with a physical iPhone. A second user reports the prompt always appears when the keyboard is dismissed (not when the button is clicked), and a third user notes the same with multiple password fields on a signup page. No root cause, fix, or PR identified; status is stalled with no team response.

title: On Android, KeyboardListener catches software keyboard input.
comment_summary: Comments from @LongCatIsLooong explain the root cause: the Android `InputConnection.sendKeyEvent()` API allows software keyboards to send backspace and enter as hardware `KeyEvent`s, which Flutter does not distinguish from physical key events. The engine has separate call paths that could theoretically be used to distinguish them, but this is not exposed to the framework. The issue is labeled P3 and developers are invited to submit a PR to remove the conflating logic in `InputConnectionAdaptor.java`. Status: stalled, P3, no fix or PR.

title: Collapsing the software keyboard doesn't lose the focus from textfield in 3.22.0, while it worked in 3.19.6
comment_summary: Root cause identified as an intentional engine change in https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/49366, which prevents focus loss when the keyboard is collapsed on Android. This broke the pattern where collapsing the keyboard also unfocused the TextField, allowing `requestFocus()` to re-open the keyboard on button press. Users confirmed the regression in 3.22.0 vs 3.19.3. A request was made for a configurable option. No fix or workaround confirmed.

title: Add-to-app should support predictive back route transitions
body:
### Use case

As of https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/52302, predictive back works in Android add-to-app everywhere except within Flutter routes (predictive back route transitions was added in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/141373). Add-to-app should support predictive back route transitions too.

Support route transitions in FlutterFragment. This would be similar to how it was added for FlutterActivity in https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/49093, but in FlutterFragment like https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/52302, which added basic predictive back support there.

Predictive back route transitions within Flutter look like this:


https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/8436039/96b6dc20-89c9-45db-b8b4-aec7e996477b

### How to reproduce

Run an add-to-app Flutter app where the Flutter part has multiple routes, like this: https://github.com/justinmc/flutter-add-to-app

Perform a back gesture to go from one Flutter route to another. Y
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Only a bot comment about an irrelevant label; no substantive new information.

title: canRequestFocus is a duplicate parameter on TextField
body:
### Use case

Instead of TextField.canRequestFocus, people should use TextField.focusNode.canRequestFocus. The parameter should be removed to avoid the complexity that comes with this duplication.

### Proposal

Deprecate TextField.canRequestFocus. Cases that use it, such as DropdownMenu, should migrate to focusNode.canRequestFocus.

### History

TextField.canRequestFocus was added in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/117504 as an oversight (see https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/130011#issuecomment-1624420263).

I attempted to fix this with https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/132273, but I ran into Google test failures that I wasn't sure how to fix.
comment_summary: One comment questions whether adding `canRequestFocus` to `TextField` was intentional; no resolution.

title: Magnifier inside FittedBox doesn't work properly

comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: ScrollUpdateNotification.dragDetails is null for non-drag scroll events (mouse wheel, trackpad) on desktop/web, so the onDrag dismissal condition is never met. PR #154675 fixed the issue but was reverted in PR #161277, so the issue is currently landed-but-reopened. A workaround using a ScrollController listener to manually unfocus on scroll was provided.

title: [Linux] FocusableActionDetector not working
comment_summary: The issue is confirmed reproducible on Linux master and beta channels but not consistently reproduced by the Flutter team member on their Linux setup. Bisection by the reporter narrowed the regression to Flutter 3.19.0; keyboard events work under Wayland but not X11 on KDE Neon. A workaround using HardwareKeyboard or Focus widget instead of FocusableActionDetector was shared, but FocusableActionDetector specifically does not trigger shortcuts on Linux while Focus does, narrowing the bug to the shortcuts/actions layer inside FocusableActionDetector. Currently open and stalled, with no PR or root cause pinned.

No error.

Tons of errors:
```

void main() {

comment_summary: Engine-level assertion failure in FlKeyEmbedderResponder (lookup_hash_table pressing_records) on Linux Wayland (confirmed on Arch + KDE 6 Wayland and Ubuntu 23.10 Wayland; does not reproduce under XWayland). The engine emits key events with physical/logical key 0 that fail the framework assertion in KeyEventManager.handleKeyData. Stack trace provided. CC'd to @dkwingsmt. title: onFieldSubmitted is not triggered in desktop platforms when `readOnly` is true
comment_summary: Root cause identified by a commenter: `_shouldCreateInputConnection` returns `false` for `readOnly: true` on desktop (non-web), which prevents `TextInputConnection` from being created, blocking `onFieldSubmitted` and also breaking Edit menu Copy/Select All on macOS. Web works because `kIsWeb` short-circuits to `true`. A `Shortcuts`-based workaround was provided. The behavior is considered inconsistent with web and may need a design decision on whether `onSubmitted` semantics apply to read-only fields.

title: [iOS] Add Translate button to text fields' context menu
body: In WWDC24, Apple announce the new Translation API https://developer.apple.com/documentation/Translation This would enable adding the Translate option to the context edit menu, which was previously blocked by not being able to access the Translate API Part of https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/107578 WWDC video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MuIFhbHNmqA
comment_summary: Comments confirm the Translation API is Swift-only, requiring engine-level Swift support tracked in #144791. A note was added to that issue.

title: Support iOS 18 Writing tools text input feature

title: Expose low level IME interactions
body:
### Use case

Currently, it's very hard for Flutter developers to make changes to Flutter's text input experience without losing access to Flutter's entire text input stack. Specifically, if I want to [example]

This problem arises because in each of Flutter's platform embedders, Flutter abstracts differences in the platforms' text input APIs and communicates with the framework using common platform channel methods ([text_input.dart](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/b44c67e2e8/packages/flutter/lib/src/services/text_input.dart)). For Flutter app developers, there is no access to the platform's unique nuances if they aren't covered in the common platform channel methods. It's not possible to access them by writing a plugin without rewriting all the rest of Flutter's text input code as well.

### Proposal

Instead of abstracting platform differences in the engine, the platform channel calls could mirror the native platform APIs. Any abstraction could be done in the framework, on 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: This is a design proposal thread. @matthew-carroll provided an extensive critique arguing the current platform channel abstraction is a lowest-common-denominator approach that violates Flutter's own stated principles. @knopp discussed technical approaches for synchronous platform-thread/UI-thread communication via FFI. Discussion converged on the need for a declared mission to port all platform IME APIs verbatim, and a related issue #150525 was filed for the merged-thread approach.
