title: Flickering context menu when re-rendering
comment_summary: Root cause identified: every call to `build` creates a new closure for `contextMenuBuilder`; `EditableText` detects the builder changed and disposes the selection overlay, which removes and re-adds the overlay entry and restarts the `FadeTransition` animation, causing the visible flicker. The workaround is to use an instance method reference (from a `StatefulWidget`) rather than an inline function, or pass the builder reference through the widget tree to avoid recreation. A deeper analysis points to three code locations as the root: `ContextMenuController`'s unconditional remove, `TextSelectionOverlay.show` always being called, and `EditableText` aggressively disposing the overlay on builder change. Refactoring to use `OverlayPortal` is suggested as the real fix (#107732). Currently open with a workaround available; no PR.

title: [web] Remove virtual keyboard timer hack on iOS Safari
body:
On iOS, we have a hack that moves the input element off screen while the virtual keyboard is popping up, and later positions the input element back where it's supposed to be. This was done to prevent Safari from scrolling the page while the virtual keyboard is popping up.

Today, we have a modern API that we can use to prevent that behavior without any hacks:
- [`HTMLElement.focus({preventScroll: true});`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLElement/focus#preventscroll)

This API is available since iOS Safari 15.5. Let's try it and cleanup the old hack if it works.

title: CupertinoTextField autocorrect highlight vertically misaligned
comment_summary: The autocorrect highlight is drawn by iOS, not Flutter, so fixing is non-trivial. PR #150770 was ruled out as the culprit. Team asked whether the offset is proportional to font size, and suggested moving FlutterTextInputView as a last resort workaround. Related to #150815.

title: iOS autocorrect highlight persists in field after hot restart
comment_summary: Comments suggest this may be related to #137666 and #150813. The hypothesis is that after hot restart the FlutterTextInputView is not properly removed, leaving the autocorrect highlight on screen. No fix or PR.

title: `Shadow` Gradient in TextField

title: [flutter web] A highlight of characters is incorrect on composing mode at TextField.
comment_summary: Comments confirm the composing-underline highlight misalignment on Flutter web across Android Chrome, iOS Safari, and desktop macOS Chrome. Does not occur on Android/macOS native apps. Also occurs with `maxLines` set to a fixed value, causing the suggestion list to render at the wrong position. A comment suggests this may self-resolve with native text inputs on web. No PR; stalled.

title: [flutter web] When converting characters from hiragana to kanji, the display is distorted at TextFiled by using Japanese Keyboard ATOK
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug does not reproduce on Android native app with ATOK, indicating it is web-specific. The issue is scoped to Flutter Web with Chrome on Android using the ATOK IME; GBoard does not trigger it. A commenter noted the doubled character also appears in a different font, suggesting it may be reproducible without ATOK by exploiting font fallback. Status is open, assigned to the web team for investigation, no PR.

title: [web] Button becomes un-tappable in Flutter Web when a TextField is present.
comment_summary: Root cause identified: the customer's index.html was missing a DOCTYPE declaration, causing Chrome Quirks mode. In Quirks mode, form elements have an unexpected margin-block-end: 1em CSS causing the flt-text-editing-host to grow by 16px per form added. Fix needed: reset margin CSS for form elements and warn developers about missing doctype.

title: [iOS] TextInputType breaks previous focus
body:
### Steps to reproduce

- Create two or more TextFields within the same column, once with keyboardType = TextInputType.number or TextInputType.phone.

- Navigate backwards using keyboard navigation (Shift+TAB) without release the Shift button.



### Expected results

Keeping Shift key pressed, every hit on TAB button moves the focus on the previous TextField.

### Actual results

Keeping Shift key pressed, every hit on TAB button moves the focus on the previous TextField. Whenever reaches the one with keyboard type, it starts to move the focus on the next TextField instead of the previous.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
void main() {
  runApp(
    MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: SafeArea(
          child: Column(
            children: [
              TextField(),
              TextField(
                keyboardType: TextInputType.number,
              ),
              TextField(),
            ],
          )
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments narrow the root cause to iOS only: when Shift is held continuously and Tab is pressed multiple times, the framework receives a synthesized Shift KeyUpEvent even though the key is still physically held down, causing the shortcut resolver to pick `NextFocusAction` instead of `PreviousFocusAction`. Issue is labeled as engine-level and @dkwingsmt was tagged for investigation into why the engine generates a spurious Shift key-up on iOS.

title: Windows desktop app completely losing keyboard focus
body:
### Steps to reproduce

Rarely it happens with windows desktop app, that the application completely stops being responsive to any hardware keyboard input. I managed to discover only one scenario where the issue can be reliably reproduced.

1. create a new project, e.g. flutter create test_app --platforms windows
2. add a new TextField widget
3. build and run
4. press Win+D to minimize all apps
5. press Win + D to regain focus
6. try writting into the TextField

### Expected results

Input appears in the TextField

### Actual results

No input appears. Also the windows alert beeping sound is triggered. Note, that the app is not responsive to the keyboard at all (e.g. Tab behavior)

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
[Paste your code here]
```

</details>


### Screenshots or Video

<details open>
<summary>Screenshots / Video demonstration</summary>

[Upload media here]

</details>


### Logs

<details open><summary>Logs</summary>

...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed reproduced on Windows 10 stable and master after Win+D minimize/restore. Also reported with `window_manager` package minimize. Root cause not identified; CC'd to @dkwingsmt. A P1 staleness reminder was posted. Status: open, stalled.

title: [web] double selection on TextField
comment_summary: Confirmed iOS-only on mobile web (both CanvasKit and HTML renderers); Chrome on desktop is not affected. The double selection is caused by Safari's native text selection being shown simultaneously with Flutter's selection. A CSS workaround (`-webkit-user-select: none` plus touch event prevention and `BrowserContextMenu.disableContextMenu()`) was shared and found to work for some reporters but not all. Still reproducible in Flutter 3.27.3; no PR filed.

title: ListView (or basically any scrollable like SingleChildScrollView) with keyboardDismissBehavior set to ScrollViewKeyboardDismissBehavior.onDrag breaks the scrolling on iPad when the finger is inside the TextField
comment_summary: Comments confirmed the bug reproduces on iPad (iPadOS 17.5.1) with stable 3.22.2 and master 3.24. The bug is iPad-specific and does not occur on iPhone. The reporter confirmed no workaround exists, characterizing it as a rendering engine or text input engine bug. Issue assigned to team-ios; no PR linked.

title: SpellCheckConfiguration for TextField doesn't work with AppLocalizations on iOS
body:
### Steps to reproduce 1. Add Internationalizing to the app by flutter_localizations and intl (https://docs.flutter.dev/ui/accessibility-and-internationalization/internationalization) 2. Add spellCheckConfiguration parameter to the TextField 3. Try to write wrong words ### Expected results Spell check works with AppLocalization on Android and iOS ### Actual results Spell check doesn't work with AppLocalization on iOS (there is no problem on Android) ### Code sample
Code sample https://github.com/tatraef/spell_bug.git
### Screenshots or Video
Screenshots / Video demonstration
comment_summary: Root cause identified in a comment: `_performSpellCheck` uses `Localizations.maybeLocaleOf(context)` which returns locale codes without country code (e.g., `ru` instead of `ru_RU`), but iOS's `UITextChecker` requires full locale codes. Workaround: add full locale codes to `preferred-supported-locales` in `l10n.yaml`. CC'd to @camsim99 and @justinmc. Status: open with known workaround.

title: Easier use of in-app keyboards with TextInputControl
body:
### Use case

Many modern applications aim to provide a unique user experience by customizing the keyboard interface. This customization can improve user interaction and offer specialized input methods tailored to the application’s needs. 

Currently, implementing a custom in-app keyboard in Flutter is cumbersome and unreliable. Existing approaches, such as creating a widget for the custom keyboard and displaying it in a `Column` where first child is our form screen, and the second is keyboard , result in a glitchy and error-prone experience, especially when switching between fields with the custom keyboard and the native keyboard. This approach also doesn't work nicely with [cupertino_interactive_keyboard](https://pub.dev/packages/cupertino_interactive_keyboard).

A more seamless and integrated solution is needed to enhance the stability and usability of custom keyboards within Flutter applications.

### Proposal

```
TextField(
  keyboardType: TextInputType.custom(
    builder
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments clarify the request is distinct from issue #29823 and from the existing `TextInputControl` mixin (introduced in PR #76072): the specific pain point is smooth switching between fields that use the native keyboard and fields that use a custom in-app keyboard, including screen resize transitions. The maintainer accepted this scoping and updated the title. Status is open with no PR, kept separate from #29823.

title: [Semantics / Accessibility] TextField is invisible to TalkBack when suffixIcon is a IconButton.
comment_summary: Triage confirmed reproduction on Android with TalkBack on stable 3.22.3 and master 3.24. A workaround using `Semantics(textField: true)` around the suffixIcon was posted. Similar to previously fixed issue #39918.

title: `DropdownMenu.onSelected` conflicts with `TextField` `onTapOutside` when `requestFocusOnTap: false` and `true`
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a `TextField` with `onTapOutside` defined and `DropDownMenu` with `onSelected` and `requestFocusOnTap` set to false. 
2. Tap into the `TextField`. 
3. Tap on the `DropDownMenu` label
    - `onTapOutside` is not triggered, The menu is opened.
4. Tap on a menu item
    - `onTapOutside` is triggered, but `onSelected` is not. Menu is not hidden.

### Expected results

- Tapping on the `DropDown` menu label should trigger `TextField.onTapOutside`
- Tapping on a menu item should trigger both`TextField.onTapOutside` and `DropDownMenu.onSelected` sand the menu should hide.

### Actual results

- Tapping on the `DropDown` menu label does not trigger `TextField.onTapOutside`
- Tapping on a menu item should triggers only both`TextField.onTapOutside` and the menu should remains opened

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dartimport 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const DropdownMenuExample(
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug also reproduces with `requestFocusOnTap: true`, not just `false`. Reproduced on both stable 3.22.2 and master 3.24.0-pre on macOS. No fix or workaround proposed; issue remains open and stalled.

title: App throws error `Failed assertion: line 505 pos 16: '!_pressedKeys.containsKey(event.physicalKey)'` when pressing SHIFT key using PDA keyboard
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. focus on textfield
2. i press key shift left on my keyboard PDA
3. error screen
4. i can't use other key later :(

I don't know 

### Expected results

```
════════ Exception caught by services library ══════════════════════════════════
A KeyDownEvent is dispatched, but the state shows that the physical key is already pressed. If this occurs in real application, please report this bug to Flutter. If this occurs in unit tests, please ensure that simulated events follow Flutter's event model as documented in `HardwareKeyboard`. This was the event: KeyDownEvent#596ab(physicalKey: PhysicalKeyboardKey#f5c25(usbHidUsage: "0x000700e4", debugName: "Control Right"), logicalKey: LogicalKeyboardKey#d0ba2(keyId: "0x200000100", keyLabel: "Control Left", debugName: "Control Left"), character: null, timeStamp: 0:00:06.248031)
'package:flutter/src/services/hardware_keyboard.dart':
Failed assertion: line 505 pos 16: '!_pressedKeys.containsKey(event.physicalKey)
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified in logs: the PDA keyboard (HT730) maps Shift Right's physical key to the Shift Left logical key, causing HardwareKeyboard._assertEventIsRegular to fail when a subsequent KeyUpEvent arrives with the correct Shift Right logical key. This is a device-specific key remapping issue on Android. No fix or PR has been proposed. Currently stalled.

title: [Webview] TextFields gets covered by keyboard after switching text field and we can't scroll to the textfields
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue is iOS-only — Android does not reproduce. When switching between text inputs inside a WebView in iOS, the scaffold resizes to avoid the keyboard but then resets to full size, preventing scrolling to the focused input. Comparison with SwiftUI shows native behavior is slightly different but allows scrolling. Issue may be related to #112354. No fix or PR proposed; open.

title: Web: Autofill doesn't populate fields with TouchID enabled
body:
### Steps to reproduce

I'm using Mac 

using Safari browser:

1. Enable Touch ID for autofilling passwords on your Mac.
2. Run the code on the web using Safari.
3. Choose the suggested username/password from the password manager.
4. A Touch ID popup will appear. Provide your biometrics or password.

using Chrome:

1. Enable Touch ID for autofilling passwords on your Mac.
2. In Chrome open Google Password Manager -> Settings -> Make sure that "Use your screen lock when filling in passwords" is turned on and "Sign in automatically" is turned off. 
3. Re-open browser
4. Run the code on the web using Chrome
5. Save username & password for this user and localhost and try to login again using suggestions from the password manager.
6. A Touch ID popup will appear. Provide your biometrics or password.

### Expected results

When I provide either fingerprint or password for TouchId and it is correct, username(email) and password fields should be populated with data from p
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: when TouchID/FaceID biometric prompt appears, the lazily rendered `<input>` element is removed as focus leaves the text field, so autofill data is not populated on return. Semantics mode workaround attempted but prevents autocomplete suggestions from appearing. Issue persists through 3.24; blocks upgrade from 3.16.9. Status: open, no fix or PR; high user impact.

title: [Windows] The suggestion window offset is wrong for the first character
comment_summary: Confirmed on Windows desktop (stable 3.22.3 and master 3.24.0): the IME candidate window jumps to the correct position only after the second character is typed. Web rendering on the same Windows host shows correct positioning from the first character. Similar behavior observed on macOS.

title: [Web] Autofill is filling the form immediately instead of opening the password manager
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the app for web, complete the form and save your credentials in your password manager.
2. Refresh the page and click any form field.

Related issue: #114620  

### Expected results

Should give the option to pick your credentials from password manager(actually working on double click).

### Actual results

It fills the form with your credentials on the first click. Also throws error in the console:

```
Error
dart-sdk/lib/_internal/js_dev_runtime/private/ddc_runtime/errors.dart 296:3       throw_
dart-sdk/lib/_internal/js_shared/lib/rti.dart 1388:3                              _failedAsCheck
dart-sdk/lib/_internal/js_shared/lib/rti.dart 1366:3                              _generalAsCheckImplementation
dart-sdk/lib/_internal/js_shared/lib/js_util_patch.dart 81:5                      getProperty
lib/_engine/engine/dom.dart 2241:24                                               DomKeyboardEventExtension.get$35shiftKey
lib/_engine/engine/platform_dispatcher/vi
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the form is immediately autofilled (without user selecting credentials) on first click, specifically in Chrome; Safari works correctly. The console error was fixed by an engine PR #53830, but the autofill behavior issue persists. Reproduced on master 3.24.0. The issue affects both debug and release modes with slightly different behaviors. Multiple users still report the issue as of stable 3.35.6 and 3.35.7. Workaround: double-click or delete and retype to trigger the password manager dropdown. Status: stalled, no fix or PR.

title: [macOS] Using Japanese IME suggestion panel produces incorrect results
comment_summary: Comments confirmed the bug reproduces on stable 3.24.0 and master 3.24.0-pre with Japanese IME on macOS. The `oldText` field in a delta does not match the text produced by the previous delta when navigating the suggestion panel, indicating the engine sends inconsistent delta state. No fix or PR linked; stalled with team-text-input.

title: SDK 3.24.0 TextField becomes focused on navigator back (pop) action
comment_summary: Root cause narrowed to the `NotificationListener<ScrollNotification>` + `FocusScope.unfocus()` pattern: when a dialog is popped, the focus is restored to the previously focused TextField. Reproduced on stable 3.24+ through 3.29.3. Related to #152001 and #111777. A workaround is to call `FocusManager.instance.primaryFocus?.unfocus()` in the dialog's dismiss handler. No fix; open.

title: [version 3.24] ThemeData's hintColor not working in TextField, which was working in version 3.22.x
body:
### Steps to reproduce

ThemeData(
      hintColor: Colors.red,

### Expected results

TextField's hint text color is read, as in previous version

comment_summary: Regression introduced by #150278 (landed in 3.24): the M3 default hint color (onSurfaceVariant) now overrides ThemeData.hintColor because M3 defaults were previously ignored. Workaround confirmed: use InputDecorationTheme.hintStyle instead of ThemeData.hintColor. Team discussed proceeding with the deprecation of ThemeData.hintColor (#116346 / #91772) rather than reverting; no fix PR yet, stalled.

title: EditableText's clipBehaviour only clips when overflowing horizontally
comment_summary: Comments confirm `_hasVisualOverflow` in `RenderEditable` only considers horizontal scroll, so vertical overflow from reduced-height `TextStyle` is never clipped until horizontal overflow occurs. Reproduced on Android and macOS. The fix suggested is to always clip when `clipBehavior != Clip.none` or to always check vertical overflow too. No PR filed; open for over 6 months, stalled.

title: Material UI: Visual bug with decorated TextFields and label animations
comment_summary: A fix was filed in PR #153398 but was not completed. The issue remains open with a partial patch label; a commenter encouraged others to pick up the PR as a starting point.

title: [Autofill] Autofill is using the old value of textfield after the value was replaced by a previously saved autofill value
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Use the app to save some credentials.
2. Restart the app.
3. Write something manually on the password textfield.
4. Go to the email textfield and select the autofill option to use the previously saved credentials.
5. The manually written password is replaced by the password from autofill.
6. Click the login button to call the finishautofillcontext and prompt the save password dialog if needed.

### Expected results

Save credentials dialog should not show up since we are using stored credentials and both the email and password are the same as before, we are not updating the credentials.

### Actual results

The user gets the save dialog prompt. The password we are trying to save is the password we initially wrote before it got replaced by the one from the autofill.


### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter/services.dart';

void main() {
  
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on master 3.24.0-1.0.pre.575 and stable 3.22.3. The root cause is that when autofill replaces the manually-typed text, Flutter's autofill context still references the old (pre-replacement) text and triggers a spurious save prompt. Affects Android only per the reporter. Currently stalled with no PR.

title: Selecting TextField or TextFormField causes app to freeze for 20-40 seconds on Linux Mint.
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Created a flutter project for linux only using the command `flutter create --platforms=linux textfield_broken`.
2. Copy pasted the code from the "Interactive example" section at the bottom of the page at https://docs.flutter.dev/cookbook/forms/text-input.
3. Run it on Linux Mint 22 Cinnamon.

### Expected results

App to run as shown on the cookbook page and work on every platform whether it be android, linux, windows, etc. i.e., TextField to receive text input and show it on screen and also focusNode to work smoothly.

### Actual results

Code compiled on Linux Mint 22. Worked as expected on web and android phone.
However, on the main machine itself, the app was freezing as soon as any of the TextField or TextFormField was being selected.

Sometimes, after 20-40 seconds, the inputs will be received and displayed on the screen and the app would start to work normally again but unfocusing and clicking on the same or any other TextField or widget
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The root cause was bisected to engine commit `7719f870550d` which included flutter/engine#52355 (AT-SPI socket/plug accessibility export for Linux). When the text field receives focus, the app issues a blocking D-Bus `GetExtents` call to the AT-SPI socket that times out after 25 seconds, freezing the UI. Affects Linux with Cinnamon desktop (Linux Mint 20.3, 21.3, 22 confirmed); not reproduced on Ubuntu, Fedora 31, or LMDE 6. The workaround is to wrap the TextField in `ExcludeSemantics`. The long-term fix proposed is to use D-Bus directly for accessibility in Flutter, tracked as issue #159460.

title: Keyevent usb-hid code and AltGr
comment_summary: Confirmed on stable 3.24.1 and master 3.25.0 on both macOS and web. Changing the macOS input source to German makes Alt/Option Right equivalent to AltGr, yet Flutter reports only Alt Right instead of AltGr. No fix or PR mentioned; CC to @dkwingsmt.

title: TextField context menu should block user interaction until hidden on supported desktop platforms
[macOS] Dismissing the IME accent panel produces invalid character
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug reproduces with a regular `TextField`, not just the `DeltaTextInputClient` sample. Two related sequences with different expected outcomes were documented: (1) press-hold u → right → left → ESC → left arrow should remove the accented letter; (2) press-hold u → right → ESC should leave an unaccented `u`. No fix or PR linked; open with team-text-input.

title: Keyboard events, dead code, should not generate character
comment_summary: The issue was confirmed on Windows (dead key generates a character on the first keystroke when using FR input method). A commenter also reproduced a related but distinct problem on Linux and macOS where the second keystroke after a dead key produces `e` instead of the composed character. The issue was confirmed on master 3.25.0-1.0.pre.147 and marked as a framework issue. No PR submitted; status is open.

title: Keyboard events, Windows, `AltGr` generates `Control Left` + `Alt Right`
comment_summary: Confirmed on Flutter master 3.25.0-1.0.pre.132: pressing AltGr with a French layout on Windows generates both a Control Left and Alt Right key event instead of a single AltGr/altGraph event. The issue does not occur on macOS or web. Root cause is that Windows reports AltGr as Ctrl+Alt at the OS level, and Flutter does not collapse this into a single altGraph event.

title: TextField Using emoji applications on the desktop crashes, and the performance of Windows and macOS is the same
body:
### Steps to reproduce


1. TextField: Input content normally and then press the add button to add emojis to the text
2. TextField uses _focusNode. requestFocus(); Get the focus, then TextCelection. collaged() changes the cursor position,
3. The application crashes when typing again

2,TextField 使用_focusNode.requestFocus();获取焦点,然后 TextSelection.collapsed()改变光标位置
3, 再次输入时应用程序崩溃


### Expected results


TextField cursor position changes, application runs normally
TextField光标位置改变， 应用程序正常运行

### Actual results

The application crashed
应用程序闪退了

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).colorScheme.inversePrimary,
        title: Text(widget.title),
      ),
      body: Center(
        child: Column(
          mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
          children: <Widget>[
            TextField(
          
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified from a macOS crash stack trace: `fml::Utf16ToUtf8` aborts when converting the text after emoji insertion because the byte offset used to set the selection (`characterToInsert.length` in UTF-16 code units) is invalid for the engine's internal model. Confirmed on macOS and Linux desktop (not Android/iOS). A Windows-specific freeze variant (infinite message loop after `requestFocus`) was reported separately. Affects macOS/Windows/Linux; issue is in `FlutterTextInputPlugin` in the engine. No PR proposed.

title: Keyboard, Linux and MacOS, dead keys, characters are wrong

title: Autocomplete field options in the SingleChildScrollView remains open even when field goes out of view.
body:

title: Backspace not working correctly when using physical keyboard in a TextFormField (keyboardType: TextInputType.none)
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a TextFormField with "keyboardType: TextInputType.none", not applying the virtual keyboard;
2. When using the TextFormField with a physical keyboard, the backspace entry is not working in the first interaction, neither targeting any listener. I need to press any other key to make it work.

### Expected results

The expected result is that the TextFormField works with physical keyboards backspace's **at the first interaction**.
At the moment, I need to press **any other key** on the physical keyboard, then the backspace work correctly.

**I need this to work because we have many devices with physical keyboard.**

### Actual results

The backspace not work or target any listener if that key **is the first** key that I interact with.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
TextFormField(
  enabled: widget.enabled,
  keyboardType: TextInputType.none,
  onChanged: widget.onChanged,
  onEditingComplete
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Issue is intermittent; triager could not always reproduce it, and it appears related to Android IME service state. The bug occurs specifically with TextFormField (not TextField) using keyboardType: TextInputType.none, and only on first focus with a physical keyboard before any other key is pressed. Suggested workaround from a commenter is to use TextField instead.

title: ScrollViewKeyboardDismissBehavior.onDrag doesn't work when a scroll view is scrollable via AlwaysScrollableScrollPhysics
comment_summary: Confirmed Android-specific; works as expected on iOS. Root cause tentatively attributed to difference in how iOS TextField loses focus (allowing keyboard dismiss) vs Android. No fix landed.

title: Text Duplication Issue with Microsoft Translate Keyboard in Flutter Text Fields
comment_summary: Confirmed on Android with Microsoft SwiftKey AI Keyboard; reproduces with English-to-Japanese translation when there is a trailing space after the source word. Reproduced on stable 3.24.2 and master 3.25.0-pre on Pixel 7 Android 14.

title: Keyboard Resets on emoji input on Android OPPO Find X5 Pro
comment_summary: Confirmed device-specific to OPPO Find X5 Pro and later reported on Honor Magic 6 Pro, Samsung S24 Ultra, and OnePlus; the SwiftKey keyboard (Microsoft SwiftKey) is the common factor across all affected devices. The issue does not reproduce on Pixel or Samsung without SwiftKey. No fix or PR mentioned.

title: VoiceOver rotor actions limited to text fields with active keyboard focus
body:
### Steps to reproduce

As I mentioned in #151029 :
Suppose there are two text fields on an interface.
1. Double-tap to activate the first text field, and the keyboard pops up.
2. Then, use VoiceOver to focus on the second text field. Note: just focus on it, don't double-tap.
3. Finally, use the rotor to perform a paste operation.
Native behavior: The text will be pasted into the second text field.
Flutter behavior: Unable to perform the paste operation.
In other words, the native rotor operation targets the text field that VoiceOver is focused on.
However, Flutter rotor operation only targets the text field that has the keyboard activated.

Please use the latest master version for testing.

### Expected results

The text will be pasted into the second text field.

### Actual results

Unable to perform the paste operation.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(M
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified by @chunhtai: Flutter only has one UITextInput plugin active in the iOS embedding, so the second text field's semantics object is connected to an inactive UITextInput; it needs to activate itself when it receives a paste or copy command. Ownership transferred to team-text-input from team-accessibility. Issue was assigned to @Renzo-Olivares but the assignee was eventually auto-removed due to no activity.

title: [Web][Edge][Production] Spacebar key sometimes does not work
body:
### Steps to reproduce

Flutter web, app running on MS Edge, production (not durign dev mode).

1. If you have one or more TextFields inside a SingleChildScrollView (mostly observed when:  the page is so long that it doesn't need to actually scroll), the spacebar might not work when clicked on.
2. If you hit F5 to "soft" refresh the page, the spacebar works again.
3. MS Edge (tested and observed on v128.0.2739.67)
4. Chrome browsers works as expected here.



### Expected results

I Want the spacebar to produce a space in the textfield

### Actual results

Nothing happens sometimes when I hit spacebar .

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
return Column(
      crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.stretch,
      children: [
        Expanded(
          child: SingleChildScrollView(
            controller: ScrollController(),
            child: Card(
              child: Padding(
                padding: const EdgeInsets
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments established that the spacebar failure is intermittent, occurs only in release/production mode (not debug), affects MS Edge but not Chrome, and is linked to a separate focus-regression issue (#59439) — when #59439 triggers, this spacebar issue also triggers, and an F5 soft-reload fixes both. The issue also affects Enter key (`onFieldSubmitted` does not fire). One commenter identified that removing the `SingleActivator(LogicalKeyboardKey.space)` shortcut from `_defaultWebShortcuts` in `app.dart` fixes it, and another resolved it by regenerating `index.html` via `flutter create . --platforms web`, suggesting a stale custom bootstrapping script in `index.html` is a contributing factor. Currently open with no upstream fix.

title: FlutterEngineSendKeyEvent function didn't work in flutter embedder of glfw
comment_summary: Comments clarify the reporter is using the custom GLFW embedder example, not the official Linux/Windows desktop engine. A team member noted the GLFW embedder is an unmaintained example. No root cause identified and no fix planned for this embedder. Status: effectively closed as won't-fix (not a maintained platform).

title: Command+Enter on Chrome causes Enter Key to be considered held down

- Start the supplied code sample in Chrome
- Press CTRL/CMD-Enter.
- Press Shift-K

### Expected results

The configured action for the Shift-K shortcut should run and the text should update to "Shortcut Triggered!"

Nothing happens.

    show

        FocusableActionDetector,

        StatelessWidget,
        Text,
        Widget,
        runApp;

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class ShortCutIntent extends Intent {
  const ShortCutIntent();
}

class MyApp extends Sta
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause as macOS (and Safari) not sending a `keyup` event for Enter when Meta is held — this is a browser/OS behavior, not a Flutter bug. The reporter confirmed the missing `keyup` event in the browser's own `keyup` listener. A Chromium bug was filed at https://issues.chromium.org/issues/366284864. A Flutter-side workaround that simulates a KeyUpEvent after 100ms was provided. Using `SingleActivator` instead of `LogicalKeySet` was suggested as a best practice regardless.

title: When using multiple WidgetSpan, getOffsetForCaret returns incorrect caret positions
comment_summary: Triager reproduced the issue on stable 3.24.3 and master 3.26 (confirmed affected platforms not explicitly listed but implied all). Issue was CC'd to @Renzo-Olivares for triage. No fix or PR mentioned; status is open with P2.

title: `TextPainter.getFullHeightForCaret` reports incorrect results for a space as the last character
comment_summary: Root cause identified by @LongCatIsLooong: fonts with a non-zero typo line gap cause `getFullHeightForCaret` to return a shorter height for trailing spaces; the fix is to use `getRectsForRange` instead but test fonts all use 0 line gap making it hard to test. Linked to #154039 as a possible regression source. Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.24.3 through 3.29.3 and master 3.32; assignee removed due to inactivity.

title: flutter web textfield  is hard to open copy and paste menu with double-tap on ios phone
body:
### Steps to reproduce

On Flutter Web for Android, after long-pressing a TextField, the paste functionality appears correctly. However, on Flutter Web for iOS, users typically double-tap to bring up the paste menu. When double-tapping, the menu briefly appears but quickly disappears. If the TextField contains text, it gets fully selected. It seems that the "select all" action overrides the paste menu. Long-pressing, however, works as expected and correctly opens the paste functionality.

### Expected results

When double-tapping on ios web, shows the copy and paste menu.

### Actual results

When double-tapping, the menu briefly appears but quickly disappears.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return  TextField();
  }
```

</details>


### Screenshots or Video

<details open>
<summary>Screenshots / Video demonstration</summary>


https://github.com/user-attachme
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on iOS 14 devices (not iOS 13 or older) running Safari; Android and older iOS not affected. The double-tap causes select-all to fire before the context menu can stabilize, overriding the paste menu. Issue is OS-version specific. A later comment reports related issues on Chrome/Android in newer Flutter versions. No fix or PR; status is open.

title: SelectableText crashes the app if custom contextMenuBuilder is used, Null check operator used on a null value
comment_summary: Root cause identified by @justinmc: `TextSelectionOverlay` disposes and recreates its `OverlayEntry`s whenever `contextMenuBuilder` changes (e.g., every rebuild when defined as an inline lambda), and if the `OverlayEntry` is disposed before `_OverlayEntryWidgetState.initState` completes, a null-check crash occurs. Regression introduced by #142463. Workaround: define `contextMenuBuilder` as a static method rather than an inline lambda so it passes equality checks and avoids unnecessary dispose/recreate cycles. Longer-term fix requires refactoring `TextSelectionOverlay` to update values without disposing.

title: [BUG] Clipboard Changes Not Detected in Flutter on Linux (Wayland) Unless App is Focused
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Run a Flutter app on a Linux system using a `Wayland` session.
2. The app should listen for clipboard changes using a timer using inbuilt clipboard.
3. Copy new content to the clipboard while the Flutter app is running but not focused.
4. The clipboard change is not detected.
5. Switch focus back to the Flutter app window. The clipboard change is now detected and updated.
6. Run the same app on `X11`, and observe that clipboard changes are detected immediately without needing to switch window focus.

### Expected results

Clipboard changes should be detected in real-time on `Wayland`, just like in `X11`, without requiring the app to regain focus.

### Actual results

When a Flutter app that is designed to listen to clipboard in realtime and display it via a text widget is run in `Wayland` in Linux it doesn't listen to the changes in clipboard unless app window is focused, right after content has been copied. The same application would work perf
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: Wayland's security model prevents apps from reading clipboard data when not focused, unlike X11. Confirmed on Fedora and KDE Neon (pure Wayland) but not on Ubuntu (which uses XWayland by default). A GTK app (Gedit) shows the same behavior, indicating this is a GTK/Wayland limitation rather than a Flutter-specific bug. The Flutter Linux team member acknowledged the issue but had no updates. Status: open, stalled.

title: [Scribe] Hover icon
body:
### Use case

Android's Scribe feature supports showing an icon when a stylus hovers over a field that supports Scribe handwriting input. Flutter should support this feature too.

![Screenshot_20240930-111720](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/71dc95cd-eefe-4d57-8292-2884a77840d5)


### Proposal

The hover cursor can be set in `onResolvePointerIcon` in InputConnection in FlutterView.java. To know where the fields are that support Scribe, we'd likely have to send the rects over from the framework.

### See also

Scribe issue: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/115607
Scribe support added in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/148784 and https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/52943.
comment_summary: Draft engine PR #55532 was filed after prerequisite #52943 merged. An assignment was set assuming @justinmc would work on it.

title: [Scribe] stylus gestures
body:
### Use case

Native Android Scribe supports several stylus gestures that make editing text with a stylus much easier. Flutter should support them too.

### Proposal

Flutter's Scribe support should include the same [gestures that are supported on Android](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/view/inputmethod/HandwritingGesture#public-methods):

 * select
 * selectRange
 * delete
 * deleteRange
 * insert
 * insertMode
 * joinOrSplit
 * removeSpace

As well as [previewing](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/view/inputmethod/PreviewableHandwritingGesture):

 * delete
 * deleteRange
 * select
 * selectRange

### See also

Framework PR: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/156020

Main Scribe issue:
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/115607

Core Scribe functionality PRs:
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/148784
https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/52943

Scribe hover icon:
https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issu
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Draft engine PR #55568 was filed. A limitation noted in triage: Flutter lacks character-level location information needed for accurate gesture-based selection; the entire character bounding box must be contained in the selection gesture.

title: [iOS] Pressing `´` immediately deletes the character on iPad Air, iOS 17.5 with Portuguese (Portugal) keyboard
comment_summary: Comments confirm the dead-key accent character deletion (pressing `´` then `a` produces `A` instead of `Á`) is specific to iPad Air with iOS 17.5 simulator using Portuguese (Portugal) keyboard; it does not reproduce on iOS 18.0. Testing on native iOS 17.5 (Reminders app) shows the same bug, indicating it is an iOS 17.5 simulator bug, not Flutter-specific. Set to low priority (P3) since iOS 18 fixes it.

title: [iOS] Portuguese (Portugal) compound character entry creates extra character
comment_summary: Confirmed still reproducible on iOS 18 (not fixed unlike sibling issue #156183). Reproduced on stable 3.24.3 and master 3.26.0-pre. iOS-only (iPad simulator with Portuguese Portugal keyboard).

title: [webview_flutter] Multiple inputs in position: fixed elements freeze
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on iOS 17.5 simulator as well, not specific to iOS 18. Suspected to be a platform view gesture handling issue rather than a plugin-level issue. Issue routed to team-ios; no fix or PR mentioned.

### Actual results

When tapping on the web view, the autocomplete bar is hidden, but the keyboard stays visible, and there's a blinking cursor inside the text field. Tapping any of the keyboard keys does not work.

import 'package:webview_flutter/webview_flutter.dart';

class

comment_summary: Confirmed Android-only (iOS unfocuses the TextField correctly when tapping the WebView, which is considered correct behavior). One commenter noted the framework does not send `TextInput.hide`, pointing to the engine as the source; related to #130652. Another commenter reported the same issue on Firefox web with a `PointerInterceptor`-wrapped WebView. No fix or PR; stalled.

title: Gboard Keyboard language change is not working when `enableSuggestions` is false
comment_summary: Root cause identified in a comment: `TextInputPlugin.java` uses `TYPE_TEXT_VARIATION_VISIBLE_PASSWORD` when `enableSuggestions: false`, which prevents Gboard's language-switching feature. This also affects `TextInputType.visiblePassword`. Confirmed on Pixel 7 (not device-specific) on stable 3.24.3 and master 3.27. A commenter notes the same issue on iOS with `visiblePassword` and suggests `name` as a fix. No PR yet; status is awaiting fix from the engine/Android team.

title: iOS Autofill "hide my email" does not work
comment_summary: Confirmed that SwiftUI TextField handles Hide My Email correctly but Flutter TextField does not: when the user selects Hide My Email, Flutter's TextField loses focus and the generated email address is never populated. The issue persists through stable 3.24 and is open for investigation with no fix or PR.

title: PhysicalKeyboardKey is not correct with Backspace and Enter when using ko/zh/ja input method on Android emulator
comment_summary: Comments narrow the scope: the issue is emulator-specific (confirmed on Pixel 4 API 34 emulator and Samsung Galaxy S10 tablet, but not on Pixel 7 or Pixel Tablet physical devices). The incorrect PhysicalKeyboardKey USB HID usage is logged but does not functionally break backspace or enter. Assigned to team-android after internal triage. Status: open, under investigation.

title: Allow more control of TextInputControls in EditableText
body:
### Use case

Some apps need the option of using their own keyboard for security reasons. You often do not want other software or hardware keyboards to be able to track what is currently being entered in a text field.
Possible use cases are, for example, banking apps or other apps that have to meet certain security requirements.

We are currently unable to publish our Flutter app because a requirement for this is that external input is not allowed. The only possibility at the moment is to implement `EditableText` and `TextField`/`TextFormField` ourselves.

### Proposal

It could be made possible for `EditableTexts` or `TextFields` to control which `TextInputs` can modify them or receive updates from `TextEditingValues` or similar. For example, you could give each `TextInputConnection` the option of defining its own filter function for all permitted `TextInputControls`. Alternatively you could at least provide the option to prohibit external `TextInputs` such as `_PlatformTextInputCo
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause: even with TextInputType.none, the platform TextInputControl still receives updates. Using readOnly=true loses cursor blinking. Custom keyboard scrolling also broken because scroll-to-field relies on View.of(context). Issue escalated to full proposal for filtering which TextInputControls can access a connection.

title: TextField doesn't receive input in focused state above PlatformView in Firefox
comment_summary: Confirmed on Firefox (not Chrome): TextField above a PlatformView (e.g., HtmlElementView) in a dialog receives focus but does not accept keyboard input. Keyboard input is received only after switching browser tabs and returning. Root cause suspected to involve focus management between Flutter's hidden textarea and the platform view iframes in Firefox. Workaround confirmed: wrapping TextField (or the whole dialog) in SelectionArea resolves the issue. PointerInterceptor did not help. Status: open, workaround available.

3. Select some of the text.
4. Now try to hit a button on the text selection controls (e.g. "copy") **using the Apple Pencil**. 

### Expected results

As in other iOS apps, it should be possible to copy, cut, etc. by hitting the corresponding button with the **Apple Pencil**.

### Actual results

The button is not hit; instead, the input seems to be directed to the underlying canvas. (See attached video.)
If the buttons are tapped with the finger, though, they work just fine.

void main() {
  runApp(const MainApp());
}

class MainApp extends Sta

comment_summary: Confirmed on iPad Air 5 with iPadOS 17.5.1 and on Flutter beta 3.27 and stable 3.22.3. Using SystemContextMenu (now the default) fixes the Apple Pencil tapping issue, so priority was downgraded to P3. A separate issue #159259 was filed for an assertion error that appears when using SystemContextMenu with Scribble.

title: Text selection toolbar is affected by TextField transformations
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a `TextField` widget in a Flutter app.
2. Wrap it with a `Transform` widget (e.g. to scale or rotate the widget).

The use case here is a _drawing app_ that allows users to transform (scale & rotate) boxes with text and provides WYSIWYG input on the TextFields, regardless of where they are placed and how they are transformed. 

### Expected results

* The text selection toolbar should always be sized in screen coordinates, i.e. always have the same size, regardless of the transformation of the TextField.
* The text selection toolbar should always be drawn parallel to the screen's x-axis (i.e. perfectly horizontal), regardless of the transformation of the TextField.

Besides,

* Text selection should still work correctly. 

### Actual results

* The text selection toolbar scales and rotates with the TextField, i.e. it will potentially be drawn too big / small or rotated.
* Only parts of the TextField respond to tap events on a rotated
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the context menu scales and rotates with the TextField transformation on Android and iOS (stable 3.24.4, master 3.27.0-pre). Root cause: `CompositedTransformFollower` applies the full transform matrix including scale and rotation when positioning the menu. A workaround is available: use `contextMenuBuilder` to apply the inverse transform to the toolbar. A framework fix is proposed by adding a `translationOnly` parameter to `CompositedTransformFollower`. A `workaround available` label was added.

title: Height of TextField decreases when using OutlineInputBorder and label
comment_summary: Confirmed on macOS and Android in addition to Linux (originally reported). The _BorderContainer CustomPaint shrinks from 72px to 56px when switching from UnderlineInputBorder to OutlineInputBorder with a label present. Routed to @victorsanni for confirmation on whether the size mismatch is intentional.

title: TextField inside CheckboxListTile results in flakey calls to onChanged
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a `CheckboxListTile` with an `onChanged` that changes its `selected` property, and its `title` being a `TextField`.
2. Click on the `TextField`. (I have only tried this on web on desktop.)

### Expected results

I would expect consistent behavior: either `onChanged` is called or it is not. Out of these options I would probably expect it not to be called: I think the user is probably trying to edit the text in the `TextField` without changing the selection/checkbox status of the `CheckboxListTile`.

### Actual results

_Sometimes_ the `onChanged` handler is called, and _sometimes_ it is not. There doesn't seem to be a clear pattern. It can happen when the `TextField` already has focus, or if it's receiving focus via the tap.

I suspect this has to do with how things are resolved in the gesture arena. If one wraps the `TextField` in a `GestureDetector`:

```dart
title: GestureDetector(
  behavior: HitTestBehavior.opaque,
  onTap: () {},
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed on macOS after repeated tapping; does not reproduce on Android. A commenter links this to #98322 as a possible gesture-arena version of that issue. The reporter notes that moving the pointer before clicking makes the spurious onChanged call more likely. Workaround (wrapping TextField in GestureDetector with opaque hit test) appears to prevent it. Status: open.

title: [web] The text cursor/caret in a TextField does not visually move in Firefox when an arrow key is held pressed
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible in Firefox on both stable 3.24.5 and master; the bug does not occur in Chrome or Safari. The cursor does not visually update while an arrow key is held down in Firefox; it only jumps to the final position on key release. Shift+arrow does show a growing selection. Classified as a fidelity bug specific to Firefox.

title: TextField as WidgetSpan mixes parent TextField initial text when delete or arrow keys used
body:
### Steps to reproduce

Have attached complete sample code which shows the issue but steps to recreate are:

1. create a new flutter app
2. create a custom TextEditingController and set the "text" value within the constructor to any text string (this is to make the issue easier to see, if this value isn't set instead of the text being replaced in the WidgetSpan TextField it is deleted, so still a problem)
3. within the custom controller add a WidgetSpan with a child of a TextField and return it as the child of a TextSpan
4. in the app add a TextField within MainApp using the custom controller as its controller
5. run the app against a Windows or Mac target (I assume linux)
6. in the WidgetSpan text field enter some text then press the delete key or arrow keys

See attached example for a bare bones app showing the problem

### Expected results

Normal text field behaviour or deleting, moving cursor etc...

### Actual results

Windows and Mac - Text field text is repla
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause explained by @Renzo-Olivares: the engine's `TextInputPlugin` is only aware of the raw text value on the controller, not the `buildTextSpan` override, so key events in the child TextField are processed against the parent's raw text. Additionally, nested TextField focus/keyboard logic has not been wired up to support this pattern. Web works correctly because it handles key events differently. The fix requires engine awareness of `WidgetSpan` lengths. No workaround other than using an Overlay.

title: [Android] Dragging outside the text should not show the magnifier
iOS - The app crashes when entering data into an input field in the WebView
comment_summary: The crash is confirmed iOS-only (reported on iPhone 12 Pro Max iOS 18.0.1 and iPhone 14 iOS 17.6.1); Android is unaffected. The symbolicated stack trace points to a UIKit accessibility call (`UIFocusRingManagerAccessibility`), and a commenter noted the crash may be unrelated to the webview_flutter_android import that the reporter removed as a workaround. The reporter confirmed that commenting out `import 'package:webview_flutter_android/webview_flutter_android.dart'` in CI/CD stopped the crashes, but team members questioned whether this was actually the cause given the accessibility-focused crash trace. The issue remains open and unresolved, with the team unable to reproduce it in debug mode.

title: Rebuild Autocomplete options at arbitrary time
body:
### Use case

When the user finishes typing in the field, a user of Autocomplete might make two network requests: one to quickly fetch some approximate options and another, longer request to get more detailed results. Autocomplete should be able to update the options with the result of each request. Currently the optionsViewBuilder is only called in response to the completion of the future returned by optionsBuilder.

### Proposal

The hacky solution is probably to introduce a controller with a method to imperatively update the options: `AutocompleteController.setOptions`.

A better solution might require a breaking change. Really it seems like maybe the options should just be passed into Autocomplete as a parameter? That would also require the ability to listen to changes in the field.

### Related

There are other request to be able to display things in the options view at certain times, for example while loading: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/147377. Solving 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: A detailed example was provided showing that `optionsBuilder` is only re-evaluated on `TextEditingValue` changes, so options fetched asynchronously after focus never appear. A draft PR #171929 proposes a `RawAutocompleteController` approach (similar to `TextEditingController`) that avoids breaking the existing API by using a separate constructor. Status: has draft PR #171929, waiting for someone to finalize it.

When we go to the next line, the `getOffsetForCaret` should return `dy` offest to be `23` and  `dx` should be `4.86...`. 


### Actual results

We type the 'i' character n number of times until we reach the end of the line. While continuing to type 'i' characters, if a line break occurs (which is rendered correctly on the screen), the Offset remains invalid.

A valid line break is only considered when we type 4 more 'i' characters on the next line.

However, if we type a different character, such as '@', as the last character and a line break occurs, the offset is correctly returned.

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'dart:ui';

import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() =>
...[truncated]

Comments flag that the offset returned is incorrect only on physical devices, possibly related to keyboard layout differences. Triaged and CC'd to @Renzo-Olivares. Status: open, no PR.

title: Exposing the iOS `FlutterTextInputView` to control keyboard directly
body:
### Use case

Searching for how to add a Done button to a number keyboard in Flutter, there are thousands of searches and no solution...

Issues such as #12220 get closed with no resolution or point you to a https://pub.dev/packages/keyboard_actions, which while popular is not native and has its issues. (Functionality / Animation / Look&Feel)

### Proposal

Since you can put almost anything above the keyboard in iOS a pure Flutter solution is impossible. There will always be someone who finds a missing feature. So the only real option, that covers everyone, is to expose the `UIView` and add your own `UIToolbar` implementation.

I looked at the engine code and found `FlutterTextInputView` inside shell/platform/darwin/ios/framework/Source

If we could somehow expose this into the `AppDelete` via a callback in response to the user interacting with a widget that opens a keyboard, then we could set up the keyboard any way we'd like (apart from the values settable from Flutter.)

Flu
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments suggest a plugin protocol design doc would be needed to expose `FlutterTextInputView` via `inputAccessoryView` to native Swift/Obj-C code.

title: The text inside textfield overlaps with scrollbar in web and desktop platforms.
comment_summary: Confirmed more prominent on web and desktop platforms. Text selection also overlaps the scrollbar. A workaround (wrapping TextField in Scrollbar) avoids the overlap but is not considered valid. Still reproducible in 3.38.2.

title: Improve documentation of `TextField`'s `decoration` property interplay with `Theme.inputDecorationTheme`
body:
### Use case

The [current documentation of flutter's `TextField`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField-class.html) contains no mention of the fact that any explicit `decoration` is merged with whatever additional decorations are defined in the surrounding theme, making it unnecessarily difficult to debug why e.g. trying to remove a field's border (if defined in the theme) is impossible without wrapping the widget with an additional `Theme` containing explicit `InputBorder.none` values.

### Proposal

As a bare minimum, some form of notice should be added to the `decoration` property informing about the potential need for inserting an applied-property-clearing `Theme` wrapper surrounding the widget.

Ideally though, users should have a less verbose way of specifying whether to merge or fully replace the active `InputDecorationTheme` either when creating, or when applying an `InputDecoration`, e.g. with a `bool? mergeWithTheme` on all constructors, while applying m
...[truncated]
comment_summary: A commenter asked if the reporter would be interested in submitting a documentation PR. No other substantive new information.

title: [Android] CJK IME candidate window position error with external keyboard on Android
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Connect a physical keyboard (e.g., a Bluetooth keyboard) to your Android device.
2. Switch to a CJK input method (e.g., Chinese) and start typing to trigger candidate words.

### Expected results

The candidate window should be displayed correctly near the cursor to ensure a seamless user experience. With the increasing popularity of Android tablets, many of which are commonly used with physical keyboards, it has become essential to address this issue. Correctly positioning the candidate window is critical for maintaining usability and consistency across devices, especially for users relying on CJK input methods. Fixing this bug will significantly improve the input experience for a growing segment of Android users.

### Actual results

On Android devices, the candidate window for the input method is incorrectly displayed in the lower-left corner of the screen. In contrast, on iOS and desktop devices, the candidate window appears in the correct position. I us
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified by a community member: `InputConnectionAdaptor.getCursorAnchorInfo()` in the Android engine never calls `setInsertionMarkerLocation`, so the IME candidate window always appears at the origin (lower-left corner). The commenter built a custom engine patch using `mLayout.getPrimaryHorizontal` and `mLayout.getLineTop/Bottom` for the horizontal coordinate, which corrected horizontal placement but left vertical at 0. The issue is in `shell/platform/android/io/flutter/plugins/editing/InputConnectionAdaptor.java`. Affects all Android devices when using a physical keyboard with CJK IME. Status: root cause confirmed, partial patch in community discussion, no official PR yet.

title: Onboard on-screen keyboard causes application unresponsiveness for several seconds on Linux desktop
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Run `flutter create app` to create a new Flutter application.
2. Navigate to the project directory: `cd app`.
3. Edit the lib/main.dart file and add a TextField widget to the app.
4. Start the application with `flutter run`.
5. Press the d key to detach the app from the terminal.
6. Open the Onboard on-screen keyboard: `onboard`.

### Expected results

The on-screen keyboard should appear and disappear without delay, and key presses should be immediately detected by the TextField widget. This behavior was functioning correctly in version 3.22.3.
Here is a video demonstrating it:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/42d69657-bebc-4e08-a9b8-bf251e195f10


### Actual results

When clicking on the TextField, the on-screen keyboard appears with a delay, and key presses are not registered in the TextField. The user interface becomes visibly unresponsive for several seconds. This behavior has been present since version 3.23.0-0.0.pre. Here is a video
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirmed this is a regression since Flutter 3.23.0-0.0.pre — before that version (e.g., 3.22.3) the Onboard on-screen keyboard on Linux worked correctly. The issue manifests as the Flutter app freezing for several seconds when the keyboard appears, then suddenly registering all buffered key presses at once. The team confirmed reproduction on stable 3.24.5 through master 3.28.0 but not on stable 3.22.3. One commenter noted a similar but possibly related issue on web mobile. Status: confirmed regression, open.

title: [iPad] Tapping a blank area in a composing region in a TextField with `stylusHandwritingEnabled:false` makes the application crash
comment_summary: Comments identify that the crash occurs only on physical iPad devices (not simulators) when tapping inside the uncommitted composing region with `stylusHandwritingEnabled: false`. The stack trace points to `[FlutterTextInputView closestPositionToPoint:withinRange:]` assuming `_selectionRects` is non-empty, but setting `stylusHandwritingEnabled: false` prevents `_selectionRects` from being populated (traced to `editable_text.dart#L4476`). Confirmed on iPad Air M1 (iOS 18.2). No PR yet; status is waiting for an engine fix.

title: CupertinoTextField Does Not Follow Primary Color for Text Selection During Autocorrect
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a Flutter app with a `CupertinoTextField` and set a custom `primaryColor` in the `CupertinoThemeData`.
2. Type text into the `CupertinoTextField` and observe the behavior during autocorrect.
3. Compare it with the native iOS `TextField` behavior.

### Expected results

- The selection handles and marked text during autocorrect in `CupertinoTextField` should follow the `primaryColor` defined in `CupertinoThemeData`.
- This behavior should match the native iOS `TextField`, which dynamically applies the app's primary color.

### Actual results

- `CupertinoTextField` does not respect the `primaryColor` for selection handles or marked text while autocorrect or typing in. 
- On iOS, the native `TextField` dynamically uses the primary color, ensuring visual consistency.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/cupertino.dart';
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: the autocorrect selection highlight is drawn by iOS natively, not Flutter, so CupertinoThemeData primaryColor has no effect. misspelledTextStyle and kMisspelledSelectionColor are constants not derived from the theme. No iOS API found to change this color.

title: Suffix in TextFormField affect the vertical alignment of the text.
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue: adding a suffix icon shifts the text baseline downward relative to a TextField without a suffix, when both are constrained to the same height (e.g., 50px SizedBox). A commenter (@justinmc) asks whether the issue disappears without the SizedBox and notes that a tall-enough suffix icon affecting baseline placement is likely the cause, and that this should not happen for a simple Icon.

title: Linux OnScreen keyboard don't always show up
comment_summary: Comments established that the issue is environment-dependent: on Ubuntu 20.04 with X11 in fullscreen, the on-screen keyboard stops appearing after the first few uses; on Ubuntu 24.04, the keyboard appears but requires tapping twice. A commenter clarified it works in non-Flutter GTK apps and web browsers in fullscreen. The team noted the behavior differs between X11 (overly responsive) and Wayland (rarely appears), and the issue was labeled for further investigation. Status: open, Linux-specific.

title: WidgetSpan wrong caret position
body:
### Steps to reproduce

I'm trying to make TextField which supports custom tag. And I would like to delete a whole tag via backspace, so I use UTF16 private use area( such as U+F0000~U+F0002) to be recognized one character.
But I have the bellow problem. It seems that the caret shows wrong position from its actual position. Do you have any idea to resolve it? Thanks. 

1. Open code sample scaffold
2. Tap "Tag1"
3. Tap "Tag2"
4. Tap "Tag3"
5. On the software keyboard, pressing space and move caret to between Tag2 and Tag3
6. On the software keyboard, tap backspace button

I debug showing caret position and found out sometimes my tag is recognized 2 characters, but sometimes my tag is recognized just 1 character. I guess this is something wrong. But the way how to fix doesn't come up with me.

### Expected results

"Tag2" is deleted because the caret is just after "Tag2".

### Actual results

"Tag1" is deleted.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample<
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: `WidgetSpan` uses a Unicode object replacement character as a placeholder in `plainText`, not the user-supplied UTF-16 private-use codepoint, causing a mismatch in selection/caret offset calculations. A workaround is to use UTF-8 private use area (`E000~`) instead of supplementary plane (`F0000~`), which maps to a single UTF-16 code unit and is handled correctly. Investigation ongoing; a commenter is looking into fixing the selection logic. Status: workaround available (use BMP private use area), no PR, open.

title: [file_selector] KeyUpEvent is not Triggered when `getSaveLocation` is invoked

### Actual Behavior
- The password save prompt appears only the first time `TextInput.finishAutofillContext(shouldSave: true)` is invoked.
- Further invocations do not display the popup unles

The issue is distinct from #116889: the save prompt appears on the first call but not on subsequent calls unless credentials are cleared and re-entered. @LongCatIsLooong and @chunhtai were CC'd as authors of the related engine patch #57209. No fix; open.

```dart
      Form(
        child: AutofillGroup(
          child: Column(
            mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue reproduces with the outlined steps (Windows Hello protecting Chrome password manager). The hypothesis is that the field loses focus during the Windows Hello PIN prompt, and Flutter cannot autofill into a non-focused field. Linked to related issues #145738, #152618, #137760, #61383. A HtmlElementView-based workaround is discussed but described as very hacky and only partially working. Status: open, stalled.

title: [iOS] Password save dialog not appearing with autofillHints on Flutter 3.27.1

### Flutter Version Comparison
Version where it worked: Flutter 3.10.5
Version where the issue occurs: Flutter 3.27.1

### Reproduction Environment
iOS Device: iPhone 11, iOS 18.2
Xcode Version: 15.3
CocoaPods: 1.16.2

### Expected results

The password save dialog should appear after entering the password.

### Actual results

The password save dialog does not appear even after entering the password.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
Widget _passwordField(BuildContext ctx) {
    final obscure = ctx.select<LoginState, bool>((state) => state.obscure);
    final validity = ctx.select<Logi
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the password save dialog regression on iOS between Flutter 3.10.5 and 3.27.1+. Video evidence shows the save dialog appearing in 3.10.5 but not in 3.27.1. The issue is linked to related autofill issues #161576, #148151, #145681, #116889, and #134723, but distinguished as specifically about the save dialog not appearing. Team members cc'd @hhuanlin, @justinmc, and @LongCatIsLooong for investigation; issue remains open with no root cause identified or PR linked.

title: [Web]TextField text selection broken on Chrome
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug is isolated to Chrome (desktop and Chrome-based browsers like Arc); Safari works fine on web, and Android, iOS, and macOS are unaffected. A commenter identified the root cause: the selection cursor jumps when the height of EditableText exceeds the text height — a threshold confirmed at ~3004px. The issue is also reproducible with SelectableText and linked to similar reports #162698 and #163607. No fix or workaround beyond using Safari is proposed; status is open and stalled.

title: Add Callbacks for Clipboard-Related Events (Paste, Cut, Copy, etc.)
body:
### Use case

Currently, with AdaptiveTextSelectionToolbar.buttonItems, we can access a list of List<ContextMenuButtonItem>? buttonItems, which allows us to add, remove, or customize each button. This makes it possible to rewrite the behavior of actions, such as the Paste button, as needed.

However, SystemContextMenu does not offer the same functionality. I am using SystemContextMenu specifically to avoid the default copy-paste dialog on iOS. In my use case, I would like to rewrite the Paste action so that my app can handle pasting images from the Clipboard, while still utilizing the native context menu.

While it might be challenging to rewrite the exact behavior of the system buttons, having a way to receive callbacks for each event (e.g., Copy, Cut, Paste) would be a good compromise. This would allow developers to respond to these events as needed without overriding the native menu behavior.

I am not sure how Flutter handles these events internally, but since actions like Paste se
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause on iOS is that Flutter engine's paste: method on the iOS embedder does not send the event back to the framework; the engine accesses the pasteboard directly and sends only the resulting text delta. A fix would require the iOS embedder to pipe the cut/copy/paste action type back to the framework as a method channel message, separate from the text delta. This is noted as potentially overlapping with custom system context menu button support. No PR or timeline; stalled.

comment_summary: Comments confirmed the soft keyboard disappears on Android Chrome mobile web but not on iOS or native Android app. The issue requires both `Visibility` widget and `SemanticsBinding.instance.ensureSemantics()` to trigger. Status: stalled, reproduced on Android mobile web only, no fix, no PR.

title: Keyboard Hints Disappear and Reappear When Toggling Password Visibility in Flutter TextField
body:
### Steps to reproduce

When toggling the visibility of a password field in a Flutter app, the keyboard hints (e.g., autofill suggestions, password manager hints) disappear and reappear. This issue occurs only on iOS 18 on emulator and on physical device. 

Create a TextField with obscureText set to true for password input.

Add an IconButton to toggle obscureText between true and false.

Run the app on an iOS 18 device or simulator.

Focus on the password field to bring up the keyboard.

Tap the visibility icon to toggle password visibility.

Observe the keyboard hints (e.g., autofill suggestions) disappearing and reappearing. 


### Expected results

The keyboard hints (e.g., autofill suggestions, password manager hints) should remain stable and not disappear when toggling password visibility.

### Actual results

The keyboard hints disappear and reappear when toggling password visibility, causing a flickering effect and disrupting the user experience.

### Code sample

<details open
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: changing obscureText triggers TextInput.updateConfig on the platform channel, and the iOS embedder resets something during that call causing keyboard hints to flicker. Confirmed on iPhone 15 Pro (iOS 18.2) with stable 3.27.3 and master 3.29.0. A separate button (not in the suffix) also reproduces the issue, ruling out focus as the cause. CC'd to @cbracken; no fix yet.

A TextInputFormatter approach was suggested but the reporter notes users expect the raw value without the suffix symbol.

title: [Web] iOS floating cursor not working when using `AutofillHints.newPassword`
comment_summary: Root cause identified: Safari does not allow the trackpad/floating-cursor mode when `input.type='password'` — this is a browser-level restriction that applies to any `new-password` autofill hint. A commenter verified this on the native MDN demo and concluded the behavior cannot be fixed without setting `type='text'`, which would break password manager autofill. Status: stalled, effectively a browser limitation on web/iOS.

title: Trigger key is not released when app gets out of focus
comment_summary: The root cause was identified: when the app loses focus (e.g., via Ctrl+L to browser URL bar), the modifier key is not removed from `HardwareKeyboard.instance.logicalKeysPressed`, leaving it stuck in a pressed state. After refocusing the TextField, the `LogicalKeySet` shortcut no longer fires because the system thinks the key is still held. This is the same underlying issue as #99330. Confirmed on stable 3.29.0 and master 3.30.0, on both web and desktop platforms.

title: Support for rendering custom carets in text fields
body:
### Use case

I want to build a UI that's similar to the video in [this Tweet](https://x.com/avstorm/status/1696401608458764517).

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/443d4da0-edf6-4b35-a33d-82468e1d101e

Essentially that demo uses the caret (cursor) in the text field as a progress bar to indicate whether the password strength is good enough, or the max number of chars in a text field are reached (think Twitters 280 char limit).

### Proposal

The `RenderEditable` class already takes two parameter to draw either above or below it as seen here:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/4ff2deafd4b9d9b8bf030db8b2163b49ecdccf10/packages/flutter/lib/src/rendering/editable.dart#L335-L336

The caret itself is also a `RenderEditablePainter` as seen here:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/4ff2deafd4b9d9b8bf030db8b2163b49ecdccf10/packages/flutter/lib/src/rendering/editable.dart#L2954-L3112

However, those parameters 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments favor making `_CaretPainter` public and wiring it via `InputDecorationTheme` or `TextSelectionThemeData`, similar to `InteractiveInkFeatureFactory`. A contributor offered to implement once the approach is decided.

title: TextField surrounded by MenuAnchor does not respond to directional arrow keys
comment_summary: Root cause identified by a commenter: the Shortcuts widget inside _RawMenuAnchorState unconditionally registers arrow-key shortcuts that consume the events before TextField can process them. A proposed fix is to only register those shortcuts when the menu is open. No PR yet; status is open.

title: HtmlElementView Key Strokes interfering with primary ScrollView
comment_summary: Comments confirm reproducibility on both stable 3.29.0 and master 3.30 on web (Chrome). A reviewer notes a related issue: drag-to-select text inside a TextField inside HtmlElementView also misbehaves and should be investigated together. The root cause (space/Enter keystrokes leaking to the parent Flutter ScrollView instead of being consumed by the HTML input element) is not yet diagnosed. Status: open, no fix or PR.

title: [Flutter Driver] The driver.tap() method right after application startup sometimes fails to open the keyboard on Android
body:
### Type of Request
Bug

### Environment
Running the `Linux_pixel_7pro integration_ui_keyboard_resize` test on an Android device with the Flutter Driver

- Application under test: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/dev/integration_tests/ui/lib/keyboard_resize.dart
- Test: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/dev/integration_tests/ui/test_driver/keyboard_resize_test.dart

The goal of the test is: "Ensure keyboard dismissal resizes the view to original size" (eg not directly related to this bug)

### What is happening?

Depending on the device executing the test and the device running the test, the following happens infrequently (but frequently enough to make the test flaky)


1. The test launches the keyboard_resize test application using Flutter Driver in `enableTextEntryEmulation: false` mode
2. The test waits for the `defaultTextField` TextField to be available via `await driver.waitFor(defaultTextField);`
3. The test taps on the `defaultTextField` TextField via
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified as a race condition on Android: `showSoftInput()` is called before `InsetsController` fires `hide(ime(), fromIme=false)`, and Android rejects the request until after that event fires. This is more frequent on newer devices and faster host machines. PR #162308 (workaround with retries) was merged; the issue was accidentally closed and reopened. The underlying race condition is still unresolved and tracked alongside flaky test issue #161300.
