A Flutter engineer confirmed the bug occurs on Android mobile Chrome but not on desktop Chrome. One commenter narrowed the cause to scroll position interaction with the number of text lines relative to the viewport. No fix or PR referenced; stalled.

title: When inputting Chinese words in the Web, the candidate box does not follow the cursor
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on Flutter master 3.30.0-1.0.pre.290 on web; works correctly on macOS and Windows desktop. The issue is web-only. A commenter linked this to #149979 as a possible shared root cause. Assigned to team-web as the fix likely lies in the web engine.

title: Mark the `TextEditingController.text` setter as `@visibleForTesting`
Textfield can't request focus in readonly when it is wrapped in a scrollview
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.29.0 and master. Comments note that requestFocus() on a readOnly TextField doesn't scroll the view to the field. The reporter's use case is a DateField component (TextField with readOnly: true and onTap for DatePicker) that needs focus for form validation error scrolling. A commenter noted that the web HTML readonly attribute does allow focus, supporting the expectation that Flutter readOnly fields should too.

title: Allow single FormFieldState rebuild
body:
### Use case

On `FormFieldState` is it possible to consider not using `Form.maybeOf(context)?._fieldDidChange()` when invoking `state.didChange(v)`? and maybe replace this with `this.validate()`? 


My request roots from performance issues on flutter web for Forms with large number of fields. In my case i have 100+ form fields in a single Form. and every time didChange is called. those fields gets rebuilt.

### Proposal

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/442015db-5b5e-43fd-8441-7cc32cf2b4b6)
comment_summary: A reproduction demonstrating all 107+ form fields rebuilding on each `didChange` call was provided with screen recordings showing the proposed fix. The issue remains open awaiting team input.

title: [Windows] Text selection in a input lost on right click
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug is Windows-specific (not reproducible on macOS or standard web). The reporter confirms it happens on both Windows desktop and Windows web (Chrome/Edge). A triager notes the `triaged-text-input` label cleanup. No fix or PR mentioned; status is awaiting further investigation.

title: iPad VoiceOver Not Announcing Characters During Cursor Navigation in TextField using external keyboard
body:
## Description
When using VoiceOver with an external keyboard on iPad devices, character-by-character navigation using arrow keys in Flutter TextField doesn't trigger audio announcements. While the cursor visually moves correctly through the text, VoiceOver fails to announce the character at the cursor position after each movement.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Connect an external keyboard to an iPad
2. Enable VoiceOver in iOS settings
3. Open a Flutter app with a TextField
4. Enter some text in the TextField
5. While in editing mode, use left/right arrow keys to navigate through the text character by character

## Expected Behavior
- As the cursor moves to each character, VoiceOver should announce the character at the current cursor position
- This behavior works correctly in native iOS applications and in Flutter on iPhone devices

## Actual Behavior
- The cursor visually moves through the text correctly
- VoiceOver remains silent and does not announce the character at the cursor positio
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed iPad-specific; works on iPhone with same app. A commenter suggested the issue may be that keyboard events interact with the framework textfield rather than UITextInput directly, and a potential fix would be to have UITextInput handle the key input.

title: [Flutter web] nextFocus() doesn't move to the next node when using TextField
comment_summary: Triager confirmed the behavior on stable 3.29.0 and master; FocusTraversalGroup does not help. The issue is confirmed to be web-specific — on iOS and Android nextFocus() works as expected. Root cause not identified; workaround is to use FocusScope.of(context).requestFocus(node) explicitly. Currently stalled.

title: Unicode characters not being rendered correctly in goldens test
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue reproduces only in golden tests, not at runtime: the Ahem test font (used by default in matchesGoldenFile) does not include the U+25CF bullet character, rendering it as a tofu box. A commenter suggests switching fonts or using loadAppFonts() properly. Status: open, P3.

title: [Proposal] SelectionArea should allow image selection
body:
### Use case

Does SelectionArea not consider supporting image selection and copying

### Proposal

Does SelectionArea not consider supporting image selection and copying
comment_summary: Comments clarify SelectionArea only supports text. Image selection requires first solving rich content copy/paste (#104206). A SelectableRegion adapter workaround exists but only copies plain-text representations.

title: Investigate adopting iOS Keyboard Layout Guide
body:
### Use case

With iOS 17, they moved the keyboard out-of-process and asynchronous. Due to this change, handling keyboard animations via Notifications, Flutter's current implementation, can cause differences in timing.

### Proposal

Keyboard Layout Guide was introduced in iOS 15 and is the "Recommended approach for integrating keyboard with UIKit". 

https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2023/10281/

I [experimented](https://github.com/vashworth/engine/blob/73e7c3f32369ffaca5b9282e0ae8c6d2110c7307/shell/platform/darwin/ios/framework/Source/FlutterViewController_layout_guide.mm) with it briefly, but we should take a closer look. See [here](https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/37719) for a list of use cases.
comment_summary: Comments suggest this change might also fix #57609. @huycozy confirmed worth looking into.

title: [Android] - IME connection closure isn't reported to app when using swipe to go back
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Tap on a text field to bring up the software keyboard
2. Swipe from the right edge of the screen to the left to trigger a back action


### Expected results

* Keyboard closes
* Text field loses focus and selection
* Flutter reports IME closure through text input connection

### Actual results

* Keyboard closes
* Text field retains focus and selection
* Flutter doesn't report IME closure through text input connection

Verified against Android version 35 using Flutter 3.27.1 and Flutter 3.29.1

TextField()

_No response_

_No response_

```console
[✓] Flutter (Channel stable, 3.27.1, on macOS 15.2 24C101 darwin-arm64, locale en-US)
[✓] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK version 36.0.0-rc5)
[✓] Xcode - develop for iOS and macOS (Xcode 16.2)

The reporter clarified the real bug is that Flutter's TextInput connection is not notified of IME closure, so Flutter still thinks the keyboard is open. Confirmed on stable 3.29.2 and master 3.31.0; currently open with no fix.

title: Keyboard hides immediately when TextField is inside ShowModalBottomSheet and has `autofocus: true`
body:
### Steps to reproduce

### Without my repository
1. Focus on TextField
2. Unfocus it
3. Open bottom sheet
4. Focus on TextField inside this bottom sheet

Steps from 2 to 4 should be done very quick.  
You can open bottom sheet with button, which unfocuses first textfield as well. And have the second TextField inside bottom sheet to have autofocus.  
Then, this steps will be done in one action.    


### With my repository
1. Clone the repository from link below
2. Run app.
3. Tap on TextField on top to focus on it
4. Tap on button on bottom

It may take more than one attempt to reproduce.  
It seams it depends on some sort of 'lag'. You can increase image count in app to create this lag. This increases the chance to trigger the issue.  


### Expected results

Bottom sheet opens up. Keyboard opens. Textfield inside bottom sheet is in focus.  

### Actual results

Bottom sheet opens up. Keyboard opens and closes immediately. But textfield is in focus as intended.  

### Code sample

<s
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause is a timing race: when the first TextField loses focus, `TextInput.hide` is called, and the bottom sheet's autofocus fires while the keyboard is still being dismissed, causing the keyboard to immediately close. Confirmed Android-only; iOS works correctly. A workaround is to delay the focus request with `Future.delayed` or `WidgetsBinding.instance.addPostFrameCallback`. Related to #129179 and #125565. Status: has workaround, open for proper fix.

title: [Windows] Issue with converting Korean to Hanja (Chinese characters) on Windows 10
body:
There is an issue when converting Korean to Hanja in a TextField after building on the Windows platform.

As shown in the attached video, when entering Korean text and pressing the Hanja key on the keyboard, a list of corresponding Hanja characters should appear. Selecting one should input the chosen character into the TextField, but this does not work.

This issue appears to be the same as #74819, which was reported as fixed in Flutter 3.19.2, as mentioned in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/74819#issuecomment-1973035874, but in reality, the problem still persists.

This issue occurs on Windows 10, while it works fine on Windows 11. It seems that [#74819 (comment)](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/74819#issuecomment-1973035874) was based on testing in Windows 11, leading to the assumption that the issue was fixed.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/478b61ba-4a1e-4d28-abd3-2bdd15b987bd

<details>
<summary>flutter doctor -v</summary>

```
[!] Flutter (Channel 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed still present in stable 3.29.2. Issue is Windows 10-specific; works fine on Windows 11. Team has only Windows 11 available and cannot verify directly.

title: Broken text selection when selecting backwards from `TextSpan` into `WidgetSpan` within `SelectionArea`
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug reproduces on stable and master across all platforms. Root cause analysis from the team: when selecting backwards from a `TextSpan` into a container holding a `WidgetSpan`, `MultiSelectableSelectionContainerDelegate._initSelection` starts searching from the first selectable in the new container rather than the last, breaking backwards traversal while forward selection works correctly. A fix would require `_initSelection` and `_adjustSelection` to detect backwards movement and start from the last selectable. No PR exists; status is open with a detailed investigation posted.

title: Full Keyboard Access on iOS Breaks External Keyboard Navigation in Flutter App
body:
### Description

Basic support for Full Keyboard Access (FKA) should now be available as noted here:

> With https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/56842 and https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/159811 merged I think the basic FKA support should be there. I'm going to close this soon (after verifying the implementation works in certain apps) and create new issues for the remaining items (see the PR description of https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/56606). Please file issues for FKA-related bugs and feature requests, thank you! 

 _Originally posted by @LongCatIsLooong in [#76497](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/76497#issuecomment-2546915345)_

However, there is an issue when using an external keyboard with Full Keyboard Access enabled:

When connecting an external keyboard to an iOS device, Flutter apps typically function correctly. However, once "Full Keyboard Access" is enabled in iOS Accessibility settings, the external keyboard stops responding. The app becomes unr
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the external keyboard stops responding with Full Keyboard Access (FKA) enabled on iOS physical devices (iPhone 12 Pro Max / iOS 18.4, iOS 17 simulator) but is not reproducible on iOS 18 simulators or some physical devices, leading to a `device-specific` label. Additional sub-issues identified in comments: (1) `onFocusChange` and `Semantics.onDidGainAccessibilityFocus` are never called when tabbing with FKA (only triggered on Space-key press), meaning Flutter cannot track which widget has FKA focus; (2) sliders are not focusable/adjustable via FKA. A commenter notes the issue does not reproduce on iOS >= 18 physical devices (90%+ of fleet). Assignee removed after no status updates; currently stalled.

title: TextInputClient.performSelectors, error thrown by package:flutter/src/widgets/focus_traversal.dart
body:
```
════════ Exception caught by services library ══════════════════════════════════
The following assertion was thrown during method call TextInputClient.performSelectors:
'package:flutter/src/widgets/focus_traversal.dart': Failed assertion: line 594 pos 12: 'sortedNodes.contains(focusedChild)': is not true.
**Either the assertion indicates an error in the framework itself, or we should provide substantially more information in this error message to help you determine and fix the underlying cause.**
In either case, please report this assertion by filing a bug on GitHub:
 https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/new?template=2_bug.yml
When the exception was thrown, this was the stack:
#2 FocusTraversalPolicy._moveFocus (package:flutter/src/widgets/focus_traversal.dart:594:12)
focus_traversal.dart:594
#3 FocusTraversalPolicy.next (package:flutter/src/widgets/focus_traversal.dart:373:39)
focus_traversal.dart:373
#4 FocusNode.nextFocus (package:flutter/src/widgets/focus_manager.dart:1238
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The crash originates from a 'sortedNodes.contains(focusedChild)' assertion in FocusTraversalPolicy._moveFocus during an insertTab: selector call. The reporter was directed to provide a reproducible sample but declined, citing that the framework's own error message instructed them to file the issue. No repro; issue is open for tracking with no fix.

title: Layout jumps/stutters on first keyboard open after rotation
body:
### Steps to reproduce

On a physical Android device (Samsung S9):
 
1. Launch the code sample (either **Start Debugging** or `flutter run --release`).
2. Rotate device to landscape.
3. Tap the text field to open keyboard, then back button to close the keyboard.
4. Rotate back to portrait.
5. Tap text field again → body jumps up for a very brief time before settling down at correct position.

The same issue occurs by reversing the orientation order (open keyboard in Portrait, close it, change orientation to Landscape, open keyboard again), though in that case it doesn't jump up, but stays hidden down.

It seems like the wrong orientation information is applied for a few frames before everything jumps into place.

### Expected results

Body (in this case the lone TextField) should animate smoothly into the correct vertical location when the virtual keyboard opens

### Actual results

Huge vertical overshoot, then jumps into the correct place (when Landscape → portrait)

or

Body remain 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the layout jump is Android-only (not iOS) and reproducible on stable (3.29.x) and master. The issue predates 3.27.4, so it is not a recent regression. Investigation with the performance overlay on a Pixel 7 revealed severe ongoing jank even with the keyboard stationary, while other devices (Poco M3) showed normal frame rates, suggesting a device-specific rendering interaction when orientation-change data arrives after the keyboard insets are applied.

### Expected results

The main web page should not scroll/bounce around
Only the flutter app/div should scroll

### Actual results

The page bounces around and the flutter div gets scrolled away

### Code sample

import 'multi_view_app.dart';

void main() {

    MultiViewApp(
      viewBuilder: (BuildContext context) => const MyApp(),

...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue: when a Flutter app is embedded in a div on a web page that itself scrolls, wheel/trackpad scroll events over enabled TextField widgets propagate out of the Flutter container and scroll the host page. Disabling the TextField stops the propagation. Confirmed reproducible on stable (3.29.3) and master (3.32.x). Related issues #159680 and #159358 and engine PR #53922 are linked. No fix has landed; status is open.

title: Ellipsis not working properly when a text overflows via constrained height instead of max lines
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug is reproducible on stable 3.29 and master 3.32 on all platforms — ellipsis is not shown when text is clipped by a constrained parent height rather than by `maxLines`. One commenter noted it may be working as intended but acknowledged it is hard to work around. A workaround using `LayoutBuilder` and `TextPainter` to compute the number of visible lines dynamically was provided; no framework fix or PR is linked.

title: iOS keyboard cannot switch brightness automatically
comment_summary: Root cause suspected: Flutter's iOS keyboard brightness is not updated dynamically while the keyboard is visible, unlike native iOS which updates immediately. Potential fix: hide/reshow keyboard or reset text input connection when didChangePlatformBrightness fires. Connection to @LongCatIsLooong's UITextView prototype noted.

title: [IOS]TextField throws assertion error on long press
comment_summary: The issue is reproducible on iOS device (not simulator) on both stable 3.29.3 and master channel. A commenter identified the root cause: `bottomBound` in `RenderEditable._calculateAdjustedCursorOffset` becomes negative when the TextField is given a constrained height via `contentPadding`, causing the `clampDouble` assertion to fire. The proposed fix is to always use `_textPainter.height` when computing `bottomBound`. Assignee was removed after no progress; a later check found it still reproduces on Flutter 3.40.0-1.0.pre.344.

title: Problems with Autocomplete and OverlayPortal.overlayChildLayoutBuilder in 3.32
body:
Relates to [#165249](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/165249) and [#164034](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/164034)
In version 3.32, Autocomplete uses the new OverlayPortal API - overlayChildLayoutBuilder. It uses information about coordinate transformation from the child to the Overlay widget during the layout phase.
Previously, there were CompositedTransformFollower and CompositedTransformTarget widgets that used coordinate transformation during the compositing phase. As a result, changes have occurred in the work of the Autocomplete widget if it is a descendant of those widgets that calculate transformations after the layout phase (during the paint and compositing phases). At least, these are CompositedTransformFollower (using the compositing phase) and Flow (using the paint phase). 
And if, when using the first one, the assertion "The paint transform cannot be reliably computed because of RenderFollowerLayer(s)" is thrown, then nothing will happen with Flow.
I think 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the assertion fires specifically for `CompositedTransformFollower` (not `Flow`), and that the assertion check is based on render object type rather than matrix comparison, making it an over-broad guard. The author demonstrates a compositing-phase correction technique (custom layer with C * L^(-1) transform) as a third-party workaround, and proposes: (1) change the assertion to compare layout-phase vs. end-of-frame matrices, and (2) provide a way to suppress it. The `LongCatIsLooong` engine author confirms `Flow` is a gap they missed and that the `CompositedTransformFollower`-in-path problem has no clean engine-level solution. Currently open with no PR; the reporter offers to submit a small PR if the team agrees on the approach.

title: [Proposal] Add a `text` parameter to `TextField` and `TextFormField` to update their values
comment_summary: Discussion focuses on scope: @justinmc suggests a textEditingValue parameter (not just text) covering selection and composing, alongside an onChangedValue callback. A community contributor posts a draft implementation showing it working by recreating the internal controller on didUpdateWidget. No official PR or prioritization decision yet; status is under consideration.

1. Tap the first TextField to open the keyboard.
2. While the keyboard is open, tap the second TextField.

The first TextField won't trigger the `onTapOutside`.

This happens only when the keyboard is opened and tries to tap another TextField.



### Expected results

Trigger `onTapOutside` while keyboard is on.

### Actual results

The first TextField won't trigger the `onTapOutside`.

```dart
Column(
    children: [
        TextField(
            controller: ctrl1,
            keyboardType: const TextInputType.numberWithOptions(
              decimal: false,
            ),
            style: const TextStyle(color: Colors.white),
            onTap: () {,
            onSubmitted: (_) {},
            onTapOutside: (_) {},
          ),
        TextField(
            controller: ctrl2,
            keyboardType: co
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause: the default groupId for EditableText is the same for every instance, so all TextFields share one TapRegion group and tapping a second field does not trigger onTapOutside on the first. A workaround of passing a unique groupId per TextField is noted, but a commenter reports it still did not work in their specific list-based setup. Confirmed on stable 3.29.3 and master 3.32.0.

title: [Firefox] macOS emoji picker doesn't input an emoji under Firefox to `TextField` due to focus being lost for too long
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Focus into any `TextField` under Firefox browser on macOS device.
2. Press `Control + CMD + Space` or `fn + E` to open up macOS emoji picker.
3. Be sure that the emoji picker is in compact mode (displayed above the field and not floating around) - if not, click on the calendar looking button in the picker so that it shrinks down.
4. Press on any emoji.
5. Notice the emoji not being pasted.

This seems to be happening due to `TextField` losing focus for too long, since if after the 4th step you manually press into the `TextField`, then the emoji is pasted. 

### Expected results

Emoji is pasted into the `TextField` (like it does under any other browser or in macOS application).

### Actual results

Emoji is not pasted.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

I am running this code with the following command: `flutter run -d web-server --web-hostname=0.0.0.0 --web-port=8080` and then open the `http://localhost:8080` URL in Firefox.

```
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The root cause was identified in a comment: when the TextField loses focus on Firefox, Flutter removes the underlying `<input>` element from the DOM, so there is no element to receive the emoji when it is picked later. Issue confirmed reproducible on both stable 3.29.3 and master 3.32.0-pre on Firefox/macOS web. No fix or PR mentioned; status is open.

title: Add a legacy-compatible input action that mimics pre-3.13 `TextInputAction.next` behavior
body:
### Use case

This proposal suggests adding a new `TextInputAction` value or an opt-in mechanism to replicate the legacy behavior of `TextInputAction.next` from Flutter versions prior to 3.13.

In older versions, pressing Enter or "Next" on the keyboard would:
1. Move focus to the next focusable widget.
2. Immediately trigger `onPressed` on a button if it was the next widget.

After Flutter 3.13, the behavior has changed:
- Pressing Enter only moves the focus.
- A second press is needed to activate the button's `onPressed`.

This change breaks intuitive form workflows and degrades UX on desktop/web where Enter is commonly used to submit or confirm forms.

In Flutter ≥ 3.13, due to changes in `FocusTraversalPolicy`, this no longer works. The same setup requires two Enter presses to move and then activate the next control, even if it's a button.

**Alternatives I've considered**  

- **Create a separate function and call it in both `onFieldSubmitted` and the button's `onPressed`.**  
  W
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed the behavior change (requiring two Enter presses to submit after `TextInputAction.next`) occurs on all platforms: Android, iOS, web, and desktop.

title: [iOS] Tapping selection to toggle the toolbar doesn't work when using system context menu
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Run code sample on the iOS simulator or physical iPhone on iOS 16.0+ where the system context menu is supported.
2. Double tap on "three" to select it.
3. Tap on the selection.

### Expected results

Tapping the selection should hide the toolbar, and a subsequent tap should reveal it.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4f35c598-ac4c-4b7d-a7a5-4314b404a472

### Actual results

Tapping the selection hides the toolbar and immediately reveals it again.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6d78d279-31ee-4d95-889f-3088355a52cd

### What is happening

On the framework side we have implemented toggling the selection toolbar when tapping on the current selection. This does not work with the system context menu as seen in the video in the actual results of this issue, tapping the selection hides the toolbar but immediately shows it again after. This happens because first the system dismisses the platform context menu when the tap happens outside the con
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: tap on selection causes platform to dismiss system context menu (Flutter responds by hiding toolbar), then Flutter also responds to the tap and tries to toggle the toolbar back on. Proposed fix: track timestamp of most recent onDismiss callback and skip present if within a threshold (~0.1s).

title: Selection Handles Overlap Context Menu in SelectionArea
body:
### Steps to reproduce

Do the following (see code and video):

 -- Wrap a **`Text`** widget in a **`SelectionArea`** widget
 -- Long press on a word: the word will be selected and the context menu will open
 -- Click on the 3-dot button to see the other context menu options: the selection handle/bubbles will be over the context menu

Some observations:

 -- The problem doesn't happen with the **`SelectableText`** widget, it only happens with **`SelectionArea`**
 -- The problem disappears if the selection handle is moved after the long press (see video)

Related issue: [#59229](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/59229)

### Expected results

(see video)

### Actual results

(see video)

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
void main() { runApp( const MyAppContextMenu() );  }

class MyAppContextMenu extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyAppContextMenu( {super.key} );

  @override
  Widget build(final BuildContext context) {
     return MaterialApp(
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: in SelectableRegionState._handleTouchLongPressEnd, the context menu is inserted into the Overlay before the selection handles, so handles render on top of the menu. Fix is to insert selection handles first so the context menu appears above them. A team member provided this diagnosis and offered to review PRs. No PR filed yet; open.

title: TextField that unfocuses on onTapOutside crashes app when placed in a list of spaced items
body:
When developing my app, I have several widgets in a column that I want spaced out if they can fit in the available space, but I want the user to be able to scroll if the widgets don't fit. This is addressed in [the Flutter cookbook, on how to create a List with spaced items](https://docs.flutter.dev/cookbook/lists/spaced-items).

After following that recipe, I added a TextField to the list using custom FocusNode behavior. I want the user pressing a completion action on their keyboard to save the user's input and unfocus the TextField (done with onEditingComplete). I also want a tap outside of the TextField to cancel the user's input, and revert the TextField to its default value (done with onTapOutside). However, if there is enough room for the entire column when the virtual keyboard is hidden, but not enough room when the keyboard is active (ie. removing the virtual keyboard gives enough extra room such that scrolling is disabled when it's hidden), this combination is dangerous; when 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The root cause was identified from the stack trace: when the user scrolls outside the TextField (triggering `onTapOutside` → `focusNode.unfocus()`), the keyboard dismisses and the scroll view's dimensions change, which causes `ScrollPositionWithSingleContext.goBallistic` → `ScrollDragController.cancel` → `_StretchController._recede` → `AnimationController.forward` to fire a `setState` during layout, triggering the "Build scheduled during frame" assertion. Affects Android (confirmed on Pixel 2 and Samsung Galaxy S24 with Gboard and Samsung Keyboard). A workaround is to add `AlwaysScrollableScrollPhysics()` to the `SingleChildScrollView`. The framework team acknowledged this is a scrolling framework bug where `ScrollableState.setCanDrag` should not trigger a rebuild during layout.

title: [iOS] Tapping "Select All" when using system context menu does not show selection handles
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Run code sample on the iOS simulator or physical iPhone on iOS 16.0+ where the system context menu is supported.
2. Tap anywhere.
3. Tap again on the collapsed selection to reveal the context menu.
4. Tap on "Select All".

### Expected results

Tapping "Select All" selects all the text and reveals the selection handles.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ab93e63d-b3b9-4519-8736-e2a31dcf852d

### Actual results

Tapping "Select All" selects all the text but does not reveal the selection handles.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b7fa54ea-3e83-4716-8400-7dfaa5a57dfa

### What is happening

Using the system context menu on iOS we do not get any signals that we pressed a button in the context menu. That update comes through `EditableTextState.updateEditingValue`, and in that case we hit this line which classifies the cause of the update to be the keyboard https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/0cf1d34e9b7614bb99f1b645d0ad84af69915fb1/packages/
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: when system context menu's Select All is pressed, the update comes through updateEditingValue classified as keyboard cause, which prevents selection handles from being shown. Proposed fix: use a custom Select All button calling editableTextState.selectAll(SelectionChangedCause.toolbar).

title: The deviceType of key event always returns `Keyboard`
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create to default application.
2. Add `KeyboardListener`.
3. Check event.deviceType.label value in onKeyEvent with Extended Controls(Directional pad) of Android TV Emulator.

### Expected results

event.deviceType.label should be `Directional Pad`.

### Actual results

Select button of Directional pad.
```console
I/flutter ( 4516): Keyboard
I/flutter ( 4516): KeyDownEvent#dc55c(physicalKey: PhysicalKeyboardKey#a1309(usbHidUsage: "0x11000000e8", debugName: "Key with ID 0x11000000e8"), logicalKey: LogicalKeyboardKey#4999d(keyId: "0x10000050c", keyLabel: "Select", debugName: "Select"), character: null, timeStamp: 0:00:01.886120)
I/flutter ( 4516): Keyboard
I/flutter ( 4516): KeyUpEvent#42c8c(physicalKey: PhysicalKeyboardKey#a1309(usbHidUsage: "0x11000000e8", debugName: "Key with ID 0x11000000e8"), logicalKey: LogicalKeyboardKey#4999d(keyId: "0x10000050c", keyLabel: "Select", debugName: "Select"), character: null, timeStamp: 0:00:01.886191)
```

Keyboard's enter 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: `KeyEvent.getSource()` on Android returns a combined bitmask (e.g., `0x301 = SOURCE_KEYBOARD | SOURCE_DPAD`), but `_convertDeviceType` in `hardware_keyboard.dart` performs exact equality checks against individual source constants, so combined values always fall through to the `keyboard` default. Fix requires bitwise AND checks instead of equality. Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.29.3 and master 3.33. No PR yet; currently open and awaiting a fix.

title: TextField automatically selected when typing
comment_summary: Comments were unable to reliably reproduce the reported auto-selection behavior on web outside of the reporter's specific macOS 15 + Chrome setup. Triage confirmed the issue could not be replicated on macOS or Windows by other contributors. The issue remains open for team tracking with no confirmed root cause or platform pattern.

title: TextFormField vertical padding on web does not align with iOS/Android
body:
### Steps to reproduce

The issue occurs on newest stable 3.32.0 and 3.29.2 version, simple by using `TextFormField` with `contentPadding` on `decoration` attribute. With the `isDense` attribute on true, the padding grows correctly on iOS/Android as the base field height is 24px by adding top and bottom padding. Starting with zero padding, you can see this is one of the few padding values which renders the same height on both platforms.

</details>

<details open>
<summary>Screenshots / Video demonstration</summary>

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9b436cca-117c-483a-9962-e053851ed6fb)

</details>

### Expected results

Bo
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: Flutter intentionally sets a different VisualDensity for desktop/web vs mobile via ThemeData, causing the padding difference. PR #166834 introduced InputDecoration.visualDensity and InputDecorationTheme.visualDensity to address this. Related to #166201.

comment_summary: A triager measured the default CupertinoTextField height as 34px on iOS, below the HIG-recommended 44x44 minimum tap target. A native SwiftUI TextField on iOS 26 was measured at 22px, suggesting native is also small. Discussion narrowed to whether native iOS actually expands the touch target to 44px; no confirmation either way. No PR or fix; open.

title: SelectableText ignores letterSpacing parameter in the TextStyle
comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: SelectableText uses EditableText internally and the cursor width (default 2px) contributes to the layout width, causing it to be wider than a plain Text widget. Setting cursorWidth: 0 reduces but does not eliminate the difference. A commenter suggests SelectionArea as a workaround; confirmed it works correctly. Status: open, P3.

title: [flutter_svg][web] SVG in TextField prefixIcon/suffixIcon blocks input when semantics are enabled
body:
### Steps to reproduce

#### Description

On Flutter Web, when using an SVG image (via `SvgPicture.asset`) in the `prefixIcon` or `suffixIcon` of a `TextField`, the text field does not accept any input when accessibility semantics are enabled. The text field appears focusable: the caret is visible, and the field gains focus as expected. However, when the user types, no text appears in the field.

#### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a `TextField` with either a `prefixIcon` or `suffixIcon` containing an `SvgPicture`.
2. Enable semantics with `SemanticsBinding.instance.ensureSemantics()`.
3. Attempt to focus and type into the field.

flutter_svg version: 2.1.0

#### Workarounds

- Adding a `semanticsLabel` to the `SvgPicture` constructor resolves the issue.
- Wrapping the `TextField` in a `MergeSemantics` widget also resolves the problem.

However, these workarounds shouldn't be necessary. The `TextField` should work as expected with `SvgPicture` and semantics enabled, without extra config
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed web-specific; works on iOS and macOS. Reproduced on stable 3.32.2 and master 3.33.0-pre. Redirected to web triage team.

title: [Google3 Bug]: Obscure Text Read OutLoud Desktop
body:
### Help us understand the severity of this issue

- [ ] causing severe production issues e.g. malfunctions or data loss
- [x] blocking next binary release
- [ ] blocking a client feature launch within a quarter
- [ ] nice-to-have but does not block a launch within the next quarter

### Steps to reproduce

1) Build the sample obscure text program from here for Windows, MacOS, and Web :  https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField-class.html
2) Use a voice over for Mac, and windows voice assistant to test the app on all 3 types
3) You'll notice the password is visibly  obscured, but read out loud by the voice readers. Except in the Web case.


For MacOS, [kSecureTextEntry](https://api.flutter.dev/ios-embedder/_flutter_text_input_plugin_8mm.html#a2551524a1b373099a3cf1bcf859da89c) seems to be used for obscureText, however I don't see it being checked anywhere when the textfield node is created. I think its created [here](https://api.flutter.dev/macos-embedder/_flutter_platform_node
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Reproduced on stable 3.32.4 and master 3.33 on macOS; Windows not verified. Root cause identified: the desktop accessibility bridge does not propagate the obscureText flag to the embedding. The fix requires work in the desktop embedding layer; no PR yet.

title: [iOS] Chinese Pinyin Input Method Leaves Invisible Characters When Focus Changes Between TextFields
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Set up iOS device with Chinese Pinyin input method
2. Create a Flutter app with two TextField widgets
3. Focus on the first TextField
4. Type Chinese pinyin letters "nihaoma" using the system keyboard (do NOT tap the confirm/space key)
5. Directly tap on the second TextField to switch focus (without confirming the pinyin input)
6. Observe the content in the first TextField

### Expected results

Behavior should match iOS native UITextField:

1. When focus is lost during pinyin input, the TextField should automatically clean up composing state
2. The TextField should contain clean text "nihaoma" (without invisible separator characters)
3. This matches how iOS native input fields handle the same scenario

### Actual results

Flutter TextField retains invisible characters:

1. The first TextField displays "ni hao ma" with invisible six-per-em space characters (U+2006)
2. These invisible separator characters are inserted by iOS input method during pinyin composit
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug on iOS 18.5 with the system Simplified Pinyin keyboard; invisible U+2006 separator characters remain in the field text after focus leaves during composition. iOS native UITextField cleans these up correctly. Reproduced on stable 3.32.4 and master 3.33. A comment notes the fix will be easier once `EditableTextState` handles `resignFirstResponder` synchronously. Status: open, no PR.

title: `TextInputAction.next` in `Autocomplete` broken in Flutter 3.32.5 when inside a `Column`
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new Flutter project.
2. Replace the contents of main.dart with the reproduction code below.
3. Run the app on Web plotform.
4. Tap into the Autocomplete field and type any character (e.g., "T") to trigger suggestions.
5. Select a suggestion by pressing `Enter ` key on keyboard.

### Expected results

When the user presses the `Enter `button on the keyboard after entering text or selecting an option in the Autocomplete field:

- Focus should move automatically to the next TextFormField below.
- This should work consistently when fields are arranged vertically using a Column.

This is standard behavior when using `TextInputAction.next` in Flutter input fields.

### Actual results

- Pressing the `Enter `key on the keyboard after interacting with the Autocomplete field does nothing.
- Focus remains on the Autocomplete field and does not move to the next TextFormField.
- No error is thrown, an
...[truncated]
comment_summary: A commenter identifies the root cause as the migration of `Autocomplete` to `OverlayPortal` in Flutter 3.22.0 (commit `b6e758a`); the `TextInputAction.next` focus traversal breaks when the Autocomplete is inside a `Column` but works in a `Row`. Verified on stable 3.32.5 and master 3.33. No PR yet; status is open.

title: Need to Disable inputAssistantItem for Apple Pencil Input to Prevent Layout Shift
body:
In my use case, I need to disable the inputAssistantItem (keyboard shortcuts bar) when using Apple Pencil input. Currently, it appears at the bottom of the screen, which causes an undesired layout shift in my app's UI.

I have explored common suggestions such as:

```dart
Scaffold(
  resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false,
)
```

However, this is not a suitable solution for my case, as I still need proper layout behavior for other inputs, and disabling resizeToAvoidBottomInset causes other layout issues.

I am also unable to find a way to override or customize the inputAssistantItem via Flutter's CupertinoTextField or TextField widgets to remove it specifically for Apple Pencil input.

Apple's official documentation:
https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uiresponder/inputassistantitem

Please let me know if there's a way to conditionally disable or customize the inputAssistantItem in Flutter for stylus-based input (e.g., Apple Pencil), or if native support for this customization cou
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments indicate the inputAssistantItem (Apple Pencil shortcuts bar) is being treated by Flutter as a software keyboard, causing the Scaffold to resize. A team member notes this should not affect layout in native apps. A native Swift workaround using method swizzling to clear leadingBarButtonGroups and trailingBarButtonGroups is provided. One engine reference to inputAssistantItem is found. Labeled as a proposal requiring native implementation. Status: open.

title: [Engine] [Android] `respondsToIsStylusHandwritingAvailableWhenAPILevelUnsupported` in `ScribeChannelTest` leaks resources

- Clone the flutter repo

### Code sample

N/A


### Screenshots or Video

N/A


### Logs

The logs below are truncated to show the issue with the test in question (`respondsToIsStylusHandwritingAvailableWhenAPILevelUnsupported `). As you can see, it reports that the test passes

<details open><summary>Logs</summary>

```console
ScribeChannelTest > respondsToIsStylusHandwritingAvailableW
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The resource leak reported in STANDARD_ERROR is caused by Surface objects in SurfaceTextureSurfaceProducer tests (SurfaceTextureSurfaceProducerTest) that are not released before the Robolectric test completes; the leak originates in a different test class than the ScribeChannelTest named in the title. A team member confirmed this and noted that test-level resource leaks are low severity since the test world is destroyed regularly, but flagged that it could indicate a production-code leak if the same code path runs in production. No PR linked; status is open.

title: SystemContextMenu support outside of editable text?
body:
### Use case

Currently, SystemContextMenu is only supported in the context of a text input field. It doesn't even work when readOnly is true, due to the lack of an active TextInputConnection (see https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/171242). So when users select text in a read only field, or in a SelectionArea, they currently see the Flutter-rendered menu, which is not quite identical to the SystemContextMenu.

### Proposal

Flutter should support showing the SystemContextMenu for non-editable text and even other cases, like long-pressing an image to show a context menu.

Under the hood, the context menu is inserted into the UITextInputView (explained in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/171242#issuecomment-3046645634), so it would be necessary to refactor this to support insertion into something like the FlutterView.
comment_summary: Comments discuss whether `SystemContextMenu` should be exposed outside editable text, noting it conflicts with Flutter's principle of owning the UI. A triage note acknowledges it is not feasible to completely eliminate the Flutter-drawn context menu for platforms without a built-in one.

title: TextInputFormatter and Samsung Keyboard Autocorrect give wrong results due to race conditions
comment_summary: Comments confirmed the issue reproduces only on Samsung devices (S10+, Galaxy S20, etc.) and not on Pixel or OnePlus. The native Android implementation has the same behavior, indicating the root cause is Samsung keyboard's autocorrect system racing with the `TextInputFormatter`. The Samsung autocorrect flushes before the formatter's state update propagates, causing the first letter to be dropped from the suggestion. `TextCapitalization.words` is a partial workaround but insufficient for names with particles. Status: labeled for team tracking, no fix actionable from Flutter.

title: Semantics excludeSemantics breaks aria-label on flutter web <input> for TextFormField
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a TextFormField wrapped with a Semantics widget.
2. Add a label and set excludeSemantics: true.
3. Run on Flutter Web using flutter build web.
4. Try to inspect the generated <input> element.
5. Observe that aria-label, it will come as 'Login_email' which is provided in Semantics Label
6. Now set excludeSemantics: false and run the Flutter web.
7. Try to inspect the generated <input> element
8. Observe the aria-label, it will come as 'Email', which is the hint text provided in the TextFormField. 'Login_email' label is not coming in this case

Tested on Chrome stable version 138.0.7204.101

### Expected results

- The <input> should have aria-label="Login_email" so it can be used in web automation tools like Selenium/Cypress.

- Excluding the child semantics should still allow mapping the label (i.e 'Login_email' in my case) to the native input.

### Actual results

- Input cannot be typed into programmatically (via Playwright/Selenium/Cypress) in flutt
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments show the aria-label issue depends on the excludeSemantics setting: with excludeSemantics true the label is correct after upgrading to 3.32.6, but with excludeSemantics false two separate input elements are rendered (one with the custom label but not interactive, one interactive with only the hint text). Reproduced on stable 3.32.6 and master 3.33.0. @yjbanov notes customer:chalk ran into a similar issue. Status: open, no PR.

title: TextField scrollPadding is not applied when inside a Drawer
comment_summary: A triager confirms reproduction on Android and iOS with stable 3.32.7 and master 3.33. A community comment provides a workaround: wrap the Drawer's ListView in a Padding using `MediaQuery.viewInsetsOf` and manually handle bottom padding. No fix or PR; status is open.

title: Gmail-style Email Input Field in Flutter
body:
### Use case

I am looking for a robust text input widget in Flutter that mimics the behavior of the "To" field in Gmail or similar email clients. This type of input allows users to type email addresses, which then transform into "chips"  upon completion ( pressing Enter, Space, or a comma, or selecting from an auto-completion list). Users should also be able to easily remove these chips by taping backspace once.
 
I have attempted to use existing packages, specifically "chip_input_text_field" (https://pub.dev/packages/chip_inputs_textfield), but encountered a significant limitation. When the cursor is positioned at the very end of a created chip, and the user presses the backspace key, the expected behavior is for the chip to be removed or, in some implementations, for the chip to revert to editable text. However, in "chip_input_text_field", pressing backspace at the end of a chip causes the chip's content to be converted back into a regular string within the text field, rather than d
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Issue redirected as a package-level limitation but then reopened as a proposal. Root cause is that WidgetSpan is treated as a single character internally, making it impossible to delete chip-by-chip via backspace. Reference made to Android's ImageSpan API. #56285 noted as related. No fix or PR proposed.

title: Tab key inserts space instead of changing focus on iOS app running on macOS (Apple Silicon)
comment_summary: Triager could not reproduce with the provided code sample on stable 3.32.7 or master using Apple Silicon. Reporter clarified the issue is specific to running the iOS app target on macOS via 'My Mac (Designed for iPad)' in Xcode, not a native macOS build. A second triager confirmed the behavior on master 3.33.0-1.0.pre-1146 using the same Mac-designed-for-iPad target.

title: TextInputAction.next on Flutter Web doesn't always select full text in the next field (inconsistent focus behavior)
body:
## Update

This issue was caused by the `_justResumed` flag being stored **per `EditableTextState`**, which was set to `true` on app resume for every `TextField`, but only reset when each individual field was focused again. This led to unreliable behavior where `TextInputAction.next`, `tab`, or programmatic focus would **not auto-select text** as expected on Web and Desktop after resume.

### Steps to reproduce

On Flutter Web, when using `TextInputAction.next` and pressing the Enter key, the focus correctly moves to the next `TextField` via:
```dart
bool nextFocus() => FocusTraversalGroup.of(context!).next(this);
```
However, sometimes the text in the newly focused field is not fully selected. Instead, the cursor is placed at the end of the existing text. This is inconsistent behavior, as the full text should ideally be selected to allow for easy replacement.

This issue seems to occur intermittently, and I haven't been able to reliably reproduce it.

This used to work  correctly in f
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: PR #157399 introduced a `_justResumed` flag stored per EditableTextState that is set on app resume for every TextField but only reset when each field individually gains focus, causing the auto-select behavior to be unreliable after backgrounding. Confirmed on stable 3.32.7 and master 3.33.0 on both web and desktop.

title: [iOS] Holding arrow keys on a physical iPad keyboard to change the cursor position in a TextFormField doesn't work
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on both iPad simulator and iOS simulator on stable 3.32.7 and master 3.33.0-1.0.pre-1064. Triager also observed that holding the left arrow key moves the cursor to the right, not left. Native iOS (Messages app) does not exhibit the problem. Android is not affected. A contributor investigating a timer-based fix asked about timer usage in the style guide; a team member clarified timers are acceptable and suggested investigating whether other repeated key event scenarios are affected before proceeding.

title: [VPAT][A11y] disabled password Textfield is missing role in voice over
body: b/429089551 b/428649303 Run the https://github.com/flutter/flutter/tree/master/dev/a11y_assessments and navigate to textfield password label and focus disabled password field VoiceOver announces: "Password, disabled". EXPECTED RESULTS: VoiceOver announces "Password, Text field, disabled" or similar.
comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause: disabled text fields are internally classified as read-only, and read-only fields use a non-text-input semantics object (also used by SelectableText). Distinguishing disabled from selectable read-only fields is required. No PR.

title: [VPAT][A11y] obscure password field is missing state announcement
body: b/428628591 Run the https://github.com/flutter/flutter/tree/master/dev/a11y_assessments and navigate to textfield password demo EXPECTED RESULTS: Talk Back announces "Editing, password, 4 characters, secure, Edit box, Password" It is missing the `secure` state announcement
comment_summary: Comments investigate whether adding "secure" to the TalkBack announcement is the expected behavior; native Gmail does not include it. The question was raised whether this requires a PR or is achievable in the test assessment app alone.

title: [iOS] Incorrect text replacement highlight after spell check highlight
comment_summary: Comments provide a detailed engine-level analysis: the spell-check highlight for `omw` is triggered via a faster `UIKeyboardTaskQueue` path (not deferred like autocorrect), so `firstRectForRange:` is called before the Flutter framework has produced a new frame with the correct text layout for `omw`. The root cause is a timing issue where the iOS system requests the text rectangle before Flutter's rendering reflects the latest text state. Status: open, no fix or PR.

title: [iOS] Triple tap gesture to select paragraph causes context menu to "flicker"
[iOS] System context menu is missing the "AutoFill" button
comment_summary: One comment suggests `autofillHints` and platform channels for AutoFill, noting this is about system context menu items (UIMenuController), similar to the Share button issue.

title: TextEditingController.value.composing is giving wrong results in Web & iOS
comment_summary: Comments confirm the wrong composing range (not resetting to -1,-1 after IME commit) affects web and iOS, while Android and macOS work correctly. A likely regression from #161593 is identified, rooted in a behavioral difference between Chinese and Japanese IMEs. A workaround using `TextEditingController.addListener` instead of `TextField.onChanged` is confirmed to work on iOS. Discussion resolved toward updating `TextField.onChanged` documentation to clarify IME composing limitations and recommend `addListener` for robust IME handling.

title: App highlights / selects trailing whitespaces in a multi-line textfield.
comment_summary: Root cause not identified. Confirmed on Android, iOS, and desktop when maxLines or minLines is set; does not occur on web. Native Android and iOS apps do not highlight trailing whitespace. Routed to @LongCatIsLooong.

comment_summary: Comments confirm password manager autofill on web is a long-standing and broad issue, distinct from more specific prior reports. Bitwarden's keyboard shortcut autofill regressed between 3.32.8 and 3.35.2. Proton Pass does not work even after downgrading. The issue is still reproducible as of 3.41.4 and 3.41.6. The reporter links related issues #127694, #66356, and #156235. Some users report partial autofill issues specifically on iOS/iPadOS/Android Chrome browsers. Status: open, no fix or PR, team-web assigned.

title: SelectionArea doesn't seem to work with RichText Widget
comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: when using SelectionContainer.maybeOf(context) inside the same build method as SelectionArea, the context is an ancestor of SelectionArea and misses the registrar; wrapping RichText in a Builder that provides a descendant context fixes selectability. The documentation for SelectionArea and SelectableRegion should mention this RichText caveat. Issue assigned to @Renzo-Olivares; auto-assignment removal bot posted.

title: Add Theme-level option to align error text with the text field border (remove default indent/padding)
body:
### Use case

Currently, when using `TextField / TextFormField`, the error text (errorText) is rendered with an indent that matches the input/hint text padding. This behavior makes the error text look visually offset from the left border of the field.

At the moment, the only way to fix this is by overriding decoration properties (error widget, custom padding, etc.) on each individual field. This is tedious and redundant in larger apps. If the padding needs to be adjusted later, developers must revisit every field manually.



### Proposal

Introduce a theme-level property (e.g., in InputDecorationTheme) that controls the horizontal alignment/padding of error/helper text independently from the input/hint text.

Example API idea:
```
InputDecorationTheme(
  errorPadding: EdgeInsets.zero, // or Alignment options
  errorAlignment: TextAlign.start,
)
```

Why it matters:

Developers often want the input/hint text to be padded, but the error text to be flush with the text field border.

Thi
...[truncated]
comment_summary: A community contributor filed PR #177826 (not yet ready) to add an errorPadding or similar property. A workaround was documented using a prefix Padding widget combined with zero contentPadding. A related duplicate issue #181755 was also closed. Issue was briefly closed but appears to have been reopened; has partial patch label.

title: ☂️ [Scribe] Support Android stylus input
body:

https://developer.android.com/develop/ui/views/touch-and-input/stylus-input

title: [ios & Android] Wrong selection for wrapped Arabic text in SelectableText
comment_summary: A commenter identified that explicitly setting textDirection: TextDirection.rtl on the SelectableText fixes the issue for purely Arabic text, but breaks mixed Arabic/English content. The underlying cause is likely incorrect line-wrapping interaction with bidi text selection, related to existing issue #39755.

title: [Android 17] Improve CJKV physical keyboard accessibility
body:
### Background

Android 17 improves the accessibility of physical keyboards for Chinese, Japanese, Korean, and Vietnamese input. To take advantage of these improvements, Flutter needs to add more information to the accessibility events it sends to Android.

### Work

Flutter's [AccessibilityBridge.createTextChangedEvent](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/24e6fedcaf3b255d9cfe285e62ab921fb9ef46d9/engine/src/flutter/shell/platform/android/io/flutter/view/AccessibilityBridge.java#L2091) needs to be updated to call [`AccessibilityEvent.setTextChangeTypes`](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/view/accessibility/AccessibilityEvent#setTextChangeTypes(int)) with the following values: 


1. `AccessibilityEvent.TEXT_CHANGE_TYPE_IN_COMPOSITION`, if the changed text was in the composing region
2. `AccessibilityEvent.TEXT_CHANGE_TYPE_COMMITTED_BY_IME`, if the IME committed text, even if the text is unchanged
3. `AccessibilityEvent.TEXT_CHANGE_TYPE_CONVERSION_SUGGESTION_SELECTED_BY_
...[truncated]
comment_summary: One comment notes the Android 17 feature has landed and Flutter can begin adapting it, referencing internal bug b/444503345.

title: `RichText` should use `_SelectableTextContainerDelegate` for its text selection
body:
## Issue:
The `Text` widget internally uses [`_SelectableTextContainer`](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/3764b0b06817c8c25b5d6f1132b77bdd59d7c8ce/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/text.dart#L831) which is a special `SelectionContainer` for text widgets and it utilizes [`_SelectableTextContainerDelegate`](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/3764b0b06817c8c25b5d6f1132b77bdd59d7c8ce/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/text.dart#L963), a specialized `StaticSelectionContainerDelegate` for `Text` widgets.

At this time these classes are private so a user of `RichText` cannot use these classes. This results in a sub-par text selection experience without it when using `RichText`.

## Solution:
We should consider moving the logic contained in `_SelectableTextContainerDelegate` down to `RenderParagraph` so `RichText` may also inherit its benefits.

See discussion: https://discord.com/channels/608014603317936148/608021234516754444/1420095466582048781

title: iOS dictation inserts an unwanted leading space in empty Text Field (not reproducible on Android and native iOS apps)
comment_summary: Confirmed on iOS 18.1.1 stable 3.35.4 and master 3.37.0-pre. Related to an old issue comment at #13771. The leading space from iOS dictation is noted as a long-standing issue.

title: DropdownMenu does not clear or update selection when user types text
body:
### Use case

Currently, the `DropdownMenu` widget allows the user to:

1. Select an option from the dropdown list (triggering `onSelected`).
2. Type arbitrary text into the input field to perform searching or filtering.
3. Tap out of focus and leave the arbitrary text visible without selecting anything or triggering any callbacks.

This is confusing as the text field is the primary feedback for which item has been selected yet it no longer reflects that. See [example](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/DropdownMenu-class.html#material.DropdownMenu.1).

### Proposal

I believe the most comprehensive solution would be:

```dart
DropdownMenu(
    autoSelect: true,       // Automatically select highlighted menu item if focus lost
    incompleteTextBehavior: // If no highlighted menu item, or autoSelect: false
        DropdownIncompleteTextBehavior.ignore, // Leave text as-is,  don't emit (current behavior)
     or DropdownIncompleteTextBehavior.emit,   // Leave text as-is,  emit `on
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the UX issue with video. Issue #175090 was noted as related. A commenter argues `ignore` should not be deprecated since typing without selecting is a valid pattern.

title: iOS: Verification code autofill duplicates when `CFBundleLocalizations` is defined as `zh_CN` in  `Info.plist`
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a Flutter app with a verification code input field.
The minimal reproducible sample project is attached — you can view repo directly [here](https://github.com/HuangRunHua/flutter_duplicate_sms_code_demo)
 or download it [sms_demo.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22861450/sms_demo.zip).

2. In `ios/Runner/Info.plist`, add the following configuration:
```xml
<key>CFBundleLocalizations</key>
<array>
    <string>zh_CN</string>
</array>
```

3. Build and run the app on a physical iOS device.

4. Send an SMS or email verification code to the device.

5. Tap the autofill suggestion bar above the keyboard (iOS autofill feature).


> Detail video: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8f008e2d-ecc0-4ddb-8529-4197ac21b304



### Actual results

- The verification code autofills twice, or duplicates in the text field. (For example, a code "123456" becomes "123456123456".)
- Removing the <string>zh_CN</string> section from `Info.plist` immediate
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Issue is iOS-specific; only reproducible with Simplified Chinese system language and `zh_CN` in `CFBundleLocalizations`. A SwiftUI reproduction did not trigger the duplication, suggesting a Flutter-side root cause. Comments identify the failure as occurring only via the 'Change keyboard' dialog SMS autofill flow on specific devices (iPhone XR, iPhone 12 Pro Max with iOS 18.6.2) but not on iPhone 16 Pro with iOS 26. No fix or PR; stalled pending further investigation.

title: [Android] Scribe broken with `TextInputType.none` and S-Pen
body:
### DESCRIPTION

There are two related issues when using an **Android stylus (like Samsung S-Pen)** with **TextField** and **CupertinoTextField**.

Issue 1 — **Keyboard opens unexpectedly**

When directly scribbling (handwriting) on a TextField or CupertinoTextField using the stylus, the software keyboard opens automatically, even though the expectation is that stylus input should not trigger the keyboard.


Issue 2 — **Scribble input inconsistency**

When using _**keyboardType: TextInputType.none**_, stylus scribble input (handwriting recognition) does not work initially on TextField or CupertinoTextField.

However, if the user first scribbles on another text field that does not have TextInputType.none, then returns to the TextField or CupertinoTextField with TextInputType.none, scribble input starts working there too.

In other words:

Initially, stylus scribble fails on text fields that set keyboardType: TextInputType.none.

After scribbling once in a normal text field (without Text
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the keyboard-opens-during-stylus-scribble issue is unexpected behavior; using the stylus should not show the software keyboard. The issue is related to the broader Android stylus support umbrella issue #175886 and the handwriting input implementation from #115607. A team comment escalated priority to P1 because keyboard appearing during stylus input is unexpected. No fix or PR identified; status is open.

title: Improve the state of native browser context menu on mobile web when using `SelectionArea`/`SelectableRegion`
body:
Native browser context menus on mobile web, specifically Android and iOS, do not function well when using `SelectionArea`/`SelectableRegion` since `PlatformSelectableRegionContextMenu` only supports right clicks.

Current issues:

- Hidden DOM element used to show native browser context menu is unaware of selected text in mobile scenarios such as touches. It is only aware of selected text in the case of a right-click. This means items like "copy", and other items that depend on knowledge of the selected text do not work.
- Native browser context menu is shown at the wrong location, or not shown at all.

iOS

Android

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dd6e1533-6047-4a8e-8354-d9ff4f1d46a6

title: TextField line painter
body:
### Use case

I need to inject a specific painter for each TextField line.

For example, I need to give a radius to TextDecoration.underline but there is no way.

### Proposal

```dart
TextField(
  style: TextStyle(
    line: Paint()
      ..color = Colors.red
      ..style = PaintingStyle.fill,
  ),
)
```
comment_summary: Comments confirm none of the linked issues address applying a custom painter to each text field line. The request remains open.

Focus any TextField → keyboard shows but arrows don't work
Perform hot restart (r in terminal)
After full restart completes, keyboard arrows suddenly wor
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue is not exclusive to Chromecast; some Phillips and Samsung TV devices are also affected, and Google can reject Flutter TV apps during review because of it. A community member who tried to fix InputConnectionAdaptor.java was unsuccessful. The issue was discussed on the Flutter Discord (hacker-android channel). No fix; open.

title: The framework should provide a mechanism to change the paragraph spacing of text
body:
## Issue
There is no mechanism to change the paragraph spacing of text in the framework.

## Proposal
`TextStyle` should add a `paragraphSpacing` member, similar to `letterSpacing` and `wordSpacing`, that allows for changing the additional space between paragraphs.

In the code below:
```dart
Text('Hello world\nGoodbye world', style: TextStyle(paragraphSpacing: 2.0))
```

`TextStyle(paragraphSpacing: 2.0)` , would add 2 pixels of space between the paragraphs separated by the line terminator `\n`.

## Use case
Accessibility would benefit greatly from this feature, as certain specifications such as [WCAG](https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG21/#visual-presentation) on the web have certain requirements regarding the paragraph spacing of text.

title: `SliverMainAxisGroup` causes `showOnScreen` calls from descendants of a pinned `SliverPersistentHeader` to reset the scroll position
body:
### Steps to reproduce

(tested on main branch from 2 weeks ago, on iPhone simulator (iPhone 16, iOS 18.0)

1. See the example code sample: https://gist.github.com/kekland/0e20282864fa1ba0f08bbfa46aaa4e4b
2. In `App.build`, there are two variants of the same UI: one with usage of `SliverMainAxisGroup` in the `CustomScrollView`, one without.
3. Uncomment line 12 that builds the UI with the `SliverMainAxisGroup`, scroll down a little bit, and tap on the text field in the header
4. Observe that the scroll position has been reset to 0.0
5. Change the UI to the one without the `SliverMainAxisGroup`
6. Perform the same actions, and observe that the scroll position stays the same

### Expected results

Scroll position shouldn't reset when `showOnScreen` is called from a descendant of `SliverPersistentHeader` when it's located in a `SliverMainAxisGroup`.

### Actual results

Scroll position gets reset.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

https://gist.github.com/kekl
...[truncated]
comment_summary: The reporter identified the root cause: `RenderSliverMainAxisGroup` does not report `maxScrollObstructionExtent` in its geometry, so `isPinned` is `false` when calling `getOffsetToReveal`, causing `showOnScreen` to calculate the leading offset as 0 and scroll to the top. A simple fix is to propagate `maxScrollObstructionExtent` from child slivers, but a commenter noted this could cause non-pinned children in a `SliverMainAxisGroup` to not scroll into view. PR #178809 was proposed as a potential fix and is awaiting review from @yiiim.

title: [A11y] User's bold text preference not respected if `TextSpan.style` has a custom font weight
body:
iOS and Android have a system setting to bold text. This can be used to make text easier to read for accessibility purposes.

Flutter exposes this setting as [`PlatformDispatcher.accessibilityFeatures.boldText`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/dart-ui/AccessibilityFeatures/boldText.html), [`MediaQueryData.boldText`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/MediaQueryData/boldText.html), and [`MediaQuery.boldTextOf(context)`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/MediaQuery/boldTextOf.html). If this setting is on, `Text` and `Text.rich` should be bolded automatically, even if `Text`'s or `Text.rich`'s `style` specifies a custom font weight.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the code sample below on any platform (the app modifies `MediaQueryData` to mimic a system where the user has turned on bolded text)
1. The text should be bolded. However, it is not.

### Expected results

The text `Hello world` should be bolded. The user's preference for bold text should override the `TextSpan`'s fon
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Only a cc to @Renzo-Olivares and @LongCatIsLooong; no substantive new information.

title: Provide a method for `InlineSpan` to override the styles of its children
body:
## Issue
I'd like to be able to override the styles of all children of an `InlineSpan` tree. Currently each `InlineSpan` has a local representation of its `TextStyle` and `InlineSpan` children, so you cannot simply wrap an `InlineSpan` with another `InlineSpan` and expect the parents `InlineSpan.style` to override it's child's. This is by design, since nested spans can define their own `TextStyle`s.

## Proposal
`InlineSpan` should have some mechanism to override the `TextStyle`s of its `InlineSpan` children. Currently `textScaler` is able to penetrate an entire `InlineSpan` tree because the `InlineSpan.build` method has a `textScaler` member. We can take a similar approach and add individual styling members to the `build` method as needed or we can add a `style` argument that is passed down to all children.

## Use case
For accessibility use cases like updating text spacing settings to match certain standards like WCAG, it is required to be able to change the spacing settings of all t
...[truncated]
comment_summary: One comment asks if the feature was already merged; the team confirmed it was not and the issue should stay open.

title: The framework should apply `paragraphSpacingOverride` to its text
body:
## Issue
As of https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/172915, `paragraphSpacingOverride` has been made available through `MediaQuery.maybeOfParagraphSpacingOverride`, and `MediaQueryData.paragraphSpacingOverride`. This is an API added to support accessibility use-cases related to text spacing, such as matching WCAG specifications on the web. The framework at this time does not utilize this value, but makes it accessible to developers to use in their own applications.

## Proposal
The framework should apply the `paragraphSpacingOverride` to its own text and components, so the framework can be compliant with specifications like WCAG out of the box. When applying `paragraphSpacingOverride` to our framework components we should consider scenarios where we should not apply it, for example in buttons, headers, navigations destinations, and likely other UI components.

title: Add AutoValidateMode.delayed
body:
### Use case

The current values for AutoValidateMode all suffer from the fact that the user is presented an error message when he hasn't done anything wrong:

  - on user interaction: if the user starts typing in a textfield that requires minimum 10 characters, displaying the error as soon as he types is questionable UX
  - on unfocus: if the user refocus the field and correct his mistake, the error message is still shown, since there is no unfocus event. 

Ultimately it's not possible to show an error message when the user is "done" because we don't know when that's the case. However we can approximate it with a delay.

### Proposal

My proposal is to add `AutoValidateMode.delayed`, so that validation happens after a delay after the last user change.

### Non Proposal

Custom validation mode / user defined validation modes.

title: KeyEvent doesn't expose modifier flags from NSEvent on macOS, breaking synthetic keyboard events from external apps
body:
### Title
KeyEvent doesn't expose modifier flags from NSEvent on macOS, breaking synthetic keyboard events from external apps

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a Flutter macOS app with keyboard event handling
2. Use an external app (like Raycast, Alfred, or any app) that sends synthetic keyboard events with modifier flags (e.g., Cmd+V to paste)
3. Add logging in both Swift (NSEvent level) and Dart (KeyEvent level) to observe modifier flags
4. Trigger a synthetic keyboard event with modifiers (e.g., use Raycast's emoji picker and select an emoji, which sends Cmd+V)
5. Observe that the NSEvent has correct modifierFlags, but Flutter's KeyEvent doesn't expose them
6. HardwareKeyboard.instance.isMetaPressed returns false because no physical Command key was pressed

### Expected results

Flutter's KeyEvent should expose the modifier flags that are present in the underlying NSEvent, independent of HardwareKeyboard state. When an NSEvent arrives with modifierFlags containing .command (0x10000
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Related issue #153907 linked. A commenter notes that third-party speech-to-text apps (Wispr Flow, Spokenly) that send synthetic key events are also broken by the same root cause. No fix proposed.

The cursor should move between Arabic characters normally without splitting or entering inside the glyph.

Editing RTL Arabic text should work the same way it works in normal native text editors.

This causes the character to visually split and makes text editing nearly impossible.

The issue happens even without custom fonts and on a clean Flutter setup.

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause traced by @LongCatIsLooong to SkParagraph's getRectsForRange returning incorrect bounding boxes when a parenthesis or '>' character precedes a newline in RTL text — the bounding box for the parenthesis is abnormally large due to font/mirroring interactions, causing the cursor to appear inside the glyph. Linked to existing issue #117140 and active PR #178258; an upstream engine/Skia fix is likely needed.

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments clarify that the onDateSubmitted and onDateSaved callbacks only fire for valid DateTime values; when acceptEmptyDate: true and the field is cleared, null is not passed to the callbacks. Proposal is to change the callback signatures to ValueChanged<DateTime?>?. Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.38.3 and master. A commenter noted it may relate to the fact that calendar-based date pickers cannot be cleared.

title: `isVisible` should be public on `SystemContextMenuController`
body:
### Use case

`SystemContextMenuController.show` will hide and show the context menu if `targetRect` differs by even a fraction of a pixel.

We are building a text field that can be visually stretched by the user (similar to Liquid Glass), and would like control over this behavior, as not every transformation should lead to the system context menu disappearing and appearing again.

We would like to work around this by calling `show()` only if the menu is not currently being displayed.
However, we don't have any safe way to do that, as we cannot know from the outside whether the menu is currently being shown, since `isVisible` is marked `@visibleForTesting`.

### Proposal

Make `isVisible` a public getter, so it's safe to consume and would constitute a breakage if removed at a later point.
comment_summary: Comments note a design challenge: the system context menu can be dismissed by the platform itself, so Flutter's `isVisible` may be stale. Passthrough views may be a prerequisite to give Flutter full ownership of visibility state.

title: Semi-transparent keyboard on iOS 26 reveals widgets that do not draw under it
body:
> [!NOTE]
> ### Workaround 1
