>
> ```xml
> <key>UIDesignRequiresCompatibility</key>
> <true/>
> ```
>
> The `UIDesignRequiresCompatibility` property is a temporary workaround until Flutter fixes this issue. You will need to remove this property in the future.
>
> For more details, see: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/BundleResources/Information-Property-List/UIDesignRequiresCompatibility

### Steps to reproduce


Platforms
iOS

When you click on the text input box in the BottomSheet Modal, the color around the rounded corner at the top of the semi-transparent keyboard that pops up is incorrect


### Expected results

.

### Actual results

When using the keyboard inside an 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: iOS 26's semi-transparent keyboard means Flutter still renders a black (or modal-barrier-colored) background under the keyboard instead of showing underlying content, because widgets like Scaffold and BottomSheet move up to avoid the keyboard inset rather than extending below it. Affects iOS 26 only; iOS 18 is fine. The fix requires changes to multiple widgets (Scaffold.resizeToAvoidBottomInset, CupertinoPageScaffold, CupertinoTabScaffold, SearchAnchor, BottomSheet) and is considered a dedicated project, not a hotfix. Workaround: set UIDesignRequiresCompatibility = true in Info.plist (temporary), or avoid mismatched transparent/opaque colors on the modal and its host.

title: SelectionListenerNotifier selection.range incorrect when selecting over WidgetSpans
body:
### Steps to reproduce

Adding a `WidgetSpan` to a `Text.rich` that's in a `SelectionArea` will cause a `SelectionListenerNotifier`'s `selection.range.endOffset` to adjust whenever the text-selection goes over the `WidgetSpans`. This prevents us from being able to compute what the selected text is, based on the offsets of the `selection.range`.

When you remove the `WidgetSpan`, everything works as expected. Placing a `SelectionContainer.disabled()` in the `WidgetSpan` does not fix the issue. Replacing the `FlutterLogo` with a `SizedBox.shrink` does not fix the issue.

### Expected results

A text-selection that includes `WidgetSpan`s should not affect the `endOffset` of a `selection.range`. 

### Actual results

A text-selection that includes `WidgetSpan`s does affect the `endOffset` of a `selection.range`.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'dart:async';

import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter/rendering.dart';

Futu
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: _SelectableTextContainerDelegate._calculateLocalRange does not account for discontinuous selection when non-selectable WidgetSpans break the selectable range, especially when selecting backwards. Issue only affects backward selection; forward selection produces reasonable ranges. No PR linked.

title: setState when SelectionArea has selection and a WidgetSpan causes flickering behavior.
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Place a `WidgetSpan` in a `SelectionArea`.
2. Select some text.
3. Call `setState(() {})`. Notice that the context menu disappears and that the selection starts flickering.

This behavior only exists if there is a `WidgetSpan` in the `Text.rich`. Removing the `WidgetSpan` resolves the issue.

This behavior is even more of an issue if we remove the `await Future.delayed(Duration(seconds: 1))` in the listener. Since we call `setState((){})` the same frame that the selection is created, you don't even see a text-selection visually when you attempt to create one.

### Expected results

Calling `setState()` should not cause the context menu to disappear or the selection to flicker/disappear.

### Actual results

Calling `setState()` when a `WidgetSpan` is in a `Text.rich` causes the context menu to disappear and the selection to flicker/disappear.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'dart:async';

import 'package:flutter/
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: non-const WidgetSpan instances are treated as new objects on rebuild, causing RenderParagraph's internal _SelectableFragments to be disposed and recreated, which resets the selection. Using const WidgetSpan avoids the issue. The same behavior affects SelectableText.rich with dynamic strings.

title: Design doc - Decoupling design from text
body:
### Document Link

https://flutter.dev/go/decoupling-design-from-text

### What problem are you solving?

This design doc aims to evaluate the framework's text and text input for areas of improvement. This is especially relevant due to the plan to decouple the `Material` and `Cupertino` libraries from the framework.

This document discusses:
* The future of text selection widgets (`SelectableRegion`) in the core framework after decoupling design libraries from the framework.
* Whether the framework should move/implement text selection UI from design libraries like `Android`/`Material` styled selection handles, `iOS`/`Cupertino` styled selection handles, `Android`/`Material` styled text selection context menu, and `iOS`/`Cupertino` styled text selection context menu in the widgets layer.
* The out of the box experience for `EditableText`.
comment_summary: One comment notes #172930 may be related.

title: Invalid Unicode in TextEditingController's `text` Crashes the App on iOS
comment_summary: The root cause is that Dart's JSON encoder escapes lone surrogates (e.g., `\uDFFF`) but iOS's `NSJSONSerialization` cannot decode that escaped form, causing a fatal crash in `FlutterJSONMessageCodec` on iOS and macOS; Android and web do not crash but emit console errors. The iOS embedder needs to be updated to handle Dart's lone-surrogate escape pattern. A secondary proposal is for the framework to assert on malformed strings at the Dart level rather than attempting to pass them through to platform services, with a note that discarding surrogates may trigger spurious `onChanged` callbacks. Currently routed to the iOS team; no PR or fix landed as of the report.

title: Pressing the keyboard emoji button and changing focus in a text field at the same time causes a crash on iOS
comment_summary: A commenter confirms the crash reproduces in a plain UIKit app with two UITextFields using the same repro sequence, demonstrating this is an underlying iOS bug (UIViewControllerHierarchyInconsistency in TUIEmojiSearchInputViewController), not a Flutter-specific issue. Confirmed specific to Japanese keyboard (not English), reproducible on iPhone 13 and 15. Status: open, tentatively P2.

title: SearchAnchor View pops unexpectedly while using Web version in Desktop mode on Android
comment_summary: The team acknowledged the bug but indicated it is low priority because Android Desktop Mode changes the user-agent and other browser behaviors in ways Flutter's adaptive framework does not account for, and the recommended path is using Flutter's built-in adaptive features rather than relying on desktop-mode browser heuristics.

title: RenderEditable.getBoxesForSelection gets selection width for longest line width
Action.overridable cannot be overridden by a DoNothingAction
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Wrap a `TextField` with `Actions` and use `DoNothingAction` for all text editing `Intent`s (see code sample)
2. Run it, type in some text.
3. Do  some text editing related shortcuts like `arrow left`, `arrow up`, `ctrl + A`, `backspace` etc.

### Expected results

1. `arrow up`, `arrow down`, `page up`, `page down`, `home` and `end` or in other words `ExtendSelectionVerticallyToAdjacentPageIntent` and `ExtendSelectionVerticallyToAdjacentLineIntent` do nothing
2. for all other intents no assertion errors is thrown

### Actual results

1. `ExtendSelectionVerticallyToAdjacentPageIntent` and `ExtendSelectionVerticallyToAdjacentLineIntent` moves caret to the start/end of the text
2. When all other intents are executed, the following assertion error is thrown (example for `SelectAllTextIntent`):
```dart
════════ Exception caught by services library ══════════════════════════════════
The following assertion was thrown while processing the key message handler:
Select
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments identify two distinct bugs. First, `DoNothingAction` cannot override `Action.overridable` because `Actions._castAction` does an `is Action<T>` check that fails for `DoNothingAction` (Dart's reified generics are invariant). Workaround: create a typed `MyDoNothingAction<T extends Intent>`. Second, `ExtendSelectionVerticallyToAdjacentLineIntent` and `ExtendSelectionVerticallyToAdjacentPageIntent` cannot be individually overridden because the underlying action in `editable_text.dart` is typed to `DirectionalCaretMovementIntent`; PR #180883 may have addressed this. A commenter proposes fixing `_castAction` to allow `Action<Intent>` to pass the type check. Status: has partial fix in #180883; root `DoNothingAction` issue open.

title: AutofillGroup does not autofill all related fields
body:
### Issue Detail

When using AutofillGroup with multiple related input fields, autofill only populates the initially focused field. Other fields within the same AutofillGroup are not automatically filled, even when they have valid autofill hints and are expected to be completed together by the platform.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Save form data on the device (any multi-field form)
2. Open the app and focus on one field
3. Select an autofill suggestion
4. Observe that only the focused field is populated

### Expected results

When selecting an autofill suggestion:

All relevant fields within the same AutofillGroup should be autofilled

Autofill should behave consistently with native platform expectations

### Actual results

Only the currently focused field is autofilled

Other fields inside the same AutofillGroup remain empty

User must manually enter remaining values or click on each field to auto fill remaining fields in same 

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</su
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments clarify that the issue only reproduces in release mode on web when `SemanticsBinding.instance.ensureSemantics()` is called in `main()`; without that call, autofill fills all fields correctly. A team member confirmed the framework does create the form with all fields and considers this a bug. The original reporter confirmed the issue persists on master channel. Status: open, being tracked by team-web.

title: iOS App Hang FlutterPlatformPlugin getClipboardData blocks main thread for 2+ seconds
body:
## Steps to reproduce

1. Enable Universal Clipboard across Apple devices (e.g., iPhone + Mac)
2. Copy content on one device (e.g., Mac)
3. Before clipboard sync completes, tap on a `TextField` in the Flutter app on iPhone
4. App freezes for 2+ seconds

**Note:** This issue is timing-dependent and intermittent, making it difficult to reproduce on demand. However, it occurs regularly in production environments and is captured by crash/ANR monitoring tools (Sentry).

**Affected iOS versions:** iOS 14+ (confirmed on iOS 26.1)
**Affected devices:** Various iPhones (confirmed on iPhone 18,2)

## Analysis

The root cause appears to be in `FlutterPlatformPlugin.mm` line 411 where `getClipboardData:` calls `[UIPasteboard string]` synchronously on the main thread.

`UIPasteboard` operations can block when:

1. **Universal Clipboard** - Syncing clipboard content from other devices requires network operations
2. **Content type coercion** - Converting clipboard formats triggers inter-process commu
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments clarify that the main-thread block on `[UIPasteboard string]` in `getClipboardData` is the reported path, but since latest Flutter uses the system context menu by default this codepath may no longer be hit for paste. A comment notes `FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm:1339` has a similar blocking `hasStrings` call for text input paste, which iOS handles with a progress indicator for slow network clipboard syncs. Reporter confirms no Sentry events after upgrading Flutter, suggesting the system context menu resolved it. A comment notes `SelectableText`/`SelectionArea` may still be affected since they never call `getClipboardData` for paste (so that path is moot for them). Status: effectively resolved for most cases via system context menu; lower-priority to fix the old path.

title: `Actions.find` et.al should return `Action<Intent>` instead of `Action<T>`.
comment_summary: One comment suggests deprecating `maybeFind`/`find` and replacing with new methods returning `Action<Intent>`, proposing names like `Actions.of`, `Actions.lookup`, or `Actions.get`.

comment_summary: The crash is a failed assertion in EdgeDraggingAutoScroller._scroll triggered when the drag target (the TableView inside a CustomScrollView) is larger than the enclosing scrollable, which violates an invariant in the selection auto-scroll logic. Confirmed reproducible on both stable 3.38.x and master. title: [SelectionArea] An incorrect context menu popped up when SelectionArea was nested
comment_summary: Root cause explained: nested SelectionArea regions each register a RawGestureDetector with the same recognizers; when a right-click is held long enough, the BaseTapGestureRecognizer exceeds its deadline and fires indiscriminately, triggering both the nested and outer menus. Wrapping with SelectionContainer.disabled also fails to prevent this. Confirmed on stable 3.38.7 and master 3.41.0.

title: [iOS] Autocorrect highlight does not go away if you scroll
body:
### Steps to reproduce 1. In a text field, type a misspelled word like `grept`. Don't accept the autocorrect suggestion. 2. Do a swipe gesture to scroll. ### Expected results Scrolling accepts the autocorrect suggestion. The autocorrect highlight is no longer visible. ### Actual results Scrolling accepts the autocorrect suggestion, but the autocorrect highlight remains visible at the text field's original location, which is clearly incorrect as the text field scrolls away. ### Code sample
Code sample ```dart import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; void main() { runApp( MaterialApp( home: Scaffold( body: ListView.builder( itemCount: 100, itemBuilder: (context, index) => TextField(), ), ), ), ); } ```

Screenshots / Video demonstration

A commenter identified the native fix mechanism: `UIScrollView` sends a private `NSNotification` on scroll that causes UIKit to dismiss autocorrect, and Flutter does not replicate this. Status: open, awaiting engine/iOS fix.

title: [Windows] TextField Cannot Completely Disable System IME
body:
### Use case

Flutter's TextField widget lacks proper control over system Input Method Editors (IME). While developers can configure keyboard layouts and input formatting, there's no reliable way to prevent IME activation - especially problematic for CJK languages where IMEs automatically engage.



### Proposal

Current properties like keyboardType, textInputAction, and inputFormatters only control keyboard appearance and text filtering, but cannot prevent IME composition states. This breaks applications requiring direct keyboard input only (OTP/PIN entry, terminals, command interfaces).

Numeric-only fields still trigger IME composition
Real-time validation disrupted by IME intermediate states
Inconsistent behavior across platforms
No workaround for CJK language environments
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue on Windows with CJK IME (specifically Chinese input on Windows 10/11 stable 3.35.6). A third-party plugin workaround exists (`force_english_ime: ^0.0.3`), and one user resolved it via direct system API calls. No upstream fix or PR has been proposed; the maintainer asked for a minimal repro of numeric fields triggering IME composition but received no structured response. Status is open with no fix, pending a clearer repro.

title: Add a Cupertino version of SelectableText
body:
While looking to fix up the last Cupertino test (test/cupertino/text_selection_test.dart) in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/181634  that had a cross import to Material, I ended up being blocked, because that test uses `SelectableText.rich` and there is no Cupertino equivalent of SelectableText yet.

We probably need a Cupertino version of this, to fix that test.

Part of https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/177415

cc @justinmc We can probably remove the Cupertino tests from the umbrella issue once https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/181634 lands, and replace it with this issue instead, since that is the only remaining point?

Moving Cupertino SelectableText tests to `test/material/cupertino/` was agreed as a short-term fix.

title: RTL TextField breaks when inserting emojis between existing emojis
body:
### Steps to reproduce

When using a TextField with RTL text direction, inserting an emoji between two existing emojis sometimes breaks the text, showing ? character.

### Expected results

Inserting emojis between existing emojis in an RTL TextField should render correctly.

### Actual results

The inserted emoji breaks the text and may render as ?.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
TextField(textDirection: TextDirection.rtl)
```

</details>


### Screenshots or Video

<details open>
<summary>Screenshots / Video demonstration</summary>

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/706052f5-2309-4bdf-a44b-6ab05848f8a7

</details>


### Logs

<details colose><summary>Logs</summary>

```console
n/a
```

</details>


### Flutter Doctor output

<details colose><summary>Doctor output</summary>

```console
n/a
```

</details>
comment_summary: Comments identify the root cause: on macOS and Linux, dynamically changing `textDirection` during IME composition triggers a `wstring_convert::to_bytes` exception in the engine (built with `-fno-exceptions`), causing an abort; on Android the IME composing state is invalidated, producing `?`/replacement characters. iOS and web cannot reproduce. A crash log from macOS shows the engine decoding an invalid JSON surrogate pair. A comment in #183112 indicates this cannot be fixed in the framework and requires fixes in each affected embedder (macOS, Linux). Status: needs engine/embedder fix.

title: [web, desktop] SelectableText - right click selects a word
comment_summary: Root cause identified: the underlying EditableText in SelectableText creates a text input connection that interferes with right-click gesture handling; on web, the browser's contextmenu event on the hidden textarea triggers a word-selection that propagates as updateEditingValue, bypassing the framework gesture. A contributor (ishaquehassan) proposed and opened PR #183110 overriding onSecondaryTap in _SelectableTextSelectionGestureDetectorBuilder; the team clarified that macOS/iOS native behavior (NSTextView) does select a word on right-click so macOS should be preserved. The web path cannot be fixed by disabling the input connection because it is needed for keyboard shortcuts and the native context menu; an engine-level fix to prevent contextmenu events on the hidden textarea is needed but not yet implemented.

title: Don't duplicate Semantics logic in TextField and CupertinoTextField
body:
We should move all possible Semantics logic out of the design languages and into EditableText, to be DRY and to make sure that direct users of EditableText can easily get nitty gritty semantics details right.

## Background

Currently, EditableText only includes a Semantics widget for toolbar operations:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/018a57179c12c7d4ee1fb225b4759d2c05047b20/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/editable_text.dart#L5800-L5805

Meanwhile [in TextField](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/018a57179c12c7d4ee1fb225b4759d2c05047b20/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/text_field.dart#L1795-L1799) and [in CupertinoTextField](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/018a57179c12c7d4ee1fb225b4759d2c05047b20/packages/flutter/lib/src/cupertino/text_field.dart#L1630-L1632), there is Semantics logic for gestures and focus that is relevant to all users of text input.

## Recommendation

We should move all possible common semantics logic out of TextField/CupertinoTextField and 
...[truncated]

title: TextField doesn't get focus back after its route becomes the top route because of a route pop
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Use a Linux/macOS/Windows. (Android/iOS doesn't have this problem)
2. Create two routes.
3. Create a `TextField` in the first route with `autofocus: true`.
4. Push the second route.
5. Pop the second route.
6. Notice the `TextField` didn't get focus back. If you listen to the `primaryFocus`, you will notice that the current primary focus will be a Focus Scope, which is supposed to pass that focus to the `TextField`.

### Expected results

The `TextField` gets its focus back on Linux and macOS, just like on Android.

### Actual results

Instead of passing the primary focus to its descendant `TextField`, which originally had the primary focus before the second route's push, it got the primary focus itself:

```
primaryFocus: FocusScopeNode#74736(_ModalScopeState<dynamic> Focus Scope [PRIMARY FOCUS])(context: _FocusScopeWithExternalFocusNode, PRIMARY FOCUS)
```

### Workaround 1

```dart
FocusScopeNode? _inheritedScope;

void _onFocusScopeFocused() {
    if (_in
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue is iOS/Android-safe but affects macOS, Windows, and Linux: after Navigator.pop, the `FocusScopeNode` of the modal scope receives primary focus instead of delegating it back to the `TextField`. Testing with `EditableText` alone reproduces the issue, confirming it is not a `TextField`-specific bug. Two workarounds are provided in the issue body itself. Status: open, no PR, affects macOS/Windows/Linux only.

title: SelectableText and SelectionArea do not work correctly when calling document.execCommand("copy") unless you right-click
body:
### Steps to reproduce

To work around some clipboard sandboxing issues when embedding DevTools inside VS Code, we have to detect `Cmd+C` and call `execCommand("copy")` for the selected contents to be copied to the clipboard.

In some cases this works, and in some other cases it does not.

At https://github.com/flutter/devtools/issues/9636#issuecomment-3936560379, @Renzo-Olivares noted that some of this handling involves text in a hidden element, but it is only updated when you right-click. This means if you do not right-click, then `execCommand("copy")` does not work, but if you do, then it works.

In the example video:

- I select the SelectionArea text (which is a `Text` inside a `SelectionArea`)
- I click the button that triggers `document.execCommand("copy")` (from `package:web`)
- I try to paste the contents, but nothing pastes
- Next, I select the SelectionArea text (which is a `Text` inside a `SelectionArea`)
- I right-click to show the context menu (but do not use it)
- I clic
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: Flutter's hidden element for clipboard support is only populated when the user right-clicks, because that code path is gated on a contextmenu event in `_platform_selectable_region_context_menu_web.dart`; `execCommand("copy")` and Edit-menu copy fire no such event. Comments propose listening to the browser's native `copy` event to update the hidden div for any copy trigger, which a contributor confirmed works in jsfiddle and that `execCommand` also fires the `copy` event. Renzo-Olivares agreed the `copy`-event approach is reasonable and invited a contributor to implement it, but the contributor got stuck on running web tests on Windows and understanding the engine-side hidden-input code. Status: no PR opened; waiting on a volunteer or team member to implement the fix.

title: [BUG] [WEB] `KeyUpEvent` is synthesized too eagerly when combining the Meta key with multiple non-modifier keys
body:
## Description

While PR #180692 successfully fixed issue #162305 (where the web embedder synthesized key up events too eagerly for normal typing / game control), a limitation remains when the `Meta` key (`Cmd` on Mac) is involved.

Because the key guard mechanism in the web embedder is still required and active when the Meta key is pressed, pressing multiple non-modifier keys simultaneously alongside it causes the guard to misbehave. Only the last pressed non-modifier key sends repeated events, which leads the framework to eventually (and incorrectly) synthesize key up events for the other physically held non-modifier keys.

*Note: This specific behavior was isolated to the `Meta` key during debugging in the PR, rather than all modifier keys.*

## Steps to reproduce

Using the `KeyboardListener` or `Focus.onKeyEvent` APIs on a Web build (JS or WASM):

1. Press and hold the `Cmd` (Meta) key.
2. Press and hold `[j]`.
3. Press and hold `[k]`.
4. Wait for over 1.5 seconds (or `> _kKeydown
...[truncated]

title: SelectionArea shows unexpected black line under overflowed text when focused
comment_summary: A single substantive comment confirms the black-line rendering artifact and labels it for the framework team to investigate the text selection layout logic. No root cause, PR, or workaround is provided. Status is open, newly triaged.

title: [macOS] TextField doesn't receive text from apps that simulate keyboard events (e.g. MacWhisper dictation)
body:
## Steps to reproduce

1. Create a minimal Flutter app with a `TextField` on macOS (see sample below)
2. Install [MacWhisper](https://goodsnooze.gumroad.com/l/macwhisper) and enable its Dictation feature
3. Focus the Flutter `TextField`
4. Use MacWhisper dictation to speak text
5. **No text appears** in the TextField

## Expected behavior

Text should appear in the TextField, as it does in native macOS apps (TextEdit, Safari, Notes, etc.).

## Actual behavior

No text appears at all. The TextField remains empty.

## Additional context

- **macOS built-in dictation** (Fn+Fn / Globe key) **works correctly** in Flutter TextFields
- MacWhisper dictation **works correctly** in native macOS apps (TextEdit, Safari, etc.)
- This also reproduces with a **vanilla Flutter project** — no custom text input handling needed

This suggests that macOS built-in dictation uses the `NSTextInputClient` protocol directly (`insertText:replacementRange:`), which Flutter's `FlutterTextInputPlugin` handles. Mac
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: MacWhisper uses CGEvent keyboard simulation (goes through NSResponder chain), while Flutter's FlutterTextInputPlugin only handles NSTextInputClient protocol messages. A commenter linked #178609 as potentially causal. A community workaround using AXUIElement accessibility API insertion is described. Moved to team-macos.

title: let devs decide when to recalculate selection region toolbar position
body: ### Use case SelectableRegion toolbar position not change after scroll, if at selection start it show at selection top, if it is scroll up to invisible, it will not change position, and there is no public api to programlly re caculate toolbar position ### Proposal for better text selection user experience, should give the ability to let devs decide when to recaculate selection region toolbar position
comment_summary: Comments confirm root cause: `SelectionOverlay._selectionEndpoints` is computed once and not refreshed on scroll. A contributor raised a PR. Labeled for team review.

title: [Windows] External voice dictation tools (e.g. Typeless, Windows Voice Typing) cannot detect Flutter TextFields
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Install an external voice dictation tool (tested with [Typeless](https://typeless.com/) and a Whisper-based tool).
2. Run a Flutter desktop app with a standard `TextField`.
3. Focus the `TextField`, activate dictation, and speak.
4. The tool **cannot detect the TextField** and fails to inject text.

### Expected results

The dictation tool should detect the focused `TextField` via Windows UI Automation (UIA) and inject text directly — as it does with native Win32, WPF, UWP, Electron, and other desktop frameworks.

### Actual results

The dictation tool **cannot locate any editable text control** in the Flutter window via UIA.

- **Typeless** falls back to a "Copy last transcription" popup — it found the window but no injectable text field.
- **Whisper-based tools** similarly fail to inject into the focused field.

Windows built-in Voice Typing (`Win+H`) and Voice Access likely have the same problem, since they rely on the same UIA / TSF infrastructure.


### 
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: (1) Flutter's AXPlatformNodeWin does not properly expose IValueProvider/ITextProvider patterns for UIA; (2) Flutter uses legacy IMM32 instead of TSF, blocking TSF-based tools like Win+H dictation. A comment suggests Flutter turns on the accessibility tree lazily, preventing dictation tools from interacting with text fields.

On iOS this means that the rotor does not show the "text selection" option when the text has

title: [Android] When using TextInputType.none pasting from physical keyboard does not work until the on screen keyboard is shown at least once
comment_summary: Comments confirm the bug is reproducible on physical Android devices with a hardware keyboard connected via USB-C (not on emulators or Mac+Android setups). The issue is that when `TextInputType.none` is set, `Ctrl+V` key events are not received by the framework until at least one character has been typed or the on-screen keyboard shown once. Investigation shows the first `Ctrl+V` is simply not forwarded from the Android embedder; subsequent ones work. Routed to the engine team (Android embedder). Status: awaiting engine investigation.

title: [Web] Korean IME composing range assertion failure after window blur (Cmd+Tab) during composition
body:
## Steps to reproduce

1. Run a Flutter Web app with a `TextField` on Chrome (macOS)
2. Switch to Korean IME and start typing (e.g., type "한")
3. **While the character is still composing** (cursor is on the character, not yet committed), press **Cmd+Tab** to switch to another app
4. Switch back to the Flutter Web app
5. Press **Backspace**

## Expected results

Backspace should delete the composed character normally.

## Actual results

Backspace does not work. The following assertion error is thrown:

```
═══ EXCEPTION CAUGHT BY SERVICES LIBRARY ═══
Failed assertion: line 1155 pos 7: 'googValue.googcomposing.end <= googValue.text.length'

composingBase: 1, composingExtent: 2, text: 한
```

The `composing` range (1–2) exceeds `text.length` (1), causing an assertion failure. After this, Backspace and other key inputs stop working.

## Root cause analysis

When the browser window loses focus (`window.blur`), a `compositionend` event fires. This removes the composing text from the DOM, but
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Reproduction confirmed on Flutter 3.41.2, macOS 26.3, Chrome with Korean 2-Set IME. A community contributor expressed interest in fixing it as they use a Korean Flutter Web app in production. Routed to team-web.

title: TextField: allow overriding contextMenuBuilder globally via ThemeData
body:
### Use case

In certain Android app configurations, it is desirable to suppress the long-press context menu on text fields entirely — for example, in focused or restricted UI environments where exposing system-level UI (share sheet, app chooser dialogs) is undesirable or disruptive to the user experience.

The only option today is to pass `contextMenuBuilder` explicitly to every `TextField` and `TextFormField` in the codebase, which is impractical at scale. More critically, this approach does not cover Flutter's own widgets that embed `TextField` or `TextFormField` internally, such as `TimePickerDialog`, `DatePickerDialog`, `SearchAnchor`, `DropdownMenu`, or any third-party package using `TextField` internally. There is no way to suppress the context menu in those without forking Flutter or the package.

A wrapper widget approach (e.g. a custom `AppTextField`) was considered, but it only solves the problem for widgets you own — not for Flutter-internal or third-party widgets. There is
...[truncated]
comment_summary: A community contributor volunteered to implement the proposal. Triage noted this involves a public API change to `TextSelectionThemeData`, requiring team review before any PR.

title: [webview_flutter]The input that gains focus on the Android side cannot scroll to the visible area.
comment_summary: Comments confirm the issue reproduces on Android with resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false and that even setting it to true (the default) does not fully scroll the input into view. iOS handles the scroll natively via WKWebView and works correctly. The fyi-text-input label was removed because this is considered a WebView input tag issue, not a Flutter text input issue.

title: Autocomplete blocks keyboard navigation using TAB
body:
### Steps to reproduce

Hello!

When using the Autocomplete widget, the keyboard navigation using TAB is sometimes blocked. It seems to only be an issue if the next widget is disabled.

Here's the Flutter version I use:

Tools • Dart 3.11.1 • DevTools 2.54.1`

### Expected results

The expected behavior appears only when the next widget is enabled. Pressing TAB should navigate to the next enabled widget:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f382e6e4-fbc2-44ce-ae98-8f2ebd47e5d0)

### Actual results

The issue seems to only appear when the next widget is disabled. In this case, pressing TAB does not navigate to the next enabled widget; the user is stucked inside the autocomplete:

![Image](https://github.com/user-
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed reproducible on macOS. Root cause identified as Autocomplete failing to advance TAB focus when the next widget is disabled — the focus cycle gets trapped inside the autocomplete. The issue was rerouted from framework team to team-text-input. A PR #159455 was referenced. No fix landed; open.

comment_summary: Comments confirm that OTP autofill from SMS does not work on iOS 18.7.6 (iPhone XS, which cannot be updated beyond that version), but a tester on iOS 18.0.1 (iPhone 13) could not reproduce it. Another commenter reproduces the issue on iOS 26.3.1 (master channel) on an iPhone 15: the Flutter framework receives no `flutter/textinput` message when the OTP suggestion is tapped, pointing to a bug in the iOS embedder. The pre-tap backspace workaround confirms the field is empty before the autofill attempt. Status: open, iOS embedder bug identified, no fix or PR.

title: iOS: NSJSONSerialization crash when deleting SMP characters
body:
**Describe the bug**
When deleting Supplementary Multilingual Plane (SMP) characters on iOS, the text input system leaves orphaned UTF-16 surrogates in the NSString. This causes NSJSONSerialization to fail with "failed to convert to UTF8" error and crash the app.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create a Flutter app with a TextField or text input widget
2. On an iOS device, paste this string: **ᴿ᭄𝑹ahul Rajput**
3. Delete it character by character until only 1-2 characters remain
4. App crashes with: "Invalid JSON message, encoding failed: (null)"

**Expected behavior**
Text input should handle surrogate pairs correctly without crashing

**Actual behavior**
NSJSONSerialization rejects the string because it contains lone (unpaired) surrogates from incomplete SMP character encoding

**Proposed Solution**
Before JSON serialization, validate and sanitize UTF-16 surrogates:
- Drop lone/orphaned surrogates
- Keep valid surrogate pairs
- Preserve all BMP characters
- Remap text-editing cursor indice
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Reproduced on both latest stable and master on iOS. A contributor implemented a fix in the engine (`FlutterCodecs.mm`) on a fork and submitted to Gerrit mirrors; a team member redirected them to submit a PR against the monorepo at `engine/src/flutter/shell/platform/darwin/common/framework/Source/FlutterCodecs.mm`. A pre-triage note suggests it may be a bug in `UITextInput.deleteBackward` rather than a duplicate of #179727. Status: P1, has a proposed fix pending a properly submitted PR.

title: [A11y] Unify Talkback and voice over Textfield announcements
comment_summary: Discussion clarifies the goal is to give Flutter Android developers the ability to set strings announced before the role announcement, matching what native Android and iOS can do. As-is, both label and hint are announced after role on Android, while label is announced before on iOS.

title: [accessibility][Android] Unable to provide an accessibility label for a TextField
comment_summary: Reproduced on physical Pixel Fold running Android 16 with TalkBack 16.2.0, but not on emulators or older TalkBack versions. Reverting TalkBack to 16.0.0 fixes the issue. Identified as likely a TalkBack 16.2.0 regression in how it interprets Flutter's accessibility tree.

title: Vertical baseline alignment mismatch between Text and collapsed TextField when changing TextLeadingDistribution
comment_summary: Comments are only triage routing: the issue was forwarded to the framework team and CC'd to @LongCatIsLooong. No root cause analysis or fix discussed.

title: [macOS] [Desktop] WebView keep receive Enter Keydown event  when web page has no input view
comment_summary: Root cause identified in comments: both Flutter and WebKit dispatch keyboard events asynchronously; when Flutter re-dispatches an unhandled Enter key, WebKit's guard (`m_keyDownEventBeingResent`) is already null, so NSWindow resends the event to the first responder (WebKit) as if it were new, causing infinite repetition. The proposed fix is to rewrite macOS event handling to be synchronous, which is now feasible since the UI and platform threads are merged.

title: [macOS] CGEvent-injected Cmd+V keystrokes lose Command modifier in keyboard handler
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a simple Flutter macOS app with a TextField
2. Install any macOS app that pastes via CGEvent injection (e.g., [Raycast](https://www.raycast.com/) clipboard manager, [Wispr Flow](https://wisprflow.com/), [SuperWhisper](https://superwhisper.com/))
3. Focus the Flutter TextField
4. Use the third-party app to paste clipboard content (which simulates Cmd+V via CGEventPost)

### Expected results
Clipboard content is pasted into the TextField (same as pressing physical Cmd+V).

### Actual results
In TextField: nothing is inserted, or a lowercase "v" character appears
Physical Cmd+V works correctly in the same field
The same CGEvent-injected Cmd+V works correctly in native macOS apps (TextEdit, Safari, Notes, etc.) and even other Electron apps

### Root cause analysis
Third-party macOS apps simulate Cmd+V by:

**Copying text to the system clipboard**
Injecting a Cmd+V keystroke via CGEventCreateKeyboardEvent + CGEventPost
The CGEvent carries the correct Comman
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified: CGEvent-injected Cmd+V keystrokes have the Command modifier flag present at the NSEvent level (performKeyEquivalent) but Flutter's internal keyboard processing loses the modifier state for synthetic events. A community commenter identified an additional pattern: posting flagsChanged events before the keyDown event (rather than only setting flags on the keyDown) may help, as some frameworks only update modifier state from flagsChanged transitions. Confirmed on Flutter 3.41.6. A workaround via performKeyEquivalent override in MainFlutterWindow.swift is documented in the issue body.

title: TextField.obscureText does not correctly handle complex characters like 👨‍👩‍👦
comment_summary: Comments reveal the current behavior (8 dots for family emoji) was previously intentional. After offline discussion, the team agreed Flutter should show a single obscured character per extended grapheme cluster. Chromium made a similar change.

title: TextField with `canRequestFocus: false` has inconsistent behavior across platforms
body:
## Background [**TextField**](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField-class.html) has several boolean flags that share similar but distinct purposes. - Setting [**enabled**](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField/enabled.html) to `false` grays it out, and it doesn't respond to any attempt to tap or scroll the text. - When [**readOnly**](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField/readOnly.html) is `true`, the text can be scrolled and selected, but not edited. - When [**canRequestFocus**](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField/canRequestFocus.html) is `false`, the text can be scrolled but not focused. Here's a [**DartPad sample**](https://dartpad.dev/?id=e334b204aaf8b3887005fe4a5c9aa48d) that shows each property in action.
## Problem ### Web When using `canRequestFocus: false`, the text field can scroll but no other type of interaction is possible. Setting `readOnly` as `true` or `false` doesn't change any behavio ...[truncated]
comment_summary: Confirmed: macOS shows context menu and allows paste with canRequestFocus:false, same as Windows. Team agrees on normalizing to web behavior (scroll only, no context menu, no selection handles). Fix deferred until after decoupling work in #184093 is complete.

title: [Android 17] Don't show password characters when using a physical keyboard
body:
# Background

In Android 17, text fields with obscured text (e.g. passwords) no longer show the last inputted character briefly when using a physical keyboard.

> [!NOTE]
> Typing with the soft keyboard continues to briefly show the last character, even if a physical keyboard is attached.

# Work

Flutter's text fields should be updated accordingly:

1. [`EditableText.obscureText`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/EditableText/obscureText.html)
2. [`TextField.obscureText`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/material/TextField/obscureText.html)
3. etc...

When `obscureText` is true and the input source is a physical keyboard, Flutter should skip the timer-based "show last character" logic currently used for soft keyboards.

One solution _might_ be to update `EditableText` to listen to hardware key events. If it receives a hardware key event, the character should not be briefly shown.

# Resources

https://developer.android.com/about/versions/17/behavior-changes-17#hide-pwd-kbd

title: [Android] Tapping Shift gets stuck into change-selection state
body:
This is a fairly recent regression. The behavior it causes is extremely annoying when a user runs into it. After some user reports, I'd made a previous attempt to find a repro but not succeeded; thanks to @GrzegorzDyrda (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/98720#issuecomment-4202898175) for pointing out Shift as the key ingredient.

### Repro
1. Open up a Flutter app on Android, with Gboard as the keyboard (e.g., a Pixel), and focus a text input.
2. Type a couple of words.
3. Tap earlier in the text input, then back at the end. The cursor will move where you tap.
4. Now tap the Shift key.
5. Again tap earlier in the text input, then back at the end.
6. **Expected:** Again the cursor moves where you tap.
7. **Actual:** A text selection opens up. One end of the selection moves where you tap; the other end remains where the cursor was. The text input remains in this state no matter how many times you tap, double-tap, etc., trying to get back to normal.

The impact is that it becomes
...[truncated]
comment_summary: Root cause identified by a community member: recent Gboard versions started sending KEYCODE_SHIFT_LEFT key down/up events through InputConnection when the user taps the on-screen Shift key. Flutter's KeyEmbedderResponder synthesizes a corresponding right-shift key down event but not the key up event, leaving the framework believing a shift key is held down, which causes subsequent taps to be treated as shift-extend-selection. The trigger could be a Gboard update rather than a Flutter regression. Affects Gboard on Android; workarounds include pressing space or backspace to reset state.

title: Support Android Restrict Implicit URI Grants
body:
New behavior coming in Android 18:

> Currently, if an app launches an intent with a URI that has the action Send, SendMultiple, or ImageCapture, the system automatically grants the read and write URI permissions to the target app. We plan to change this behavior in Android 18. For this reason, we recommend that apps explicitly grant the relevant URI permissions instead of relying on the system to grant them.

https://developer.android.com/about/versions/17/behavior-changes-all#restrict-implicit-uri-grants

- [ ] Update the image_picker plugin to explicitly grant URI permissions for its [ACTION_IMAGE_CAPTURE intents](https://github.com/flutter/packages/blob
...[truncated]

title: [Android 17] Switch to the system context menu
body:
# Background

### Android 16

Android 16 and below shows an “App pasted from your clipboard” toast when an app pastes content from another app.

### Android 17

Android 17 moves the text selection toolbar from the app’s process to a system process. This lets Android distinguish between programmatic pastes and user-initiated pastes. Pastes that are determined to be user-initiated will no longer trigger the paste toast.

However, Flutter doesn't benefit from this usability improvement since it renders its own text selection context menu on Android.

# Work

Flutter should use Android's text selection toolbar. This would ensure that users do not see the paste toast for user-initiated pastes.

# Resources

Google-internal doc: [go/flutter-android-secure-paste](http://goto.google.com/flutter-android-secure-paste)

iOS secure paste, which updated Flutter to use iOS's system context menu: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/103163

Android's docs on context menus: https://developer.andr
...[truncated]

title: Text selection context menu does not reappear on non-fling scroll end
body:
## Description

When a context menu (toolbar) is visible and the user performs a short, non-fling scroll that keeps the selection in view, the toolbar does not re-appear after the scroll ends. A subsequent scroll causes the toolbar to appear at the beginning and remain visible throughout the entire scroll, overlapping UI elements like AppBars.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Open an app with a scrollable TextField containing enough text to scroll (e.g., TextField(maxLines: 5, controller: TextEditingController(text: 'Hello world! ' * 200)))
2. Long press to select a word and show the context menu
3. Perform a very short, slow scroll (non-fling) — just enough to move the content slightly while keeping the selection in view
4. Release the gesture

### Expected: 
The toolbar re-appears after the scroll ends since the selection is still in view.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1e991f0c-7abd-4761-b409-48ad1ab5a667

### Actual: 
The toolbar stays hidden. A second scroll causes the tool
